title: Plant Disease Prediction with the Help of Deep Learning based on the Analysis of Leaf Images
authors: C. Balakumar, M. Jenastin
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The issue of plant diseases remains a significant threat to the worldwide food production and requires solutions to disease detection that are fast, accurate and scalable. Conventionally applied methods of diagnosis, including: hand field scouting and lab tests, are too slow, labor-intensive, and subject to human error, particularly during large scale cultivation. Recent technological progress in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) has brought in the groundbreaking solutions to early and accurate prediction of the disease. In this paper, the author will review how AI-based models, such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Random Forest, Support Vector Machines (SVM), and hybrid deep learning models can be used to detect plant diseases on the basis of visual information, as well as environmental data. These models are very productive in recognizing patterns, automated feature extraction, and real-time prediction, and they are frequently much more precise than their human counterparts. Early warning systems and precision agriculture are further improved by the integration of the IoT sensors, drones, and satellite imagery. Regardless of the issues concerning the lack of data, model generalization, computational cost, and adoption of AI and ML on a farmer level, there are considerable prospects to enhance crop protection, minimizing yield losses, and sustainable agriculture. The paper identifies the current progress, limitations and future research in the development of strong, availability and scaleable AI-based plant disease prediction systems.

title: Choosing the Best Model to Handle Small Data
authors: Alexander Sak, Irina Sak
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This article examines Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) methods for determining the efficiency of university faculty members. DEA includes several mathematical models, from which it is necessary to choose the most suitable one for the best grouping of faculty. The article also discusses the principal component analysis method (PCA), used to reduce data dimensionality. This allows us to consider a greater number of features, which provides a more objective approach to evaluating faculty efficiency. To scale the classification results obtained by DEA methods, the possibilities of machine learning and neural network methods are explored on a small dataset. Both traditional machine learning methods are considered: Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Naïve Bayes, as well as three-layer fully connected neural networks and graph convolutional neural networks, including in combination with a random forest in the output layer.

title: IEEE Standard for Framework for Reliability Prediction of Hardware
authors: N/A
publish: N/A
abstract: This standard covers The framework for the reliability prediction for electronic hardware is covered in this standard. The required elements for an understandable and credible reliability prediction with information to evaluate the prediction results and to assess their usability for the intended application are identified. A reliability prediction generated according to this standard would have sufficient information concerning inputs, assumptions, data sources, methodologies, and uncertainties so that the risk associated with using the prediction results can be considered. This document does not provide instructions for how to perform a reliability prediction and does not make recommendations about the use of a specific methodology.

title: An Intelligent Adaptive Access Control Framework using Policy Gradient Reinforcement Learning
authors: F Rahman, Lalnunthari, Najmitdinov Akhadkhon, Mohammed Ridha Abbas Yousif, Bharani B R, M. Murali
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The adaptive access control mechanisms combined with policy gradient reinforcement learning can dynamically adjust the behavior of users in real time. The approach enables intelligent, situational decision-making in hospital information systems, which promotes their safety and effectiveness. The classical models of access control within healthcare are highly founded on the predetermined positions and set, rule-based permissions. These systems do not react to new threats and insider anabiosis, as well as unknown access patterns, and as such, are prone to incredibly dynamic clinical environments. In order to address these deficiencies, the Policy Gradient-based Dynamic Role Assignment framework applies reinforcement learning to continue maximizing role assignment. It relies on policy gradient techniques, through which access privileges are provided based on user actions, the context, and the past trend of the behavior in real-time. The Policy Gradient-based Dynamic Role Assignment is not responsive to risk scenarios by strictly separating roles. Nonetheless, it distributes them with dynamism as per the risks and needs available. An access decision making process that is context-sensitive and can increase the level of data security because only the authorized personnel can access sensitive patient data in the right circumstances. The beneficial effects of the supplementary policy entries on healthcare organizations are the heightened compliance of regulations, less breaches of policies, and enhanced access control. Past researches suggest adaptive role shifting within a dynamic healthcare context to be advantageous, improves the protection of confidentiality, and eases clinical practice. The dynamism of the conventional access control systems in clouds of healthcare information technology is developed into a strong and developed model which is known as the PG-DRA model.

title: Modeling the Operation of a TGM-96B Steam Boiler Equipped with a Condensing Heat Utilizer
authors: I.L. Ionkin, O.A. Urvachev, V.M. Supranov, D.A. Khokhlov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: A computational study was performed to assess the impact of a condensing heat utilizer (CHU) with air preheating and humidification on the operation of a natural-gas-fired TGM-96B boiler. The study was carried out using a previously developed validated model implemented in the Boiler Designer software package. The calculations show that heating and humidifying the cold air supplied to the boiler by means of the CHU slightly reduces the boiler efficiency; however, the net useful heat recovered in the CHU more than compensates for this loss.

title: Wrapper Model based Feature Selection On Diversity Dataset
authors: Chithirala Bala Subramanyam, Hemanta Kumar Bhuyan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Many Approaches for resolving different issues have been made possible by machine learning. Feature selection is a key component of machine learning for many data analysis tasks. It is necessary to select specific features from a pool based on data analysis, even if the approach is novel. The main goal of this feature selection strategy is to reduce dimensionality while maintaining high accuracy across different feature selection methods. Thus, the machine learning model is considered, including wrapper techniques that efficiently assess the dataset and aim to outperform current approaches. A specific dataset is used to illustrate the suggested model, which is assessed using a variety of machine learning techniques based on wrapper methods. A few features are selected from the feature sets of various datasets, and the results are compared.

title: Information Technologies in Organizing Group Activities of Students in Engineering Education
authors: Yulia Dianova, Yekaterina Feoktistova, Konstantin Nosov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The work demonstrates methods for organizing student learning activities in engineering geometry and computer graphics classes. The emphasis is placed on the use of digital educational resources in active learning methodologies through the organization of group work. The research presents the interconnection between group types of learning activities and digital educational resources in the context of their functional purpose. The proposed forms of work make it possible to significantly increase student motivation in practicing basic skills for problem-solving, ensure guaranteed participation in teamwork and the achievement of intermediate results, and encourage learners to reflect on their own actions while improving their computer graphics skills. These working methodologies have been tested in the organization of instruction at Perm Polytechnic University.

title: Design and SAR Analysis of a Compact Polyester-based 2×2 Textile MIMO Antenna for Biomedical Applications
authors: Ms.S.Leena Gifty, K. Kalai Selvi, M.Vijayaraj
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: This paper presents a compact 2 X 2 MIMO antenna made of polyester textile material for biomedical data transmission in WBAN systems along with its design and analysis. Its lightweight, durable, and flexible characteristics are beneficial for wearable applications. A flexible polyester substrate of dielectric permittivity 1.48 and loss tangent 0.01 was used to design the antenna. Each antenna element comprises microstrip patches to obtain dual band operation in the C-band. In order to further enhance impedance matching and radiation performance etched slot structures are used. The antenna elements exhibit stable gain characteristics, extremely a low envelope correlation coefficient (ECC < 0.01), and high isolation. On the other hand, the simulated Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) analysis confirms safe on body operation as the values are significantly lower than the safety limits.

title: Improving Coronary Artery Disease Prediction Accuracy with an Ensemble Approach and Two-Layered Voting Framework
authors: C. Hrishikesava Reddy, B. Saigokul, G. Naga Pavani
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Heart disease remains the leading cause of death worldwide, underscoring the importance of accurate and timely prediction tools. This paper proposes a step-forward model to predict coronary heart disease using ensemble learning and TLV. Both generalization and statistical diversity are enhanced through a mixed dataset comprising 297 records from UCI and 70,000 records from Kaggle. Feature selection techniques such as Chi2, MI, and ANOVA are applied to reduce dimensionality and identify informative attributes. The classifiers employed include decision trees, support vector machines, random forests, multilayer perceptrons, TLV-Soft, TLV-Hard, and batch classifiers. The proposed batch variant outperforms SVM (65.6%) and random forest (85.9%), achieving an accuracy of 95.3%. The assessment of the proposed framework is supported by extensive experimental evaluation using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score, demonstrating clear evidence of model reliability. Unlike seminar-style reviews, this work presents a rigorously tested predictive system with validated outcomes, achieving 95.3% accuracy and outperforming traditional classifiers. In this approach, bagging (random forest decision trees) and LightGBM are used as meta-learners. By combining predictions from diverse base models and learning at the meta-level, the framework improves robustness and reliability while reducing bias and variability. This methodology offers a scalable and efficient solution for early diagnosis of coronary heart disease in clinical decision support systems.

title: Computational Nonsmooth Analysis: Modern applications and recent developments [Special Issue on the Mathematics of Deep Learning]
authors: Lai Tian, Anthony Man-Cho So
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Magazine
abstract: Neural networks (NNs) have transformed the field of artificial intelligence, showcasing remarkable abilities in learning complex representations, scaling to large datasets, and driving advancements across a wide range of applications. The training of these networks often involves optimizing functions that are not only nonconvex but possibly also nonsmooth, which introduces significant challenges in algorithm design and analysis. In this article, we provide an overview of recent advancements in the theoretical and computational aspects of nonsmooth optimization, thereby highlighting an area of growing importance that we refer to as “computational nonsmooth analysis.” We also demonstrate the relevance of these advancements in some representative applications. Lastly, we discuss some fundamental open questions in this area, the answers to which will impact the use and understanding of nonsmooth models in modern deep learning and signal processing.

title: Digital Financial Platforms Enhancing Supply Chain Financing Performance through Inter-Organizational Integration
authors: Ahmad Ali, Mohammed H M Qeshta, Amro Saleem Alamaren, Khaled M Algraibeh
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The sudden growth of digital financial forums has particularly changed the supply chain financing operations where automated operations, shared financial information, and platform-based coordination have taken the center stage of financial performance. Supply chain financing systems also are often faced with inefficiencies as a result of piece meal information, sluggish processing, and lack of coordination between partners. The digital financial platforms offer combined space between buyers, suppliers, and financial institutions that facilitates fast-tracked financing processes and an increase in financial collaboration. A questionnaire was developed in questionnaire form and sent to the professionals involved in supply chain finance and digital financial systems, almost 290 valid answers were received. Data were analyzed using partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modelling (PLS-SEM) using SmartPLS. The results show that Digital Financial Platform Capability has a high positive impact on Inter-Organizational Financial Integration, which in turn has a positive impact on Supply Chain Financing Performance. Also, the platform capability has a direct positive impact on the financing performance. The mediation analysis verifies the fact that Inter-Organizational Financial Integration mediates the relationship between platform capability and financing performance whereby the source of mediation is partial. The findings can be added to the body of FinTech and supply chain finance research as they explain how online platforms can improve financial coordination and performance. Practically, the study can help companies aiming at enhancing the efficiency of financing with the help of digital financial ecosystems.

title: Optimization Of Detection and Calculation of Overlapping Mango Fruits and Illumination Variations Using Yolov11-Seg with Unsharp Masking Algorithm, Gamma Correction
authors: Muh. Ikhsan, Novy Nur R.A Mokobombang
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study addresses the challenges of mango fruit detection and counting in complex horticultural environments, with a particular focus on the problems of occlusion (overlap) and extreme illumination variation in overhead drone imagery. To overcome the limitations of standard detection models in low-light conditions and for closely spaced fruits, we propose an integrated framework that combines YOLOv11-segmentation with advanced image enhancement techniques, namely Gamma Correction (GC) and Unsharp Masking (UM). Gamma Correction was used to normalize the light distribution in shadow areas, while Unsharp Masking supported by Bilateral Filtering enhanced edge contrast to distinguish mangoes from the dense foliage of the canopy. The methodology involved circular drone scanning at a constant distance of of 1.5 m to capture a diverse dataset in Maros Regency, Indonesia. The experimental results showed that the improved YOLOv11-segmentation model significantly outperformed the baseline model, achieving a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 95.5%, an F1-score of 93.45%, and successfully identifying 2,085 true positive instances. This integration of pixel-level segmentation and preprocessing enabled the system to detect hidden fruits that the standard model failed to recognize, thus providing a robust and precise automated solution for crop management and yield estimation in the horticulture industry.

title: Startup-Q: A Hybrid Quantum-Classical Framework for High-Fidelity Venture Capital Forecasting using ZZ-Feature Mapping
authors: S Selvaraj, S Hemalatha, K Jothimani, M Yeshwanth, M K Swadhini, K Naren Karthick
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Predicting the success of early-stage startups remains a foundational challenge in venture capital due to nonlinear market dynamics and high-dimensional growth signals. This paper introduces Startup-Q, a hybrid algorithmic framework that integrates classical ensemble methods with Quantum Machine Learning primitives. The main contribution of this work is the development of a co-processing layer that maps financial metadata into a high-dimensional Hilbert space using ZZ-Feature Mapping. Utilizing a dataset of 49,770 startup records, we engineer a SuccessScore to capture multidimensional growth. While classical models achieve robust performance, the proposed Quantum-inspired Support Vector Regression demonstrates superior resilience to recent market shifts from 2023 to 2024. This research confirms that quantum-enhanced features are more resilient to temporal data drifts, achieving higher predictive accuracy on contemporary datasets compared to traditional linear baselines. These findings highlight the potential of hybrid architectures in providing precise risk assessment tools for the venture capital ecosystem.

title: Optimal Trajectory Generation and Feedback Tension Control of a Robotic Space Tether Launching System
authors: Shuai Liu, Sichen Wang, Molong Duan
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: In space, accurately delivering payloads enables inspection, monitoring, and debris-removal missions. Space tether systems have long been explored for momentum exchange, deorbiting, and inspection. However, existing methods have not considered using a robotic arm in a space tether system to actively accelerate and launch payloads. Also, most prior models neglect the tether mass or approximate it as massless. To close these gaps, a robotic space tether launching system with a unified modelingcontrol framework is established. A full nonlinear model is derived considering the tether’s mass distributions and directional tension coupling. An optimal control scheme is formulated that combines feedforward profiles with tension-feedback regulation for robustness to uncertainties and disturbances. Simulation results verify accurate targeting with specified terminal velocity, enabling propellant-free, high-precision deployment.

title: A Cooperative Transport System of Vehicle and Drones in Obstacles Areas
authors: Xinghan Sun, Yi Zhou, Dongsheng Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Researchers have been concentrating on the cooperative system of multi-rotor drones and ground vehicles for transport and logistics in recent years, which could overcome the short range and insufficient load capacity of drones. These problems are known as Flying Sidekick Traveling Salesman Problem (FSTSP) or Vehicle Routing Problem with Drones (VRPD). However, existing research mainly considers the operations in a barrier-free or road environment. Complex obstacle areas such as disaster terrains can really play to the advantage of drones. Therefore, this paper proposes a solution to the Vehicle Routing Problem with Drones in Obstacle Areas (VRPD-O), which introduces environmental obstacles the vehicle can’t pass through. To solve the problem, firstly, the mathematical model of VRPD-O is established in the form of mixed integer linear programming equations, considering the special case of target nodes locating within obstacle areas. Second, the alternative paths of ground vehicle in obstacle areas are obtained using path planning algorithm. In addition, a heuristic optimization algorithm based on cluster analysis is proposed. Last, numerical experiments under different obstacle scenarios are carried out to verify the effectiveness of the proposed solution. The optimization algorithm is proved to reduce the computation time, and its reasonable parameter analysis is also studied.

title: A Comparative Study of Multi-Horizon Directional Prediction for Cryptocurrency Markets
authors: Maria Frasca, Filippo Dal Lago, Davide La Torre
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Directional forecasting in cryptocurrency markets is challenging due to high volatility, strong noise components and pronounced horizon dependence. This work investigates multi-horizon directional predictability of ETHUSDT daily prices within a unified and temporally consistent evaluation framework. The forecasting task is formulated as a horizon-aware classification problem and addressed by comparing per-horizon tree-based models with multi-task deep sequence architectures, including LSTM-, Transformer- and NBeats-like models. To reduce label noise and focus on economically meaningful movements, the analysis is restricted to periods characterized by sufficiently large price changes, and decision thresholds are selected through validation-driven tuning rather than fixed a priori. Experimental results reveal a clear dependence on the prediction horizon: short-term horizons remain largely dominated by noise, while statistically and practically meaningful directional signals emerge at medium-to-long horizons <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(20-30$</tex> days). In this regime, XGBoost consistently achieves the strongest performance, reaching ROC-AUC values up to 0.737, while LSTMbased models represent the most competitive deep learning alternative. Feature importance analysis based on gaindriven measures highlights the relevance of funding rates, technical momentum indicators and on-chain network variables, indicating that medium-term price dynamics are influenced by a combination of derivative market sentiment, trend formation and underlying blockchain activity. These findings suggest that horizon-aware modeling and selective forecasting can uncover non-trivial directional information in cryptocurrency markets, with potential implications for medium-term trading, risk management and the design of regime-aware decision-support systems.

title: Parameter Identification Method for Double-Sided Lcc Wireless Power Transfer Systems
authors: Xiaobo Wan, Haiyun Zhu
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: During operation of wireless power transfer (WPT) systems, variations in mutual inductance and load parameters adversely affect the transmission efficiency and output power. To address this issue, parameter identification techniques can be employed to simultaneously estimate both parameters, thereby aiding in the formulation of control strategies and enhancing overall system performance. Existing parameter identification methods often rely on additional communication circuits, which suffer from high cost, susceptibility to interference, and insufficient real-time performance. Therefore, this paper proposes a combined method for mutual inductance and load identification utilizing the Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) and the Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF). First, a state-space model of the system is established, with the parameters to be identified transformed into state variables. The UKF algorithm is then applied. By leveraging the superior global optimization capability and simple parameter setting of the WOA, the noise covariance matrix within the UKF is optimized, significantly enhancing identification stability. This method requires only the measurement of the voltage of the primary-side compensation capacitor to achieve high-precision simultaneous estimation of mutual inductance and load parameters. Finally, multi-condition simulations on the MATLAB/Simulink platform validate that the proposed method maintains high identification accuracy under varying loads, different mutual inductance conditions, and dynamic load switching scenarios. The maximum relative parameter identification error remains below tow percent, without the need for additional communication modules. This approach provides effective support for the efficient and stable operation of WPT systems under dynamic conditions.

title: Graph Neural Transformer with Structural Attention Bias for Robust Multi-Disease Soybean Leaf Classification
authors: K Venkataramana, M. Shanmugapriya, Mansi Aggarwal, R Sujitha
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The soya foliar diseases lower the crop production and need automated diagnostic systems to apply precision agriculture. Yet, the visual overlapping symptoms, the patterns of distribution of lesions, and field variability create a major challenge to traditional convolutional and transformer-based models. In this work, a Graph Neural Transformer (GNT-Soy) is suggested to classify soybean leaves with many diseases and combine relational inductive bias with global self-attention. The architecture initially obtains patch-level features in the form of a convolutional stem, builds a patch graph based on spatial adjacency and feature similarity, updates node features with Graph Attention Networks (GAT), and then uses graph-biased transformer encoding to reason about the global context. A 70/15/15 stratified split was used as model on a six-class (Healthy, Soybean Rust, Bacterial Blight, Frogeye Leaf Spot, Downy Mildew, Target Spot) soybean dataset. The results of experiments are higher with 96.84 of accuracy +- 0.41, Macro-<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{F} 1=0.964+-0.005$</tex>, AUROC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.991+-0.002$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MCC}=0.956$</tex>, and Cohen Kappa <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.955$</tex>. The presented method has better accuracy in classification (with 2.78 the difference with a vanilla Vision Transformer 94.06% accuracy) and less Expected Calibration Error (ECE) of 0.017, in contrast with 0.026, which increases the predictive reliability. The analysis of ablation proves that graph construction and graph-biased attention add a cumulative <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{+ 2. 8 \%}$</tex> accuracy improvement to transformer-only baselines. The results show that multi-disease soybean leaf classification with a combination of transformer attention and graph-based structural modeling is far better in terms of discrimination and calibration.

title: Deep Intelligent Optimized Privacy Preserving in Internet of Things Cloud System
authors: S. Mary Evanchalin, R. Mallika Pandeeswari, R. Ravi
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The cloud system usage and application have become a trending research area in smart applications. However, the Internet of Things (IoT) has become a more widely used database due to its availability and flexibility. However, securing IoT data is a challenging task amid various abnormal events. Even though it is challenging, it is one of the most important tasks for maintaining data integrity in cloud storage. This work introduces a novel Vulture Long Former Privacy Network (VLFPN) to protect data from third parties. After predicting abnormal user activity, the cloud database was secured by enabling the user trust system, which stores the user details and ID. After this, the cloud database is accessible only if the authentication layer answers the security question. Through this process, the proposed replica achieves 98.6% risk-detection accuracy and a very low error rate compared to other baselines.

title: Smart Transit: Real-Time Monitoring for Optimized Public Transportation
authors: M. Anusia, Y. Harold Robinson, M. Bagya Lakshmi
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Conventional tracking solutions frequently depend on extra IoT components, such as GPS and GSM modules, which raises implementation costs and creates maintenance issues. This project intends to create a real-time, software-based car tracking system using the Google Maps API and Firebase Real-time Database in order to get around these restrictions. This system uses the GPS embedded into the mobile device to collect coordinates, which are then sent to a Firebase database housed in the cloud for real-time updates. The data is immediately synchronized and shown on a web interface that is connected with Google Maps for live visualization. Without the need for extra hardware, this cloud-based design guarantees effective data transfer, minimal latency, and platform independence. educational, and personal applications. The study comes to the conclusion that integrating Firebase with Google Maps offers a dependable, scalable, and affordable solution for contemporary vehicle tracking and monitoring.

title: FinFET based Breath Analyzer for Rapid Detection of Respiratory Infections
authors: A. Jansi Rani, I. Vivek Anand, R. Thangameena
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The early detection of respiratory issues is often difficult using conventional diagnostic methods, as many existing techniques require complex laboratory procedures, specialized equipment, and invasive testing methods. These limitations can delay diagnosis and increase the cost of medical evaluation. To overcome these challenges, modern research focuses on non-invasive and rapid diagnostic approaches. Nowadays, breath analysis has gained significant attention because human breath contains different Volatile Organic Compounds (VOCs) that can indicate various metabolic and respiratory conditions. In this paper, a biosensor based on a FinFET (Fin Field Effect Transistor) is designed and analyzed to study variations caused by molecules present near the sensing surface. FinFET is considered a high sensitivity device because its three-dimensional multi-gate structure provides strong electrostatic control over the channel region. This characteristic enables the device to detect very small electrical changes occurring at the sensing surface. The device structure is developed and simulated using Synopsys Sentaurus TCAD, which allows detailed analysis of nanoscale semiconductor devices. The sensing region of the device incorporates Hafnium Oxide as a high-k dielectric material to enhance electrical performance and sensing capability. The interaction of VOC molecules with the sensing surface influences the electrical behavior of the device, leading to variations in parameters such as electric current and threshold voltage. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed FinFET-based biosensor exhibits measurable electrical variations corresponding to changes occurring near the sensing region. Therefore, the proposed system highlights the potential of FinFET technology for high sensitivity biosensing applications in future non-invasive respiratory monitoring and healthcare diagnostic systems.

title: ACVA: An Intelligent Voice-Enabled Agricultural Chatbot Using Deep Learning and NLP for Sustainable Farming Support
authors: KALINGARANI G, RAHUL BRITTO I, PUVANESH B A
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Innovation in the agricultural sector is necessary to ensure that there are sustainable production and responsible consumption in the sector. The Smart Agricultural Chatbot Voice Assistant proposed is an application of the Neural Networks of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) to realize real-time decision support to farmers. The system is an agricultural advisor that is virtual and offers individual, location-based advice related to crop choice, pest and disease control, soil wellness, irrigation roster, and market tendencies. The MLP model handles data on a variety of agricultural parameters (soil properties, weather conditions and crop properties) to come up with accurate information, which is based on data. Meanwhile, NLP allows farmers to communicate with the system through naturallanguage queries, which makes the communication process more natural and easier to use. Voice recognition technology also improves the accessibility where farmers can access the advisory services without having to have high levels of digital skills as well as having to have any physical device to interact with. By combining smart data analytics and chat interfaces, the proposed chatbot has the potential to considerably boost the level of productivity, resource optimization, and sustainability practices in a farm. The effectiveness of this can be evidenced by the experimental results of over 92 percent intent recognition accuracy, 1.3 seconds mean response time and 95 percent user satisfaction, which compares to most other available agricultural advisory systems.

title: Researching the Efficiency of Source Code Defect Prediction Using a Modified XGBoost Algorithm
authors: Timur Fatkhulin, Yuri Leokhin, Svetlana Dymkova
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper presents an analysis of modifications to the XGBoost machine learning algorithm. The aim of the study is to improve the efficiency of source code defect prediction using a modified version of the XGBoost algorithm. The relevance of the study is due to the need for timely defect prediction in source code, which allows both enterprise equipment and various critical infrastructure to function in the required mode. It is important to note that in the conditions of the modern information society, the correct functioning of computing systems and network equipment is an integral component. Taking this into account, predicting defects in program code is extremely important. During the study, experiments were conducted to train XGBoost models using the modifications considered. The experimental results were analyzed, and a conclusion was made on the most suitable method of modifying the algorithm for solving the problem of predicting defects in source code. The object of the study is defects in program code. The subject of the study are metrics for assessing the quality of error prediction in source code.

title: Comprehensive Dynamic Modeling and Control of the Flexible Foundation-Active Magnetic Bearings-Rotor System
authors: Yang Yan, Ximing Liu, Kun Wang, Xi Chen, Shiqiang Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Conventional dynamic models lack the capability to incorporate the intricate coupling effects and resulting dynamic uncertainties when the magnetically suspended flywheel (MSF) is supported by a flexible foundation, leading to restricted performance in current vibration suppression methods. To address this challenge, a comprehensive framework for dynamic modeling and computation is presented in this article. This article introduces velocity spinors to describe system motion efficiently and calculate kinetic energy. This approach significantly simplifies the calculation process in modeling the high-dimensional flexible foundation-active magnetic bearings (AMBs)-rotor system without compromising accuracy. With this model, we quantitatively analyze the influence of flexible support characteristics and flywheel control parameters on system stability and coupled vibration. Furthermore, corresponding methods for stable control and vibration suppression are proposed. The accuracy of the established dynamic model and computational framework is validated through experiments performed on the MSF prototype. This model allows researchers to optimize the support characteristics of the flexible foundation and the flywheel control parameters before on-orbit operation, ensuring the stable operation and active vibration suppression of the MSF under varying support conditions.

title: RA-SSU: Towards Fine-Grained Audio-Visual Learning with Region-Aware Sound Source Understanding
authors: Muyi Sun, Yixuan Wang, Hong Wang, Chen Su, Man Zhang, Xingqun Qi, Qi Li, Zhenan Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Audio-Visual Learning (AVL) is one fundamental task of multi-modality learning and embodied intelligence, displaying the vital role in scene understanding and interaction. However, previous researchers mostly focus on exploring downstream tasks from a coarse-grained perspective (e.g., audio-visual correspondence, sound source localization, and audio-visual event localization). Considering providing more specific scene perception details, we newly define a fine-grained Audio-Visual Learning task, termed Region-Aware Sound Source Understanding (RASSU), which aims to achieve region-aware, frame-level, and high-quality sound source understanding. To support this goal, we innovatively construct two corresponding datasets, i.e. finegrained Music (f-Music) and fine-grained Lifescene (f-Lifescene), each containing annotated sound source masks and frame-byframe textual descriptions. The f-Music dataset includes 3,976 samples across 22 scene types related to specific application scenarios, focusing on music scenes with complex instrument mixing. The f-Lifescene dataset contains 6,156 samples across 61 types representing diverse sounding objects in life scenarios. Moreover, we propose SSUFormer, a Sound-Source Understanding Trans- Former benchmark that facilitates both the sound source segmentation and sound region description with a multi-modal input and multi-modal output architecture. Specifically, we design two modules for this framework, Mask Collaboration Module (MCM) and Mixture of Hierarchical-prompted Experts (MoHE), to respectively enhance the accuracy and enrich the elaboration of the sound source description. Extensive experiments are conducted on our two datasets to verify the feasibility of the task, evaluate the availability of the datasets, and demonstrate the superiority of the SSUFormer, which achieves SOTA performance on the Sound Source Understanding benchmark

title: RD-VTA: Rule-Data Guided Video-to-Audio Generation for Fine-Grained Footstep Sound
authors: Qiutang Qi, Haonan Cheng, Hengyan Huang, Long Ye, Shaobin Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: It is challenging to implement visually guided fine-grained footstep sounds based on a limited number of samples in complex scenes. This is due to the interference of redundant information in the complex background of the visual scene for audiovisual mapping. As well as the complex coupling of sound features makes audiovisual fine-grained linear mapping difficult. To address the mentioned problems, we propose an automated video-to-audio generation method (RD-VTA) for footstep sound that incorporates data-driven and rule-based modelling approaches. First, we design a data-driven masked footstep sound generation network (DM-AFSG) to acquire audiovisual temporal concordance. The network is capable of separating visual sound objects, reducing background redundant interference, and generating initial target sounds that capture temporal cues. Secondly, a rule-based fine-grained footstep sound adjustment method (RT-AFSG) is designed based on visual guides such as material, motion type and displacement distance. The proposed RT-AFSG effectively achieves diverse sounds with a limited number of sound samples through sound texture analysis and modification. Moreover, it constructs the mapping relationship between different visual cues and footstep sounds, and realizes the fine variation of footstep sounds. To adequately validate the effectiveness of the method in terms of audiovisual temporal consistency and content granularity, we perform objective synchronization metrics and subjective human evaluation on the footsteps audiovisual dataset VAFoot. The experimental results show that the method obtains an average of 5% improvement in sound synchronization performance and significantly outperforms several existing methods in terms of sound content granularity. We encourage readers to watch and listen to the footstep sound results on our demo website: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://quinntt.github.io/RD-VTA/</uri>.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classification of Crack Propagation in Asphalt Mixtures Using Cyclic SCB Tests Under Varying Temperature and Notch Depth
authors: Abdul Khalil Ahadi, Erkut Yalcin, Fatih Demir, Ragip Ince, Mehmet Yilmaz
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study presents a data-driven framework supported by fracture mechanics analysis to investigate the temperature-dependent cracking behavior of asphalt mixtures using the Semi-Circular Bending (SCB) test. SCB specimens with varying notch depths were tested at 23°C and 0°C, and key fracture parameters, including elastic modulus, fracture toughness, and fracture energy, were estimated using the Effective Crack Model (ECM) to support interpretation of crack evolution behavior. The results indicate that decreasing temperature increases the variability of fracture parameters, with the elastic modulus showing the most pronounced sensitivity, while fracture toughness exhibits comparatively smaller variation. To enable automated crack-stage identification, a Weighted Residual Attention-Based Convolutional Network (WRAC) was developed to learn visual crack patterns from SCB test images. The model achieved average F-score and accuracy values exceeding 0.85 under both temperature conditions and outperformed a baseline CNN by approximately 5–6%. The findings demonstrate that the proposed framework offers a reliable data-driven approach for identifying crack development stages, while fracture analysis provides physical context for interpreting the observed behavior under different environmental conditions.

title: A novel compact pneumatic turbine-driven transducer for MR Elastography_supp1-3701175.pdf
authors: Albrecht Ingo Schmid
publish: N/A
abstract: Magnetic Resonance Elastography (MRE) can be used to estimate biomechanical parameters, e.g., shear stiffness of human tissue. As such, it is an imaging-based extension of palpation. To perform MRE, mechanical waves are induced into the tissue by transducers or actuators. The resulting periodic microscopic tissue displacements are recorded using phase-contrast magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). Good transducers need sufficient vibration amplitudes and frequency stability with little contributions of higher order harmonics. For clinical use, it needs to be compact for comfort as well as interference with RF coil placement. Safe operation in strong electromagnetic fields is a must. Setup procedures should be simple and quick. Here, a new compact pneumatically driven gravitational transducer (CPT) for MR elastography is presented. It consists of rotating unbalances stacked on either side of a turbine. Additional insertable masses allow adjustment of the vibration amplitude at a set frequency. Additive manufacturing and milling were combined to produce the transducer. The flat design of the CPT allows for placement between body and receiver coil with minimal sensitivity loss in most relevant targets. PID control is used to precisely control vibration frequency, based on optical detection of rotation cycles. It adjusts a valve in the air supply of the turbine. It is constructed using non-magnetic, non-conducting materials, which makes it inherently MRI-safe. The electronics, fittings and valves are placed in a control unit (CU) outside the magnet room. A hose and two optical fibres connect the transducer and the CU. The performance of the transducer was thoroughly bench tested and phantom experiments in the MRI scanner demonstrated the feasibility and robustness of the system. The frequency control was excellent for a pneumatic system (FWHM 0.04-0.20 Hz) across a wide range (50-130 Hz), while amplitudes of higher harmonics are very low (1.9-8.1 % 1<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">st</sup>, 0.2-4.6 % 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nd</sup> order). Imaging data confirm that the CPT generated displacements which could be recorded with MR elastography pulse sequences. In conclusion, we present a new MRE pneumatically driven gravitational transducer, Its advantages are the combination of the following features: It requires no electrical components in the vicinity of the MR scanner. It performs equally well as electromechanically driven systems while it outperforms pneumatic designs in terms of frequency stability. Its compact design allows for flexibility in placement with a variety of RF coils and target tissues in the body. It consists of low-cost components and is easily used. We conclude that the combination of the CPT’s features constitute a substantial improvement in MRE transducer design.

title: Adsorption Performance and Numerical Simulation of Charged Particle Trajectory of IFD Electrostatic Filter Under Electric-Flow Multi-Physical Coupling Fields
authors: Chenyang Liu, Tao Li, Xiaoquan Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: In this paper, a new type of air purification product, IFD(Intense Field Dielectrics) electrostatic filter, is studied on the trajectory of charged particles under the electric-flow coupling fields. The electric-field model optimization and the electric-flow field simulation of IFD insulation structure was carried out by COMSOL. The simulation results show that the electric field distribution in the IFD channel is relatively symmetrical, and the average electric-field in most area is about 2.45kV/mm. The flow field distribution shows that the flow velocity distribution is consistent in the middle of the IFD channel, while the flow velocity at the entrance presents a trend of first rising and then decreasing. The trajectory of charged particles is closely related to particle size, wind speed and applied DC voltage. The capture probability increases with the increase of particle size and decreases with the increase of wind speed. Finally, the IFD electrostatic filter sample was prepared, and the high voltage electrostatic adsorption test was carried out. The test results showed that the overall wind resistance of the IFD electrostatic filter was only 6.4 Pa, and the adsorption efficiency of 2 μm particles could reach 95.7%, respectively. The adsorption efficiency increased with the increase of the charged particle size. The developed IFD product with a high PM2.5 adsorption efficiency can be applied in future industrial field widely.

title: An Optimal Design of Time-of-Use Tariff to Mitigate Overloads From Residential Electric Vehicle Charging on Distribution Networks
authors: Feng Li, Ilhan Kocar, Antoine Lesage-Landry
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: High penetration of electric vehicle (EV) charging creates equipment overload risks in power distribution networks, potentially reducing asset lifetime. This article develops an optimal time-of-use (TOU) tariff strategy to mitigate overloads by shifting residential EV charging demand, without requiring communication infrastructure or direct load control policies. We formulate a bi-level optimization problem to determine optimal rate levels that restore equipment lifetime while minimizing utility costs. The problem is reformulated as a convex optimization with proven convergence properties, enabling an efficient solution for large-scale networks. The solution guarantees that no new overload occurs after shifting the EV loads. A novel time-series analysis method is used to estimate loading impacts and to evaluate the effectiveness of candidate strategies. The proposed strategy has a simple TOU structure consistent with utilities' rate structure in place, making it suitable for adoption. We validate the approach on the IEEE 8500-node test feeder. Results demonstrate that at 80% EV penetration rate, the strategy reduces transformer overload peaks by 20%, while maintaining equipment's nominal lifetime and achieving 42.2% cost savings for utilities compared to early equipment replacement.

title: Exosystem-Based Dynamic Input Control Strategy for Load Mitigation in Variable-Speed Wind Turbines Under Above-Rated Wind Conditions
authors: Shaodong Wang, Qinmin Yang, Shuzong Xie, Wenchao Meng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Despite the significant progress in wind power generation in recent years, how to mitigate the structural fatigue loads on critical components while regulating power output, thereby extending the service life of wind turbines (WTs), remains a highly concerned and challenging research topic. In this article, an exosystem-based dynamic input control strategy is proposed for variable-speed WT (VSWT) systems operating above-rated wind speed to achieve this dual objective. Specifically, the nonlinear VSWT model describing the drive-train dynamics, tower dynamics, and pitch actuator dynamics is first linearized and represented as a linear parameter varying model. Subsequently, an exosystem for simulating the external disturbance signal and the desired reference signal is incorporated into the dynamic input control strategy, aiming to reduce the difficulty of solving multiobjective optimization control problems. In addition, to improve the accuracy and real-time performance of the measured wind speed, the continuous wave light detection and ranging (CW-LIDAR) simulator is used to provide the effective wind speed value, and exosystem dynamics are constructed using the CW-LIDAR measurement data. Finally, comprehensive comparative studies between the proposed scheme and several pitch controllers are conducted under multiple scenarios using the OpenFAST (fatigue, aerodynamics, structures, and turbulence) platform on the NREL 5-MW VSWT, thereby verifying the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: Real-to-Sim-to-Real: Learning Agile and Robust Recovery Skills with Terrain Imagination via Adversarial Imitation
authors: Tangyu Qian, Huayang Yin, Zhen Kan
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Legged animals can self-right and stand up from arbitrary postures across diverse environments. Empowering robots with animal-like recovery capabilities expands their real-world applications. However, existing recovery controllers that rely on predefined trajectories are limited to flat terrain, whereas current learning-based policies often produce overly aggressive or sluggish control outputs, leading to poor hardware performance. To bridge the gap, this paper proposes a novel real-to-sim-to-real learning framework that enables quadruped robots to acquire both agile and robust recovery skills. Unlike previous learning approaches that train from scratch, we employ adversarial imitation learning with hardware-collected demonstrations to promote natural motions and enhance learning efficiency. For robust recovery on complex terrains, a terrain imagination module is integrated to predict key terrain properties via onboard sensing. To ensure reliable sim-to-real transfer, the recovery policy is trained with domain randomization, terrain curriculum learning, and geometry decomposition techniques. The trained policy can be generalized to various quadruped robots and is directly deployed on the Unitree Go2 Hardware. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first time that a small-sized quadruped demonstrates such advanced recovery skills across diverse environments, outperforming prior studies with more powerful hardware. The project page is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://rsr-recovery.github.io/</uri>.

title: Gradient Design and Verification of Lightweight High-Temperature Thermal Protection Components
authors: Haoning Shi, Le Mi, Kun Liu, Teng Liu, Fankong Meng, Yu Jin, Yun Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Manned lunar exploration spacecraft face significant challenges in high-temperature thermal protection due to extremely high heat flux plume during landing and takeoff, as well the high-temperature radiation caused by multiple engine coupling. To meet the high-temperature thermal protection requirement and lightweight constraints of spacecraft, a gradient design method of high-temperature thermal protection component is proposed, which employs lightweight materials with high-performance. A transient thermal analysis model for high-temperature thermal protection components is established by coupling radiation conduction and interlayer thermal conduction. The optimized lightweight design can be conducted based on the established model. Focusing on the typical thermal protection requirements of a manned lunar exploration spacecraft, the design of high-temperature thermal protection components is completed. Experimental verification indicates that the components can satisfy the thermal protection requirements of a surface temperature of $850^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ on the outer surface for 930 seconds, with a temperature rise on the inner side not exceeding $55{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The areal density of the proposed hightemperature thermal protection components is less than 3.2 $\mathrm{kg} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$. This research provides a reference for the design of hightemperature thermal protection systems for spacecraft.

title: An Explainable Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning Approach for Personalized and Health-Constrained Meal Recommendation
authors: Bhavesh P, Nayantra Ramakrishnan, Nagendra Prabhu S, G. Shivashankar, Moorthi K, Vaissnave Vaissnave
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Personalized dietary recommendation systems must balance nutritional adequacy, health constraints, and user preferences while ensuring long-term adherence. However, existing approaches often struggle to model sequential dietary behavior and multi-nutrient dependencies simultaneously. To address these challenges, this work proposes a Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning Model (HRM) for adaptive dietary recommendation. The framework integrates heterogeneous nutritional graph representations with transformer-based semantic food encoding to capture both structured nutritional relationships and contextual food understanding. A high-level policy generates long-term nutritional goals based on user health conditions, while a low-level policy selects meals that satisfy these goals under dietary constraints. The model is optimized using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to enable continuous learning from user feedback and evolving nutritional states. Experiments are conducted on real-world nutritional datasets combined with simulated dietary profiles representing diverse health conditions such as diabetes and general wellness. The proposed HRM is evaluated against heterogeneous graph neural networks, transformer-based recommenders, and neural collaborative filtering. Results show that HRM outperforms all baselines, achieving an 11.3% improvement in cumulative reward and a nutritional balance score of 88.9%. The framework also ensures high dietary safety with a 98.5% allergen-safe recommendation rate and improves user adherence to 92.6%. These findings demonstrate that hierarchical reinforcement learning provides an effective and adaptive framework for personalized dietary planning by jointly optimizing nutritional balance, safety, and user engagement.

title: IoT-Enabled Real-Time Power Quality Anomaly Detection and Disturbance Classification for Ghana/Zambia Distribution Networks
authors: Obed Asamoah Nyarko, Anderson Mwamba, Maxim Andreevich Silaev
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: Power quality monitoring in Sub-Saharan African distribution networks faces significant challenges due to unbalanced loading, long radial feeders, and a lack of automated detection infrastructure. This paper proposes a real-time Power quality monitoring framework using a hybrid convolutional neural network and bidirectional long short-term memory deep learning model designed for both utility and educational applications. Using a digital twin of a modified IEEE 13-bus system, parameterized to reflect Ghanaian and Zambian grid conditions, a dataset of 250 disturbance samples was generated at a 20 kHz sampling rate. By employing a sliding-window augmentation strategy, the proposed model achieved an overall classification accuracy of ninety percent, with near-perfect detection of voltage swells and harmonics. The results demonstrate the model's robustness in handling phase unbalance and short-duration transients. Beyond technical monitoring, the framework serves as a "Virtual Power quality Laboratory," bridging the gap between classical power engineering and AI-driven grid management for engineering students in the region

title: Sequence Modeling Architectures: Foundations [Special Issue on the Mathematics of Deep Learning]
authors: Felix Sarnthein, Arshia Afzal, Razvan Pascanu, Antonio Orvieto, Caglar Gulcehre, Jonas Geiping, Francesco Tonin, Albert Gu, Volkan Cevher
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Magazine
abstract: With the success of deep learning in the past decade, neural networks transitioned from simple layer stacking to sophisticated, highly complex designs. This article provides an overview of such deep learning architectures for sequence modeling, with a focus on their core mathematical principles: 1) we review the evolution of common sequence models from recurrent neural networks (RNNs), to Transformers, and modern state-space models (SSMs), exploring their foundational components such as gating, attention, and linear dynamical systems mechanisms. 2) We present general linear sequence mixers as a unifying framework to highlight the relationship between architectures and compare them in terms of inductive biases and algorithmic complexity. 3) We examine how practical considerations such as parallelizability and hardware awareness dictate their success. Finally, we discuss their impact on diverse fields of signal processing, including time-series analysis, natural language, and computer vision.

title: Comparative Analysis of Detection and Segmentation Architectures using YOLO Variants and Deep Segmentation Models for Gastrointestinal Polyp Detection and Segmentation
authors: Sourav Das Polok, Md Zillur Rahman Saimon, Salil Batra, Pravallika Kondapalli, Muhammad Aumlanul Abrar, Gouri Shankar Chakraborty
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Gastrointestinal polyps are benign growth in the form of abnormal tissue structures that grow on the inner lining of the digestive system and have been found to be the main cause of colorectal cancer. These polyps need to be identified and carefully defined in endoscopic images early, to enable timely diagnosis, and to decrease mortality rates of cancer. Nevertheless, the automated analysis is quite a difficult task because of differences in the polyp size, shape, texture, and the illumination conditions. Comprehensive comparative analysis of advanced deep learning architectures gastrointestinal polyp detection as well as gastrointestinal polyp segmentation is performed on the Kvasir-SEG dataset in their study. In the detection task, the YOLOv9, YOLOv10, and YOLOv11 are evaluated based on preciseness, recall, F1-score, and the mean Average Precision (mAP). To segment, U-Net, U-Net++ and TransUNet are compared in their accuracy, Dice coefficient and Intersection over Union (IoU). Experiments prove that YOLOv11 has the highest detection accuracy, 93.8% whereas TransUNet has a better segmentation accuracy 96.9%. The results indicate the trade-off between predictive accuracy and model complexity and offer useful advice on how one can pick efficient architectures in clinical environments. The following work is to consider a hybrid segmentation architecture combining detection-based localization with a transformer segmentation network that promotes additional performance improvement and reduced computing costs.

title: AI-Transparency and Algorithmic Trust in Smart LMS Adoption: An Explainable-AI Extended TAM Model
authors: Boumedyen Shannaq, Said Almaqbali, Oualid Ali
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: With artificial intelligence (AI) integrated into Learning Management Systems (LMS), there has been academic discussion on problems of transparency, trust, and user anxiety, but these issues have not been properly researched in the conventional Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). This present work responds in this way by adding to the TAM framework with constructs of AI Decision Transparency, Trust in AI Decisions, and positioning AI Anxiety as a moderating variable. The survey was conducted through quantitative methodology, using convenience sampling, a total of 276 valid responses were received and further data analysis was done using SmartPLS. Behavioral Intention was explained by the structural model at 59.5 per cent (R 2 = 0.595). It has been found that AI Decision Transparency has a strong positive correlation with Trust (0.626, p 0.001) and that Trust, in its turn is significantly correlated with Perceived Usefulness (0.432, p 0.001) as well as Behavioral Intention (0.158, p 0.001). Even though the direct effect of AI Anxiety on the intention was substantial (0.321, p 0.001), the hypothesised moderating role was not reinforced. Comprehensively, the results emphasize the fact that transparency-based trust is a key process that enables the adoption of AI-enabled LMS, thus providing credible theoretical evidence and practical suggestions to the ethical use of AI in higher education.

title: Dynamic Load-Aware Task Scheduling Method for Aerospace Situation Simulation with DMA-LSTM Prediction
authors: Runnan Qin, Wenming Xie, Wenlin Tang, Xiao Zheng, Jingru Dong, Jingyi Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: To meet the demand for large-scale data-model fusion computation in aerospace situation simulation, we propose a task scheduling method capable of perceiving dynamic load changes. Based on a container cloud architecture, this method utilizes a designed DMA-LSTM (Double Moving Average - Long Short Term Memory network) network to predict node load, enabling dynamic load migration to optimize resource allocation. It effectively enhances node scaling efficiency and ensures load balancing during large-scale computation. Experimental results demonstrate that this task scheduling method outperforms mainstream algorithms-including elastic scaling algorithms (HPA, DMA-HPA) and task scheduling algorithms (round-robin, pure random, weighted random methods)-in terms of node scaling efficiency, task response time, and load balancing, achieving superior resource allocation and availability.

title: CSI-RS Overhead Reduction in 6G
authors: Zhengxiang Ma, Zhigang Rong, Jialing Liu, Baoling Sheen, Ruikang Yang, Juan M. Roa, Renjian Zhao, Weimin Xiao, Miguel Dajer, Anthony C.K. Soong
publish: 2026 35th Wireless and Optical Communications Conference (WOCC)
abstract: This paper explores techniques to reduce the CSI-RS overhead for massive MIMO in 6G. A structured mapping between antenna ports and CSI-RS REs is introduced to facilitate lowering the CSI-RS density in terms of RE/RB/port. Its performance advantage over unstructured mapping is studied. Although these techniques are powerful, the performance improvements were limited. Hence, AI/ML based algorithms are also explored and our results showed that significant CSI-RS overhead reduction is possible without impacting performance.

title: A Soft Voting Ensemble Approach for Early Prediction of Cardiovascular Disease Risk
authors: V. Chandana Sri, P. Amrutha Nagavalli, James Deva Koresh Hezekiah
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Cardiovascular disorders remain among the most serious health challenges worldwide, making early risk identification extremely important. This study proposes a machine learning–based framework for predicting the likelihood of heart disease by combining XGBoost and Random Forest algorithms into a unified ensemble model. The dataset is carefully prepared through median-based handling of missing values, normalization of input features, and correction of class imbalance using the SMOTETomek method. A soft voting mechanism is adopted to combine the outputs of both models, allowing the system to benefit from their complementary strengths. The ensemble achieves a testing accuracy of 99.05%, demonstrating strong predictive capability and consistent classification performance. The results indicate that integrating multiple learning algorithms can significantly enhance reliability in heart disease risk prediction tasks.

title: Technical Advancements and Strategic Frameworks for the Maintenance and Repair of Civil Infrastructure Using Fiber-Reinforced Polymer (FRP) Materials
authors: Ahmed Alshaikha, Hamdy M. Mohamed, Mohamed Abduljalil Alam
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The deterioration of reinforced concrete (RC) infrastructure has emerged as a critical global concern, with maintenance backlogs resulting in substantial financial burdens each year. In the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) region, the combination of extreme temperature variations and high chloride exposure significantly accelerates the corrosion of steel reinforcement, often leading to premature structural degradation well before the intended design life is reached.

title: Automated System for Assessing the Degree of Deterioration of Road Markings Based on Deep Learning and Multiparametric Aggregation
authors: Artem Bagdasaryan, Sergei Gaponenko, Ksenia Polyantseva
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: The paper presents a method for automated assessment of the condition of road markings based on computer vision and deep learning methods. An approach is proposed that combines neural network semantic segmentation with algorithmic formalization of defects in the form of a set of calculated features, including erasure, fading, fragmentation, sharpness of borders and geometric integrity. For segmentation, the U-Net, FCN, and DeepLabV3 architectures have been studied, fine-tuned on a combined dataset using combined loss functions and taking into account class imbalance. An algorithm for the integral assessment of the condition of road markings based on weighted aggregation of features with subsequent classification into three levels of suitability has been developed. Experimental results show that FCN and DeepLabV3 architectures provide more stable segmentation compared to U-Net. The proposed system makes it possible to obtain a reproducible and interpretable assessment of the condition of road markings and can be used in the tasks of automated monitoring of road infrastructure.

title: Numerical Simulations of Particles Fluidization and Transportation in the Asteroid Sample
authors: Xu Kong, Yuncheng Lin, Jianfeng Man, He Zhang, Fuming Zeng, Fan Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: The gas-driven fluidization and transportation of particles are simulated using Euler-discrete element method in the asteroid sample. The distributions of velocities of gas and particles are predicted in the sample. Two zone structure with a core and an annulus are appeared along radial direction, achieving particles fluidization. The pneumatic conveying of particles consists of an induction time and initial fluidization and transport time under the gas driven condition. The maximum collecting solid mass flux exists at the time of about 2.5 s. Simulated exit solid mass flux agree with experimental measurements at the atmospheric environment.

title: Drag Free and Attitude Control System design for gravitational wave detection satellites
authors: Haoyue Zhang, Dong Ye, Peng Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: This paper focuses on the Drag-Free and Attitude Control System (DFACS) design for space-based gravitational wave detection missions. First, based on the stringent scientific requirements, the control objectives and the operation scheme in science mode are formulated. Second, the environmental and actuation noises acting on the spacecraft are modeled in the frequency domain using transfer functions, and a complete dynamical model of the spacecraft with two test masses is established. To address the strong couplings inherent in this system, a decoupling procedure is performed, resulting in single-input single-output (SISO) control loops. Subsequently, for each loop, sensitivity and complementary sensitivity functions are constructed according to the control objectives, and a $H_{\infty}$ suboptimal control problem is formulated within the frequency domain framework. Considering that solar radiation pressure (SRP) dominates in the ultralowfrequency band of the deep-space environment, an additional feedforward compensation channel is introduced on top of the traditional $H_{\infty}$ controller to pre-cancel the induced forces and torques, thereby alleviating the burden on the control loops, releasing robustness margins and improving the overall control performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed DFACS effectively suppresses environmental disturbances and actuator noises, ensuring that both the residual accelerations of the test masses and the inter-satellite pointing errors satisfy the stringent scientific specifications, thus verifying the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed control scheme.

title: Methodology for Studying Noise in Digital Cameras
authors: V. L. Zhbanova, D. A. Filatov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper presents a method for studying digital camera noise. A color target based on the RAL color atlas has been developed. Three digital cameras were selected as research objects. Experimental studies were conducted. High noise levels were detected in the blue channel, caused by the illumination conditions during the experiment. To improve the quality of the experiment, a type A light source, which has a yellowish-orange tint, can be replaced with a source with a neutral white color temperature tint. Qualitative analysis can predict the technical capabilities of the receiver, evaluate the distortion of the input signal and the stability of the matrix. The developed noise determination method should be universal and easy to use, and the results are informative and visual. Such topics pertain to the implementation of information transmission systems and methods, signal reception and processing, as well as technologies for image and signal processing, analysis, and recognition, and computer vision, which constitute strategic directions for the development of science and technology. The proposed method formed the basis of laboratory work for the courses "Digital Image Processing" and "Optical and Light Measurements". Conducting experimental measurements will help students consolidate their knowledge of the alignment of optoelectronic devices and systems, as well as improve their knowledge of lighting engineering.
