title: A Current-Driven SSHI for Piezoelectric Vibration Energy Harvesters
authors: Luigi Costanzo, Alessandro Lo Schiavo, Massimo Vitelli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: An SSHI-based interface circuit for piezoelectric vibration energy harvesters is presented and experimentally validated. The developed solution relies on current-driven regulation of the rectifier output voltage downstream of a parallel SSHI stage. By measuring only the rectifier output current, the proposed Current-Driven SSHI (CD-SSHI) interface directly and continuously sets the rectified DC voltage to its optimal value under time-varying vibration conditions, without resorting to the perturbative approaches of classical Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) techniques. The CD-SSHI reaches and tracks the Maximum Power Point (MPP) without the steady-state oscillations typical of perturb and observe MPPT, and without the power interruptions characteristic of the fractional open circuit voltage MPPT. Moreover, the proposed CD-SSHI interface significantly increases dynamic performance compared to an SSHI coupled with a conventional implementation of the P&O MPPT control. A theoretical analysis based on a general equivalent circuit model is developed to support the proposed circuit. Experimental results obtained from a prototype implementation demonstrate the effectiveness of the CD-SSHI interface in terms of both steady-state efficiency and dynamic response.

title: An Integrated Simulation-Experimental Framework for Prediction of Pollution Flashover on Railway Catenary Insulators_supp1-3702664.mp4
authors: Huai Zhao
publish: N/A
abstract: The pollution flashover is a key factor influencing the electrical performance of catenary insulators. The current studies about pollution flashover prediction of insulators based on the flow field and electric field are fragmented. In this work, a systematic prediction framework for pollution flashover of catenary insulators is developed by combining flow-electric coupling simulations and laboratory experiments. The temporal evolution of contamination mass density on the insulator surface is obtained from flow-field simulations and wind tunnel experiment. A mapping relationship between surface contamination and conductivity under water-saturated conditions is then established. By simulating the electric-field at different conductivity levels, the framework accurately predicts the evolution of the leakage current. The pre-flashover leakage-current amplitude is determined from flashover tests and used as the threshold for predicting the flashover instant. This work provides a theoretical basis for scheduling maintenance and enhancing the safety of railway catenary systems.

title: PositiveAssist: Energy-Gated Control for Positive Power Assistance of Exoskeleton in Walking, Running, and Stair Climbing
authors: Juncheng Zhou, Weibo Gao, Jin Sen Huang, Kurt Lin, Wanqi Wu, Hao Su
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Assistance timing is a critical factor in exoskeleton control. This article introduces the energy-gated controller, which uses only two thigh-mounted inertial measurement units to estimate hip kinematics and predicted mechanical power so that assistive torque is delivered primarily during positive work phases. Our proposed method combines a power-based gating mechanism with a biomechanics-informed torque synthesis strategy that reconstructs extension assistance from a delayed flexion profile, without gait-phase detection or task-specific mode switching. Experimental results from six participants <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(n = 6)$</tex-math></inline-formula> show that using a single fixed parameter set selected a priori, over <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$97.8\% \pm 1.2\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> of delivered mechanical power remained positive during walking and above <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$94.8\% \pm 1.6\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> during running, across 0.75–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.5\,\mathrm{m/s}$</tex-math></inline-formula> without parameter retuning. Under the same fixed parameters, the controller was further tested in five overground nonperiodic locomotion tasks and showed smooth assistance during the tested gait transitions, indicating potential for real-world deployment.

title: Relative Pose Estimation for Nonholonomic Robot Formation With UWB-IO Measurements
authors: Kunrui Ze, Wei Wang, Shuoyu Yue, Guibin Sun, Kexin Liu, Jinhu Lü
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: This article studies the problem of distributed formation control for multiple robots by using onboard ultra wideband (UWB) distance and inertial odometer (IO) measurements. Although this problem has been widely studied, a fundamental limitation of most works is that they require each robot’s pose and sensor measurements are expressed in a common reference frame. However, it is inapplicable for nonholonomic robot formations due to the practical difficulty of aligning IO measurements of individual robot in a common frame. To address this problem, first, a concurrent-learning-based estimator is first proposed to achieve relative localization between neighboring robots in a local frame. Different from most relative localization methods in a global frame, both relative position and orientation in a local frame are estimated with only UWB ranging and IO measurements. Second, to deal with information loss caused by directed communication topology, a cooperative localization algorithm is introduced to estimate the relative pose to the leader robot. Third, based on the theoretical results on relative pose estimation, a distributed formation tracking controller is proposed for nonholonomic robots. Both 2-D and 3-D real-world experiments conducted on aerial robots and grounded robots are provided to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: DDGA: Domain Distance Guided Active Domain Adaptation for Unpaired Super-Resolution
authors: Zai-Zuo Tang, Yu-Bin Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: While additional training samples enhance the performance of unpaired super-resolution (SR) networks, they inevitably escalate training costs. To address this issue, we propose a Domain Distance Guided Active Domain Adaptation (DDGA) method for unpaired SR, which intelligently selects additional training samples to maximize network performance improvement while minimizing the number of selected samples. In the sample selection process, to solve the problem of incompatibility between the additional samples and the target domain, we propose a three-fold metric that combines compatibility, complementarity, and representativeness to align the distribution of the additional samples with that of the target domain. Additionally, a domain representation method based on relative offset is proposed, transforming the task of texture detail representation into a score-based representation, effectively reducing the reliance of active domain adaptation (ADA) methods on texture details for unpaired SR tasks. Extensive experiments validate DDGA's effectiveness, demonstrating a 0.538 dB performance improvement in unpaired SR networks while introducing only 2% additional samples.

title: STFD-SNN: A Physics-Constrained Spiking Neural Network Framework for Maritime Radio Environment Map Reconstruction
authors: Liu Yi, Youchen Fan, Yufei Guo, You Fu, Shengliang Fang, Ming Lei, Qichen Wang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The escalating disparity between the supply and demand of maritime radio spectrum resources necessitates the construction of high-fidelity Radio Environment Maps (REM) for effective spectrum situational awareness and dynamic management. However, this task is severely hampered by distinctive maritime challenges, including extreme data sparsity, highly dynamic electromagnetic propagation characteristics, and complex spatio-temporal correlations, which significantly degrade conventional terrestrial REM reconstruction methods. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a hierarchical REM reconstruction framework that synergistically integrates Spiking Neural Networks with physical constraints. Our contributions are threefold. First, we devise an adaptive Unmanned Aerial Vehicle sampling strategy based on a refined Ant Colony Optimization algorithm, incorporating a hierarchical priority decision mechanism and joint heuristic function to improve data collection efficiency under sparse sampling. Second, we architect a Frequency-Spatio-Temporal Attention (FSTA) -enhanced Spiking Neural Network (SNN) model that captures spatio-temporal dynamics from sparse observations for high-precision 2D REM completion. Third, we introduce a physics-guided knowledge distillation paradigm that embeds maritime electromagnetic propagation models as multi-stage soft constraints through three coordinated mechanisms, which direct input correction via height-weighted physical deviation terms, and loss-level supervision penalizing physically inconsistent 3D reconstructions. Extensive simulations conducted in a high-fidelity maritime scenario demonstrate, which is constructed using real geographic environments, GMTED digital elevation data, and representative meteorological conditions. Our framework outperforms tensor completion U-Net and PINN baseline across various sampling rates. Notably, at sampling rates of 20%, 50%, and 80%, the proposed framework consistently attains superior Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) and Normalized Mean Square Error (NMSE), with the Physical Residual Metric (PRM) further serving as a diagnostic indicator confirming internalization of physical priors.

title: Embroidery Actuator Utilizing Embroidery Patterns to Generate Diverse Fabric Deformations
authors: Yuki Ota, Yuki Funabora
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This paper presents a novel Embroidery Actuator, a fabric-integrated pneumatic actuator that enables diverse and controllable deformations through embroidery pattern design. Unlike conventional fabric actuators that rely on fiber- or thread-shaped actuators, the proposed actuator is fabricated by directly stitching an inflatable tube onto the fabric using a cord-embroidery technique. The embroidered thread and the fabric jointly form a sleeve that constrains the expansion of the inflatable tube, converting internal pressure into targeted bending or stretching deformations. By varying the embroidery pattern, such as zigzag or cross configurations, different geometric constraints can be realized, allowing for flexible control of deformation direction and magnitude. Analytical deformation models based on the Neo-Hookean model and Lagrange's equations were developed to predict the relationship between pneumatic pressure and bending angle. And then,experiments demonstrated that the actuator achieved 47 degrees of flexion on the fabric surface side and 165 degrees on the reverse side by altering the embroidery pattern. Additionally, the created model expressed deformation with an error margin of several degrees.

title: A CUDA-Accelerated Iterative Inversion Framework for High-Resolution Brain MRI Reconstruction
authors: Anjali K S, Pankaj Krishna, Nagadevi S, Anand M, Sathyapriya L
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The reconstruction of high-resolution brain MRI is critical for accurate neurological diagnosis; however, conventional CPU-based reconstruction techniques incur substantial computational cost and prolonged processing time. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes a cloud-based reconstruction framework implemented using a Jupyter Notebook environment (Notebook Everywhere), enabling scalable and hardware-flexible experimentation. The reconstruction pipeline is executed on both CPU and GPU platforms to facilitate systematic performance evaluation. GPU acceleration is assessed using NVIDIA RTX 3050, RTX 3060, and RTX 4060 GPUs. The framework employs optimized large-scale matrix operations and iterative solvers, including Conjugate Gradient and Regularized Least Squares methods, applied to raw k-space MRI data to generate high-resolution 2D and 3D brain images with improved noise suppression and spatial fidelity. Performance is quantitatively evaluated using GPU reconstruction time (ms), peak GPU memory usage (MB), throughput (images/sec), Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), and Structural Similarity Index (SSIM). Experimental results indicate significant reductions in reconstruction time and improved throughput on GPU platforms while maintaining comparable PSNR and SSIM values, thereby preserving diagnostic image quality. These findings demonstrate that cloud-enabled GPU acceleration substantially enhances computational efficiency and supports near real-time MRI reconstruction for clinical and research applications.

title: Development of Software for Calculating Nuclear Reactor Components Using the Finite Element Method
authors: Dmitry E. Egorov, Elena V. Poznyak, Andrey T. Alekseev, Anton A. Tutnov, Evgeny S. Krutko, Vadim S. Tkachenko
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article describes software developed to numerically simulate thermal and mechanical processes in nuclear fuel rod components. Finite element analysis is used to assess the stress-strain state, accounting for transient thermal strains, pressure, and fuel swelling due to radiation. The software was developed as part of a student research project in the Department of Robotics, Mechatronics, Dynamics and Strength of Machines at the National Research University "Moscow Power Engineering Institute" (MPEI), "Applied Mechanics" specialization, educational program "Dynamics and Strength of Machines, Instruments, and Equipment".

title: Design of a Virtual Testbed for Modeling and Simulating a Small USV Dynamics in Waves
authors: Dmitriy A. Shlemin, Edgar M. Naissance, Oleg V. Kubryak
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: In this paper, a virtual testbed based on the MCSim Python library is presented for modeling and simulating the behavior of a small unmanned surface vessel (USV) under wave conditions. The developed simulation environment allows comprehensive analysis of the platform’s 6-DOF dynamics, as well as assessing the performance of the path following and motion control algorithms in the presence of wave disturbances.

title: A Multi-Segment Logarithmic Spiral Continuum Robot With a Stretchable Central Backbone: Design and Coupled Kinematic Modeling
authors: Huaicheng Huang, Pingsong Deng, Guolei Wang
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel continuum robot design inspired by the logarithmic spiral, uniquely characterized by its stretchable central backbone and segment-based actuation. This bio-inspired structure grants the robot exceptional flexibility and an extensive workspace. The robot is characterized by its stretchable elastic backbone and segment-based actuation. We developed a detailed coupled kinematic model based on an enhanced piecewise constant curvature (PCC) framework, which incorporates dynamic compensation for backbone length and a systematic analysis of coupling effects. This allows for mapping high-level shape commands directly to actuator controls. A 12-DoF physical prototype was constructed to validate our model experimentally. The qualitative results demonstrate the robot's extensive workspace and its remarkable ability to achieve complex configurations, including S-shapes and spatial spirals, confirming the design's high flexibility. Furthermore, a coupled kinematic model is developed to effectively map shape commands to actuator controls, enabling the robot to achieve complex configurations. Experimental validation confirms the model's effectiveness in controlling the robot's unique morphology in open-loop scenarios.

title: RIS Element Phase-Offset Calibration based on RSRP Reports
authors: Narayan Prasad, Tao Luo, Meilong Jiang, Kobi Ravid, Junyi Li
publish: 2026 35th Wireless and Optical Communications Conference (WOCC)
abstract: Calibration of a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) in cellular systems is constrained by a practical obstacle: low-complexity user equipment (UE) may report only reference signal received power (RSRP), in contrast to the complex-valued channel state information (CSI) or in-phase/quadrature (I/Q) measurements assumed by most prior art. This paper develops a complete framework for RIS phase-offset calibration from magnitude-only feedback. We formulate RSRP-based calibration as a constrained phase-retrieval problem under a single-offset-per-element impairment model, and propose a family of phase-only calibration codebooks based on—Hadamard-randomization, adjacent-element grouping, dither-plus-binary-quantization, and a hybrid schedule—that explicitly balance reflected power toward a designated buddy receiver against the pattern diversity required for magnitude-only recovery. We introduce a one-shot manifold-optimization solver that jointly updates the element drifts and unknown measurement phases on a single torus manifold, and extend it to an uncertainty-aware variant that accommodates bounded cascade-channel errors. We further adapt a zero-shot calibration principle from phased arrays to RISs for commissioning-time initialization. Simulations on quaternary RISs with one or more buddy receivers show that the proposed codebooks and solvers substantially outperform naïve and independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) random baselines and closely approach a genie upper bound.

title: Design of a Spaceborne Phased Array Antenna for Ultra-Low Orbit
authors: Shilin Dong, Jie Zhang, Yakai Dong, Zhanchun Fan
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: The Earth’s atmosphere in the ultra-low orbit is characterized by relatively higher density and higher atomic oxygen concentration. In order to make the satellite fly better in the ultra-low orbit, all the equipment on the surface of the satellite needs to carry out low profile design and atomic oxygen protection design. Antenna is a necessary equipment on the surface of satellite. To solve the above problems, we proposed a design scheme of antiatomic oxygen tile-type phased array antenna in this paper. We adopt a tile-type design to reduce the projection area of the antenna in the direction of flight. To address the need for atomic oxygen protection, we proposed an improved scheme that adjusts the size of the antenna’s radiating element to offset the impact of atomic oxygen protection on the antenna’s resonant frequency, which can effectively reduce the loss of the antenna’s electrical performance. According to actual measurement verification, both the antenna’s standing wave and EIRP have been improved, which proved the proposed scheme has good engineering application value.

title: A Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning Framework for Intrusion Detection
authors: Nivedhidha M, Ramkumar M P, Dharani M, Emil Selvan G S R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) allows multiple participants to collaboratively build models while keeping their individual raw data private. This makes it an ideal fit for Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) deployed in distributed and privacy-sensitive environments. However, IDS that use FL share some weaknesses, including data from clients being non-Independent Identically Distributed "IID," unstable updates, and potential poisoning attacks. The Fed-IDS is an innovative approach to federated intrusion detection that seeks to address the problems posed by trust and these other issues through the use of Principal Component Analysis- (PCA) based clustering and distance-based anomaly measures. In the Fed-IDS framework, the initial pre-processing of the MalMem2022 dataset consists of centralizing missing values, label encoding, normalizing, and using SMOTE to augment data for each client through Dirichlet partitioning. After pre-processing, the dataset is redistributed to each client to create true distributed environments. Feature reduction is performed using PCA, and clusters are established using K-Means+ to determine cluster centroids to be used in the calculated distance from a sample to a centroid. These metrics are used to improve the feature matrix and aid in the classification and reliability estimation of clients. Local client learning is also conducted by calculating the mean cluster distance based upon each clients' client-side data.

title: Enhancing ACO Stability and Performance for Urban VRP via Clustering and Intersection Penalty
authors: Jin Xu, Jinxing Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: This study addresses a complex variant of the Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP) with capacity constraints and split deliveries. To address this issue, this paper proposes the Clustering and Intersection-aware ACO Algorithm, an Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) algorithm that incorporates a state transfer strategy based on clustering and modifies the pheromone updating strategy through the introduction of an Intersection Penalty Factor. This reduces redundant path crossings and improves solution quality. The experimental results demonstrate that the Clustering and Intersection-aware ACO Algorithm outperforms the traditional ACO algorithm in urban distribution across a range of map sizes, markedly reducing the overall cost of distribution paths, and yielding a highly stable solution. This research presents a novel approach for optimizing complex VRPs, offering valuable insights for practical logistics scheduling.

title: Mixed-Integer Programming for Multi-Agent Motion Planning and Encirclement of Moving Targets in 3D Space
authors: Runhui Long, Yun Wang, Hanyue Fan, Yifan Shen, Zhongqiang Ren, Sutthiphong Srigrarom
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: This paper considers a Moving-Target Multi-Agent Motion Planning (MT-MAMP) problem that seeks collision-free paths for multiple agents to collectively encircle a few moving targets along straight lines, and each target has a requirement on the minimum number of agents for encirclement. MT-MAMP is challenging as it involves not only inter-agent collision avoidance, a fundamental challenge in MAMP, but also encirclement of moving targets that involves task allocation in a dynamic environment. While various approaches have been developed for MAMP and its variants, we are not aware of any prior research on MAMP that considers encirclement of moving targets. To address MT-MAMP, we formulate the problem as a mixed-integer program (MIP) by leveraging several existing techniques in the field of MIP for motion planning, and introduce a notion of shrinking encirclement, which allows the agents to tighten their encirclement as time evolves. We verify our MIP with up to 15 agents and 4 targets in various cases, which verifies the correctness and effectiveness of our approach for MT-MAMP.

title: Business Intelligence Systems as a Strategic Driver of Digital Innovation
authors: Ibrahim A. Abu-AlSondos, Rana Husseini Frangieh, Saeed Hameed Aldulaimi, Worood Ahmad Melhem, Mahmoud Alghizzawi, Aws Raed Mahafdhah
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This work explores how artificial intelligence–when integrated into business intelligence systems-can become a strategic lever for digital innovation, while simultaneously becoming a potential source of trust erosion if accountability and governance principles are absent. Despite the widespread adoption of analytics and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> in business intelligence, the literature tends to divide into two paths: one that examines the impact of business intelligence and analytics on performance, decision-making, and competitive advantage and another that focuses on responsible AI principles such as fairness, transparency, accountability, and risk management. This separation results in deficiency in explaining the “missing link” between responsibility as an organizational capacity and trust as an intermediary mechanism, and then between trust and sustainable competitive outcomes within digital innovation systems. This research adopts a theoretical approach based on an integrative literature review and the development of test proposals/hypotheses linking the dimensions of responsible integration and trust with the outputs of digital innovation and competitive advantage. It also identifies contextual variables (such as data governance maturity and organizational pressure) that can strengthen or weaken these relationships. The research contributes theoretically by bridging disparate literatures and practically by providing a guiding model that enables organizations to transform responsibility from a standard slogan into an operational and strategic capability that generates both value and trust.

title: Evaluating the Performance of Machine Learning Frameworks for Operating in Secure Enclaves
authors: A. A. Bardovsky, S. G. Fomicheva
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: The general task of secure scaling, updating, and (pre)training deployed ML models is formalized, from which the particular optimization task of automated selection of “edge/fognode + enclave + ML-framework + ML-model” architectural configurations for edge/fog computing environments is highlighted. A comparative analysis of modern machine learning frameworks in the context of their readiness to function in crowded enclaves is carried out. The results of an experimental evaluation of the performance of these frameworks in Intel SGX enclaves are presented. The unavailability of the Tensor Flow and Tensor Flow Lite frameworks for functioning in these enclaves for real-time object detection is noted.

title: Fuzzy Logic-Based Congestion Estimation in Network-on-Chip Routers Under Video Object Plane Decoder Traffic
authors: Denis Kutuzov, Alexey Osovsky, Nataliya Maltseva, Georgy Popov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper proposes an approach for proactive detection of congestion in routers of Network-on-Chip (NoC) systems based on fuzzy logic. The input parameters of the model include the packet injection rate (PIR), the average occupancy of router input buffers, and the structural metric of Betweenness Centrality (BC), enabling the joint consideration of both dynamic traffic characteristics and the topological importance of nodes. To construct the dataset, cycle-accurate NoC simulations were performed using the Noxim simulator. A regular <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> mesh topology with deterministic XY routing and Poisson traffic injection is considered. As a workload scenario, the VOPD (Video Object Plane Decoder) benchmark is used, representing a directed acyclic task graph with pronounced heterogeneity in both computational and communication demands. The developed fuzzy inference system provides an estimation of the probability of congestion occurrence at network nodes. The fuzzy logic framework enables accurate modeling of nonlinear relationships among system parameters and ensures smooth transitions between different network operating regimes.

title: A Comparative Study of Modern Object Detectors for Robust Apple Detection in Orchard Imagery
authors: Mohammed Asad, Ajai Kumar Gautam, Priyanshu Dhiman, Rishi Raj Prajapati
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: For predicting yields, counting apples, robotic harvesting, and monitoring crops, it is very important to be able to accurately detect apples in orchard images. But changes in light, leaf clutter, thick fruit clusters, and partial occlusion often make performance worse. To provide a fair and reproducible comparison, this study establishes a controlled benchmark for single-class apple detection on the public AppleBBCH81 dataset using one deterministic train/validation/test split and a unified evaluation protocol across six representative detectors: YOLOv10n, YOLO11n, RT-DETR-L, Faster R-CNN (ResNet50-FPN), FCOS (ResNet50-FPN), and SSDLite320 (MobileNetV3-Large). Performance is evaluated primarily using COCO-style mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95, and threshold-dependent behavior is further analyzed using precision-recall curves and fixed-threshold precision, recall, and F1-score at IoU = 0.5. On the validation split, YOLO11n achieves the best strict localization performance with mAP@0.5:0.95 = 0.6065 and mAP@0.5 = 0.9620, followed closely by RT-DETR-L (0.6012 and 0.9506) and YOLOv10n (0.5941 and 0.9501). At a fixed operating point with confidence ≥ 0.05, YOLOv10n attains the highest F1-score (0.780870), whereas RT-DETR-L achieves very high recall (0.970218) but minimum precision (0.224605) because of many false positives at low confidence. These findings show that detector selection for orchard deployment should be guided not only by localization-aware accuracy but also by threshold robustness and the requirements of the downstream task.

title: Digital Twin-based Out-of-Distribution Detection in Autonomous Vessels
authors: Erblin Isaku, Hassan Sartaj, Shaukat Ali
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology

title: Sentiment Forecasting By Data-Driven Models
authors: Othoniel Joseph, Prathyusha Sukumar, Rayner Ulloa, Avimanyou Vatsa, Alexander Casti
publish: 2026 35th Wireless and Optical Communications Conference (WOCC)
abstract: Forecasting stock prices and corresponding emotions is a complex process. Economic and financial forecasting leverages financial planning and growth predictions for the country and the industrialist, significantly impacting investors’ lives and financial losses. Therefore, this study investigates the impact of integrating sentiment analysis data on the accuracy of LSTM sequential models and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for predicting semiconductor stocks’ daily closing prices. Also, the predictive performance of models trained solely on historical prices and technical indicators (Volume, RSI, MACD) versus models augmented with a general sentiment score derived from financial news, with missing sentiment values imputed using forward-fill. The performance metrics, Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Directional Accuracy, are used to evaluate the models. In conclusion, results indicate that incorporating forward-filled sentiment data marginally improved average directional accuracy across the tested stocks but did not consistently reduce RMSE or MAE compared to models without sentiment features. Moreover, the Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network (ST-GNN) outperforms sequential models. The ST-GNN enhances predictive performance through dynamic edge modeling, improved data fusion, and adaptive learning. It also demonstrates that fusing quantitative and qualitative data within a graph-based deep learning framework can yield more stable, accurate stock forecasts by incorporating financial indicators, stock data, and sentiment analysis.

title: Physics–Learning Hybrid Adaptive Control for Soft Continuum Robots via ANCF Model and Sparse Online Learning Methods
authors: Yu Rong, Guoying Gu
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: The intrinsic compliance and time-varying material properties jointly pose significant challenges in developing an accurate and computationally efficient controller for soft continuum robots. To address these problems, this work presents a physics-learning hybrid feedback controller that integrates an Absolute Nodal Coordinate Formulation (ANCF) model with lightweight data-driven methods, achieving online adaption and robust motion control. In this sense, the forward and inverse pose mappings are both decomposed into a physics-based component and a learning compensator. Specifically, the ANCF-based model physically characterizes the quasi-static motions and provides feedforward control predictions. Meanwhile, a sparse online Gaussian process (SOGP) is employed to compensate the residual error of the physical model. The SOGP can evaluate the novelty of the newly collected data and maintain a small training dataset, thus releasing the computational burden. Additionally, a radial basis function neural network (RBFNN) is used to compute feedback control signals for the desired goals. Parameter update for two modules completes within 25 ms, since they both operate on compact datasets and admit analytical expressions. Compared to the open-loop controller, the presented feedback controller reduces about 65% position error and 70% orientation error, showing online adaption and high robustness to diverse disturbances.

title: Super-Resolution Assisted Mask-Aware Face Recognition for Low-Quality CCTV Surveillance
authors: Vijayalakshmi, Raghul S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The uses of the surveillance systems are important in the current smart city security systems, but the process of identifying the suspects with low-quality CCTV footage is a difficult task because of blur, noise, occlusion and partial visibility of the face by masks. This study presents a Super-Resolution Assisted Mask-Aware Face Recognition architecture that aims at reliably recognizing a person based on a low-quality surveillance footage. The system incorporates several modules of deep learning such as Real-ESRGAN to improve super-resolution reconstruction, YOLOv8-Face to find real-time face, Vision Transformer with mask-aware attention to extract features in an occlusion aware manner and ArcFace (ResNet100) to generate robust facial embeddings. Frames of CCTV footage are extracted at low resolution and super-resolution reconstruction is used to improve fine facial features. The detected faces are then processed by the mask-aware attention mechanism that lays emphasis in visible parts of the face like the eyes and forehead and ignoring the covered parts. Facial embeddings are extracted and compared to the suspect reference image on the cosine similarity and by using the Softmax-based confidence scoring. The proposed system is proved to have face recognition accuracy of 96.8%, detection precision of 97.2% and recall performance of 95.9% even in adversarial masked and low-resolution conditions through experimental evaluation. The findings indicate that image restoration should be combined with attention-based face analysis as it ensures greater reliability in recognition. The suggested framework presents a powerful automated approach to law enforcement agencies in terms of decreasing the workloads of manual CCTV surveillance and real-time surveillance real-time-based identification of the suspect in a faster and more accurate manner.

title: A Novel Identification and Measurement Method for Transient External Heat Flux in Lunar Orbit
authors: Qinghua Gao, Shouwen Liu, Boying Lin, Dongliang Wu, Yanqiang Bi, Yan Zhou, Chenwei Hong
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Space external heat flux significantly affects the thermal design of spacecraft. In lunar orbit, external heat flux includes solar radiation, lunar infrared radiation, lunar albedo, and the cold black space environment. Generally, numerical simulation methods are used to calculate the external heat flux of spacecraft. Alternatively, infrared remote sensing technology or microwave radiosensors directly measure the lunar surface temperature. However, there is no precedent for directly measuring lunar orbit external heat flux using sensors. This paper proposes a transient radiant heat flux differentiation method. It relies on temperature differences caused by inconsistent surface radiation characteristics. This method enables the measurement of solar radiation, lunar infrared radiation, and lunar albedo in lunar orbit, filling the technical gap in this field. The physical processes of transient heat flux measurement for solar radiation and lunar surface infrared radiation are theoretically analyzed. A new measurement mechanism is proposed. Two sets of thermal control coatings with different solar absorptivity and infrared emissivity are selected to establish the transient identification and measurement scheme for external heat flux. The design scheme is verified through theoretical analysis and experiments. Results show that the transient infrared heat flux identification error is less than $3 \%$, and the solar radiant heat flux identification error is less than $6 \%$. This confirms the correctness of the proposed identification and measurement method.

title: AI Applications in Information Systems and Their Role in Supporting Organizational Decision-Making: A Study on Jordanian Institutions
authors: Mohammed Moghazi Ezzeldein Ahmed, Hussain Ahmad Awad, Omar Mohammad Alquabeh
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study investigates the role of artificial intelligence (AI) applications in information systems in supporting organizational decision-making within Jordanian institutions. Using a quantitative methodology, the findings demonstrate that AI applications significantly enhance both decision quality and timely decision-making. Furthermore, decision quality and timely decision-making substantially influence overall organizational performance, with timely decision-making exerting a particularly strong impact on institutional effectiveness. This study provides valuable empirical evidence for the literature on AI, information systems, and organizational decision support, highlighting the key factors of the proposed research model.

title: Entrepreneurial Culture in the Age of Artificial Intelligence: A Strategic Framework for Sustainable Development in the Mena Region
authors: Mohammed Salman Nimah, Basim Faisal Abdul Nayef, Khamees Naser Mohammed, Ibrahim Hadi Salem Aljumaili, Qayssar Khaleel Mhaidi, Marwan Abdeldayem
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study aims to highlight the role of entrepreneurship in achieving sustainable development in MENA in its three dimensions: economic, social, and environmental. This is done in light of the global trend towards entrepreneurship culture in general, and in MENA in particular, and to assess MENA regional and global standing while analyzing its strengths and weaknesses. The study was based on several methodologies, including the descriptive analytical approach, which reviewed and analyzed previous studies related to the topic and highlighted the contribution of the current study. The deductive approach was used to analyze the study variables to clarify the role of entrepreneurship in achieving sustainable development. Additionally, the modern scientific approach was employed to construct and formulate a standard model to test the relationship between entrepreneurship and sustainable development during the period (2020-2024). The study recommends to support entrepreneurial projects through economic development policies, including public policies that minimize regulatory and administrative barriers, compliance costs and tax burdens, ensure market functioning and competition, and guarantee effective bankruptcy laws. Working to overcome difficulties in obtaining the necessary funds to finance projects. Increasing support for training and continuing education through the establishment of community centers for entrepreneurship. The necessity of directing entrepreneurial projects towards encouraging investment in clean energy that achieves environmental preservation and sustainable development in its environmental dimension

title: Cyber Range for Cybersecurity Research in Digital Substation Networks
authors: Artyom Gavrilov, Anatoly Minzov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This article focuses on a training testbed that represents a secured local area network of a digital substation. The paper examines the testbed’s composition, its functional capabilities, and potential applications in the educational process. Additionally, the article outlines the procedure for organizing cybersecurity competitions using the developed testbed as a cyber range.

title: On the Convergence, Implicit Bias, and Edge of Stability of Gradient Descent in Deep Learning: Reviewing recent progress [Special Issue on the Mathematics of Deep Learning]
authors: Hancheng Min, Lachlan Ewen MacDonald, René Vidal
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Magazine
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) trained via gradient descent (GD) with random initialization and without any regularization enjoy good generalization performance in practice despite being highly overparametrized. To theoretically understand this puzzling phenomenon, many works on convergence analysis for GD algorithms on NNs have been developed over the last half-decade. In this article, we review these research efforts and discuss how they address three specific questions related to this puzzle. The first question is why GD finds a global minimum efficiently, which the literature has addressed by studying what level of overparametrization (width, depth, etc.) and what type of initialization lead to a benign optimization landscape along the training trajectory, facilitating a linear convergence rate of GD. The next question is why the global minimum found by GD generalizes well, which has been addressed by showing that overparametrization induces an implicit simplicity bias along the GD trajectory. More recently, it has been observed that in practice, training DNNs with far larger learning rates than theoretically permissible results in faster convergence and better generalization. This leads to the third question of why faster convergence and better generalization can be achieved with a large learning rate, which has been recently addressed by identifying a self-stabilization mechanism and implicit bias toward flat minima.

title: Maximum Versoria Criterion Based Filtering for Robust SINS/GNSS Integration
authors: Yi Mao, Wei Gu, Feng Zhu, Pingping Ji, Tingting Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: In practical applications of the Strapdown Inertial Navigation System/Global Navigation Satellite System (SINS/GNSS) integrated navigation system, complex operational environments and electromagnetic interference always lead to signal obstruction and disturbance, resulting in nonlinear mixed Gaussian noise in the signals received by satellite receivers. However, existing filtering algorithms designed for non-Gaussian noise often suffer from high computational complexity of kernel functions and low filtering accuracy. To address these limitations, a Maximum Versoria Criterion-based Unscented Kalman Filter (MVCUKF) algorithm is proposed, which introduces the Versoria function as the cost function and integrates it with the measurement update phase of the Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) to derive the Maximum Versoria Criterion (MVC). MVC adjusts the weights of anomalous measurements in non-Gaussian noise environments to mitigate the impact of interference noise on state estimation, while reducing computational complexity without compromising filtering accuracy under non-Gaussian noise conditions. Finally, the results of simulation experiment demonstrate that the proposed filtering algorithm significantly mitigates the impact of non-Gaussian noise on SINS/GNSS integrated navigation, enhancing the accuracy of navigation filtering and proving the robustness.

title: Cross-Attention-Based Intelligent Video Temporal Grounding for CCTV-Based Crime Identification in Smart Cities
authors: Abdul Jawwad, Akil Krishna, Ch. Madhavi Sudha
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The key to automated processing of the long CCTV surveillance data in the intelligent cities is intelligent video temporal grounding, which allows detecting crimes on time and situational awareness. It is suggested in this paper that the adoption of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) as a context-aware query refinement system and frozen crossattention multimodal transformers (SlowFast R50 + CLIP) as a fine-grained temporal localizer would form a two-stage Multimodal Video Temporal Grounding (MMVTG) framework. Some of the major issues that have been tackled through the framework include sparse events, query bias, and cross-domain generalization. Experimental analysis of UCA dataset shows a large improvement in the accuracy of temporal localization (7388%), temporal Intersection over Union (tIoU 7889%), incident detection accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(86-90 \%)$</tex>, and response latency over baseline techniques. The suggested MMVTG system can be used in scalable real-time surveillance and offers a solid solution to intelligent smart city monitoring.

title: Explainable AI for Smart Irrigation using Soil Moisture and Climate Data
authors: Vignesh R, Janani C, Dhyanesh M, A. Syed Ismail
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Efficient planning of irrigation is very important for sustainable agriculture in Tamil Nadu as climate variability and growing water scarcity requires data-driven decision making. Conventional methods of scheduling irrigation are based on limited single station weather observations, does not consider spatial climate variability and even ignored soil heterogeneity often resulting in inaccurate estimates of water requirements, which are also not easily interpreted. This study focuses on proposing an explainable and spatially aware irrigation prediction system that incorporates multi-location satellite-derived climate data from the NASA POWER API) called POWER API, soil classification based on FAO framework of Food and Agriculture Organization International (FAO) and advanced machine learning regression models to predict irrigation requirements for a trajectory of days in litres per hectare. The area under study is Tamil Nadu, whole Each observation is collected at daily intervals establishing a complete data set of 54,810 observations over 30 spatial grid locations covering the period 2020-2024. Multiple regression models such as Linear Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest and XGBoost have been systematically analysed for ensuring robust model selection, to prevent bias induced by model dependence on a single method. Random Forest gave the highest overall performance with R2 value of 0.9999 with MAE of 279 L/ha and XGBoost performed best in terms of generalization with a cross validation R2 value of 0.9973. The reason for the high R2 values are explained to the structured nature of the data set, consistent environmental relationships, and feature engineering for the three spatial and temporal dimensions. Explainable Artificial Intelligence using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) that rainfall, longitude and temperature are the biggest predictors, but soil type has a significant moderating effect on water retention capacity. In addition, the system includes real-time simulation function to model dynamic irrigation demand, which can improve the practicality of the use. The framework proposed is to support precision irrigation, promote transparency and trust in the AI-driven recommendations, and contribute to sustainable water resource management in the smart agriculture systems.

title: Development of a GIS Methodology for Assessing the Environmental Safety of Urbanized Territories
authors: Ilya V. Korolev, Natig M. Muradov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel, reproducible, and spatially explicit methodology for the comprehensive assessment of environmental safety in urban areas, grounded in Geographic Information Systems (GIS). The approach uniquely integrates three core components: (1) legally binding requirements of Russian sanitary legislation—particularly those concerning Sanitary Protection Zones (SPZs) and permissible emission thresholds; (2) multi-temporal remote sensing data (including satellite imagery and land surface temperature indicators); and (3) advanced spatial analysis techniques implemented in the open-source QGIS platform. The methodology employs a multi-criteria decision-making framework to construct an integrated environmental risk index that accounts for cumulative impacts from industrial sources, traffic emissions, green space deficiency, and proximity to hazardous facilities. The proposed method was pilot-tested in the Kapotnya district of Moscow—a historically industrialized and socially vulnerable area with documented air quality concerns. The GIS-based assessment revealed that approximately 12% of the district’s residential territory is exposed to elevated cumulative environmental risks that were not identified in official state environmental reports or urban planning documentation. These "blind spots" primarily resulted from the aggregation of multiple low-intensity stressors that, when considered individually, fall below regulatory thresholds but collectively pose significant health hazards.

title: Self-Supervised Meta-Adaptive Multimodal Graph Attention Learning for Clinical Decision Support
authors: Sharmistha S, Shrinidhi C, Pandeeswaran C
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Clinical decision support a novel hybrid, multimodal, and graph-based learning, meta-learning, cloud-native AI system can be used to achieve better patient prognosis prediction and reduce prescription errors. The system uses modality-specific encoders that are contrastively trained to combine structured EHR information, modality-specific time-series vital signs and unstructured prescription text to generate strong patient embeddings. A capsule-based layer that explains higher-level features is represented by the model of Graph Attention Networks (GAT), in which the relations between the symptoms, the diagnoses, the labs, and medications are described. The knowledge graphs are used in a special prescription safety engine, which tells the drug-drug interaction, contraindication, and dosage aberrations. The meta-learning with prototypical networks is able to learn new patients or sparse-data clinical setting within a relatively short period of time. In self-supervised learning (SSL), one trains on large unlabeled data so as to strengthen the representational quality. Guided explainability Attention-guided explainability is used to induce the most significant factors influencing predictions and increase trust among the clinicians. It has been shown in experimental conditions that superior prognosis, security prescription and excellent generalization in various conditions of low information, is achieved which is a scalable, versatile and understandable clinical decision support system.

title: Lunar Regolith Volatile Induction Heating Extraction Device: Design and Validation
authors: Runqi Yuan, Junyue Tang, Zihao Yu, Shengyuan Jiang, Zhan Sun, Bo Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: In-situ analysis of volatiles in lunar polar regolith represents an important direction in current lunar exploration. However, due to the relatively limited energy available in the lunar polar regions and the low thermal conductivity of lunar soil, traditional resistance heating methods for in-situ volatile extraction face challenges such as high energy consumption, slow heating rates, and failure to reach required temperatures. The Lunar Regolith Volatile Detection Payload onboard the Chang’e7 rover employs an induction heating method to extract water and other volatiles from lunar soil. Based on Faraday’s law of electromagnetic induction, this paper describes the design and working principle of the Lunar Regolith Volatile Induction Heating Extraction Device and establishes a performance test platform to experimentally validate its heating capabilities. Tests conducted in a vacuum environment demonstrated that the heating frequency and induction power exhibit a resonant characteristic-first increasing and then decreasing. Under a 15V power supply, a maximum heating power of 60 W was achieved, enabling precise temperature control between $50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and $700^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, with a temperature deviation of $\leq 50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and a heating time of $\leq 120$ seconds. These results meet the requirements for in-situ volatile detection in lunar regolith under orbital conditions. This study provides technical support for the in-situ extraction and analysis of volatiles in lunar polar regolith.

title: Configuration Design and Planning Algorithms for Modular Robots in On-Orbit Assembly
authors: Yao Jiang, Meibao Yao, Hongwei Zhang, Xueming Xiao, Kun Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: In low-gravity environments, modular selfreconfigurable robots are attractive for tasks such as satellite reconfiguration, structural expansion, truss assembly, and robotic arm deployment, yet planning remains highly challenging due to the NP-hard nature of module assignment and the requirement to maintain both connectivity and reachability throughout reconfiguration. To tackle these difficulties, we establish an integer programming model with penalty functions that enforce connectivity and reachability, and we design a hierarchical mechanism to decouple global assignment from local motion planning. At the upper level, the proposed CMGA (Convolution-MST-Genetic Algorithm with Gaussian mutation) efficiently addresses the NP-hard assignment problem by integrating structural preprocessing, refined initialization, and Gaussian mutation to accelerate convergence and improve global exploration. At the lower level, an A* search generates feasible, connectivity- and reachability-preserving paths for individual module relocations. Experiments on truss-assembly and satellite-reconfiguration scenarios demonstrate that this layered framework significantly outperforms conventional methods in convergence speed and movement cost. Overall, the results highlight the effectiveness of combining a hierarchical optimization mechanism with cooperative spherical module design, offering practical computational tools for autonomous modular robotic reconfiguration in future on-orbit missions.

title: Real-Time Torque Gap Compensation for Electric Cars On Rough Terrain
authors: S. Divya, G. Sudha Ram, V. Ashok, M. Dharun Pandi, S. Tamizharasan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Challenges encountered when operating an electric vehicle include uneven ground surfaces as well as having large gaps between multiple torque sensors which negatively impact vehicle performance. The proposed model is designed to create a real-time torque compensation tool so that an electric vehicle travels smoothly. This is done by using an electric vehicle platform equipped with an integrated set of systems that provide the capabilities of performing real-time torque compensation. The torque compensation system uses tilt readings taken by an MPU6050 on the electric vehicle, to determine the characteristics of the terrain traversed by the electric vehicle, for real-time processing of readings accomplished using an ESP32 microcontroller that drives a DC motor to deliver an adequate level of energy to the axle of the electric vehicle. The proposed model has also utilized differential analysis of the tilted position of the electric vehicle to provide a means of adjusting the output from the torque sensor based upon the characteristics of the surface on which the electric vehicle is operated. Experimental results produced an improvement in the development of torque between motors, a decrease of 40% to overall slip at each wheel of the electric vehicle, an average pitch tolled at an acceptable level of ±2 degrees relative to the reference axis and an improvement in power supply efficiency.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-based Link Optimization in MANETs over Time-Varying Fading Channels
authors: Khushbu Rai, Dr. Bhawana Pillai, Jitendra Agarwal, Dr. Bhupesh Gour
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Mobile Ad Hoc Networks are inherently dynamic and unpredictable with their nodes constantly moving about, as well as experiencing signal loss (fading) on their channels. In this research, a Deep Reinforcement Learning method for optimizing links in a time-varying Rayleigh fading environment for MANETs was developed. The problem was formulated as a Markov Decision Problem and solved using a Deep Q-Network (DQN). The simulation showed an improvement in throughput of the network, an increase in the number of packets delivered, and a reduction in energy usage of each node over other heuristic and classical methods that used Q-learning. The DQN's learned policy adapted well to both different fading and mobility conditions. Additionally, the spatial interference was minimized through the smart power adjustments. Overall, this research demonstrated that the DRL method is able to efficiently solve the complex wireless optimization problems even in environments which are very random and change frequently.

title: SafeBeat Rx—Moving ECG AI Beyond Diagnostics to Recommending Treatment
authors: Rachita Navara, Margaret Ferari, Walker McHugh
publish: IEEE Pulse
abstract: Drug-induced QT prolongation is the leading cause of FDA nonapproval and a key safety concern for many medications, including cardiac drugs, antibiotics, psychotropic drugs, and oncology treatments. Conventional automated QT algorithms have shown variable reliability in recordings with abnormal or irregular morphology, with misclassification rates up to 75%. This article presents SafeBeat Rx, an AI-powered ECG platform that provides beat-by-beat ECG analysis, enabling full transparency and optional adjustment of measurements for each individual ECG heartbeat. Validated against expert cardiologist adjudication, SafeBeat addresses a critical gap in postdischarge cardiac surveillance and represents a novel approach to guideline-adherent pharmacological disease management.

title: An Explainable and Uncertainty-Aware Deep Learning Framework for Histopathological Breast Cancer Detection
authors: Rajkumar Pandiarajan, Kailasam Selvaraj, Vimal Sekar, Venkadesh V, Sowmiya N, Anuja J
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Breast cancer diagnosis from histopathological images remains a challenging task due to complex tissue morphology and variability in visual patterns. This work presents an integrated deep learning framework designed to support automated and reliable classification of benign and malignant breast tissue samples. A transfer learning strategy based on a pre-trained ResNet50 network is employed to extract discriminative features while reducing training complexity. To improve decision reliability, uncertainty estimation is incorporated using Monte Carlo Dropout, enabling probabilistic confidence assessment through multiple stochastic forward passes. In addition, gradient-based activation mapping is applied to visualize spatial regions influencing classification outcomes, thereby enhancing interpretability and clinical transparency. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed approach achieves stable convergence, improved generalization, and confidence-aware predictions. By combining feature learning, uncertainty modelling, and visual explanation within a unified architecture, the framework provides a trustworthy decision-support system for digital pathology applications.

title: An Explainable Retrieval-Augmented AI Framework for Detecting Food and Nutrition Misinformation
authors: S Sreeraj, Gokul R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Misinformation about food has turned into a significant concern of popular health because of the rapid proliferation of unproven nutrition attributions in the Internet space. Dietary, food safety, and health advantage information is highly likely to be deceptive and affect the behavior and choice of the people. The currently used misinformation detection methods are mostly based on the key-word-match methods, rule-filtering, or other traditional machine learning classifiers which are not scientifically grounded and have no explaination. In response to this issue, this paper suggests NutriGuard, an artificial intelligence-based food misinformation detection system that is based on natural language processing, semantic embedding, and a Retrieval-Augmented Generation architecture. This system takes in nutrition claims submitted by users and converts the claims into semantic vectors, then finding suitable scientific support at an established knowledge base of peer reviewed nutrition literature. The evidence obtained is framed with the contextual thinking in order to categorize claims into true, false or misleading and give justifiable reasons supported by references. Experimental analysis on a dataset of online nutrition claims indicates that the given system reaches an accuracy of 96.85% and preciseness of 95.9%, recall of 96.3% and F1-score of 96.1%, which is better than the traditional misinformation detection models. The findings also prove that, when evidence-based retrieval is combined with the language model reasoning, the reliability and transparency of the food misinformation detection systems are greatly enhanced. NutriGuard is a framework that offers a sound platform on how to market scientifically sound nutrition facts and allow the informed decisions of the populace.

title: Engineering Education on Professional Curriculum Practical Computer Science
authors: Igor Krepkov, Alexander Yermakov, Oleg Zhnyakin
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article discusses a systematic approach to the development of professional educational curriculum on practical computing science. The authors justify the need for a profound transformation of curricula in response to the strategic objectives of technological sovereignty and digitalization of the economy, as well as global challenges in the field of digital transformation. The article proposes the use of the enterprise architecture (EA) concept as a methodological framework, which ensures the integrity and practical orientation of training. Based on an analysis of labor market trends and changes in students' educational motivation, the article formulates key principles for the development of the curriculum, as well as detailed educational trajectories for the main blocks of competencies. The article concludes with a model for training highly qualified IT specialists who are capable of immediately and effectively solving fullstack production tasks.

title: Adaptive Admittance Control for Conflict-free Climbing of a Dual-arm Robot in Space Stations
authors: Jin Sun, Siyong Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Traditional fixed admittance control methods suffer from significant internal force conflicts in dual-arm space robots during climbing maneuvers, which can damage actuators and destabilize the system. In this paper, an adaptive admittance climbing control method is proposed to reduce such force conflicts in dual-arm space robots. First, the kinematic and dynamic models of the dual-arm space robot are introduced. Next, a slavemaster control architecture is implemented to coordinate robotic arms, where one arm operates in position mode while the other is controlled by an adaptive admittance controller. Additionally, an adaptive admittance control method based on a backpropagation network is proposed to manage the slave arm. The virtual impedance gains are adjusted online using the backpropagation network. By dynamically tuning the impedance parameters, force conflicts during climbing tasks can be significantly reduced. Simulations in MATLAB/Simulink environment demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Eye Focused Efficientnetb3 for Lightweight and Accurate Crossed Versus Normal Eye Classification
authors: S. Leela Lakshmi, Seeniappan Kaliappan, Md Ankushavali, Avani Mittal
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This research will be focused on the implementation of EfficientNetB3 in the automatic classification of Crossed Eyes and Normal Eyes using a dataset in Kaggle, which contains 534 images of the same cases. To improve the focus of the EfficientNetB3 model and minimize background bias, a strategy of region of interest was adopted to extract the region containing the eye, which was then resized to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224 \times 224$</tex> and normalized. The dataset will be split into training, validation, and test sets at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80 \%, 10 \%$</tex>, and 10 %, respectively. The transfer learning will be applied to the EfficientNetB3 model by training it using ImageNet and a light classification head for 25 epochs using the Adam optimizer and binary cross-entropy loss. On the independent test data set containing 53 images, the accuracy of the proposed model was 90.57 %, and the precision/recall/F1-score for Crossed Eyes and Normal Eyes were <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.857 / 0.900 / 0.878$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.938 / 0.909 / 0.923$</tex>, respectively. The confusion matrix showed only four errors, and from this, it is confirmed that the model is stable in terms of generalization. The above results confirm that the proposed model, i.e., EfficientNetB3 along with ROI-based preprocessing, can be used to achieve an effective and computationally efficient solution for the problem of automated crossed-eyed

title: An Empirical Study of Version-Control and Validation Workflows for Enterprise AI Systems
authors: Abhijit Choudhary, Avimanyou Vatsa, Rohan Puppala, Aarav Rao
publish: 2026 35th Wireless and Optical Communications Conference (WOCC)
abstract: Today, AI systems have been embedded in enterprise software and are surrounded by a large array of infrastructure, supporting code, and processes of traditional software. An AI system inherits all the challenges of regular code, as well as AI-specific system-level challenges, thereby introducing risks of technical debt and costly future maintenance. This warrants a hybrid framework that, on the one hand, mitigates AI-specific issues through a validation approach that caters to both stochastic and deterministic behaviors typically observed in LLM-powered systems. On the other hand, it emphasizes adopting standard software version control practices, in which every feature promotion passes through quality gates that involve both automated checks and human-in-the-loop review, ensuring controlled evolution and backward compatibility of the system. Therefore, this study revisits the underpinnings of enterprise workflows across key system boundaries in software development and employs an LLM-as-a-Judge approach to mitigate AI system drift.

title: An Intelligent UPI Fraud Detection Framework Using Machine Learning for Secure Digital Payments
authors: Shabana Sultana, Harshitha H S
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The rapid embrace of the digital payment systems has greatly changed the nature of a financial transaction and it has become a high risk of fraud. Unified Payments Interface Transactions are large and real-time; therefore, they need smart and efficient fraud detection systems that can guarantee safe online payments. The authors present in the paper a smart framework of detecting fraud acts with the help of machine learning that helps detect fraud in connection with Unified Payments Interface transactions with great precision and consistency. The proposed model will consist of feature learning via deep learning and a robust classification model capable of aggregating complex transaction patterns and nonlinear relationships typically associated with fraud. Transaction data containing both legit and fraudulent transactions are used to train and evaluate the model. The structure is intended to be applied in close to real time; therefore, it applies to real-life implementation in digital payment systems. This has been demonstrated in the experimental results, where the proposed solution has been shown to outperform traditional fraud detection tools in terms of detection accuracy, resiliency, and scalability. The work is novel in that it possesses a hybrid learning architecture, offers a more robust representation of transaction behavior, and maintains excellent classification performance across different types of fraud. The received findings indicate that the proposed structure would contribute to reducing the financial loss by half and raising the user confidence, as it would enable them to identify the fraudulent conduct within the digital payment ecosystems in a timely manner and with a high level of accuracy and prevent the safety and maintenance of the modern cashless payment systems.
