title: FBG-Based Wearable Sensors for Healthcare 4.0: A System-Level Review with AI, Generative AI, and XAI Integration
authors: Pradeep Kumar, Ruchi Antil, Eugene Su, Chao-Ching Ho
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Areas in Sensors
abstract: Wearable sensor (WS) technology has advanced rapidly in recent years, driven by the increasing demand for continuous physiological monitoring. Among various sensing approaches, fiber Bragg grating (FBG) sensors have emerged as strong candidates for wearable systems due to their lightweight nature, electromagnetic immunity, multiplexing capability, and high sensitivity to strain, temperature, and motion. This review presents recent developments in FBG-based wearable sensors within the framework of Healthcare 4.0, where sensing, computation, and communication are integrated into a unified cyber-physical architecture. A structured literature review methodology is adopted to ensure comprehensive coverage and reproducibility. The fundamentals of FBG sensing and their implementation in wearable platforms are first outlined. Representative applications-including MRI-compatible cardiorespiratory monitoring, carotid pulse assessment, skin temperature mapping, gait analysis, and joint posture detection-are examined to highlight the performance advantages of FBG systems. A comparative analysis is further provided to distinguish FBG wearables from conventional electronic sensors in terms of sensitivity, robustness, and compatibility with clinical environments. Key challenges, such as interrogator size, power consumption, and placement-dependent sensitivity variations, are critically discussed. Emerging solutions are explored through the integration of artificial intelligence (AI), explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) for model interpretability, generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) for synthetic data augmentation and improved generalization, and singular value decomposition (SVD)-based signal decoupling and dimensionality reduction for efficient edge processing. Additionally, advancements in compact photonic interrogators and fog-cloud data processing frameworks are highlighted. Therefore, FBG-based wearable technologies demonstrate strong potential for enabling reliable, high-precision, and intelligent health monitoring in next-generation Healthcare 4.0 systems.

title: Matching Comes First: Efficient Certificateless Lattice-Based Bilateral Access Control with On-Demand Matching
authors: Xin Gao, Huaqun Wang, Mengxue Yang, Hua Dai, Debiao He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: The proliferation of data-driven services on cloud platforms, coupled with stringent regulatory frameworks like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), necessitates crypto-graphic solutions that ensure secure and efficient data exchange. However, simultaneously achieving post-quantum security, bilateral access control, data authenticity, simplified key management and efficiency remains a critical challenge. To address these issues, this paper introduces a certificateless lattice-based matchmaking encryption (CLLME) to provide post-quantum security while obviating key escrow and certificate management. The proposed scheme enforces bilateral access control, allowing both data senders and receivers to specify matching access structures; decryption is thus contingent upon mutual authorization. Moreover, to prevent the costly decryption of numerous irrelevant ciphertexts, CLLME embeds a lightweight authenticity operation, which enables retrieval of useful data, effectively creating a high-performance filter for encrypted data streams. We formally prove that CLLME achieves indistinguishability against chosen-plaintext attacks and existential unforgeability against chosen-message attacks under lattice-based assumptions. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that CLLME maintains favorable communicational and computational efficiency, confirming its suitability for practical and scalable deployment in regulation-compliant, large-scale data sharing environments.

title: Aligning Perception, Reasoning, Modeling and Interaction: A Survey on Physical AI
authors: Kun Xiang, Terry Jingchen Zhang, Yinya Huang, Jixi He, Zirong Liu, Yueling Tang, Ruizhe Zhou, Lijing Luo, Youpeng Wen, Xiuwei Chen, Bingqian Lin, Jianhua Han, Hang Xu, Hanhui Li, Bin Dong, Xiaodan Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: The convergence of embodied intelligence and world models has catalyzed growing interest in integrating physical laws into AI systems. While prior surveys have examined world models and embodied intelligence separately, we focus on the progression that connects these capabilities as a unified developmental pathway from passive observation to active physical comprehension. This survey provides a systematic framework revealing how physical AI advances through four interconnected stages: perception transforms sensory data into structured physical representations, reasoning derives explanations from observed phenomena, modeling enables predictive simulation grounded in physical principles, and embodied interaction closes the loop through physical manipulation and environmental feedback. Each stage enables and enhances the next: perceptual grounding supports causal reasoning, reasoning unlocks predictive capabilities, and robust models drive genuine physical interaction. Through analysis of developments spanning architectural innovations, training methodologies, causal inference, and embodied systems, we synthesize how physical understanding emerges through cumulative integration across this progression. Our framework reveals the evolution from isolated, task-specific solutions toward integrated architectures that advance from pattern recognition toward causal reasoning and counterfactual prediction. This perspective provides foundations for next-generation physical AI systems with direct implications for safe, generalizable, and interpretable deployment across robotics, scientific discovery, and autonomous systems. We maintain a continuously updated taxonomy repository at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/AI4Phys/Awesome-AI-for-Physics</uri>.

title: Adaptive Endmember Bundle Extraction with a Modified Differential Evolution Algorithm for Addressing Spectral Variability in Geology-1 Hyperspectral Imagery
authors: Linpei Yang, Ruyi Feng, Banxiao Ruan, Kaijun Yang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: High-precision extraction of alteration mineral information provides crucial guidance for hyperspectral remote sensing mineral exploration. Currently, widely used hyperspectral datasets (such as ZY-1 02D and GF-5) lack targeted design in spectral coverage and sensitive bands for minerals. The successful launch of China's first dedicated hyperspectral satellite for geology and mineral exploration - Geology-1 - has addressed this gap and provides an optimal data source for detailed alteration mineral identification. However, mineral spectra are susceptible to variation under complex geological conditions. To effectively characterize this spectral variability, the endmember bundle method is adopted, which depicts an endmember category through a set of spectra. Existing endmember bundle extraction methods suffer from low computational efficiency and inadequate accuracy when processing high-dimensional hyperspectral data under complex geological conditions, which limits the effective interpretation of mineral information. To address this challenge, we propose an Adaptive Differential Evolution Endmember Bundle Extraction method (ADE-EBE). Based on a multi-stage optimization framework, this method transforms high-dimensional discrete space into low-dimensional continuous space for dynamic optimization. By employing an adaptive differential evolution algorithm to globally search for optimal endmember combinations, it significantly enhances the stability and accuracy of endmember bundle extraction. Experiments on simulated dataset and Geology-1 hyperspectral imagery show that ADE-EBE achieves superior overall performance compared to traditional and state-of-the-art methods in terms of spectral fidelity, abundance accuracy, and computational efficiency.

title: MMDOK: A Multi-modal and Multi-scale Disease-oriented Fusion Framework with Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
authors: Wei Li, Xun Gong, Lin Fan, Xinxin Li, Jiao Li, Xiaobin Sun
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Learning medical visual representations directly from image-report pairs has become an emerging topic in representation learning. However, the heterogeneity and complementary nature of medical reports and images pose challenges to adaptively fusing. We propose a Multi-modal and Multi-scale Disease-oriented fusion framework with Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (MMDOK). This method utilizes the multi-scale features naturally present in medical images and reports and explores feature fusion between multi-view or multi-modal images and textual reports. Specifically, MMDOK designs a text encoder and two image encoders to enhance the diagnostic model by adaptively integrating text supervision and additional image information. To enhance the model's ability to extract information across different semantic scales, we introduce a Bidirectional Cross-Attention (BCA) module and a Cross-Modal Clustering (CMC) module. The proposed CMC module effectively captures disease consistent latent features from both images and clinical reports, alleviating overfitting and improving generalization to out-of-distribution test sets. To reduce cross-modal redundancy, we introduce a Disease-Oriented Attention (DOA) module, which adaptively assigns modality-specific weights based on disease labels, enabling more effective and context-aware feature fusion. Additionally, we embed Kolmogorov-Arnold Network layers into various modules to enable efficient and accurate feature extraction through enhanced nonlinear representation capacity. We evaluate our model's performance, achieving an average accuracy of 94.27% and 83.67% with and without text supervision on a public lung disease dataset, and 87.63% and 82.1% on a private submucosal tumors dataset, outperforming state-of-the-art alternatives.

title: Agent xG Core: Agentic AI for Next-Generation Mobile Core Network
authors: Maria Katarine Santana Barbosa, Kelvin Lopes Dias
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: To meet the stringent requirements of emerging applications and the increasingly complex network management and operation, the Next Generation Mobile Networks (NextG), or 6G, will adopt an AI-native architecture on the Core Network (CN). In this movement, the Third Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) has extended the cellular CN with new function as a first step toward integrating analytics, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and machine learning. However, those new functionalities are constrained by a centralized approach and managerial complexity. Furthermore, with the rise of Large Language Models (LLMs), a new era in network orchestration and management begins, leveraging and empowering the Intent-based Networking (IBN) paradigm. In addition, AI agents and Agentic AI integrate Reasoning and Acting (ReAct), enabling the usage of such intents to continuously interact with the network. Unlike state-of-the-art approaches that primarily employ Agentic AI to mitigate deployment and configuration complexity in the CN, this paper introduces AgentxGCore, which leverages an Agentic AI-Native layer to extend the 3GPP architecture and enable a system based on the existing APIs across the Beyond Next Generation Core (xGC) domain. This proposal establishes an AI-driven closed-loop for continuous optimization based on real-time information, enabling self-organization and self-adaptation. Our approach involves a multi-agent specialized system, divided into a network planner agent, capable of visualizing the network state and developing a plan to meet the intents, and a network executor, responsible for criticizing and executing the plan. To validate the proposed solution, an environment was built using an open-source CN, heterogeneous datasets, and different LLMs were employed to demonstrate its effectiveness.

title:
Nanoporous design for enhancing full-band solar-blind absorption in Ga
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    photodetectors
authors: Xieyu Lin, Jialong Li, Dan Yang, Linghua Wang, Haizhong Zhang, Xiaoqiang Lu, Minmin Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nanotechnology
abstract: Gallium oxide, characterized by its ultra-broad bandwidth, high electron drift velocity, and excellent thermal stability, demonstrates significant potential for solar-blind ultraviolet photodetectors (SBUV-PDs) that can be applied in both military and civilian contexts, such as missile warning, fire detection, and secure communications. However, the conventional metal semiconductor-metal (MSM) structure of photodetectors results in suboptimal responsivity due to reflection and absorption at the metal-semiconductor interfaces, as well as the inability of the semiconductor to effectively optimize light absorption. In this work, we propose a periodic nanopore design in Ga2O3 thin films, which capitalizes the coupling between incident light and the electronic oscillations on the Ga2O3 surface, thereby increasing the effective absorption area as well as inducing optical interference within the structure. Compared to the structure without surface modifications, the photodark current ratio (PDCR) increases by an order of magnitude, from 2.39×10⁷ to 4.26×10⁸, and the responsivity is enhanced by a factor of 5.87, rising from 0.55 to 3.23 A/W. This study is expected to offer a reliable strategy for the fabrication of high-performance SBUV-PDs.

title: Tractable Approximation of Labeled Multi-Object Posterior Densities
authors: Thi Hong Thai Nguyen, Ba-Ngu Vo, Ba-Tuong Vo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: Multi-object estimation in state-space models (SSMs) wherein the system state is represented as a finite set has attracted significant interest in recent years. In Bayesian inference, the posterior density captures all information on the system trajectory since it considers the history of the states. In most multi-object SSM applications, closed-form multi-object posteriors are not available for non-standard multi-object models. Thus, functional approximation is necessary because these posteriors are very high-dimensional. This work provides a tractable multi-scan Generalized Labeled Multi-Bernoulli (GLMB) approximation that matches the trajectory cardinality distribution of the labeled multi-object posterior density. The proposed approximation is also proven to minimize the Kullback-Leibler divergence over a special class of multi-scan GLMB model. Additionally, we develop a tractable algorithm for computing the approximate multi-object posteriors over finite windows. Numerical multi-object tracking experiments, using a simulated social force model with uninformative observations, and a real-world social force pedestrian dataset are presented to validate the proposed approximation method.

title: Text-Driven Relation Manipulation of Diffusion Imagery
authors: Yiming Li, Peng Zhou, Hongwei Hu, Xiaokang Qin, Jun Sun, Yi Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Text-guided image manipulation has recently attracted significant attention. Prevailing algorithms predominantly focus on modifying the appearances of existing instances, such as texture and attribute editing, while they often fail to address the interactions between different instances or achieve fundamental structural changes, such as multi-object editing. This paper introduces a novel text-guided manipulation task named “relation manipulation”, aimed at fundamentally altering the structure of images. This task is capable of modifying the quantity of instances and, more importantly, enhancing the understanding and editing of interactions among diverse instances. Our approach comprises two main components: relation customization and multi-region guided diffusion. Relation customization fine-tunes specific relationships using a compact dataset of exemplary relations, facilitating nuanced understanding and implementation of instance interactions. Multi-region guided diffusion employs gradient optimization to update the generation process across multiple regions, integrating a fine-grained attention control strategy to minimize regional interference and conflict. Additionally, the demonstrated applications of our method in multi-region inversion underline its potential in practical scenarios, such as relation manipulation of real images and consecutive image manipulation. Compatible with different variants of Stable Diffusion models, our approach seamlessly integrates into the Stable Diffusion WebUI, enabling high-quality image generation and exceptional control over extensive manipulation. This makes it a robust tool for both academic research and creative industries.

title: Spacecraft Mission Scheduling Based on Event-Activity Network and Improved ACS
authors: Chenhui Luan, Di Fei, Aiying Jing, Shengran Dong, Xia Xue, Zhihui Li, Rong Dang, Yong Feng
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: The development of spacecraft involves complex scheduling issues with strict resource constraints and distinct task priorities. Formulating a full-life-cycle scheduling plan during the project initiation of a spacecraft is of great significance. This paper proposes a method based on an event-activity network and an elitist Ant Colony System (ACS) integrated with a dynamic parameter strategy. By establishing an event-activity network, milestone events and task units are encoded to achieve dynamic scheduling optimization under multi-mode resource constraints. An intelligent queuing mechanism is used to resolve resource conflicts, and a randomly perturbed ACS method is designed to improve resource scheduling efficiency. The verification results of the spacecraft case show that this method can effectively solve resource conflict problems, and the improved ACS further enhances task efficiency, shortening the task cycle by approximately $26 \%$ compared with traditional methods, thus providing decision support for spacecraft engineering management.

title: AI-Assisted Channel Estimation for 6G Massive MIMO using Hybrid CNN–Transformer Networks
authors: Saveetha.T, R.Ramya
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Accurate channel estimation remains a key challenge in 6G massive MIMO systems, particularly in mmWave and THz frequency bands where channels are sparse, highly dynamic, and constrained by limited pilot resources. To address these issues, this work introduces a Hybrid CNN–Transformer based channel estimation framework (HyCT-CE) that combines the strengths of local spatial feature extraction and global spectral dependency learning through an effective fusion strategy. Simulation results, conducted using standardized 3GPP CDL and EVA channel models, show that the proposed approach achieves up to 42% improvement in NMSE compared to standalone CNN and Transformer models, with a consistent reduction of 30– 40% across an SNR range of −10 dB to 30 dB. In addition, the model reduces pilot overhead by nearly 33% while maintaining stable performance even under low SNR and high mobility scenarios. The inference time is approximately 2.9 ms per sample on RTX-class GPUs, demonstrating its feasibility for real-time applications. Overall, the proposed method offers a practical and scalable solution for reliable channel estimation in future 6G wireless systems.

title: SAHT-Net: A Sparse Attention Hyperspectral Transformer Network for Martian Mineral Abundance Unmixing
authors: Bo Wan, Sicong Liu, Wenxuan Jia
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Mapping Martian surface minerals is essential for understanding geological evolution and identifying past aqueous environments. Hyperspectral imaging provides detailed spectral information for mineral identification. However, conventional Martian hyperspectral abundance unmixing techniques rely on manually constructed spectral libraries or endmember extraction directly from images. These approaches are constrained by the inherent difficulty in acquiring pure endmembers and demand substantial prior knowledge. To address these challenges, this paper proposes SAHT-Net, a sparse attention hyperspectral Transformer network. SAHT-Net automatically learns discriminative spectral features from mixed pixels, eliminating the need for prior library construction. By incorporating endmemberaware token representations with a sparse attention mechanism, SAHT-Net ensures physically plausible and sparse abundance estimation. Experimental results obtained on a synthetic dataset and a real CRISM hyperspectral dataset demonstrate that SAHTNet achieves accurate and reliable mineral abundance unmixing, outperforming existing methods.

title: AI in Engineering Education: Vocabulary Acquisition at a Non-Linguistic University
authors: Tatiana S. Larina, Viktoria V. Iakovleva
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article addresses the issue of acquiring profession-oriented lexical units in a foreign language at a nonlinguistic university. It analyzes the challenges encountered by technical university students during the language learning process, specifically the overwhelming volume of highly specialized vocabulary, time constraints, varying levels of prior preparation, and a lack of student motivation. To address these issues, a stepwise methodology for integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) into the educational process is proposed, wherein AI functions as a teaching assistant. The paper describes tasks for teacher preparation using AI, as well as interactive classroom activities, such as simulated dialogue with a chatbot, comparison of lecture notes, role-playing games, and others. The study concludes that the application of AI before and during foreign language classes can help transform the learning process, turning routine vocabulary memorization into a practice-oriented immersion into the students' future engineering profession. This approach aims to increase student motivation while preserving the instructor’s key role as a mentor.

title: Energy Storage Integrated DC-AC Converter for Green Energy Petroleum Engineering System
authors: Haochen Shi, Kai Wei
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: This paper presents topology and control algorithm of the energy storage integrated DC-AC converter, which can significantly reduce components and cost of the green energy petroleum engineering system. Firstly, topology of the proposed DC-AC converter is demonstrated and derived from original three-level converter. Then, the equivalent circuit and switching states of the proposed DC-AC converter is discussed. After that, the mathematical model of the SVPWM is presented, and the current direction of each vector is also analyzed. On this basis, simplified SVPWM and power management algorithms are derived, and selection of vectors, dwell time and regional criterion are given. Simulations result and theoretical analysis of the proposed energy storage integrated DC-AC converter is provided to verify the effectiveness of the proposed DC-AC Converter.

title: An LLM-Based Agent Framework for Intelligent Power Module Design
authors: Ruiting Ke, Jianfeng Tao, Xiaojian Ding, Chengliang Liu
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: This paper proposes a large language model (LLM)driven agent for power module design. Unlike conventional automation pipelines, it employs a standardized tool interface contract (description-schema-function) to encapsulate modeling, simulation, optimization, and validity checking into a callable tool pool. This enables the LLM to select and orchestrate tools at runtime based on intent captured from dialogue and intermediate feedback. Most tools in this pool are specialized for power module design tasks, such as topology is encoded with a directed adjacency matrix for geometric modeling, and check tools flag die overlaps and boundary violations. For robust execution, constraints are handled in two layers: (i) hard constraints are enforced within tool functions or immutable deterministic chains; (ii) instance-dependent constraints are evaluated by validity checking tools or via log parsing, with results fed back to the LLM. An agent-in-the-loop chip placement optimization task validates the agent's effectiveness, and the implementation is released as open source at https://github.com/henkuailederen/Agent-power-module-design.

title: An Explainable Graph-based Framework for Intelligent Financial Transaction Risk Detection
authors: Harish N N, Kanagapriya S K
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Millions of digital transactions are carried out by financial institutions each day, and the process of detecting suspicious financial transactions is getting more complicated. The conventional rule-based monitoring frameworks typically do not detect complex laundering pattern and usually produce a significant number of false-positive alert. This paper introduces RISKNET, a graphical financial risk analysis model which combines both behavioral transaction properties and structural network properties to identify suspicious financial activity. The proposed system represents the model of the financial transactions as a directed graph, in which the accounts are the nodes and the flows of the transactions are the edges. The use of graph measures like degree centrality, PageRank is accompanied by behavioral measures like transaction frequency, and changes in values. These characteristics are examined with the help of an Isolation Forest anomaly detection model that produces continuous risk scores on the account. Visual validation of the Elliptic Bitcoin transaction dataset shows that the suggested architecture is able to provide an accuracy of 95.2%, the precision of 94.3%, and the recall of 93.1%, which are better than a variety of baseline machine learning methods. The findings affirm the notion that sophisticated transaction risk detection performance and false alerts are greatly enhanced by network structure incorporation with behavioral analysis.

title: Advanced Use of the Boiler Designer Software for Creating Digital Twins of Complex Thermal Power Plant Equipment under Steady-State and Transient Conditions
authors: V. M. Supranov, G. I. Doverman, K.A. Pleshanov, D.A. Khokhlov, I.G. Petrov, R. S. Maslov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: Power engineering equipment is characterized by high complexity and a large amount of metal in its design. Therefore, errors in its design and calculations must be minimized, and ideally eliminated. This is facilitated by using specialized engineering software, one of which is Boiler Designer. The software is fully localized, which is critically important for Russia. The paper discusses the results of applying the software to various tasks and objects, outlines its benefits, and presents recommendations to users. Based on this, conclusions are drawn about the merits of the software.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning and Rule-Based Framework for Comprehensive Vulnerability Assessment in Modern Networked Systems
authors: Shanmuganathan V, Sibhi K, Esther P
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Vulnerability assessment in modern networked systems is crucial for evaluating important infrastructures where graph-theoretic, modelling and optimisation methodologies assist in identifying weaknesses, reducing facility loss and prioritising reinforcement initiatives. The classification of facility significance significantly impacts total vulnerability outcomes as it is crucial for assessing system survivability. This work presents an integrated strategy utilising the Security Vulnerabilities Dataset to overcome limitations in current methodologies. During preprocessing scalar and normalization procedures are utilised and principal component analysis is implemented for feature extraction. A HBRCS is subsequently established to integrate vulnerability assessment in modern networked systems. The belief rule structure, which can express incomplete, fuzzy, and imprecise causal links, facilitates the construction of a knowledge-driven belief rule basis (KBRB) from domain expertise and a data-driven belief rule base (DBRB) using empirical data. A fusion technique integrates these into an optimised Hybrid Belief Rule Base (HBRB) which facilitates a belief reasoning and decision-making module for classifying query patterns. The results indicate that the suggested model attains an accuracy of 96.02%, hence confirming its efficacy in providing dependable vulnerability evaluations. This approach improves adaptability, interpretability and performance in assessing vulnerabilities of contemporary networked systems.

title: SmartFloor: AI-Powered Natural Language to 3D Architectural Visualization Framework
authors: Donthamoni Jhansi, G. Balakrishna
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: This paper introduces SmartFloor, an intelligent architectural planning system that uses natural language building descriptions to generate structured 2D floor plans and their associated 3D models. The system combines hybrid language parsing, constraint-based spatial reasoning, machine learning-based normalization, and heuristic layout synthesis. Unlike purely generative systems, SmartFloor models adjacency and circulation relationships using graph-based representations and enables multi-objective optimization. Experiments conducted on residential and industrial building examples show a high accuracy of 96.2% in entity extraction, high layout coherence, and improved design time efficiency over manual drafting. The system also shows robust performance in Windows and Jupyter settings, proving its applicability.

title: An Intelligent LSTM-Based Network Risk Prediction Framework for Time-Stamped Cybersecurity Logs
authors: Pushplata Patel, Chilukuri Bala Venkata Subbarayudu, Abdumutalliev Abdulakhad Abdusamad Ugli, Chrispin Jiji, R. Srinivasan, Yasir Mahmood Younus
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The rapid increase in the time-stamped security logs that record various and different attack patterns is increasing the vulnerability of computer networks. These logs contain useful temporal information, whereas conventional intrusion detection systems typically do not model sequential dependencies and time-lagged relationships among events. The fact that time-stamped cybersecurity logs have grown at a rapid rate, has complicated the process of detecting the changing patterns of attacks within the present computer networks. Conventional intrusion detection schemes tend to not capture serial dependencies and time-based association between occurrences and therefore cannot act promptly and respond predictably. The paper is a proposal of a smart threat prediction system that combines Temporal Pattern Learning and Long Short-Term Memory networks in proactive network risk analysis. The suggested TPL-LSTM model would process and examine sequential security logs to learn latent time variants and long-term dependencies of computer attacks. The framework can detect potential threats early and monitor intrusion in real-time in enterprise Security Operations Centers by establishing lagged relationships among log events. The results of the experimental works prove that the suggested method is more accurate in predictions and quicker on reactions than the traditional method of detection. The research is also a contribution to the field of cybersecurity because it presents a predictive deep learning framework that can be used to improve situational awareness and proactive defense mechanisms against impending attacks in a network.

title: Lightweight Inference-Time Backdoor Attack Detection for Resource-Constrained Internet of Drones using Tiny Vision Transformers
authors: Rahgul M S, Sophia S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The Internet of Drones (IoD) increasingly relies on machine learning models for perception and autonomous decision-making in safety-critical applications. However, the use of pre-trained or third-party models introduces significant security risks, particularly backdoor attacks, where hidden triggers embedded during training can manipulate model behavior at inference time. Detecting such attacks in resource-constrained drone environments is challenging due to strict limitations in computation, memory, and energy. This paper seeks to design a lightweight and efficient inference-time backdoor detection framework appropriate for real-time deployment in IoD systems. To achieve this, a Tiny Vision Transformer (Tiny-ViT)-based approach is proposed that operates without requiring access to training data, labels, or gradient information. The method extracts low-dimensional statistical activation features from selected transformer blocks and performs activation consistency analysis using adaptive thresholding on unlabeled inputs, enabling the detection of both known and previously unseen backdoor triggers. Experimental results on the VisDrone dataset demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves a detection accuracy of 96.5% and an F1-score of 96.3%, with a low false positive rate of 2.9%. In addition, the method introduces minimal overhead, with only 21.4 ms detection latency and 29 MB memory usage, while preserving normal model performance. These findings confirm that the proposed approach provides an effective and practical solution for enhancing the security and reliability of IoD-enabled intelligent systems deployed on resource-constrained platforms.

title: Robust Infrared Reflective Sensors in Low-Cost Leader-Follower Mobile Robots System
authors: Zhiheng Li, Yurui Jin, Yanhua Zhang
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: This study evaluates the robust performance of low-cost infrared sensors in Leader-Follower tracking tasks under varying ambient-light conditions within a real-world environment. We use low-cost 3Pi 32U4 robot equipped with dual infrared sensors to measure angular deviation, and compare the two sensor types in a real-world environment. Experimental results show that the bump sensor offers superior robustness in distance and angle estimation under various lighting conditions, while the line sensor performs better in close-range line-following scenarios. This study provides quantitative guidance for sensor selection in low-cost robotic platforms and contributes to the development of small-scale robotic systems.

title: Intellectualization of a robotic Measuring System Based on the Self-Control Function
authors: Dmitry Zhukov, Anatoliy Shvetcov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The purpose of this article is to develop a self-control methodology as an intellectualization function for robotic measuring systems. The article discusses solutions for the intellectualization of robotic measuring systems. The algorithm of the self-diagnostic function for the intellectualization of a robotic measuring system is conditionally divided into two stages. The first stage of self-control is focused on checking suitability during initial startup or immediately before the first execution of control procedures in production at the beginning of a work shift. The reference values for self-control and measurement objects are the end measures of length available at the production site, which does not require the additional manufacture of a standard. The second stage of self-control consists of checking suitability during the performance of control and measurement operations throughout inter-operational control (in real time) during the working day. In the second stage, the ANOWA dispersion analysis method is used and the stability of the measurement process is monitored. Dispersion analysis allows you to analyze the influence of an industrial robot and a digital indicator on the error. The method allows for effective verification of several system parameters: offset, stability, and linearity, as well as deviations in the repeatability of the robot or measuring device in real time during inter-operational control, in contrast to the solutions presented in the cited publications.

title: Interpretable Ensemble Machine Learning Approaches for Sleep and Lifestyle Health Prediction
authors: Shri Venkatesh Babu Bharaneedharan, K. Sankar, S. Vairachilai, Abhay Kumar Sinha
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Lifestyle behaviors and demographic factors play a crucial role in the quality and efficiency of sleep and thus there is a demand to have an accurate and interpretable predictive model. The paper compares the performance of classical and ensemble machine learning models on a 1,000-patient dataset and involves such characteristics as age, physical activity and substance consumption, and sleep-stage indicators. According to the experimental findings, Decision Tree was found to have an accuracy of 91.0 percent, whereas the Support Vector Machine and the Random Forest only had 88.5 percent and 88.0 percent, respectively. The ensemble methods such as XGBoost, CatBoost, stacking, and blending always delivered the highest score of 91.0% accuracy, with AdaBoost showing the lowest score at 67.5% and KNN and Naive Bayes showing relatively lower scores. To improve interpretability, Explainable Artificial Intelligence models, including SHAP, LIME, Individual Conditional Expectation (ICE), and Permutation Feature Importance (PFI), have been used, which allowed a global and local explanation of the model behavior. The analysis found out the frequency of exercise, the ratio of deep sleep, awakenings, and lifestyle practices such as caffeine and alcohol use as a significant predictor of sleep outcomes. In general, the combination of explainable approaches with ensemble learning creates a strong and clear structure of sleep prediction to support individual health tracking and lifestyle optimization.

title: When Engineering Meets Care Coordination: A Student’s Journey to Solve Health Care’s Invisible Bottleneck
authors: Naheem Noah
publish: IEEE Pulse
abstract: Health care referral coordination remains one of the most persistent inefficiencies in the U.S. health care system, with millions of annual referrals still managed through phone calls, faxes, and manual spreadsheets. This article documents one computer science Ph.D. student’s journey from personal frustration to building Carenector, an AI-powered care coordination platform. Drawing on knowledge graph modeling, privacy-preserving ephemeral computation, and simultaneous constraint satisfaction, the platform reframes the referral process as an engineering matching problem. The system’s knowledge graph represents facilities and services across multiple regions, updating dynamically to reflect real-time availability, without storing any personally identifiable patient information. Initially deployed as a consumer-facing tool for individuals and families, the platform has since expanded to an institutional version targeting hospital discharge planning teams, with pilot results demonstrating significant time savings per referral. This work highlights how computational engineering principles, applied to workflow design, data modeling, and human-AI collaboration, can transform even the most entrenched manual processes in health care.

title: Fiber Optic Transport Solutions for 6G Mobile Networks
authors: S. S. Kogan, L. N. Isaeva, A. V. Lobzov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: In this paper we discuss fiber optic transport solutions for 6 G mobile networks. Features of solutions for mobile networks of the 6 G, the main open radio access segments in 6G architecture are shown. The optical transport network/wavelength division multiplexing fiber transport solutions are a critical element of 6 G mobile network infrastructure. We systematized technologies, except for optical transport network/wavelength division multiplexing, used at the transport fiber-optic level of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 G}$</tex> mobile communication networks. We have summarized midhaul, backhaul level features identified for 6 G networks. We have shown that the core network layer in 6 G is not just a “fast Internet,” but an intelligent, converged, cloud-based “brain” that will connect everything from humans to devices and simulations, working as a single, self-optimizing system.

title: Synthetic Data-Driven Paradigms for Robust Lunar and Martian Exploration: Bridging Simulation and Real-World
authors: Hengling Chen, Shengqiang He, Luxin Jiang, Cheng Yang, Wu Yang, Zhenwei He, Chengzhi Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: The scarcity of authentic geological and environmental data from lunar and Martian surfaces presents a critical bottleneck for advancing robotic exploration. To address this, we propose a novel framework leveraging high-fidelity 3D digital simulation and rendering engines to generate reliable synthetic datasets as the primary data source. Our approach efficiently constructs task-specific synthetic scenarios from minimal photographic information, physics-based terrain modeling, and mission-specific operational requirements (e.g., visual images, depth map, etc.). Crucially, The effective transfer of AI models trained on synthetic data to real-world is also a key point, which is significate to bridge the “synthetic-reality gap,” significantly reducing reliance on scarce physical data while ensuring model generalizability. The synthesized datasets and transferable AI models enable more robust autonomous navigation, barrier detection, and scientific analysis in future missions, directly enhancing the feasibility and safety of deep-space exploration.

title: Hybrid Physical–Machine Learning Model for Photovoltaic Power Prediction Under Semi-Arid Climate Conditions
authors: Mustapha Ezzini, Mohammed Ennejjar, Raja Mouachi, Abdelali El Gourari, Mohammed Boukendil, Mustapha Raoufi
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Accurate photovoltaic (PV) power prediction is essential for efficient energy management and reliable integration of solar energy into electrical grids. This paper proposes a hybrid physical-machine learning approach for photovoltaic power prediction under semi-arid climate conditions. The proposed method integrates a calibrated Huld physical model with a Random Forest regression algorithm to estimate and correct systematic prediction errors under real operating conditions. The model is developed and validated using 6,220 measurements collected from a 2.34 kW grid-connected photovoltaic installation located between Marrakech and Benguerir in Morocco. The input variables include solar irradiance components, module temperature, ambient temperature, relative humidity, and wind speed. Although the dataset is derived from a single experimental site, it captures a wide range of meteorological variability representative of semi-arid climates. Performance evaluation demonstrates that the proposed hybrid model significantly improves prediction accuracy compared to conventional physical models and standalone machine learning approaches. The hybrid model achieves a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 123 W, a coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(R^{2}\right)$</tex> of 0.971, and a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 9.6%. The results confirm that the proposed hybrid approach provides accurate and physically consistent photovoltaic power prediction under semi-arid climatic conditions, while offering a transferable framework for similar environments.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Human Skin Disease Identification with EfficientNetV2-L and SVM
authors: G. Gokulnathan, Y.Karthick Raja, Y. Manimegalai, P. Obulaxmi, G. Nagarajan, M. Sakthivel
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Dermatological conditions are among the most common health problems in the world, yet they very frequently are difficult to properly diagnose because visually similar conditions, as well as the color and image artifacts frequently alter their appearance both rural areas generally are faced with a major shortage of dermatology professionals to provide their patients with access to these services. To better address these diagnostic shortcomings this paper presents a Hybrid Deep Learning Framework that utilizes both lower levels of computing resources as well as higher precision in detecting skin diseases based on human anatomy specifically. After the input image is preprocessed, image quality is improved using one of the EfficientNetV2-L algorithms, with a final output of high-level features extracted from the three model types in each framework. The hybrid deep learning framework model is designed so that extracted features are processed by using an algorithm that provides a means of effectively classifying each instance of extraction using state-of-the-art classifiers based on CNNs rather than simply training a CNN classifier as done in previous studies. Further the use of Support Vector Machines (SVM) allows for the classification of numerous instances of visually similar skin diseases to be separated from each using fewer classifiers limiting the possibility of over fitting as well as improving generalizability. Finally, the model is optimized to be fully functional within various clinical environments using an ONNX runtime which also provides accelerated running performance. Overall analysis of the ISIC data demonstrates 94% accuracy with an exceptionally low incidence of false positive results indicating an excellent level of clinical support for making a good clinical decision.

title: Precision Tumor Detection: Enhancing Clustering Models with Hybrid Intelligence for Medical Imaging
authors: Faiza Iram, Mohammed Ali Shaik
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Class imbalance, low-contrast lesions, and inter-slice contextual variability are the issues, which hinder the accurate tumor detection in medical imaging. The research paper presents a Hybrid Intelligence- Augmented Tumor Detection (HIATD) framework that involves the use of semantic voxel embeddings and hierarchical clustering with an adaptive gating fusion mechanism. A 3D CNN-based Transformer was used to preprocess, augment, and encode input MRI and CT volumes of BraTS 2021 and TCIA multi-institutional cohorts. A combination of cluster level context embedding and semantics features produced voxel-level tumor probability. The macro-F1, AUC, and inference latency are 0.843, 0.927 and 42 ms/scan, respectively, and the calibration is better (ECE = 0.087) than baseline U-Net and Attention U-Net models. HIATD is both more sensitive to low-contrast and small tumors by stabilizing the cluster assignments on heterogeneous tissue and is generalized to domain shifts by offering a reproducible and clinically deployable infrastructure of high-precision tumor localization of multi-institutional medical imaging datasets.

title: Development and application of a hand-transmitted vibration measurement system based on LabVIEW
authors: Yang Yu, Hao Fu, Zhe Wang, Tingting Wu, Yiyao Wang, Junliang Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: A hand-transmitted vibration measurement system based on LabVIEW was developed to evaluate the response characteristics of the hand-arm system to vibration. The mechanical impedance and vibration transmissibility can be accurately measured through a dynamic signal measurement and analysis system and several sensors. Mechanical impedance reflects the resistance of the hand-arm system to vibration, while vibration transmissibility describes the transmission characteristics of vibration in the hand-arm system. The measurement results indicate that the mechanical impedance and vibration transmissibility of the handarm system, as measured by the developed system, are consistent with existing research findings, which verifies the measurement system’s effectiveness and accuracy. This study provides a method reference for the analysis of biodynamic response characteristics of the hand-arm system.

title: Voice-Based Parkinson’s Disease Detection Utilising Autoencoder-Guided Graph Neural Networks
authors: Veena B
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Early diagnosis of PD is complicated by the subtle and non-linear nature of vocal impairments occurring in the initial stages of the disorder. Traditional voice-based approaches to PD diagnosis mainly rely on supervised machine learning techniques combined with handcrafted features, which often lack generalization and interpretability. To address such limitations, this work proposes a novel explainable framework that combines unsupervised representation learning along with graph-based deep learning for Parkinson’s disease detection from voice signals. In the presented approach, a deep autoencoder learns compact latent representations of high dimensional speech features in an unsupervised manner without using class labels, and the reconstruction error acts as an anomaly-aware measure of interpretability to capture the deviations associated with pathological speech patterns. Then, voice samples are modeled as nodes in a similarity-driven graph and a GCN is trained to jointly exploit learned feature representations and inter sample relationships for classification. In the experimental study, using the benchmark Parkinson’s voice dataset, it has been demonstrated that the proposed framework allows for achieving robust performance in the classification task and improving the discrimination between patients with Parkinson’s disease and healthy subjects. Autoencoder driven anomaly characterization combined with graph neural networks allowed the enhancement of predictive accuracy and model transparency; therefore, it can be positioned as an effective and interpretable tool for early Parkinson’s disease screening and further clinical decision-support systems.

title: Deep Relational Knowledge Distillation Hashing via Relaxed Masking Triplet Optimization for Large-scale Image Retrieval

title: League Training-Based Dual Optimization Method for UAV Maneuver Decision-Making
authors: Haojie Zhu, Mou Chen, Chao Yan, Zengliang Han, Tongle Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Currently, most existing learning-based autonomous maneuver decision-making methods for Uncrewed Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) focus on network weights optimization, overlooking the critical role of network architecture optimization in enhancing decision-making performance. To address this issue, we propose a dual optimization method that improves autonomous maneuver decision-making for high-performance UAVs through concurrently learning network weights and architecture. Specifically, we utilize the recurrent Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) algorithm and Differentiable Architecture Search (DARTS) to optimize the weights and discover the optimal architecture of decision-making policy, respectively. Furthermore, we designed a league training mechanism that regards the policies with the discovered architecture as league players to further optimize their weights. The maneuver performance of league players is evaluated to facilitate the architecture search efficiency. Lastly, we validate the stability and generalization of the proposed method through a series of simulations. The results demonstrate that our method exhibits superior maneuver decision-making capabilities, outperforming existing algorithms in terms of win rates.

title: A Conversational Brain-Artificial Intelligence Interface
authors: Anja Meunier, Michal Robert Žák, Lucas Munz, Sofiya Garkot, Manuel Eder, Jiachen Xu, Moritz Grosse-Wentrup
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: We introduce Brain-Artificial Intelligence Interfaces (BAIs) as a new class of Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs). Unlike conventional BCIs, which rely on intact cognitive capabilities, BAIs leverage the power of artificial intelligence to replace parts of the neuro-cognitive processing pipeline. BAIs allow users to accomplish complex tasks by providing high-level intentions, while a pre-trained AI agent determines low-level details. This approach enlarges the target audience of BCIs to individuals with cognitive impairments, a population often excluded from the benefits of conventional BCIs. We present the general concept of BAIs and illustrate the potential of this new approach with a Conversational BAI based on electroencephalography (EEG), termed EEGChat. In particular, we show in an experiment with simulated phone conversations that the Conversational BAI enables complex communication without the need to be able to generate language. Our work thus demonstrates the ability of a speech neuroprosthesis to enable fluent communication in realistic scenarios with non-invasive technologies.

title: Parallel-Elastic Actuation with Reactive Latch Elevates Robotic Hopping Performance: Jump Height and Continuity_supp5-3701316.mp4
authors: Pakpong Chirarattananon
publish: N/A
abstract: While many animals exhibit impressive hopping capabilities, machines have struggled to match their performance. Current hopping robots face limitations in power density, energy efficiency, and control stability. Here, we present a parallel-elastic actuation mechanism with a reactive latch that optimizes energy transfer, enabling a legged robot to achieve hopping heights and continuity previously unattainable. This mechanism efficiently stores and releases energy, extending the actuation period over the aerial phase while minimizing stance time. Our robot achieves a maximum hopping height of 3.6 meters, surpassing both human and animal records while demonstrating sustained, high-frequency hopping cycles with minimal power requirement. By integrating inertia-based onboard sensorimotor autonomy, we demonstrate stable, controlled hopping in environments without external aid. These results represent a step toward bridging the performance gap between biological and robotic locomotion, with potential to influence the design of future legged systems.

title: An Attraction-Repulsion Dual-Mode Magnetically Actuated Guidewire for Superselective Navigation in Endovascular Intervention
authors: Yanding Qin, Zhuocong Cai, Shuning Wang, Qingsong Xu, Hongpeng Wang, Jianda Han
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Current magnetically actuated guidewires mainly rely on magnetic attraction for manipulation, potentially causing vascular injury due to high acceleration during steering. In this article, an attraction-repulsion dual-mode magnetically actuated guidewire (DMAG) is proposed, where a magnet and a miniature coil are sequentially embedded into the distal tip of the guidewire. Under an external magnetic field, the magnet can stabilize the distal tip's direction via magnetic repulsion; the coil generates magnetic moments in different directions by tuning the current, thereby realizing active distal tip steering. In addition to the capabilities of conventional magnetic guidewire designs, the proposed DMAG is also capable of providing precise and safe steering control to the distal tip via magnetic repulsion. Analytical modeling is established and the feasibility of steering is verified via finite element simulation. Subsequently, a series of in vitro experiments demonstrate DMAG's capability in precise steering. Furthermore, experiments on a human vascular phantom show the effectiveness of the active steering control of the distal tip in the repulsion mode. The proposed design successfully avoids the risks of stepwise deflection and elastic potential energy accumulation during steering, thereby significantly improving the safety of endovascular intervention.

title: Towards Improved Standards for Open RAN Interoperability: A Factor Screening Experiment
authors: Aziz Kord, Mary Gregg, M. Keith Forsyth, Julia L. Sharp, Jason B. Coder, Vu Le
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: As Open Radio Access Networks (Open RAN) move toward large-scale deployment, the industry requires a transition from binary “pass/fail” conformance testing to a rigorous performance metrology that characterizes system stability in multi-vendor environments. This study introduces a statistical factor screening methodology, utilizing a Resolution V fractional factorial design, to isolate the key configuration parameters that govern interoperability in a commercial-grade 7.2x functional split testbed. By executing a 1,024-run automated experimental campaign, we identify critical non-linear "performance boundaries" where specific combinations of modulation coding, power control, and UE scaling cause functional decoupling between disaggregated O-DU and O-RU components. Our results demonstrate that O-RU interoperability is not a static state but a dynamic boundary of functional invariance. This work provides a mathematically grounded framework for Mobile Network Operators (MNOs) to prioritize high-impact factors in certification and badging, accelerating the maturity of secure, high-capacity Open RAN ecosystems.

title: Correlation-Guided Progressive Interaction and Gated Fusion for Multi-Modal Optical and SAR Remote Sensing Image Object Detection
authors: Xiao-Nan Jiang, Ze-Xian Huang, Fan-Lu Wu, Yao Fu, Shi-Jie Fan, Xin Gao, Ao Han
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Multi-modal remote sensing object detection faces challenges of significant nonlinear imaging discrepancies and semantic feature misalignment between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery. Existing fusion algorithms often struggle to effectively distinguish complementary information from modality-specific noise, leading to feature redundancy or even negative transfer. To address these issues, this paper proposes a feature-level fusion strategy based on correlation-guided progressive interaction and gated fusion, which is embedded into a single-stage detection network to enhance multi-modal detection performance. For the feature enhancement stage, a Correlation-Guided Progressive Spatial Interaction (CGPSI) module is designed. This module utilizes Cross-Modal Spatial Correlation Modeling (CMSCM) to construct guidance priors and couples them with a Progressive Spatial Modulation (PSM) mechanism to generate spatial weight maps that dynamically evolve with the training process. Simultaneously, bidirectional parallel Attention-based Cross-Modal Retrieval (ACMR) is employed to capture complementary contextual features across a global receptive field. Subsequently, the generated dynamic weights are utilized to perform fine-grained spatial weighting and filtering, accurately extracting discriminative gains and suppressing cross-modal noise interference. Finally, the modulated complementary information is integrated into the backbone path through residual connections to achieve the deep integration of complementary information, significantly strengthening the representation capability of target features. For the feature fusion stage, a Gated Weight Fusion (GWF) module is adopted to identify discriminative differences in dual-modal features, effectively suppressing background noise and clutter interference in complex scenes by generating adaptive spatial weight maps. Experimental results on the OGSOD (Optical Image Guided SAR Image Object Detection) dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$mAP_{50}$</tex-math></inline-formula> of 0.873, significantly outperforming existing multi-modal detection algorithms. Ablation studies further confirm the synergistic superiority of the proposed modules: the CGPSI module enhances target feature intensity through deep interaction, while the GWF module achieves superior false alarm suppression through precise gain allocation, collectively ensuring the detection robustness of the model in heterogeneous remote sensing scenarios. Our code is accessible at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/ciomphzx/DualBranch-OptSAR-YOLO</uri>.

title: Design of Hybrid Magnetic Bearings Based on Integrated Equivalent Magnetic Network Modeling and Sensitivity Analysis for Optimization
authors: Di Wang, Ruizhi Cao, Ximing Liu, Shiqiang Zheng, Yun Le
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Hybrid magnetic bearings (HMBs) play a critical role in allowing magnetically suspended turbomolecular pumps to attain ultra-high vacuum conditions. However, current modeling and optimization approaches for HMB design do not account for the magnetic field coupling between the HMBs and the motor. This omission leads to deviations in the model, inaccurate optimization directions, and difficulties in designing magnetic suspension systems that simultaneously achieve high load capacity and a more compact integrated structure. To address this issue, this article presents a comprehensive HMB design method integrating modeling, sensitivity analysis, and optimization. First, the integrated equivalent magnetic network model is established to analyze the electromagnetic characteristics of the HMBs, which accounts for magnetic coupling effects. Then, a pointwise sensitivity analysis is proposed to quantify the influence of design variables on multiple objectives across the feasible domain and is incorporated into a multiobjective particle swarm optimization algorithm to efficiently guide the search direction. Compared with the preoptimization design, the proposed method improves the weighted average of the maximum radial forces of the two HMBs by 57.03%, reduces the axial length by 11.52%, and mitigates the magnetic coupling by 71.85%. Finally, both simulation and experimental results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: MIMO-BICM Capacity Approximations for Link Level Abstraction
authors: Federico Penna, Hyukjoon Kwon, Dongwoon Bai, Kee-Bong Song
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Bit-interleaved coded modulation (BICM) capacity is a useful metric to assess the quality of MIMO channels. However, its practical use as a link-level abstraction metric (e.g., for precoder selection in cellular networks) is hindered by the high computational complexity. In this letter, we address this problem by proposing numerically efficient, sample-based approximations for MIMO-BICM capacity. Furthermore, we derive a connection between sample-based BICM capacity and empirical bit error rate, thus providing a theoretical justification for data-driven precoder selection criteria. The effectiveness of the proposed metrics when applied to 5G-NR precoder selection is validated by numerical simulations, showing gains above 1dB in signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) when compared to state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Temporal-Aware GPU Resource Allocation for Distributed LLM Inference via Reinforcement Learning_supp1-3702578.pdf
authors: Jialong Li
publish: N/A
abstract: The rapid growth of large language model (LLM) services imposes increasing demands on distributed GPU inference infrastructure. Most existing scheduling systems follow a reactive paradigm, relying solely on the current system state to make decisions, without considering how task demand and resource availability evolve over time. This lack of temporal awareness in reactive approaches leads to inefficient GPU utilization, high task migration overhead, and poor system responsiveness under dynamic workloads. In this work, we identify the fundamental limitations of these instantaneous-state-only scheduling approaches and propose Temporal Optimal Resource scheduling via Two-layer Architecture (TORTA). TORTA introduces a spatiotemporal scheduling framework that captures both long-term workload patterns and short-term execution constraints. It adopts a two-layer design: a macro-level scheduler leverages reinforcement learning and optimal transport to coordinate inter-region task distribution, while a micro-level allocator refines task-to-server assignments within each region to reduce latency and switching costs. Experimental results across multiple network topologies show that TORTA reduces average inference response time by up to 15%, improves load balance by approximately 4-5%, and cuts total operational cost by 10-20% compared to state-of-the-art baseline methods.

title: AutoMedic: An Automated Evaluation Framework for Clinical Conversational Agents with Medical Dataset Grounding
authors: Gyutaek Oh, Sangjoon Park, Byung-Hoon Kim
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Evaluating large language models (LLMs) has recently emerged as a critical issue for safe and trustworthy application of LLMs in the medical domain. Although a variety of static medical question-answering (QA) benchmarks have been proposed, many aspects remain underexplored, such as the effectiveness of LLMs in generating responses in dynamic, interactive clinical multi-turn conversation situations and the identification of multi-faceted evaluation strategies beyond simple accuracy. However, formally evaluating a dynamic, interactive clinical situation is hindered by its vast combinatorial space of possible patient states and interaction trajectories, making it difficult to standardize and quantitatively measure such scenarios. Here, we introduce AutoMedic, a multi-agent simulation framework that enables automated evaluation of LLMs as clinical conversational agents. AutoMedic transforms off-the-shelf static QA datasets into virtual patient profiles, enabling realistic and clinically grounded multi-turn clinical dialogues between LLM agents. The performance of various clinical conversational agents is then assessed based on our CARE metric, which provides a multi-faceted evaluation standard of clinical conversational accuracy, efficiency/strategy, empathy, and robustness. Our findings, validated by human experts, demonstrate the validity of AutoMedic as an automated evaluation framework for clinical conversational agents, offering practical guidelines for the effective development of LLMs in conversational medical applications.

title: MM-SAG: Multi-Modal Semantic-Aware Graph for Multi-Label Image Classification of Kitchen Waste
authors: Hai Qin, Jintao Li, Qiaokang Liang, Wei Sun, Dan Zhang, Yaonan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Multi-label image classification aims to recognize multiple object labels within an image. In the field of intelligent waste sorting, efficient classification can enhance the accuracy of robotic sorting. However, most existing waste classification tasks are single-label, and there is limited research on multi-label classification of urban kitchen waste, especially in complex backgrounds and diverse categories. To address this, we propose a Multi-Modal Semantic-Aware Graph (MM-SAG) framework, which includes a semantic-aware module designed for instance-level label relationship mining. The captured semantic features are then processed through graph convolution to generate a label correlation matrix, enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of label correlation mining. To improve the integration of visual and linguistic modalities, we design an improved multi-head attention mechanism module. This module re-encodes and aligns visual and textual features, further enhancing feature extraction capabilities. Experimental results show that our proposed method achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 83.1% on the MLKW dataset, delivering state-of-the-art performance. The method's strong generalization capability is also validated on public datasets VOC2007 and MS-COCO.

title: Approximate Bilevel Graph Structure Learning for Histopathology Image Classification
authors: Sudipta Paul, Amanda W. Lund, George Jour, Iman Osman, Bülent Yener
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The structural and spatial arrangements of cells within tissues represent their functional states, making graph-based learning highly suitable for histopathology image analysis. Existing methods often rely on fixed graphs that may not capture complex tissue interactions. In this work, we propose ABiG-Net (Approximate Bilevel Optimization for Graph Structure Learning via Neural Networks), a novel framework that learns patch interactions within whole slide images (WSI) or large regions of interest (ROI) while simultaneously learning discriminative node embeddings for the downstream classification task. ABiG-Net hierarchically models tissue architecture by constructing local patch-level graphs from cellular organization and learning a global image-level graph that captures sparse, biologically meaningful patch connections through first-order approximate bilevel optimization. Here, ‘‘bilevel" denotes a nested optimization strategy where classifier parameters are optimized at a lower level using training data, while graph adjacency parameters are optimized at an upper level using validation loss. The first-order approximation removes costly second-order hypergradient terms, reducing memory and runtime while keeping image-level graph learning tractable for large WSIs. Clinically, the learned long-range patch interactions link spatially separated but morphologically related regions, producing interpretable, diagnostically relevant region-to-region evidence. Experiments on two histopathology datasets demonstrate its effectiveness: on the Extended CRC dataset, ABiG-Net achieves 97.33±1.15% accuracy for three-class colorectal cancer grading and 98.33±0.58% for binary classification; on the melanoma dataset, it attains 96.27 ± 0.74% for tumor–lymphocyte ROI classification. These results show that first-order approximate bilevel graph structure learning provides an adaptive, interpretable, and tractable alternative for large-scale computational pathology.

title: A GaN-Based Buck Converter Resilient to Battery Voltage Fluctuations with 96.3% Peak Efficiency
authors: Chi-Yu Chen, Po-Jui Chiu, Yu-Ting Huang, Xiao-Quan Wu, Chien-Wei Cho, Sheng-Hsi Hung, Ke-Horng Chen, Xi Zhu, Ying-Hsi Lin, Tsung-Yen Tsai, Shian-Ru Lin, Hann-Huei Tsai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The voltage fluctuations of 48 V batteries in electric vehicles challenge the robustness of switching converters by disturbing the output voltage during cold-crank and load-dump events. To address this issue, this paper presents a 48 V-to-12 V buck converter employing gallium nitride (GaN) devices to enable high switching frequency (FSW) operation and feedforward control, thereby enhancing line transient performance and stabilizing loop dynamics. However, higher FSW also increases the slew rate at the switching node, imposing stricter noise immunity requirements on circuit operation. To overcome this limitation, a noise-immune sub-nanosecond level shifter (NSLS) is introduced to ensure fast and reliable signal transmission for high-speed operation. In addition, a Miller plateau leakage elimination (MLE) technique mitigates unintended gate coupling induced by high dV/dt, effectively reducing leakage losses. Beyond high-frequency operation, an adaptive P-type ramp generator (APR) facilitates feedforward control by emulating a P-type current mirror, eliminating input-voltage dependency in the control loop. Fabricated using a 0.5-μm GaN process, the proposed converter achieves a peak efficiency of 96.3% while significantly improving transient performance under input-voltage variations.

title: Migration Dynamics and the Evolution of Cooperation in Multilayer Networks: A Comparative Study of Q-Learning and the Moran Process
authors: Rong Zeng, Jinming Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Migration on multilayer networks captures how individuals move across diverse social environments to interact with others. From a macroperspective, such movements may stem from group dynamics or institutional rules, whereas from a micro perspective, they arise from learning behaviors such as trial-and-error, imitation, or reinforcement learning. Modeling these dynamics is crucial for understanding complex social systems. Recently, the Q-learning mechanism has attracted significant attention, especially in the emerging fields of artificial intelligence and human behavior research, where it has shown great potential. However, unlike traditional rules such as the Moran process, the evolutionary features of Q-learning combined with migration on multilayer networks still lack systematic research. To address this gap, we construct a two-layer network model by introducing an interlayer migration mechanism, in which an individual’s habituation to the current layer is explicitly considered. By incorporating a mean-field Q-learning approximation, we conduct an analysis of the model. Under diverse network structural configurations and intralayer game paradigms, we investigate the evolutionary outcomes of group behavior, where intralayer strategy updating is governed by either Q-learning or the Moran process. The results based on Q-learning indicate that when agents choose actions corresponding to the current optimal state values, both the cooperation level of the system and the average payoff of agents are enhanced. Across different game parameters, Q-learning demonstrates a more stable proportion of intralayer cooperators compared with the Moran process, while also exhibiting a larger difference in average intralayer payoffs between the two layers in most cases. Results from both updating rules suggest that the layer with a larger average degree generally achieves a higher proportion of intralayer cooperators, and under certain conditions, a clustering effect of cooperators may emerge.

title: A Novel CO2-based Real-Options Methodology to Integrate Uncertainty and Flexibility in Infrastructure Appraisal: an illustrative application in Offshore Energy Infrastructure
authors: Paravano Alessandro, Neri Alessandra, Brizzante Gregorio, Bosani Alessandro, Cagno Enrico, Locatelli Giorgio
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: In infrastructure planning, it is critical to deal with uncertainties. Various methods incorporate uncertainty and design flexibility into infrastructure planning, primarily from an economic perspective, often disregarding the environmental dimension, including greenhouse gas emissions (hereafter, CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>-eq emissions). Methods accounting for CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>-eq emissions, such as Life Cycle Assessment (LCA), typically overlook uncertainty and design flexibility. In this study, flexibility is conceptualized as infrastructure flexibility embedded in the design of the infrastructure itself. To address this gap in infrastructure project management, this paper introduces a Real Options Analysis (ROA) model to assess infrastructure design options for CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>-eq emissions, providing a decision-support framework to mitigate uncertainty throughout an infrastructure's lifecycle. Without loss of generality, we tested the model with offshore energy infrastructure, evaluating the option to integrate Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) to mitigate wind energy curtailment. We adopt discounted CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>-eq emissions as a decision metric, recognizing the environmental impact of flexibility-driven infrastructure planning. The novel ROA model employs Monte Carlo simulations and optimized exercise thresholds to determine when and under what conditions BESS investments yield net carbon benefits. Robustness is assessed through sensitivity analyses across alternative curtailment dynamics, carbon intensity trajectories, and discounting assumptions, confirming the stability of the flexibility exercise threshold. By extending the ROA beyond economic valuation, this study presents a novel infrastructure project management framework that integrates flexibility into strategic decision-making to support infrastructure sustainability, addressing critical challenges in managing uncertainty, optimizing infrastructure investments, and achieving long-term decarbonization objectives.
