title: Satellite-Enhanced Deep Learning for Agricultural and Hydrological Drought Forecasting in Data-Scarce Indian Regions
authors: Kanishka Duary, Jigyasha Rajgarhia, Viji D.
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Drought forecasting is essential for agricultural planning and water resource management, particularly in data-scarce regions where long and consistent observational records are limited. Although deep learning approaches have recently been explored for the prediction of drought, many existing studies focus primarily on meteorological indices and rarely investigate the contribution of satellite-derived vegetation and subsurface water indicators in small-sample conditions. This study proposes a lightweight and reproducible deep learning framework for satellite-enhanced drought forecasting in Indian regions. Agricultural drought in the Marathwada region is modeled using the Standardized Precipitation Evapotranspiration Index (SPEI) together with the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI), while hydrological drought in the Bundelkhand region incorporates soil moisture and groundwater indicators derived from satellite and reanalysis products. Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and a lightweight transformer encoder are evaluated under identical experimental conditions to assess their suitability for limited time-series data. Experimental results indicate that LSTM models produce more stable and consistent predictions than transformer-based architectures when the available training data is limited. A sensitivity analysis of temporal window sizes and an ablation study on vegetation inputs further highlight how model complexity and feature selection influence forecasting performance in data-constrained environments. These findings demonstrate the importance of aligning model design with data availability and emphasize the practical value of openly accessible satellite data for transparent and reproducible drought forecasting.

title: A Survey of Structural MRI-based Classification of Alzheimer's Disease: Traditional Machine Learning to Deep Neural Architectures
authors: Binoop Kumar. C.A, K. Renuka
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disease characterized by structural atrophy of the brain, which is measurable using structural Magnetic Resonance Imaging (sMRI) quantitatively. Automated sorting of Cognitive Normal (CN), Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI), and AD participants has now become a crucial area of research to enable early diagnosis and response therapy. This survey is an extensive and systematic analysis of computational methods created to classify AD using sMRI with a focus on the methodological transition of traditional Machine Learning (ML)-based methods to advanced Deep Neural architectures. This review was organized logically by preprocessing strategies, extracting features, learning paradigm, and evaluation protocols. Conventional pipelines based on Voxel-Based Morphometry, Region-of-Interest Volumetry, Cortical-Thickness Measures, and Manual-Engineered Texture Measures, combined with Support Vector Machines and Random Forests, are examined. Subsequently, the Deep Learning (DL) models, such as 2D and 3D Convolutional Neural Networks, and Hybrid Models, are compared in terms of their representational learning capability and diagnostic accuracy. Benchmark datasets, especially the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) and the Open Access Series of Imaging Studies (OASIS), are discussed in terms of the experimental tendencies and methods of validation. The major issues, such as heterogeneity of data, imbalanced classes, restricted generalizability, and interpretability of the models, are defined. Finally, future research recommendations are provided to enhance the development of strong, explainable, and clinically viable AD diagnostic systems.

title: Carbon Tax and Willingness to Pay: Evidence From High-Pollution Industries in Indonesia
authors: Maulia Sabrina, Nuraini A Nuraini A, Rita Meutia, Juanda Surya
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study investigates the factors influencing the willingness to pay (WTP) for carbon tax among communities residing near high-pollution industries in Indonesia, specifically in Tangerang and Cilegon. A total of 400 respondents were selected using stratified random sampling to ensure representativeness across regions and demographic characteristics. Data were collected through questionnaires, and hypotheses were tested using multiple logistic regression analysis. The results indicate that trust in government and attitudes toward climate change significantly affect WTP for carbon tax, whereas knowledge of climate change does not exert a significant influence. This indicates that governmental legitimacy and the psychological engagement of the public are more decisive than cognitive understanding alone in shaping financial support for environmental policies. The study contributes to the literature on environmental taxation by highlighting the interplay between social trust, behavioral attitudes, and policy acceptance, and offers practical insights for policymakers aiming to implement carbon taxation in developing countries with high industrial pollution.

title: An Empirical Comparison of Dense, Hybrid, and Reranked Retrieval under Monolingual and Multilingual Embeddings for Indic Languages
authors: Alok Chedambath, Nikhil Shinde, Raghavendra Pratap Bohra, Subashini B
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Information retrieval in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems remains strongly biased toward languages with abundant resources. Assessing RAG performance across a range of morphologically rich languages uncovers substantial performance gaps. We benchmarked nine different search and retrieval pipelines under three settings: Monolingual, Multilingual (Shared Embedding Space), and Pivot-Based (Translation-focused). This research study has covered four languages: English, Hindi, Bengali, and Tamil. Each pipeline combined tailored dense embedding models with sparse BM25 retrieval and cross-encoder reranking. The experiments highlight strict trade-offs between precision and latency. Monolingual hybrid retrieval (P2) delivered strong recall, achieving a Bengali Recall@10 of 0.9712 while keeping latency at a moderate 1222.96 ms. In contrast, an advanced pivot-based reranking configuration (P9) pushed retrieval performance further, reaching a Recall@10 of 0.9887, but at the expense of a prohibitive 8138 ms latency. Moreover, hybrid search methods that depend on translation pivots (P8) reduced Bengali Recall@5 to 0.8763, as translation artifacts undermine the exact keyword matching needed for high-precision retrieval.

title: Trajectory Planning for Formation Reconfiguration near Sun-Earth L2 Libration Point Based on Convex Optimization Method
authors: Qian Li, Jun Ying Du, Hong Wei Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: This paper proposes a trajectory planning method based on convex optimization for spacecraft formation reconfiguration near the Sun-Earth L2 libration points. Firstly, an optimal control problem is formulated with the objective of minimizing fuel consumption, which subjects to constraints including relative dynamics, thrust magnitude, initial and terminal states and collision avoidance. The nonlinear equations of motion are linearized around the periodic nominal orbit in the vicinity of the libration point to obtain a linearized relative dynamics model. Subsequently, the nonconvex terms of the dynamics related to mass are eliminated by initializing and iteratively refining the mass sequence of the spacecraft. As the collision avoidance constraints are nonconvex, the linearized formulation is derived via convexification based on the reference trajectory. Then, to improve convergence, a trust variable is introduced to bound the deviation between the actual and nominal trajectories. The successive convex optimization framework is employed to solve the problem, where the first iteration excludes collision avoidance constraints and its solution is used as the nominal trajectory for subsequent iterations. Finally, these results confirm that the proposed method can efficiently solve the formation reconfiguration problem near libration points, and the convexified optimal control problem is equivalent to the original nonconvex problem.

title: An Enhanced Lung Cancer Detection in CT Scans using SE-Modified DenseNet121 with FPN and Focal Loss
authors: Mallareddy Adudhodla, Deeti Hemanth Kumar
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Lung cancer is a serious disease caused by uncontrolled cell growth, often linked to smoking, which can spread in the body and remains the leading cause of cancer-related deaths worldwide. Computed Tomography (CT) scans are essential in lung cancer detection, offering high-resolution images that enable early identification of lung nodules and abnormal tissue growth. Though the existing Deep Learning (DL) models offer better accuracy, they are prone to challenges including data imbalance, high processing cost and low sensitivity in identifying subtle pulmonary nodules and fine-grained lung features. To overcome existing limitations in lung cancer diagnosis, an advanced SE-DenseNet121 integrated with Feature Pyramid Network (FPN) and Focal Loss model is proposed for accurate lung cancer detection. The model performs robust multi-scale feature extraction and classification with the capability of identifying and differentiating various lung cancer types from medical images. This approach consists of four major phases including data pre-processing, data augmentation, dataset splitting, and classification. Preprocessing of images is done through resizing and normalization of images to enhance consistency in features and training stability. Rotation and flipping can augment data and improve generalization and decrease overfitting. SE-DenseNet121 is a channel-attention based deep feature extractor, FPN, is a multi-scale feature extractor, and Focal Loss is a class imbalance solution to better detection results. Experimental validation using the LUNA16 dataset with 80% training and 20% testing data achieved 92.00% accuracy, 88.00% precision, 85.00% recall, 84.89% F1-score, and approximately 92.00% AUC across multiple epochs.

title: Chronos-2 Inspired Cold-Start Weather Forecasting: A Transformer-based Approach for Limited Historical Data Scenarios
authors: A. Akilandeswari
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: One of the most important problems in the meteorological application is cold-start forecasting in which predictions have to be based on little historical data available. The current paper introduces a transformer architecture based on Chronos-2, which is a design that is directly targeted at weather forecasting with as minimal historical backdrop as possible. Multi-feature weather time series data spanning have been used to evaluate both traditional and standard transformer baseline using multi-feature weather time series data spanning 2 decades. Our model based on Chronos-2 shows the best performance with the RMSE of 1418.56 which is better than the LSTM (1464.46) and the standard Transformer (1611.95) by 3.14 percent and 13.56 percent respectively. With just 30 days of historical data (lookback window), the model attains R2 of 0.3198, which indicates that it has good cold-start features. The study conducted a thorough analysis of the model and thereby the model appears to be consistent over 7-day forecast horizons with MAE of 324.01 thus, is most applicable to the operational weather prediction systems where historical data is not available. The design includes multi-head self-attention networks with positional encoding and residual links and allows effective time pattern dynamics to be obtained using short-period historical records.

title: Handy Mouse Cursor Control using Hand Gesture in Deep Learning
authors: J. Mala, Karuppusamy S, Shreenivasamoorthy T, Madhan Raj R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: As the interaction between human and computer is improving rapidly, so a new way to execute a touchless control is essential to improve adaptability and user quality. So this system involves IOT and computer vision and machine learning methods. Gesture mouse system mainly comprises of three important parts: hand gesture training, hand gesture recognition, and hand gesture implementation. This system grants users the ability to execute common mouse controls and some other keyboard controls through hand gestures captured and recognized by a computer cam or individual IOT webcam. This system use a hand landmark(keypoints) detection model which identifies 21 key points on the hand, executing precise tracking of hand and finger positions, angles and movements in the XYZ plane. This hand gesture recognition model searches these landmarks to differentiate hand positions into reanalyzed gestures, while the user custom gesture training part allows users to create and save and execute their own gesture mapping that can be further improved with even more controls. This project gives real time hand gesture detection and control execution by utilizing MediaPipe for hand gesture tracking with the help of computer vision for real time capturing and machine learning algorithms for user customization. This implementation uses MediaPipe to detect and track hand movements precisely for better real time detection. This system gives intuitive cursor control, parameters that can be adjusted, and support for multiple hand gesture tracking with moving or dynamic hand gesture.

title: A Neural Network Approach to the Detection and Classification of Road Infrastructure Objects in Intelligent Transport Systems
authors: Sergei Gaponenko, Daria Matyunina, Marina Manzhos
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: Intelligent transportation systems require reliable computer vision techniques to analyze the traffic situation in real time. However, existing object detection algorithms show a significant reduction in accuracy in real-world scenes, including noise, blurring, adverse weather conditions, and difficult lighting. This paper proposes a neural network approach to the detection and classification of road infrastructure facilities based on the integration of image preprocessing techniques and modern deep learning architectures. A generalized computational pipeline is proposed, which includes the stages of image quality improvement, object detection and classification. Additionally, a formalization of the detection task and a mechanism for increasing stability through adaptive input data processing are introduced. The application of single-step detectors of the YOLO family as a basic component of the system is considered. An analytical analysis of the proposed approach has been carried out, demonstrating its applicability in the tasks of monitoring road infrastructure, autonomous driving systems and intelligent transport management.

title: MIMO-NOMA Technology: Technical Limits and Application Areas
authors: Ilya Grishin, Grigoriy Fokin, Alla Kalinkina, Alexander Sinilnikov, Vladimir Sevidov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper critically assesses MIMO-NOMA viability for 5G-Advanced and 6G. The downlink SINR model is refined through interference ordering in successive interference cancellation; energy efficiency dependence on cluster size is formalized under hardware constraints (peak-to-average power ratio, ADC resolution, SIC latency); and a minimum channel difference threshold of three to five decibels is derived assuming Rayleigh fading, a two-user cluster, and a residual SIC coefficient near zero point one. The resource optimization problem is shown to be non-convex, requiring iterative methods. Differentiated recommendations are given: the technology is niche, suitable for static scenarios (fixed wireless access, massive machine-type communications uplink, mmWave hotspots), but not for high-mobility macro-cells or ultra-reliable low-latency communications. Limitations include a single-cell model and simplified channel assumptions, providing a structured basis for scenario-specific evaluation pending experimental validation.

title: Meta-Learning Enhanced Transformer Networks with Adaptive Swarm Intelligence Optimization for Stock Market Trend Forecasting
authors: Mohideen AbdulKader M, Sanjay Narasimhan, Oviyaa Rathinasankar
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Forecasting in the stock market is a complex process because financial time-series data are not stationary, volatile, and very nonlinear. Proper forecasting of the stock patterns is vital to investors, risk control and automated trading systems. The proposed study will design a powerful and adaptive forecasting system that will address the weakness of classic models and deep learning methods which tend to be weak in identifying long term dependencies and also hyperparameters. The suggested model is a Transformer network with meta-learning that allows learning new stocks and new market environments quickly, and adaptive swarm intelligence optimizer, which tunes vital hyperparameters, such as learning rates, attention heads, network depth, and lookback window size automatically. The model is tested on Kaggle Stock Market Dataset with the historical prices of the NASDAQ stock market and ETF, including technical indicators (MA, RSI, MACD, and Bollinger Bands). The experimental findings prove that the suggested strategy presents RMSE, MAE and directional accuracy of 0.024, 0.018, and 98.4 percent, respectively, which is better than the baseline models LSTM, BiLSTM, and general Transformers. High volatility, interpretable attention patterns and high trading profitability are also indicative of the model. These results indicate that the hybrid meta-learning swarm-optimized Transformer is a scalable, adaptive and highly accurate model of stock trend forecasting in the real world.

title: Simulation of an Electromagnetic Frequency Multiplier for an Induction-Resistive Heating System
authors: Maksim Fedin, Elena Kachalina, Sergey Korobkov, Maria Bulatenko, Artem Kalinin, Pavel Petrov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article addresses the challenge of improving the energy efficiency of induction-resistive heating systems (IRHS) by using increased supply voltage frequencies. It presents the results of the development and simulation of a frequency tripler based on three single-phase transformers, designed to convert the industrial frequency of 50 Hz to 150 Hz. The structural calculation of the transformer, the analytical calculation of the tripler parameters, and the IRHS system are provided. Simulation performed in the MATLAB/Simulink environment confirmed the operability of the proposed circuit. It is shown that the use of a tripled frequency enhances the skin effect in the ferromagnetic pipe of the IRHS, leading to an increase allocated power and, consequently, improving heating efficiency. Time diagrams of currents and voltages were obtained, and the forms of output signals and load power were analyzed.

title: A Graph-Probabilistic Model of Inter-Camera Association of Vehicles in Distributed Road Video Surveillance Systems
authors: Vadim Borzov, Marina Moseva, Mikhail Gorodnichev
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper presents a graph-based probabilistic approach to multi-camera vehicle tracking in road surveillance networks. Cameras are modeled as graph vertices, while admissible inter-camera transitions are represented as directed edges between exit and entry zones. Each transition is described by base and temporal probabilities, enabling candidate filtering before appearance-based matching. Experimental results show that proposed model reduces search space of inter-camera association while preserving true candidates in most cases. Approach provides an interpretable foundation for topologyaware vehicle tracking in urban camera networks.

title: An Intelligent Public Safety Threat Prediction Framework Using Deep Q-Learning and Agent-Based Simulation
authors: Nidhi Mishra, Md Afzal, Muhidinov Ayubbek Nuritdinovich, Indumathi G, Ali Abbas Hadi, M. Murali
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: In modern urban surveillance systems, precision and timely forecasting of safety threats to the people are vital in the prevention of crime and the effective management of emergencies. Combining large-scale urban data with artificial intelligence improves situational awareness and supports smart decision-making. Most current methods rely on fixed predictive models that do not capture, resulting in limited real-time responsiveness and low predictive precision. Such systems are often unable to track dynamic environmental changes and the intricate interactions among urban entities. To address these shortcomings, the paper proposes a new framework, Urban Crime and Emergency Response Management in Real-Time, based on Deep Q-learning and agent-based simulations. The framework combines deep reinforcement learning with an agent-based simulation environment to enable context-aware, adaptive threat prediction. Intelligent agents develop optimal response policies using the Deep Q-Learning algorithm as they continuously interact with a dynamically changing urban environment. The system simulates various emergency scenarios, identifies potential hazards, and recommends timely intervention strategies to enhance operational efficiency. As indicated by the experimental results, the proposed DQL-ABS framework is highly effective in terms of prediction accuracy, reduced response time, and improved coordination between safety management units, compared to traditional static frameworks. These findings indicate the framework's ability to enable proactive, data-driven management of public safety and to enhance smart city infrastructure through adaptive, smart threat mitigation measures.

title: Deterministic Temporal Abstraction for Quadrupedal Backflip: A Time-Scheduled Phase Decomposition Approach
authors: Jinze Tao, Muye Pang, Xinda Chen, Chenghan Zhang
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: Quadrupedal robot backflip represents one of the most challenging acrobatic motions, requiring coordinated control of limbs, precise timing, and balance recovery. This research presents a reinforcement learning framework for training quadrupedal robots to autonomously learn and execute backflip motions. The key contribution is a deterministic temporal abstraction framework that decomposes the backflip motion into distinct temporal phases with fixed time boundaries. Unlike the Options framework where termination conditions depend on states and introduce non-stationarity during hierarchical policy learning, the proposed approach employs predetermined temporal boundaries that guarantee complete phase coverage in every training episode. This time-driven formulation enables gradient contributions from all phases from the first training iteration, transforming the reward landscape from a hierarchically gated structure into a globally visible optimization surface. By designing temporally structured reward functions combined with physical constraints, stable learning under the Proximal Policy Optimization algorithm is achieved. The approach is implemented in a GPU-accelerated physics simulation environment and demonstrates successful backflip execution. Additionally, comprehensive ablation studies are provided demonstrating the necessity of temporal phasing and each reward component. The framework addresses critical challenges in learning dynamic, high-dimensional motion control tasks through structured temporal reward design.

title: Blockchain-based Smart Contract Frameworks for Secure AI Healthcare Systems: A Systematic Review of Privacy-Preserving Methodologies
authors: V. Jothi Prasad, S. Nagendra Prabhu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is changing the healthcare industry by automating the process of medical records, diagnostic, and clinical decision-making. Nevertheless, the growing dependence on data-driven systems is of concern in terms of patient privacy, data integrity, and system security. To deal with these challenges, blockchain technology, in combination with smart contracts, can be used as a decentralized and transparent solution. The paper is a systematic review of the current developments in smart contract frameworks based on blockchain technologies and safe AI-driven healthcare systems. The review addresses four key aspects, which include data privacy preservation, secure information exchange, decentralized access management, and AI-blockchain integration. The recent technologies like federated learning, zero-knowledge proofs, and deep learning-based blockchain validation are examined in terms of their contribution to the improvement of patient confidentiality and system-level security. The paper also assesses a suggested multi-layered infrastructure combining artificial intelligence processing, authorized blockchain, and role-based smart contracts. The comparison indicates that the federated learning approach with permissioned blockchain has an optimal security- versus-computational efficiency ratio. The review establishes that smart contract automation has a substantial potential to benefit healthcare data governance, but notes that scalability, interoperability and energy efficiency issues remain a challenge. This overall overview confirms the potential of blockchain and smart contracts to facilitate dependable, most open, and efficient healthcare frameworks that may meet the security and privacy requirements of the contemporary healthcare setting.

title: GPS-Aided Attitude and Position Estimation for Aircraft Stability and Control Systems: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Rami Ahmed Mohammed, Mohammed Qasim Sulttan, Wisam Essmat Abdul-Lateef
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Accurate attitude and position estimation is a fundamental requirement for modern aircraft stability and control systems. Estimation errors can significantly affect flight performance, robustness, and safety. Traditional inertial navigation systems (INS) provide high-rate motion measurements but suffer from long-term drift and sensor bias. To overcome these limitations, Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS), particularly the Global Positioning System (GPS), are widely integrated with INS to provide reliable navigation solutions. This paper presents a comprehensive survey of GPS-aided attitude and position estimation techniques used in aircraft stability and control applications. The review discusses the fundamental principles of GPS measurements and examines different GPS/INS integration architectures, including loosely coupled, tightly coupled, and deeply coupled configurations. In addition, various sensor fusion algorithms such as the Kalman filter, extended Kalman filter, unscented Kalman filter, and particle filtering approaches are analyzed. Recent advances in carrier-phase-based estimation and data-driven estimation techniques are also reviewed. The paper further highlights the interaction between navigation estimation and aircraft control performance, providing a comparative analysis of existing methods and identifying key challenges and research directions. The survey aims to provide researchers and engineers with a consolidated reference for designing robust navigation and control systems for next-generation aerospace platforms.

title: Deep Learning and Partially-Observed Boundary Conditions for Reaction-Diffusion Equations: Numerical Experiments With Pollution Data
authors: Amelia Kunze, Herb Kunze, Davide La Torre
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Reaction-diffusion equations are fundamental models for spatially distributed processes in physics, chemistry, biology, and environmental science. In many applications, the boundary conditions in such a model, which are often also time-dependent, may only be partially observed, and, as a result, the model problem cannot be solved immediately. In this paper, we explore the use of deep neural networks to address this situation in two ways: a rather direct “one-shot” learning approach and an iterative “staggered” learning approach. In each approach, the produced deep neural network plays the role of the function in the boundary condition. Our results show that the latter staggered learning approach leads to an improvement over the one-shot learning approach. This preliminary exploration signals that more sophisticated treatments should be explored in the future.

title: Entropy-Aware Temporal Behavioral Modeling for Early-Stage Ransomware Detection using System-Level Telemetry and Sliding Window Analytics
authors: Irshad Ahmed R, S Murugaanandam, Dinesh Kumar S, Swapnil Shankar Talke
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Ransomware has remained a pressing threat on modern computing systems due to its speed coupled with flexibility and ability to avoid traditional signature threats. The problem yet to be addressed by the proposed research will be an untimely detection of ransomware by the mere judgment to a system-wide method of telemetry, rather than signature malware identification. It is based on the developed approach utilizing Sysmon events generated, sliding-window-based behavioral modeling, and entropy analysis as the way to recognize pieces of evidence of malicious encryption activity in real-time. The system determines the ransomware activity through the file operations speed of entropy-related characteristics in comparison with the large load user activity of file compression or large copy operations. An entropy-weighted scoring system may also be employed as a technique of estimating a false positive free severity. Experimental tests of the system in normal conditions, with benign, high-volume and simulated ransomware were done and demonstrated that the system is robust against false alarms and gives correct early-stage detection. The results highlight the applicability of entropy-conscious behavioral analysis as viable and practicable model of real-time ransomware protection.

title: MORL: An Intelligent Cost and Reliability-Aware Task Scheduling in Multi-Cloud Data Centers
authors: C Veena, Seshu Bhavani Mallampati, Raveendrababu Vempati, P Ramya, A Vanaja, Santhosh Kumar Medishetti
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Multi-cloud datacentres are increasingly executed in a dynamic cost-of-use electrical pricing, with variable energy charges playing a major role in the operational efficiency and sustainability, and adaptive task scheduling is an important research topic. This paper suggests a Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning (MORL)-based adaptive task scheduling model that aims to effectively complete a joint optimization of resource use and reliability in time-varying electricity prices. The main goal is to reduce the Degree of Imbalance (DoI) among the virtual machines and increase the VM success rate and lower the task failure rate in multi-cloud setting distributed geographically. The suggested MORL agent is dynamically trained to learn the best scheduling policies through its interaction with the environment and using real-time signals of electricity prices in the reward function. The simulations were done extensively on CloudSim using the real-world Alibaba workload traces to make them practical and viable in terms of scalability assessment. Experimental outcomes show that the MORL approach decreases the Degree of Imbalance by 18.7%, increases the VM success rate by 12.4% and reduces the task failure rate by 15.9% than the optimal performance of the baseline. Moreover, scheduling decisions made according to the model are more stable during peak electricity pricing hours and makes certain that the resources are allocated cost-conscious without loss of service reliability. Such results validate that the combination of multi-objective reinforcement learning and electricity price awareness can dramatically boost the adaptive scheduling efficiency of multi-cloud data centers, which can offer scalable and intelligent next-generation energy-aware cloud infrastructure.

title: Interpretable Vision Transformer–based Retinal Disease Detection using HOG Features and Guided Graph Segmentation
authors: Mary Shalini A, Jenifer P
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Diabetic eye diseases are vision complications that are brought about by sustained high level of sugar in blood, Cataract, diabetic retinopathy and glaucoma, are all retinal diseases with major causes of irreversible blindness in the globe. The blindness requires the early and precise diagnosis to ensure proper treatment and preventive measures. Nevertheless, the current convolutional neural network (CNN)-based methods are mainly locally-oriented methods that frequently do not utilize global contextual modeling and interpretability. Also, frameworks that rely on segmentation are susceptible to noise and changes in illumination, which restricts their effectiveness in screening in the real world. In order to overcome these drawbacks, this research is going to introduce an interpretable Vision Transformer (ViT)-based retinal disease classification system with the addition of Guided Filtering, Graph-Based Segmentation, and Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG) feature extractor. The guided filter is used to increase the retinal structural information and keep the vessel boundaries intact. Graph-based segmentation isolates clinically relevant areas and HOG descriptors are used to learn the discriminative edge and texture pattern of which pathological changes are related. The representations extracted are categorized with a Vision Transformer that is trained with the Lion optimizer so as to achieve a steady convergence, and a better generalization. Grad-CAM visualization has been added to give explanations and show clinically significant areas that affect model decisions. The proposed ViT-HOG framework is evaluated experimentally on a multi-class retinal dataset, where it is shown to be even more accurate in classification with 95% and AUC of 0.99, performing better than traditional CNN frameworks, including ResNet50 and DenseNet121. The mean time to infer an image on GPU hardware is 0.42 seconds, which proves a fit to use in real-time clinical practice. The sensitivity analysis also justifies strong ability with respect to small segmentation errors. The findings suggest that the suggested framework has better accuracy, interpretability and computational efficiency in scalable applications in retinal disease screening.

title: Deep Learning Approaches for Detection and Classification of Tomato Leaf Diseases: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Rajendran Sivakumar
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Deep learning (DL) has arisen as a formidable solution for automated plant diseases diagnosis, offering robust performance in complex visual environments such as crop fields. This work offers a comparative comparison of multiple DL approaches for the identification and categorization of tomato foliar illness using picture data. A variety of advanced convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, such as ResNet, Inspection, MobileNet and transfer learning models, are trained under a unified experimental protocol. Their performance is evaluated using various performance metrics to capture both diagnostic quality and computational efficiency, consistent with recent works on tomato disease detection and model benchmarking. The results show that deeper transfer learning–based models generally achieve superior accuracy, while lightweight architectures remain attractive for real-time and edge deployments. This comparative analysis provides practical guidance for selecting appropriate DL models according to accuracy–efficiency trade-offs and deployment constraints, and demonstrates the potential of these approaches to support early, reliable tomato leaf disease diagnosis in smart agriculture systems.

title: Radio Frequency Spectrum Optimizing for Multicast Data Transming in 4G and 5G Multi-Zoned Networks
authors: Egor Melikhov, Elena Stroganova
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This report is devoted to developing a hybrid multimedia content delivery radio frequency spectrum optimization and network architecture for future mobile communication networks. Evolution of multicast transmission standards from eMBMS technologies to 5G NR-MBS and physical layer optimization methods are discussed. Implementation of a multi-zoned system based on TowerOverlay topology with FeMBMS is presented, allowing for joint use of broadcasting and cellular infrastructures. It is demonstrated that using the HTHP approach and intelligent traffic management can significantly enhance spectral efficiency without significant changes to subscriber devices.

title: Distributed Ledger Fintech Systems for Improving Financial Transparency and Trust in Supply Chain Finance
authors: Mohammed H M Qeshta, Ahmad Ali, Salma Al-Shammari, Khaled M Algraibeh
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The growing use of FinTech based on blockchain solutions have gradually affected financial coordination mechanisms in supply chain finance networks where secure transaction verification, real-time visibility and records that cannot be changed have become essential to inter-organization collaboration. Risks related to supply chain finance systems include information asymmetry, slow verification and lack of trust between partners. The conventional monetary procedures often limit transparency, which may cause sabotage of collaboration and increased financial risk. A designed survey was created and it was distributed to practitioners operating in supply chain finance and digital financial platforms resulting in almost 275 questionnaire responses that were valid. An analysis of the data was done using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modelling (PLS-SEM) with the use of SmartPLS. The results suggest that the impact of Blockchain FinTech Capability on Financial Transparency has a positive significant impact on the Trust among the partners in the supply chain. Also, Blockchain capability exhibits a direct positive effect on the result of trust. The mediation analysis proves that there is a partial mediating role of Financial Transparency between the relationship between Blockchain capability and Trust. The findings would add to the previous FinTech and supply chain finance literature that clarifies the way in which blockchain-based functions facilitate financial visibility and inter-organizational trust. The practical use of the research relates to the problem that the adoption of blockchain is useful to develop better collaboration and financial stability among firms.

title: Computational Image–based Identification of Monkeypox Infection
authors: Selvi. T, Chellammal. S, Rupak. L. R, Sathiya. S, Kathiravan. S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Monkeypox is a modern viral disease with zoonotic characteristics that requires prompt and accurate diagnosis to reduce the number of people in the community and provide the prompt clinical intervention. Traditional methods of diagnosis such as visual examination and polymerase chain reaction methods are resource-consuming and require the availability of experts. This paper presents an automated Monkeypox detection system with the use of clinical images of lesions on the skin based on a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The system incorporates image preprocessing, deep feature extraction and supervised classification to effectively treat Monkeypox-infected lesions and non-infected cases. A web application based on Flask is made to provide real-time predictions with confidence score and simple medical advice towards screening. The predictive performance and strength of the model are high due to experimental assessment in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The offered solution is an affordable, scalable, and quick screening tool that would help to identify diseases early and serve the needs of healthcare professionals to make clinical decisions.

title: An End-to-End Layout-Aware System for Intelligent Form Understanding
authors: Pooja Damavarapu, Kavinya Mahesh, Krishna Khare, Mogili Greeshma, Radha D
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Forms are widely used across multiple sectors, yet automated understanding of scanned and handwritten forms remains challenging due to complex layouts and noisy visual content. This paper presents a layout-aware automated form understanding and summarisation system for structured information extraction from document images. The proposed approach integrates OCR with layout-aware transformer models to perform token-level semantic classification into categories such as questions, answers, and headers. Three architectures—LayoutLMv1, LayoutLMv3-base, and a Modified LayoutLMv3—are evaluated, where the enhanced model incorporates geometric feature embeddings, an additional refinement layer, global attention pooling, and a CRF decoding module to improve contextual consistency. A spatial reasoning algorithm pairs question–answer entities, detects missing fields, and validates form completeness. A hybrid summarisation module combining rule-based methods with FLAN-T5 generates concise summaries. Experimental results show that the Modified LayoutLMv3 achieves superior performance with an overall accuracy of 81% and F1-scores of 0.85 for both question and answer classes, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed enhancements.

title: Freeze–Release: A Transient Supervisory Control Layer for Mixed-Criticality Traffic Under Overload
authors: Nurgüneş Yordanov, Bulent Çavuşoğlu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Shared wireless resources often carry both safety-critical and best-effort traffic, creating a trade-off between safety reliability and throughput under overload. This paper presents Freeze–Release (FR), a lightweight two-mode supervisory controller that temporarily shifts PRBs toward safety traffic when risk is detected from backlog, backlog growth, and head-of-line slack. FR is evaluated in a discrete-event downlink simulator against Always-Normal and Always-Freeze under best-effort overload. Results show that FR maintains zero safety deadline violations while substantially improving best-effort throughput over permanent freezing (2579 vs. 1026.5 pps in the Poisson case) and keeping safety tail latency close to Always-Freeze (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">p99</inf> = 0.299 ms vs. 0.214 ms). Under periodic multi-source safety traffic, FR further reduces freeze usage to 30.3% while achieving 3379 pps with zero violations. These results show that short, risk-aware interventions can protect safety traffic without unnecessary throughput loss.

title: An IoT based Voice-Activated Wheelchair for Healthcare and Mobility Assistance
authors: Sivasangari Ayyappan, Devaraju Thangellamudi, Sudha Rajesh, C.A.Subasini, Kiran, Jami Venkata Suman
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The design of an IoT-based voice-controlled wheelchair for healthcare and mobility aid purposes to make the movement of human beings easier and simpler is of significant importance. The majority of the available wheelchairs are either difficult to handle or require constant support from someone else, thereby affecting the freedom of the user. To address this challenge, the system they designed enables users to control the wheelchair using voice commands and gestures via an android mobile application. The mobile application communicates with the wheelchair via Bluetooth technology and sends commands to be processed by the microcontroller, which then manages the motors accordingly to move the wheelchair. In addition to the mobility of the user, health monitoring capabilities such as heart rate and body temperature sensors have been integrated to keep the health of the user in check. The application of IoT technology. The design of the intelligent health monitoring system incorporates IoT technology, thus providing the option to make the system applicable to smart healthcare and remote monitoring capabilities. The proposed wheelchair will be simple, reliable, energy-efficient, and cost- effective, hence very suitable for real-time healthcare and assistive technology. Overall, this system increases user independence, reduces caregiver dependence, and enhances the quality of life by offering smart mobility and health support.

title: E-Learning and Its Role in Building Cross-Border Capacities in the Era of Global Governance
authors: Marwan Mohammed Abbood, Zeyad khalaf Ismail, Muhammed Nawar Hussein, Abeer Ali Abdalazeez, Saeed Hameed Aldulaimi
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study examines the importance and role of elearning in building capacities worldwide. It also studies its role in developing institutional performance, particularly at the crossborder level, within the framework of a knowledge dissemination model influenced by globalization, which has rendered the world a highly interconnected global village. In this study, e-learning is addressed as one of the new sources of power in the contemporary international system. This is due to the continuous technological developments and the emergence of concepts such as global governance, smart systems, and artificial intelligence, which have greatly affected patterns of knowledge and learning globally through the ability to spread and reach more beneficiaries than before. The research problem of this study is framed around the following question: To what extent are contemporary e-learning models capable of reducing knowledge gaps between countries? In particular, it examines whether these models can challenge the prevailing knowledge geography that privileges Western-based knowledge and learning paradigms while marginalizing the roles of developing and smaller countries worldwide. This study hypothesizes that e-learning represents an important tool in the modern era for producing knowledge worldwide, and works to enhance and develop competencies, particularly in developing important sectors in developing and small countries. The significance of this study lies in highlighting the role of e-learning as a new mode of influence worldwide and a model for knowledge governance that transcends national borders. This study also sheds light on the importance of e-learning as a new model for knowledge sustainability through the continuous dissemination of educational resources. The current study is based on a systematic approach that relies on the analytical method. It gives a broader understanding of the impact of e-learning in promoting knowledge around the world by analyzing the current reality of e-learning, development opportunities, and future challenges.

title: The Influence of Using Large Language Models in the Educational Process: Trends, Challenges and Recommendations
authors: A.I. Ryzhkov, A.A. Kulikovskaya, M.V. Choporov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This article presents the results of an experimental study on the impact of large language models (LLMs) on the quality of student training in IT disciplines. The research was conducted at MPEI during the fall semester of 2024/2025 academic year. The experiment involved 150 students and 30 instructors of the courses "Programming" and "Data Analysis." The methodology of the experiment, including the division of participants into experimental and control groups, is described. A formalized indicator of educational quality is proposed, considering both the success rate in performing tasks and the level of critical justification of decisions. A "dual control" strategy was developed aimed at reducing students’ dependence on AI tools. The results showed that structured application of LLMs with pedagogical support enhances learning quality compared to uncontrolled use (Q = 0.78 versus Q = 0.74; p = 0.005). Addressing the pedagogical challenge posed by high LLM adoption rates (80% in IT) in higher education, focusing on mitigating dependency and cognitive skill degradation risks associated with unmonitored AI use. Objectives of research: to experimentally assess the impact of structured LLM application with pedagogical support versus uncontrolled use on the learning quality of IT students; to map LLM usage patterns among students and instructors; to compare learning quality metrics (Q) between controlled (experimental) and uncontrolled (control) LLM groups; to propose and validate a formalized quality indicator (Q) emphasizing critical justification (β=0.6); to develop and test the "dual control" strategy for fostering autonomous engagement with AI tools.

title: Large Language Model-Supported Label Expansion for Multi-Label Classification of Turkish E-Commerce Reviews
authors: Oğuz Ergin, İlhan Aydın, Mehmet Kaya
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study addresses the multi-label text classification problem on Turkish customer reviews collected from e-commerce platforms. The goal is to automatically label each review with multiple categories simultaneously, such as price, delivery, product quality, and satisfaction. First, a baseline model was established using One-vs-Rest Logistic Regression on Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) representation as a classical approach. Subsequently, BERTurk and ELECTRA-Turkish pre-trained Transformer models were trained using multi-label fine-tuning. The distinctive aspect of the study is the semi-automatic expansion of the label pool using a large language model prior to model training and the enrichment of the final target outputs by normalizing the obtained labels. Experiments were evaluated using subset accuracy and micro/macro/weighted F1 metrics on the validation set. The results show that the TF-IDF-based method achieved 41.43% subset accuracy and 82.42% micro-F1, while Transformer-based models produced higher performance (BERTurk: 54.23% subset accuracy, 87.95% micro-F1, 63.51% macro-F1, 85.95% weighted F1; ELECTRA-Turkish: 51.50% subset accuracy, 87.05% micro-F1, 58.03% macro-F1, 84.90% weighted F1).

title: Feature Fusion–based Glaucoma Detection through Deep Learning using Handcrafted and Deep Representations
authors: Vaddagani Madhuri, Jayadeep Pakala, Nalanagula Harini, M Janakidevi, A.Lakshmanarao, P.Chandra Sekhar Reddy
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Glaucoma is a leading cause of irreversible vision loss worldwide, making early and reliable detection from retinal fundus images essential for effective clinical intervention. While deep learning models have shown promising performance, relying on a single type of representation may limit robustness across varying image conditions. This paper presents a feature fusion–based framework that integrates handcrafted texture descriptors and deep representations for automated glaucoma detection. Initially, convolutional neural network (CNN) models are evaluated as end-to-end baselines to establish reference performance. Handcrafted features, including GLCM, LBP, and HOG, are then extracted to capture local texture characteristics, while deep features are obtained from a pretrained DenseNet121 model to encode high-level semantic information. The handcrafted and deep features are fused to form a comprehensive representation, followed by feature selection to reduce redundancy and enhance discriminative capability. Five machine learning classifiers—Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, Extra Trees, K-Nearest Neighbors, and Naive Bayes—are employed for classification. Experimental results on a publicly available retinal fundus dataset demonstrate that the proposed fused-feature approach consistently outperforms individual feature representations and CNN baselines, achieving superior accuracy, F1-score, and AUC. The findings confirm that combining complementary handcrafted and deep representations provides a robust and effective solution for automated glaucoma detection.

title: Implementation of a Two-Level Transport System Model Using Remote Agglomerations of Large Cities as an Example
authors: D. D. Varkentin
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: The article is devoted to the development of a twolevel transport system with the integration of various modes of transport in order to increase the speed of public transport by integrating high-speed and flexible modes of transport. The methodology of this study is a comprehensive approach aimed at increasing the accessibility and efficiency of high-speed trunk transport, which is the transport backbone of a modern city. The methodology includes the organization of coordinated work between flexible and trunk transport, as well as the improvement of the flexible transport system by improving the parameters of the route network, which aims to maximize the coverage of the population and minimize travel time. The main focus of the work will be on parameters that are crucial for attracting the public to the transport system, such as the speed of communication, the volume of passenger traffic, and the availability of transport facilities at various levels, including transfer hubs.

title: A Trust-Aware Hybrid Intelligence Platform for Explainable Cyber Threat Analysis and Proactive Defence in Next-Generation Internet of Vehicles Infrastructure
authors: Bethi Saiteja, Vupulluri Sudha Rani
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Internet of Vehicles (IoV) has become one of such disruptive technologies which allows to transform the transportation system by connecting vehicles, infrastructure, and cloud-based services. Nevertheless, with the growth of the complexity of the IoV systems, as well as their interconnection, the latter become more susceptible to cyber threats. Existing security measures are inadequate particularly explainability and proactive defence. The paper presents a trust-based hybrid intelligence platform that aims to improve the cyber threat analysis and the cyber threat defence infrastructures in the next-generation IoV infrastructures. The platform integrates machine learning methods with trust models to deliver explanation of cyber threats, which is transparent and usable. Experimental analysis on a real world IoV dataset indicates an enhancement of 12% in the accuracy of threat detection and a 15 percent decrease in false positives as compared to that of the traditional defence models. The proposed platform provides a dynamic and defendable defence strategy with explainable features capable of responding to changes in threats and enhancing security of IoV infrastructure to a great extent.

title: Research on Compatibility Analysis Methods between Mega Constellations and Geostationary Satellites
authors: Yiwei Qi, Xiang Gao, Xiujuan Yao, Xue Li, Zhimin Chen, Yanan Fan
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: With the rapid development of large-scale low Earth orbit (LEO) constellation systems, frequency and orbital resources are becoming increasingly scarce. Achieving effective and reliable compatibility analysis is the foundation for the efficient utilization of these resources. Existing compatibility analysis methods usually adopt time-domain extrapolation based on satellite positions to statistically evaluate interference results. However, for megaconstellations such as OneWeb and Starlink, traditional timedomain extrapolation methods for compatibility analysis face difficulties in computational cost and efficiency, making them insufficient to support the simulation requirements of megaconstellations. Therefore, this paper proposes a compatibility analysis method based on the spatial probability distribution of satellites. The interference-to-noise ratio is selected as the evaluation criterion, and the probability density of the subsatellite point of a reference satellite is used to map the interference results under the spatial distribution of the constellation. By placing the reference satellite within a predefined global grid, all possible spatial distributions of satellites are constructed to obtain the statistical results of I/N. Simulation results verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. Under the premise of ensuring the reliability of the simulation results, the proposed method reduces computational cost and improves computational efficiency.

title: Frequency-Guided Multi-Perspective Prompt Learning for Visible-Infrared Person Re-identification
authors: Jiawei Liu, Yong Wu, Yufei Zheng, Hao Tong, Guozhi Zhao, Zheng-Jun Zha
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Pre-trained vision-language models enhance single-modal person re-identification by leveraging high-level semantic knowledge through prompt learning. However, in visible-in-frared person re-identification, existing prompt learning frameworks primarily emphasize the spatial encoding of modality-shared pedestrian features, inevitably entangling identity-specific characteristics with modality-specific details. Consequently, they struggle to fully disentangle and exploit critical modality-specific cues from the entangled information and fail to construct robust modality-invariant discriminative representations by effectively integrating identity-specific information with features from both modalities, thereby hindering effective cross-modality alignment. To address these limitations, we propose a novel Frequency-Guided Multi-Perspective Prompt Learning (FGMP) framework for visible-infrared person re-identification, which decouples identity-specific and modality-specific information via frequency-guided feature disentanglement, establishes discriminative pedestrian semantics and global modality prototypes through multi-perspective prompts, and performs cross-modality feature fusion, collectively reducing modality discrepancies and facilitating robust pedestrian representation. Specifically, FGMP introduces the Frequency Domain Gated Decoupler (FDGD), which leverages the inherent discreteness of frequency representations to decompose pedestrian features into modality-shared and modality-specific components, overcoming the limitations of prior spatial-centric approaches that entangle identity-specific cues with modality-specific information. Building on this, a heterogeneous prompt learning strategy is employed to generate ID-specific and modality-specific prompts, enriching instance-discriminative information and global modality prototypes by activating CLIP’s inherent multi-modal knowledge and forming complementary high-level semantic interactions. Finally, a multi-modal feature fusion module is designed to seamlessly integrate representations from different modalities and identity-specific characteristics, yielding unified and discriminative pedestrian representations. Extensive experiments conducted on three widely used datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed FGMP.

title: Quantitative Time-Resolved Imaging of Surface Acoustic Waves by Dual-Comb Interferometry with a Fiber-Based Sagnac Interferometer_supp1-3701200.mp4
authors: Kazuki Maezawa
publish: N/A
abstract: The interferometer is constructed with polarization-maintaining fibers, providing high phase stability, robustness against environmental disturbances, and a compact configuration that significantly reduces alignment effort. In addition, the introduction of a stroboscopic scheme into the dual-comb interferometry enables rapid scanning of the surface acoustic wave (SAW) temporal waveform for both fundamental and nonlinear surface vibration components. Using this method, we present quantitative two-dimensional movies of propagating SAWs on a gold thin film. The proposed approach enables fast, stable, and quantitative visualization of both fundamental and higher-order SAW dynamics, and is expected to serve as a powerful tool for characterizing a wide range of linear and nonlinear acoustic devices.

title: HiSDevRec: Hybrid Interaction and Semantic-Aware Software Developer Recommendation
authors: Xinqiang Xie, Jiahui Liu, Xiaochun Yang, Bin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
abstract: Software developer recommendation is a critical task for online collaboration platforms. Identifying suitable developers remains challenging due to difficulties in capturing interaction behavioral preferences and semantic relevance, as well as handling data sparsity and cold-start issues. Traditional approaches typically model developers’ competencies (e.g., skills, expertise, and experience) or infer their preferences from historical task interactions. However, they often underperform because they fail to address several key challenges: (1) effectively capturing deep interaction behavioral preferences; (2) modeling latent textual semantics between developers and tasks; and (3) integrating behavioral preferences with semantic relevance in a unified manner. To address these challenges, we propose HiSDevRec, a novel dual-modality hybrid framework for developer recommendation that jointly models interaction-based behavioral preferences and textual semantic relevance. Specifically, we introduce a hybrid representation learning approach that integrates ID-based behavioral patterns with semantic representations derived from textual information. We further employ graph attention networks to capture higher-order propagation over the interaction graph, generating enriched representations for developers and tasks. In addition, we design a self-supervised disentanglement mechanism to explicitly separate behavioral and semantic representations, improving recommendation performance while enabling factor-level interpretability. Finally, we conduct extensive experiments on two real-world datasets, along with a case study and a user survey in an industrial setting. The results demonstrate that HiSDevRec consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines.

title: Cross-Modal Fusion with UVI Enhancement for Salient Object Detection in Low-Light ORSI Images
authors: Jianlong He, HuiLan Luo, Xia Cai, Ziran Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Salient object detection (SOD) in optical remote sensing images (ORSIs) remains challenging under low-light conditions, where illumination degradation severely reduces object visibility and boundary clarity. Existing ORSI-SOD methods, mainly designed for well-lit scenes, often suffer substantial performance drops in this setting. To address this issue, we propose DarkSalNet, a dual-branch framework for low-light ORSI-SOD. DarkSalNet jointly exploits the raw low-light RGB input and a UVI representation consisting of two ultraviolet-like chromatic channels (U, V ) and an intensity channel (I), generated by a perceptually guided color-space transform. Two cross-modal integration modules are further introduced: (1) a Cross-Modal Fusion (CMF) module that performs bidirectional gating for adaptive spatial feature calibration, and (2) a Semantic Cross-Aggregation (SCA) module that employs cross-attention with dual-path refinement to enhance semantic alignment. Extensive experiments on six synthetic low-light ORSI-SOD datasets and one real low-light NSI-SOD dataset demonstrate that DarkSalNet achieves state-of-the-art performance under varying illumination levels. Code and datasets are available at: https://github.com/ elaxEgan/DarkSalNet.

title: A Ruthenium-Coated Low-Adhesion Nanogap MEMS Switch with 2.5-V Actuation, 27-ns Switching, and Ω-Level Contact Resistance
authors: Tae-Soo Kim, Do-Hoon Lim, Sung-Ho Kim, So-Young Lee, Su-Min Jeon, Yeong-Min Cho, Yong-Bok Lee, Jun-Bo Yoon
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: Power gating reduces standby leakage in highly integrated CMOS systems, however, CMOS sleep transistors introduce area overhead and residual leakage. MEMS switches are attractive alternatives because an air gap suppresses off-state leakage and metal-to-metal contact provides low on-state resistance. However, achieving low-voltage actuation, fast switching, low resistance, and turn-off recovery remains challenging. Nanogap scaling reduces switching time and contact resistance, but also aggravates stiction as the restoring force decreases with the gap; increasing the spring constant improves recovery but raises the actuation voltage. This letter demonstrates a ruthenium-coated nanogap MEMS switch that lowers contact adhesion instead of relying on a high restoring force. A native ruthenium oxide layer on ruthenium suppresses metallic bonding at the contact interface. The fabricated switch with a 10-nm source–drain gap turns on at 2.5 V and exhibits 2.29-Ω contact resistance and 27-ns switching time. The device recovered to the off state for up to 3×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sup> cold-switching cycles before failure by stiction. These results demonstrate low-voltage operation, ultrafast switching, and Ω-level contact resistance in a nanogap mechanical switch.

title: Ionic Field-Effect Transistor Achieving Large Memory Window and Enhanced Retention via Ultrathin Ozone-Oxidized Barrier Layer
authors: Jongseon Seo, Geonhui Han, Laeyong Jung, Junghoon Park, Jongho Park, Suhwan Lim, Wanki Kim, Daewon Ha, Hyunsang Hwang
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: We propose an ionic field-effect transistor (Ionic FET) based on a WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3-x</sub>/HfO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> ECRAM stack. The device stores information via ionic-charge-induced threshold voltage (V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sub>) modulation, thus enabling analog state control with improved scalability over conventional charge-based storage. To relax the fundamental trade-off between ion injection and retention, an ultrathin ion barrier layer is introduced via ozone oxidation, which effectively suppresses ion back-diffusion while preserving sufficient ion injection. As a result, the proposed device achieves a large memory window of ~7.6 V with linear V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sub> modulation, enabling stable 3-bit multi-level operation with low state variability (σ/μ < 0.1) and robust endurance over 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> program/erase cycles. Furthermore, the ozone-treated barrier layer significantly increases the activation energy for ion back-diffusion, yielding a projected room-temperature retention exceeding 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup> sec.

title: Comparative Analysis of High-Torque-Density Stator-Dual-Set Permanent Magnet Machines for Electric Power Steering System
authors: Jiahui Huang, Yanding Bi, Weinong Fu, Shuangxia Niu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: Electric power steering (EPS) systems are critical for modern vehicle safety and driving refinement, requiring electromechanical actuators with high torque density, dynamic response, energy efficiency, and reliability. As a core component, the electric machine is essential for achieving these performance metrics. This paper proposes and comprehensively analyzes a stator-dual-set permanent magnet (SDSPM) machine with various permanent magnet (PM) arrangements for EPS applications. The proposed design utilizes a passive, low-inertia rotor, enabling rapid torque dynamics essential for responsive steering and minimizing rotor heat generation. Different from conventional asymmetric-stator-pole flux reversal PM (FRPM) machines, the SDSPM topology incorporates interior PM on the stator main teeth to amplify the 2nd- and 3rd-order harmonics of the equivalent stator magnetomotive force (MMF). This enhancement substantially improves the prominent working harmonics of the air-gap flux density, thereby increasing torque density. Three SDSPM variants with II-type, V-type, and U-type PM configurations are developed and evaluated. The proposed SDSPM machines demonstrate significant improvements in output torque and efficiency compared to conventional FRPM machines, with the U-type configuration exhibiting the highest torque density. Finally, a prototype of the U-type SDSPM machine is fabricated and tested to validate the analysis.

title: Energy-to-Peak Output Feedback Control of Discrete-Time Markov Jump Linear Systems
authors: Marcos G. Todorov, André M. de Oliveira
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This paper considers the $\boldsymbol{\ell }\_{2}$-$\boldsymbol{\ell }\_\infty$ (energy-to-peak) performance of discrete-time Markov jump linear systems (MJLS), with feedthrough disturbances affecting the output (also known as the non-strictly proper case). An $\boldsymbol{\ell }\_\infty$ output norm which is different from the one from the existing literature is considered. We provide lower and upper bounds to the $\boldsymbol{\ell }\_{2}$-$\boldsymbol{\ell }\_\infty$ induced norm, which can be computed through the solution of Lyapunov equations or linear matrix inequalities (LMIs). The upper bound corresponds to the upper diagonal entry of a Gramian-like matrix, mirroring classical results from the literature of linear time-invariant systems. The lower bound endows our approach with a necessity certificate, which can be used to assess the conservatism of the upper bound (a feature which does not exist in the current literature). It is shown in a simple example that, dissimilarly to what occurs in the linear time-invariant case, in our setup the $\boldsymbol{\ell }\_{2}$-$\boldsymbol{\ell }\_\infty$ norm can be arbitrarily larger than the $H\_{2}$ norm. This issue is dealt with through the introduction of a so-called $H\_{2}$-consistent case, where it is proven that the $\boldsymbol{\ell }\_{2}$-$\boldsymbol{\ell }\_\infty$ norm is upper bounded by the $H\_{2}$ norm (as in classical linear time-invariant approaches). Finally, new methods (based on linear matrix inequalities) are devised for the design of mean square stabilizing controllers with a prespecified energy-to-peak guaranteed cost, in the same vein as generalized $H\_{2}$ control.

title: A Wearable Sensor-Driven Monitoring Framework for Hypoxic Respiratory Key Parameters Using Sensitivity-Based Estimation
authors: Meitong Li, Jing Chen, Dawei Shi, Shiyue Jia, Lingling Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Wearable sensors provide continuous physiological measurements, such as $\text{SpO}_{2}$ and heart rate. However, in hypoxic respiratory applications, such sensors primarily monitor superficial symptoms, while the key underlying parameters remain nonmeasurable. To address this issue, this work proposes a process monitoring framework driven by a novel sensitivity-based parameter estimation approach. The framework utilizes a physics-informed model with 18 key parameters as the monitoring target, where the change in two critical parameters is used to assess the hypoxic adaptation index (HAI). The estimation method incorporates a sensitivity-based groupwise optimization and an identifiability-based collective variational inference, which assigns greater trust weights to high-quality signals to mitigate the impact of low-quality data. Monitoring effectiveness is validated via an in silico experiment, which demonstrates normalized mean absolute error reductions of 3.28% (individual) and 4.30% (population) over the baseline. The robustness of the framework and the effectiveness of the HAI are subsequently confirmed in a real-world intermittent hypoxic training experiment that involved noisy sensor data from 19 human subjects. This work provides a robust and generalizable framework for monitoring nonmeasurable key parameters in other complex dynamic systems.

title: A Physics-Guided LSTM-TL Based Thermal Neural Network for Temperature Prediction in IGBT Modules
authors: Xiaotong Zhang, Yongchao Gao, Hanqing Zhao, Xiangqian Tong, Wentao Ma, Junpeng Ji, Tao Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Accurately estimating the junction and critical material layer temperatures of insulated gate bipolar transistor (IGBT) modules is essential to prevent equipment damage from IGBT overheating, and minimize maintenance expenses. Existing data-driven models suffer from limited comprehensibility and poor physical interpretability. To address this issue, a fusion neural network is proposed, which integrates long short-term memory (LSTM) with fundamental heat transfer physics for IGBT module temperature prediction. First, we examine the heat transfer processes within the module and embed the underlying physical principles into a deep learning architecture. This approach resolves the parameter dependency issues inherent in conventional thermal network models by incorporating physical loss constraints and LSTM network. Second, transfer learning (TL) is employed to fine-tune the LSTM network, integrating experimental data to enhance the alignment between simulation results and actual observations. Consequently, the proposed neural network model achieves enhanced transparency and interpretability. Experimental validation demonstrates that compared with finite element method simulation and LSTM network, the thermal neural network based on physics-guided LSTM-TL significantly reduces prediction errors to 0.13%–0.21% while enhancing generalization performance.

title: Chain Reaction: A Triple-Chain Architecture for Sensitive Thumbnail-Preserving Image Encryption
authors: Ruoyu Zhao, Wenying Wen, William Puech, Rushi Lan, Yushu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Recently, thumbnail-preserving encryption (TPE) has attracted significant attention because it maintains certain visual information in encrypted images, thereby achieving a trade-off between visual usability and privacy protection. However, existing TPE schemes utilize independent encryption between pixels, blocks, or channels, which results in excessive robustness and consequently introduces security vulnerabilities. To address this issue, we propose a triple-chain architecture to achieve sensitive TPE. This architecture integrates three interdependent encryption chains at the pixel, block, and channel levels, establishing cryptographic dependencies such that any minor modification to either the original or encrypted image leads to completely divergent encryption/decryption outcomes. Specifically, the architecture introduces three chained encryption mechanisms: (1) pixel-level chained encryption propagates encrypted pixels outputs as the part of subsequent inputs; (2) block-level hashing derives new keys from plaintext-key concatenations; and (3) channel-level dependencies are established via iterative key derivation. This cascading structure ensures cryptographic interdependency across all levels. The experimental results verify that the proposed architecture achieves sensitive TPE for the first time while preserving the TPE characteristics of visual usability and privacy protection.
