title: A Transfer Learning-Based Framework for Real-Time Driver Distraction Detection on Edge Devices
authors: Yuvraj Singh, Amrita Roy, R. Nageshwar Rao
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Distracted driving incidents is a significant risk to today’s road and driver safety, and accounts for a significant portion to the number of crashes, injuries and fatalities across global road systems.With the goal of improving safety on the roads, this paper proposes an approach of development and implementation of a deep learning system, based on the visual input provided by the cameras installed on the dashboard of the automobile, which can automatically detect and classify the driver distraction behaviors. Specifically, the proposed approach uses transfer learning with the pre-trained VGG16 model architecture, combined with staged fine-tuning on the State Farm Distracted Driver Dataset which is made up of ten different behavioural classes. The proposed architecture includes a multi-step training pipeline that involves data augmentation, data preprocessing via normalization techniques, and later the unfreezing of convolutional layers in stages to maximize convergence and generalization. The experimental validation shows that the model achieves a validation accuracy of around 95.54% on the benchmark dataset in differentiating among complex categories of distraction by drivers, including texting, engaging with mobile phones, eating or drinking, operating vehicle controls, and grooming. Beyond accuracy, the model achieves macro-averaged F1-score of 0.955, with per-class precision and recall exceeding 0.93 across all ten behavioral categories. Deployment of Flask-based microservice via Docker facilitates inference on a frame-by-frame basis in real-time, creating a scope for pragmatic use in conjunction with Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) and vehicle safety systems.

title: A Resonant DC-Link Converter with Wide-Range Soft-Switching and Enhanced Voltage Utilization for Wireless Power Transfer Applications
authors: Youzheng Wang, Hongchen Liu, Qikun Zhou, Longnv Li, Xuqiang Yang, Fei Gao, Patrick Wheeler
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: To address the urgent demands for high efficiency and a wide voltage operating range in wireless power transfer (WPT) systems for electric vehicles (EVs), this paper proposes a topology based on a resonant DC-link. Retaining the functions of single-stage power factor correction (PFC) and voltage boosting, this topology achieves soft-switching operation over a wide load range and effectively elevates the steady-state level of the DC bus voltage, thereby significantly improving the voltage utilization ratio and output capability of the inverter. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that the proposed scheme can maintain zero voltage switching (ZVS) conditions for main switches across wide input voltage and load ranges, reduce switching losses and electromagnetic interference (EMI), and simultaneously boost the DC voltage via the resonant peak to enhance the power transfer adaptability of the system. The effectiveness of the proposed topology is validated by a 3.3-kW WPT experimental prototype with a 15cm air gap, and experimental results show that the system attains a peak efficiency of 94.2% over the full load range. Compared with the scheme employing the Z-source resonant converter, the proposed topology exhibits remarkable advantages in soft-switching range, voltage boosting capability and efficiency, and thus provides a high-performance circuit and control solution for high-frequency and high-power WPT systems.

title: Thermal and Electrical Stress Differences of SiC MOSFET Under Active/Reactive Loads in AC Power Cycling Test
authors: Tianyang Wang, Qi Li, Dafang Wang, Guohao Yang, Jinhuang Zhao, Jiuxiao Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Thermal and electrical stresses are key factors influencing the reliability of SiC MOSFETs. The alternating current (AC) power cycling test (PCT) has emerged as a more representative reliability test method for applications compared to traditional direct current (DC) PCT. However, the distinction between thermal and electrical stress under active and reactive load conditions during AC PCT remains largely unexplored. This paper presents a comparative study of active and reactive load AC PCT using a SiC inverter topology. Theoretical analysis and experimental verification show that under identical packaging and testing conditions, the differences in electrical stress (dv/dt at the gate and source, Vds overshoot) between the two loads are negligible. The difference in thermal stress between the two loads depends on the current and the modulation index. When an increased current causes the SiC MOSFET to enter synchronous rectification, the thermal stress on the active load exceeds that on the reactive load. Moreover, the higher the modulation index, the greater the difference in thermal stress. These findings support the design of endurance testing for SiC MOSFETs under AC PCT.

title: Semantic Optimization of Digital Service Delay and Response Speed in Edge Computing for 6G Networks
authors: Xibing Shi, Cui Li, Haoming Wang
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: Sixth-generation mobile networks are expected to support diverse delay-sensitive services, including healthcare, industrial automation, connected vehicles, and extended reality. In these environments, service effectiveness depends not only on successful data delivery but also on whether the delivered information is timely, relevant, and actionable for the target task. This article presents a semantic-aware edge optimization framework for reducing digital service delays and accelerating response speeds in heterogeneous 6G environments. The proposed approach interprets incoming requests according to semantic relevance, contextual urgency, and task importance, and converts this interpretation into adaptive scheduling and execution decisions at the edge. A semantic-aware service architecture and end-to-end optimization workflow were developed to support request interpretation, semantic priority scoring, differentiated execution, and feedback-driven adaptation. The framework was evaluated on a heterogeneous edge benchmarking platform using healthcare, industrial IoT, vehicular, and XR service streams under low, medium, high, and burst load conditions. Compared with conventional, QoS-aware, and context-aware scheduling, the proposed Semantic Optimization framework consistently achieves a lower end-to-end delay, higher deadline satisfaction, stronger robustness under congestion, and better cross-scenario generalization. These results show that semantic communications can be operationalized beyond transmission design and used as a practical edge-intelligence mechanism for task-oriented and latency-sensitive 6G digital services. The observed improvements were primarily driven by semantic-aware prioritization mechanisms rather than parameter tuning, as all the compared strategies were evaluated under identical system configurations.

title: FZKP: Alleviating Dataflow Complexity to Exploit Fine-Grained Parallelism for ZKP Acceleration
authors: Ziheng Xiao, Mingyu Yan, Mingyu Gao, Runzhen Xue, Duo Wang, Xiaochun Ye, Dongrui Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: Zero-knowledge proof (ZKP) is a promising cryptographic protocol, but its practical deployment is hindered by the time-consuming proof generation. The proof generation inherently exhibits high-degree parallelism, yet challenges persist in exploiting fine-grained parallelism due to the dataflow complexity, impeding previous work to achieve optimal acceleration. In this work, we propose FZKP, a ZKP accelerator that utilizes two novel fine-grained dataflows coupled with two forward-flow microarchitectures to alleviate dataflow complexity, efficiently exploiting fine-grained parallelism. The proposed dataflows simplify the dataflow pattern for parallel execution, disclosing fine-grained parallelism at a low cost. The microarchitectures employ a base design to handle large bit-width intermediate results for timely consumption. They then replicate and combine the base design following the proposed dataflow to facilitate parallel execution. When evaluated in 12 nm, FZKP achieves an average speedup of 10.3× and 2.2× over the state-of-the-art GPU-based solution and ZKP accelerator on real-world workloads, respectively.

title: Practical Multi-Model Dynamic Matrix Control for Power Plant Superheated Steam Temperature
authors: Yuhan Zhang, Xingwei Zhou, Hongkun Xiong, Hong Zhou
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Controlling superheated steam temperature in thermal power plants is challenged by large inertia, time delays, and load-dependent dynamics, which severely limit traditional cascade PID controllers. To address these industrial issues, this paper proposes a practical multi-model Dynamic Matrix Control (DMC) strategy embedded in a cascade architecture. Step-response models are identified from operating data across 30% to 100% load conditions. A linear interpolation weighting mechanism dynamically schedules the DMC to mitigate model mismatch during load variations. The principal novelty of this work lies in performing load-indexed scheduling directly on non-parametric step-response coefficients, which guarantees a physically realizable blended model and preserves the lower-triangular Toeplitz structure required by the DMC formulation. Consequently, the scheduler introduces no additional online matrix inversion or optimization steps, ensuring full compatibility with the stringent CPU and memory constraints of industrial distributed control systems (DCSs). The strategy is rigorously validated through extensive MATLAB/Simulink simulations encompassing set-point tracking, disturbance rejection, and model–plant mismatch scenarios. Compared with five independently tuned cascade PI controllers, the proposed scheme reduces overshoot from approximately 12%– 13% to below 1.3% and the 2% settling time by 52%–58% across the full 0%–100% load range while requiring only a single set of DMC tuning parameters. Results demonstrate that the multi-model DMC eliminates overshoot, significantly reduces settling time, and exhibits superior robustness against random disturbances and valve nonlinearities compared to well-tuned PID control, proving its strong potential for real-world distributed control system deployment.

title: SAS: Semantic-aware Sampling for Generative Dataset Distillation
authors: Mingzhuo Li, Guang Li, Linfeng Ye, Jiafeng Mao, Takahiro Ogawa, Konstantinos N. Plataniotis, Miki Haseyama
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Signal Processing
abstract: Deep neural networks have achieved impressive performance across a wide range of tasks, but this success often comes with substantial computational and storage costs due to large-scale training data. Dataset distillation addresses this challenge by constructing compact yet informative datasets that enable efficient model training while maintaining downstream performance. However, most existing approaches primarily emphasize matching data distributions or downstream training statistics, with limited attention to preserving high-level semantic information in the distilled data. In this work, we introduce a semantic-aware perspective for dataset distillation by leveraging Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining (CLIP) as a semantic prior for post-sampling. Our goal is to obtain distilled datasets that are not only compact but also semantically class-discriminative and diverse. To this end, we design three semantic scoring functions that quantify class relevance, inter-class separability, and intra-set diversity in a pretrained semantic space. Based on image pools generated by existing distillation methods, we further develop a two-stage strategy for effective sampling: the first stage filters semantically discriminative samples to form a reliable candidate set, and the second stage performs a dynamic diversity-aware selection to reduce redundancy while preserving semantic coverage. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets, image pools, and downstream models demonstrate consistent performance gains, highlighting the effectiveness of incorporating semantic information into dataset distillation.

title: Multispecies Response-ability in More-than-human Design Practice: Fabulation with Tides
authors: Jiwei Zhou, Raphael Kim, Iohanna Nicenboim, Anton Poikolainen Rosén, Fernanda Soares da Costa, Netta Ofer, Serena Pollastri, Heidi Biggs, Doenja Oogjes, Bahareh Barati
publish: Companion Publication of the 2026 ACM Designing Interactive Systems Conference

title: A Novel Healthcare Records Privacy-Preserving and Dynamic Error-Free Communication Medium based on Blockchain enabled IoT Technology
authors: Anguraju Krishnan, Rajesh Arunachalam
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The process of healthcare is rapidly becoming digital, and the number of the medical devices that are associated with the IoT is really staggering, which produces an enormous amount of real-time patient data and requires the proper, efficient, and safe delivery of the latter. The new Blockchain based platform of the IoT as described in the paper incorporates the IoT which is based on privacy preserving healthcare records, lightweight encryption (AES) functionality, hash capability (SHA-256) and blockchain-based validation of consensuses and preprocessing on the edges. As shown by the experimental test, the suggested system will be described by high accuracy (98.4), a massive reduction of latency (180 ms), a high degree of energy consumption (1.7 J/transaction), and high degree of resistance to cyber-attacks (average detection rate 98.48). The AI-based analytics, federated learning, and the implementation of quantum-protected blockchain can be considered the methodology that can be adopted to address the improvements in the future since this stage will help to develop the described relationship further.

title: Deep Learning-Based Protein Structure Pattern Recognition for Intelligent Cervical Cancer Diagnosisanalytics
authors: Venkata S P Dendukuri, Akshiitha Kamasani, Harika Chennupati, Leela Aditya Dakoju, Suvarna Vani Koneru
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: One of the leading causes of cancer related deaths in women across the world, especially in the underdeveloped countries, is cervical cancer. The survival rates need to be detected early, yet the conventional methods of diagnosis such as Pap smear and HPV tests are normally ineffective due to their lack of sensitivity and human error. In order to bypass this complication and properly diagnose cervical cancer, suggest a new deep learning-based measure, which makes use of the analysis of protein structure. The method, which uses protein data obtained in the Protein Data Bank is used to produce structural representations that model the subtle aspects of cancer- related proteins, including the sparse matrices and contact maps. By developing a deep learning model on protein structures and performing dimensionality reduction models, it is possible to classify cervical cancer proteins with extremely high accuracy. This computational model delivers an automatic diagnostic system, which is scalable and capable of improving the reliability of cervical cancer diagnosis, and hence less reliance on the conventional techniques. Our results show that deep learning can have an effect on cancer diagnosis. Actually, our strategy can contribute to the early diagnosis by the means of more rigorous and effective detection tools. The paper has facilitated the entry of computational biology and cancer research and therefore brought about a highly powerful interface between bioinformatics and medical diagnostics. The proposed model achieves better performance, with accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score of 96.2, 95.4, 94.8, and 95.1, respectively.

title: Productivity Improvement Analysis Using FlexSim Simulation: A Case Study at Electronics Company
authors: Eko Vieri Kurniawan, Aswan Munang
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: FlexSim-based Discrete Event Simulation (DES) is highly effective for analyzing and optimizing the productivity of the electrical cable production line at this company. The company faced challenges including machine workload imbalance, idle time, and bottlenecks at the Quality Control and Manual Packing stations. Analysis of the existing conditions identified a single bottleneck at the cabling machine (processing time 27.08%) causing extreme idle time at the Quality Control station (97.89%) and Manual Packing (91.77%). The 3D simulation model was validated with a deviation of 1.33% from the actual conditions. Two interventions were applied simultaneously: the addition of one parallel cabling machine unit and the consolidation of the QC and Manual Packing functions. As a result, output increased by 44.59% (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$148 \rightarrow 214$</tex> units/ 24 hours), average idle time decreased by 11.11 percentage points, blocked time was reduced from 8.5% to 5.3%, and cabling machine utilization increased from 27.08% to 40.28%. These findings confirm the effectiveness of DES as a data-driven decision support tool for manufacturing production system optimization with minimal investment.

title: TeaRAG: A Token-Efficient Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework
authors: Chao Zhang, Yuhao Wang, Derong Xu, Haoxin Zhang, Yuanjie Lyu, Yuhao Chen, Shuochen Liu, Tong Xu, Xiangyu Zhao, Yan Gao, Yao Hu, Enhong Chen

title: The Influence of Artificial Intelligence on Management Effectiveness Through Transformational Leaders' Decision-Making
authors: Bui Thanh Khoa, Tran Trong Huynh, Duong Kim Ngoc
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The rapid adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) in corporate management has significantly impacted leadership and decision-making, particularly in the field of information technology (IT). Nonetheless, empirical studies examining the collective impact of AI elements on management efficacy via the decision-making quality of transformational leaders are few, especially in emerging countries such as India. This study investigates the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) adoption, AI-driven analytics, and AI automation on management effectiveness (ME) and the quality of decision-making in transformational leadership (TL-DQ) within Vietnam's information technology(IT) industry. The quantitative survey included 356 IT professionals from 25 firms in Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. Data analysis was performed using WarpPLS 7.0's Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). All three AI elements enhance TL-DQ, thereby augmenting managerial efficacy. TL-DQ serves as a crucial intermediary between the AI dimensions and managerial efficacy. The results enhance the theoretical synthesis of the Technology Acceptance Model, Transformational Leadership Theory, and Adaptive Structuration Theory within a multifaceted AI framework in an emerging economy, offering IT organizations pragmatic guidance for leveraging AI to achieve sustainable competitive advantage.

title: NMPC-Based Optimal Momentum Distribution for Post-Capture Detumbling Control of Cable-Driven Space Robot
authors: Zhiwei Wu, Lei Yan, Wenfu Xu, Tianhong Cheng, Bin Liang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Cable-driven redundant space manipulators (CDRSMs) combine high maneuverability with inherent compliance, rendering them ideal for on-orbit servicing missions involving the capture and stabilization of tumbling targets. A critical challenge in post-capture operations is mitigating the target’s residual angular momentum, which may destabilize the system. This paper proposes an optimal detumbling control framework integrating momentum conservation principles and nonlinear model predictive control (NMPC). First, the dynamics of the coupled manipulator-target-base system are derived. A momentum redistribution strategy is then designed to reduce base disturbances. Additionally, a coordinated control scheme is introduced to simultaneously optimize joint velocities of the manipulator and reaction wheel speeds, accounting for basemanipulator dynamic coupling. Numerical simulations demonstrate the framework’s efficacy.

title: Quantum Key Distribution Communication Channel with Unmanned Vehicles Under Various Atmosferic Conditions
authors: Elena P. Stroganova, Sergey E. Grychkin, Evelin Y. Bushuev
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper explores the feasibility of using an atmospheric optical channel in a quantum key distribution communication system for communicating with unmanned aerial vehicles. The paper examines the specifics of turbulence in the tropospheric ground layer where the atmospheric link terminals of communication systems are located. The paper describes the methodology and a laboratory experimental setup. The paper measures the key optical, polarization, and network parameters in the presence of disturbance sources in the atmospheric optical segment. An experimental study of the atmospheric channel communication system under various weather conditions was conducted. The results of the experimental study of the influence of climatic factors on the characteristics of the atmospheric optical channel of the communication system showed a significant impact of climatic factors. The data obtained also allow us to formulate recommendations on the feasibility of using a quantum key distribution communication system with an atmospheric optical channel under various climatic factors and atmospheric turbulence.

title: AMAC: An Adaptive Multimodal Framework for Dysarthric Speech Recognition and Interactive Rehabilitation
authors: Tejasri S, Sharique Mukadam, Beaulah Jeyavathana R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Individuals suffering from dysarthric speech due to neuromotor conditions such as cerebral palsy experience deteriorating speech intelligibility and difficulty communicating through automatic speech recognition systems. The study proposes the development of AMAC (ReVoice) that acts as an adaptive multimodal platform for improving both communicative and rehabilitation outcomes using visual speech recognition techniques. The proposed solution employs a hybrid approach involving a 3D Convolutional Neural Network (3D-CNN) architecture and Transformer-based model for analyzing mouth motion and abnormal speech actions. Transfer learning is employed to mitigate the scarcity of dataset by leveraging GRID and TORGO datasets. The solution is implemented as a real-time web application using WebSocket-based inference to enable low-latency processing alongside improved system robustness. The findings reveal superior performance outcomes of the proposed approach relative to baseline solutions such as Whisper and AV-HuBERT.

title: High Power Supply and Control Management Technology Based on SiC Devices
authors: Baolei Dong, Haoyue Deng, Xiao Wang, Lidong Shi, Xu Huang, Zirui Zhu
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: In view of the demand for high-efficiency and lightweight power supply for China's space vehicles, this study carried out research on high-power power supply and control management technology based on SiC devices, broke through reliable SiC device application technology, power management strategy and control technology, and completed the development of a power prototype. The output voltage range of the prototype is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 \mathrm{V}-300 \mathrm{V}$</tex>, the output power is 4 kW, the conversion efficiency is up to 97%, and the power density reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2000 \mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex>, which provides an efficient and integrated energy management system for aircraft and lays a foundation for the development of aircraft platforms in China.

title: Hybrid User Profiling with TF-IDF and Cosine Similarity for Personalized Academic Book Recommendation
authors: Muhammad Haikal Md Imran, Zarina Dzolkhifli, Mohd Hafiz Mohd Hassin, Suryanti Awang
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Recommender systems have enhanced content discovery in large-scale commercial platforms; however, their application in small-scale academic libraries remains constrained by data sparsity and limited user interaction records. This paper proposes a hybrid user profiling approach for personalized academic book recommendation using Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) and cosine similarity. The model constructs a unified user preference vector by combining two behavioral components: a centroid representation of users' favorite books to capture long-term academic interests and a vectorized representation of recent search history to reflect short-term information needs. These components are aggregated using a weighted scheme to form a hybrid profile, which is matched against candidate books through cosine similarity over TF-IDF-based metadata representations. The approach does not rely on collaborative filtering, making it suitable for domain-specific libraries with limited datasets. The system was implemented as a mobile application and evaluated using a collection of 500 academic books. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method generates relevant recommendations with a response time below three seconds and achieves satisfactory usability performance. The findings demonstrate that hybrid content-based profiling offers an efficient and practical solution for personalized recommendation in small-scale academic environments.

title: Application of the Modified Prony Method to the Problem of Identification of a Noisy Signal
authors: Olga V. Opalikhina, Mikhail A. Zhelavskiy
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: The article describes the problem of identifying an information signal isolated against the background of a high level of additive noise. To approximate the information parameters, a modified least squares method was chosen. It is based on the decomposition of the data matrix into singular numbers. The modified Prony method avoids biased frequency estimation and attenuation of the information component of the signal. The simulation of the sample data is performed in the Wolfram Mathematica computer environment. The program code is written in C++. The algorithm provides a block method for processing information parameters of both exponential and sinusoidal information signal models.

title: A Real-Time Fire Detection System in Automobile Tunnels Based on the YOLO Architecture
authors: Daniil Lukin, Sergei Gaponenko, Marina Manzhos, Daria Matyunina
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: Fires in car tunnels pose a critical threat due to the rapid spread of smoke, limited visibility and difficult evacuation. Traditional fire alarm systems have high inertia and are prone to false alarms in tunnel environments. In this paper, we propose a real-time visual fire detection system based on the lightweight YOLO architecture for flame and smoke detection in a video stream. The proposed approach includes hierarchical decisionmaking logic and aggregation of frames in a time window, which increases the system's resistance to noise and short-term artifacts. A specialized dataset reflecting the tunnel conditions was used for training and testing. Experimental results show an accuracy of up to 0.92 with a processing delay of less than 50 ms per frame. The developed system demonstrates high efficiency of early detection of fires and can be integrated into intelligent monitoring systems of transport infrastructure.

title: AIF-MFF: Adaptive Inference Framework for Multi-Focus Image Fusion Via Dynamic Receptive Field Modulation and Cross-Scale Contrastive Alignment
authors: A. Sudharani, Ramya M, Jayapal Lande, Ridhi Jain
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Multi-focus image fusion (MFIF) aims at creating one all-in-focus image based on several partially focused images. The current techniques generally assume fixed receptive fields and stationary degradation profiles, which are not generalizable with respect to different focal stacks and camera properties. This paper presents an inference framework based on adaptive inference that is used to change the scale of receptive fields and the weight of feature aggregation based on the estimated focus likelihood at the time of inference. A multi-exit focus sensor is used to estimate spatially varying blur kernels with no ground truth, while a gated fusion controller is used to adapt fusion strategies based on cross-scale consistency. In contrastive warm-up, a contrastive warm-up matches the representations of aligned and misaligned source pairs without the need to use pixel-precise training data. The results of the experiments conducted on four benchmark datasets are better fusion quality and perturbation stability, which can have a feasible inference budget. The framework brings into manifest the connection between architectural flexibility and fusion fidelity, and indicates that inference-time modulation provides a way to sensor-agnostic deployment.

title: The Impact of Influencer Marketing on Product Perception and Its Effect on Purchase Intention in Indonesia's Beauty Industry
authors: Arneta Marsya Nafisa, Zefanya Ekberg Dip Kaur, Yuniarty
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Currently, the use of influencers on social media in Indonesia is increasingly widespread, particularly in the beauty industry. However, several key factors significantly influence consumer purchase intention. This study aims to examine the impact of influencer marketing strategies on purchase intention in Indonesia's beauty industry by analyzing related variables, including Influencer Post, Influencer Storytelling, Influencer Credibility, Product Perception, and Purchase Intention. The research employed a quantitative design with data collected cross-sectionally through a questionnaire survey. A total of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 9 1}$</tex> Indonesian respondents were recruited using purposive sampling, defined as having purchased beauty products after viewing influencer content. Data analysis was conducted using Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) - Partial Least Squares (PLS) through SmartPLS. According to the findings of this study, Influencer Post and Product Perception affect Purchase Intention in Indonesia's beauty industry. Additionally, the presence of Influencer Credibility and Influencer Storytelling influences Product Perception, which also affects the consumer's intent to purchase. This study provides insights, benefits, and knowledge regarding the influence of influencer marketing strategies on purchase intention in the Indonesian beauty industry.

title: Comparative Analysis of Russian-Developed CAD Software in Engineering Education
authors: Natalia A. Martynenko, Evgeny Ya. Chakheev, Nikita V. Ivankov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This study presents a comparative analysis of Russian-developed computer-aided design (CAD) systems utilized by students in engineering and computer graphics courses across all fields of study. The systems under consideration include KOMPAS-3D, nanoCAD, and T-FLEX CAD. The primary goal of this research is to identify the functional advantages and limitations of each software package, as well as to determine an optimal strategy for their integration into educational activities.

title: Forecasting Power Consumption By Extracting Features From A Time Series
authors: Konstantin Seregichev, Aleksey Sukonschikov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article discusses methods for predicting power consumption based on time series analysis. As part of the study, a method for the automated extraction of characteristics from time sequences is proposed, aimed at their further use in machine learning tasks. This approach makes it possible to effectively transform the initial data into informative features suitable for building and training predictive models. The study is based on data on electricity consumption in commercial facilities, which makes it possible to increase forecasting accuracy and optimize energy management. The paper presents the results of comparing various combinations of estimated indicators and loss functions within the framework of a single machine learning model. Special attention is paid to estimating the uncertainty of forecasts and analyzing the influence of time factors on the accuracy of predictions. The results of the study demonstrate that the use of modern machine learning methods makes it possible to achieve high accuracy in predicting power consumption based on historical data.

title: Lung Disease Detection with Hybrid CNN Models using only Chest X-Ray Images on the Basis of Deep Learning
authors: B Yogeshwaran, K Ramya, M Revathi, A Jenifer
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Lung infections, pneumonia, tuberculosis, and Covid-19 pose significant health concerns globally. Therefore, it is important to develop diagnostic tools that are accurate and expedient. Chest radiographs are commonly used to screen for lung disease. However, it is often not possible to manually interpret a chest X-ray due to the large amount of radiological knowledge required, leading to a delayed or inconsistent diagnosis. The research proposed in this paper proposes a hybrid deep learning model designed by combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with transfer learning to diagnose lung diseases via chest X-ray images. For the feature extraction component of the design, we will use several pre-trained CNN models: VGG16, ResNet50, MobileNetV2, a) EfficientNetB0. Each pre-trained model will have operating classifications based on disease-specific classification implemented using custom CNN layers and will use both pre-processing to enhance chest radiographs and data augmentation to create robust, generalised images for our model. By using our methodology, we expect accuracy in classification to improve and will reduce the time and cost required for training. Since our experimental analysis shows that the hybrid model has the potential to assist in distinguishing lung diseases, the implemented system has the ability to facilitate the early identification of lung diseases and will ultimately assist healthcare personnel by providing rapid, verifiable, and AI-augmented diagnoses.

title: Smart Gloves for Sign Language Translation and Gesture Bot Control
authors: Nagapriya Kamath K, Swathi K
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The proposed system includes a smart glove that helps to communicate with mute or audio-impaired individuals by translating their sign language movements into text messages or commands. This system uses flex sensors as well as motion sensors or accelerometers to sense hand movements that identify specific words or alphabets. This information is then sent to the ESP32 Microcontroller, which uses IoT technologies to process this data. In this system, a Gesture Control Bot is also integrated, which uses hand gestures to perform specific tasks as per the commands sent by the smart glove system. This system uses the ESP-NOW Protocol to communicate wirelessly, covering a distance of 150m to 200m, which is much higher compared to Bluetooth communication. The accuracy of this system for translating alphabets is 80.76%, while for translating phrases, it is 82.26%.

title: Optimal Orientation Design of Heat Flux Under Power Constraint During the Transfer Orbit
authors: Di Jin, Junjun Mao, Yu Fu, Chongwei Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Some spacecraft orbit transfer last for a long duration with limited electric power, facing extreme cold environments. In the flight phase when the attitude is not constrained, how to effectively utilize the orbital heat flux and reduce the heating power, so that the equipment of the spacecraft can meet the allowable lower temperature limit, is an important content of the overall design and thermal control design of the spacecraft. Based on the background of the power-off launch of a communication constellation, a method for identifying the optimal upper stage orientation that maximizes the orbital heat flux of satellite is proposed. After simulation analysis and flight test verification, the optimal orientation of the upper stage spin axis can increase the satellite temperature by $6^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.

title: AFSA: Autonomous Few-Shot Prototype Adaptation for Open-Set Deepfake Detection
authors: Ishank Yadav, Sonali Beura, Shyamapada Mukherjee
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Deepfake generation technology evolves continuously, rendering static detectors obsolete whenever a new generative model is released. This brittleness stems from a structural limitation: closed-set classifiers learn decision boundaries only for generators seen during training and possess no mechanism to recognize or recover from distributional shift at deployment time. We formalize this failure mode as open-set continual detection and address it with AFSA (Autonomous Few-Shot Prototype Adaptation), a framework that decouples shift detection from adaptation without retraining the encoder. The central contribution is a margin-based drift detector: the decision margin m(z) = d<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">real</inf> − d<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">fake</inf> collapses toward zero when an embedding enters a prototype-uncovered region, providing a geometry-driven, label-free signal that triggers diversity-aware selection of K support samples and a closed-form prototype bank update in 12 ms with no gradient computation. Across two open-set scenarios cross-paradigm (GAN→Diffusion) and within-family (StyleGAN2→StyleGAN3) AFSA recovers from 47.2% zero-shot accuracy to 96.5%–99.9% with as few as K = 5 labeled samples, and 96.8%–99.9% at K = 20 (AUROC 0.987–0.998, FPR/FNR < 5.6%). Encoder fine-tuning at 32× greater computational cost yields strictly lower accuracy (94.4%), confirming that the frozen-encoder design is preferable in the low-data regime. Ablation studies confirm that each of the four framework modules generator-specific prototypes, drift detection, diversity-aware selection, and EVT open-set rejection contributes independently and is non-redundant.

title: Experience in Implementing the SimInTech Dynamic Simulation Environment in Electrical Power Engineering Disciplines
authors: Alexandr Polyakov, Olga Malova
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper deals with Russian dynamic simulation environment SimInTech and its main capabilities for modelling electrical units. It covers experience in its use at the Department of Electrical Stations of the National Research University "Moscow Power Engineering Institute". Examples of the SimInTech’s application in laboratory work of varying complexity for undergraduate and master's students are presented. The paper also covers its use for network calculations in a course project on the topic "Design of an electrical substation".

title: Fixed-Time Lag Formation Control for Second-Order Nonlinear Multiagent Systems With External Disturbances
authors: Xuechen Wang, Qinhong Fu, Sining Yu, Yan-Fen Song, Jin-Liang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: This paper utilizes virtual leader and sliding mode control methods to tackle the fixed-time lag formation problem for a seconde-order nonlinear multiagent system (MAS) subject to external disturbances. A novel distributed fixed-time observer is given for the agents. Based on the obtained accurate state information of the virtual leader, a sliding mode control protocol is developed to guarantee the fixed-time lag formation for the MAS. Finally, a numerical example is presented to validate the effectiveness of the devised observer and control strategies.

title: Laser Communication System with Adaptive Optics Implementation
authors: Vladimir Toporovsky, Ilya Galaktionov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper examines the critical role of adaptive optics (AO) in modern free-space laser communication systems, where atmospheric turbulence significantly degrades signal quality. We analyze the main physical effects, such as beam wander, scintillation, and wavefront distortion, that limit communication range and data rates. The article describes the operating principles of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A O}$</tex> systems, including wavefront sensing and real-time beam correction using deformable mirrors. The benefits of AO implementation are demonstrated, such as increased link stability, higher throughput, and extended operational range. Current technical challenges, including system complexity, response speed, and cost, are discussed. The paper concludes with an overview of promising research directions and the future integration of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A O}$</tex> in next-generation optical communication networks. The paper is focused on the implementation of the AO system for free-space laser communication after resizing of the laser beam after atmospheric turbulence path transferring.
