title: Green Economy as a Strategic Direction to Support Economic and Ecological Transformation in MENA Region
authors: Qasim Ali Raheemah Farttoosi, Sebyan Tareq Saeed Al-aaraji, Abdulrazaq Shabeeb, Faisal Ghazi Faisal, Mohammed Yousif
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study looks into the efforts made in the MENA region to develop a green economy, which spends about 1.2 trillion dollars each year on protecting the environment. These costs could be stopped or used for economic goals and projects that help both the economy and the environment. They would also create wealth and help the economy grow. This research is important because it tries to show the different chances that come with moving towards a green economy, and it also points out the main difficulties and problems that are still stopping the change, compared to what is happening in other countries. The goal is to figure out which priorities should be focused on to get rid of this delay. Research methods used to check how much development has happened—the improvements in how the environment and economy work together on a certain level. The study looks at the results and signs that were found and then gives suggestions based on them.

title: Acoustic Beam Structure Impact on Quasicollinear Acousto-Optic Cell Transmission Functions
authors: Ekaterina I. Kostyleva, Sergey N. Mantsevich
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: An experimental study of the acoustic pulse duration influence on the spatial resolution of ultrasound beam structure visualization in paratellurite crystal quasi-collinear acousto-optic cell (AOC) is presented. Detailed spatial distributions of the acoustic power are obtained over the entire volume of the AOC in the (1-10) plane and in the orthogonal plane using time-resolved acousto-optic (AO) diffraction of a narrow laser beam. It is shown that applying short acoustic pulses with a duration of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mu \mathrm{s}$</tex> provides high-quality images of the acoustic power distribution and enables visualization of small-scale inhomogeneities within the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A O}$</tex> interaction region. Based on the experimental data, the impact of inhomogeneous acoustic beam structure, acoustic power absorption in paratellurite, and temperature gradients on the shape of the quasi-collinear AO cell transmission function is analyzed. The obtained results are important for optimizing quasi-collinear AO filters and dispersion delay lines used for multi-channel spectral filtering and femtosecond laser pulse shaping.

title: A Large Language Model Framework for Automatic PLC Control Logic Generation from Natural Language
authors: Surya B, Ram K Shivany
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The use of IEC 61131-3 programming languages to implement programmable logic controller (PLC) control logic is commonly used in industrial automation systems. Traditional PLC programming however, involves specialized skills and high levels of manual programming and may result in higher development time and possible errors in programming. This research project offers a proposal of AutoCode AI, an intelligent system that will produce IEC 61131-3 compliant control logic based on natural language specifications. It has a refined LLaMA-3.1-8B language model, structural prompt engineering, syntax verification, safety verification, and simulation-based testing modules. Experimental analysis reveals that the suggested framework attains 96.8 % accuracy in generation of control logic when it comes to low complexity tasks, 95.2 % control logic generation when it comes to tasks of medium complexity, and 91.6 % on highly complex industrial control situations. The system also saves on 63.8 % of the time spent in developing PLC whilst achieving a safety verification accuracy of 97.5 %. These findings suggest that the effectiveness and the reliability of AI-based automation programming are highly effective and efficient in the development process as opposed to conventional programming. The paper concludes that the AutoCode AI can be a helpful intelligent assistant in programming the industrial PLC. The future will be based on the inclusion of digital twin environments, the extension of multi-language PLC support, and the reinforcement learning to optimize the adaptive control logic.

title: Modern Challenges of Integrating Artificial Intelligence into Educational Processes
authors: Ekaterina Orlova, Nikita Proshkin, Ekaterina Terekhova, Ruslan Rogachev, Tatiana Lugovaia, Igor Ryabov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into educational processes has acquired strategic significance amid digital transformation, with 83% of Russian higher education institutions already implementing AI technologies and the educational AI solutions market exceeding RUB 5 billion. This study aims to systematize contemporary challenges in AI adoption within Russian higher education, addressing a critical gap in comprehensive analysis at the intersection of pedagogical, ethical, and organizational dimensions. A systems approach was employed, incorporating statistical data analysis, case studies from leading national universities, and sociological research. This enabled the identification of key challenges across four domains: ethical and legal regulation, human resource capacity, faculty adaptation, and financial constraints. The study quantitatively assessed the severity of these challenges and analyzed associated risks for learners, including technological dependency and erosion of critical thinking skills. A suite of strategies for effective AI integration is proposed, emphasizing the targeted use of AI as a complement—not a substitute—for independent student work, training in the critical evaluation of AI-generated outputs, and the development of localized department-level guidelines. The findings lay the groundwork for a balanced approach to AI integration that fosters an ethical, human-centered digital educational environment.

title: Parameter Optimization and Multi-Scheme Comparison of a Lunar Lifting Platform
authors: Yipeng Zhang, Zhengfeng Zhang, Yanhong Zheng, Tianyi Zhang, Jiayi Fu, Kai Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: The development and utilization of lunar resources are of great significance for human sustainable development, while the cargo handling capacity of lunar materials directly affects the efficiency of research station construction. This study focuses on the parameter optimization design of lunar surface lifting platforms, with particular emphasis on analyzing the impact of characteristic length and characteristic height on capture range, placement range, and structural weight. Mathematical models are established for optimization analysis and design. The study compares the schemes of boom/cable hybrid cranes and tower cranes, finding that tower cranes exhibit greater advantages under low-load and small-size conditions. Additionally, considering the unique characteristics of the lunar environment, the study highlights the need to address issues such as structural stiffness, deformation control, and dynamic stability. It also explores the extended functions of the lifting platform, providing technical support for the future construction of lunar research stations.

title: Development of the Hardware and Software Complex "Assistant Speech Therapist"
authors: Elena Sergeevna Lysenko, Vladimir Lvovich Simonov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article presents the development and implementation of a software and hardware complex for speech therapy practice, namely, for objective speech correction. The development of the complex was carried out within the framework of the educational and research laboratory of the department. The key functional capabilities of the complex are outlined, including pronunciation analysis, visualization of correct pronunciation (graphics), and the performance of breathing exercises using the modified "Disc-turntable" probe substitute. This software and hardware complex supports complex correction of speech breathing and sound reproduction based on their synchronous monitoring. Biofeedback allows monitoring of breath control and sound proper articulation with sensors, while graph review and outcome assessment provide interactive feedback. The use of the software and hardware complex "Assistant Speech Therapist" increases the motivation of patients and increases the efficiency of speech therapist work. This hardware complex was tested in a speech therapy center for people of different ages. The results confirmed the benefits of using the complex in speech therapy practice. The complex is based on the Arduino microcontroller platform. The complex can be finalized and scaled as part of further research for implementation in educational and medical institutions.

title: Inverse design photonic couplers with machine learning tools
authors: Shuwei Guo, Pingfan Wu
publish: 2026 35th Wireless and Optical Communications Conference (WOCC)
abstract: We present an inverse design framework that explicitly incorporates the fabrication constraints. We introduce a titanium dioxide (TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) based height varying metasurface, as a visible-light color router for CMOS image sensor. Besides superior color splitting and light concentrating capabilities, this height-varying metasurface also performs like an anti-reflection coating.

title: Mapping Halal Integrity Risks in Halal Fashion Supply Chains: A Risk-Based Resilience Framework
authors: Aisya Nadiefa Shabira, Nabila Noor Qisthani, Syarif Hidayatuloh
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Halal fashion supply chains face unique integrity risks arising from material ambiguity, chemical processing, and operational handling practices that are often overlooked in conventional resilience studies. Existing halal supply chain research has predominantly focused on the food sector and certification governance, leaving the operational risk structures in textile manufacturing insufficiently explored. This study aims to map halal integrity risks and develop a risk-based resilience framework for halal fashion supply chains. A case-based analytical design was employed, integrating SCOR-based supply chain mapping, expert-driven risk identification, and a structured risk assessment supported by the House of Risk approach. Data were collected through field observation, expert interviews, and severity-occurrence questionnaires to identify and prioritize risk agents across supply chain stages. The results reveal that material origin risk, process contamination risk, and handling and storage risk are the dominant categories affecting halal integrity, with the Make and Source stages exhibiting the highest vulnerability levels. High-priority risk agents were translated into resilience capabilities, including anticipation, absorption, recovery, and adaptation, forming a structured resilience-oriented framework. The findings highlight that halal integrity should be treated not only as compliance requirement but also as a structural driver of supply chain resilience. This study contributes to halal fashion research by offering an operational perspective that integrates risk mapping with resilience theory, providing practical insights to improve supplier governance, production control, and adaptive capability development in halal textile manufacturing environments.

title: Virtual Reality (VR) Integration in Teaching English to Technical University Students
authors: Zh. Yarullina, V. Martynov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This article explores the potential of using virtual and augmented reality technologies in teaching English to university students. Several examples of using these technologies in English language teaching described in various studies are analyzed. The study identifies the main advantages and disadvantages of using virtual reality in English language teaching. The practice of virtual reality integration in teaching English to the students at a technical university is described in detail. Some examples of created assignments using VR videos are provided. To identify the main advantages of VR integration a survey of technical university students was conducted. The results indicate that virtual reality integration in English language teaching to technical university students improves their academic performance, motivation, interest, and engagement in learning English.

title: Gated Cross-Attention Fusion: An Interpretable Multi-Modal Framework for Intelligent Healthcare Diagnostics
authors: K. Baby, S. Kaliappan, Jayapal Lande, Yashasvi Saini
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The growth in the proliferation of heterogeneous healthcare data, such as medical imaging, electronic health records (EHRs), physiological signals, and clinical text data, offers a major opportunity in improving diagnostic precision. However, conventional unimodal and late-fusion models fail to account for the complex and complementary interactions between these different sources of data, and they lack the interpretability essential for clinical adoption. This paper aims to propose a novel, interpretable multi-modal deep learning framework to perform intelligent healthcare diagnostics. The architecture employs modality-specific encoders - hybrid CNNVision Transformers for imaging, Transformer-based networks for structured EHRs, 1D-CNN-BiLSTMs for physiological signals, and ClinicalBERT for free-text - to learn modalityspecific representations. These are integrated via a Gated CrossAttention Fusion (GCAF) mechanism that adaptively weights each modality according to perceived reliability to ensure robust performance with noisy/missing data. To build clinical trust, the framework includes unified interpretability (Grad-CAM, SHAP, and temporal saliency) and calibrated uncertainty estimation (temperature scaling). Experimental results using stratified 5-fold cross validation show that the mean diagnostic accuracy is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$96.4 \% \pm 0.7$</tex>, macro-F1 is 95.7, and AUC is 0.982. This approach greatly surpasses unimodal and late-fusion baselines towards a transparent and high-performance basis for next-generation clinical decision support systems.

title: EdgeFishCam: An Edge AI-based Fish Detection Through YOLO and Image Enhancement Techniques
authors: Nurul Asmi Amalia, Kahlil Muchtar, Hizir Sofyan, Hammam Riza, Muhammad Subianto, Maya Fitria
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Underwater fish detection is a key task in marine monitoring and aquaculture, but its performance is often degraded by low illumination, turbidity, scattering, and color distortion in underwater environments. This paper presents an underwater fish detection framework that integrates CLAHEbased image enhancement with YOLOv3 object detection and demonstrates its deployment on an edge AI platform using a Streamlit-based interface. The DeepFish dataset (6,517 images with 15,463 annotated fish) was used for training and evaluation, while a local Indonesian dataset from Sabang, Aceh (3,111 annotated images) was employed to evaluate crossdomain generalization. Several CLAHE variants were quantitatively compared using UIQM, UCIQE, and LOE, with Blending CLAHE combined with Percentile Stretching selected as the optimal pre-processing method. Detection performance was evaluated under multiple scenarios, including training and testing on original and enhanced images, direct cross-domain testing, and fine-tuning on local data. Experimental results show that image enhancement improves detection accuracy on DeepFish (mAP@0.5 from 96.15% to 97.05%), while fine-tuning is necessary to mitigate domain shift and achieve reliable performance on local waters. The proposed end-to-end system was successfully deployed on a Jetson Orin Nano and operates entirely offline, providing real-time detection results through a web-based interface.

title: Voice-based Early Parkinson’s Disease Prediction using Multi-Modal Wavelet–Deep Learning Fusion
authors: Veena B
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Early and accurate detection of Parkinson’s disease represents challenges since the related vocal impairments present subtle changes along with progressive degradation. Traditional machine learning methods rely heavily on handcrafted acoustic features and fail to model deep signal-level variations. This work presents a novel multi-modal wavelet-deep learning fusion model for accurate PD prediction using the publicly available UCI/Kaggle Parkinson’s voice dataset. First, the raw biomedical voice features are normalized and transformed into one-dimensional signals, from which the discriminative CNDWT features are extracted using Haar decomposition with db3 reconstruction. In parallel, the standardized raw features are fed through a lightweight 1D CNN to learn deep temporal representations. Finally, a fusion layer combines the raw features, wavelet-derived statistical coefficients, and CNN embeddings to allow the model to capture both global and localized voice distortions associated with PD. Experiments conducted on a benchmark dataset have demonstrated that the proposed hybrid architecture significantly outperforms traditional classifiers in terms of high accuracy, robust generalization, and improved sensitivity toward early-stage PD samples. This multimodal fusion approach has proved that wavelet-based signal decomposition combined with deep neural feature extraction is an effective method for clinical decision support and early Parkinson’s disease screening.

title: Digital Transformation of Engineering Education as a Driver of the Regional Innovation System
authors: Nikita Proshkin, Oleg Bucnev
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This article examines the problem of digital transformation in engineering education as a strategic tool for integrating universities into regional innovation systems. A conceptual model of an innovation ecosystem is proposed, based on the synergistic interaction of key stakeholders: students and graduate students, faculty and researchers, as well as business representatives. The architecture of a specialized digital platform is developed, integrating the management of intellectual property rights (IPR), interaction with industry partners, and student project activities into a unified ecosystem. The platform is implemented using modern technologies (Vue.js, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Redis) and includes intelligent services based on large language models to automate innovation commercialization processes. Implementing the platform will overcome key barriers between academia and business, reduce the time-to-market for innovations, and increase the economic return on scientific research. The research results form a theoretical foundation for transforming universities into economic drivers of regional development through the systematic integration of knowledge, technologies, and human capital.

title: Deep Learning Framework for Predictive Smart Tourism Management with Multi-Stakeholder Decision Support
authors: Salman Latheef T A, Dharaneeshwar S, Abisheak Ananthan S, Pravin P
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The destinations in tourism are becoming more dangerously affected by difficulties connected with the appearance of considerable numbers of visitors which are unexpected and damages the infrastructure and jeopardizes the security of the tourists at the peak season. Traditional tourism management standards are based on past records and by hand survey which are not real-time and predictive. This paper presents an online management web application of Smart Tourism, which is a role-based, AI-powered digital ecosystem where three autonomous portals are considered: Tourists, Tourist Guides and Service Providers, and Government Officials. The system is a combination of machine learning-based predicting tourist footfall, classifying congestions, planning a tourist itinerary personally, and analyzing infrastructure adequacy to justify sustainable tourism governance. Various sources of data are examined, such as the transportation patterns, the presence of accommodation, weather patterns, and seasonal activities to predict the inflow of tourists and show the regions of high risk congestion. Explainable algorithms are also used so as to have transparency in decision-making. The system is applicable to smart cities and large-scale tourism ecosystems because the experimental analysis leads to prediction accuracy and high efficiency of operation than the conventional tourism management methods.

title: Development of an Intelligent Robotic System for Automatic Assembly of Composite Structures within the Framework of Industry 4.0
authors: Daniil Zubrilchev, Igor Merkuryev, Ivan Merkuryev
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This article presents a new project-oriented methodology for training students focused on developing intelligent robotic systems equipped with optoelectronic, force/torque, and tactile sensing capabilities for performing precision assembly operations in fully automatic mode. The methodology relies heavily on fundamental engineering education that integrates analytical and numerical modeling using domestic software packages Fidesys and Simintech. These tools enable the analysis of system dynamics, stress-strain states of structural elements, and motion control implementation under conditions closely approximating real-world scenarios. Drawing on the analysis of a specific automatic assembly task for complex parts, the article outlines a training pathway for highly qualified specialists, encompassing stages of engineering design, mathematical modeling, virtual commissioning, and experimental validation of the new robotic complex. Particular emphasis is placed on developing students' system integration skills, which are essential for creating robots capable of replacing human labor in routine, monotonous operations.

title: The Role of Augmented Reality in Fostering E-Buying Decision of Fashion Products of Gen Z in Vietnam
authors: Tuan-Kiet H.V. Truong, Uyen Thi Thao Tieu
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study investigates how AR-enabled digital marketing influences the online fashion purchase decisions of Generation Z in Can Tho City, Vietnam, with a particular focus on the mediating effect of Perceived Hedonic Value (PHV). Grounded in the S-O-R framework, the research conceptualizes AR-enabled digital marketing as the integration of features like virtual try-on and interactive visualization into digital campaigns. Data collected from 337 Gen Z participants were analyzed using SPSS 24.0 and AMOS 24.0 through Cronbach's alpha, EFA, CFA, and Structural Equation Modelling (SEM). The findings demonstrated a good model fit (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\chi}^{\mathrm{2}} / \text{d f} \boldsymbol{=} \text{2. 5 3 7}$</tex>; CFI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.926$</tex>; RMSEA <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.068$</tex>) and revealed that the Novelty of AR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\boldsymbol{\beta}=0.138)$</tex>, the Informativeness of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{A R}({\beta}=0.283)$</tex>, and AR-enabled Digital Marketing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta=0.289)$</tex> exert positive and significant effects on PHV. Notably, PHV robustly impacts Buying Decision <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\boldsymbol{\beta}=0.712)$</tex>, affirming that emotional engagement serves as the primary motivator behind Gen Z's shopping behavior. This study extends prior research on AR-assisted online shopping by examining AR-enabled digital marketing in the online fashion context of Gen Z consumers in Can Tho City, Vietnam. In this study, AR-enabled digital marketing refers to the integration of AR features, such as virtual try-on, interactive product visualization, and immersive promotional content, into digital campaigns to enhance consumer engagement and shopping experience. The study contributes by testing the mediating role of perceived hedonic value within the S-O-R framework in an emerging-market setting and by distinguishing the effects of novelty, informativeness, and AR-enabled digital marketing on buying decision.

title: Negation-Aware Test-Time Adaptation for Vision-Language Models
authors: Haochen Han, Alex Jinpeng Wang, Fangming Liu, Jun Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: In this paper, we study a practical but less-touched problem in Vision-Language Models (VLMs), i.e., negation understanding. Specifically, many real-world applications require models to explicitly identify what is false or non-existent, e.g., radiologists may search for images that exclude specific conditions. Despite the impressive transferability of VLMs through large-scale training, they suffer from a critical limitation that fails to handle negation. To address this challenge, existing methods attribute its root cause to the scarcity of negation training data and propose to fine-tune VLMs on massive data containing explicit negation. Undoubtedly, such data-centric solutions demand substantial data and computational resources, limiting their sustainable widespread adoption. To tackle negation in a low-carbon manner, we empirically observe that the key obstacle lies in the dual-concept shifts between the affirmation and negation distributions. Therefore, we propose a Negation-Aware Test-Time Adaptation (NEAT) method to efficiently adjust distribution-related parameters during inference. In brief, NEAT can reduce distribution shift in consistent semantics while eliminating false distributional consistency in unrelated semantics. Extensive experiments on the various negation understanding tasks verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. Remarkably, with less than 0.01% of trainable parameters, NEAT achieves comparable or superior performance to state-of-the-art post-training approaches. Our code is available at https://github.com/hhc1997/NEAT.

title: Active Magnetic Bearing PID Implicit-Model Optimization Approach Based on a Biobjective UCB-Controlled Heuristic Algorithm
authors: Mingqu Zhou, Yixuan Shuai, Dong Jiang, Zicheng Liu
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Active magnetic bearing is an advanced bearing system with the advantage of no contact for high-speed rotation systems. The performance of the active magnetic bearing (AMB) system is greatly influenced by the parameters of the widely used PID displacement controller. However, existing PID optimization methods for the AMB system greatly rely on the accurate AMB system model, which limits their applications and makes the PID optimization process time-consuming. In this article, an implicit-model optimization approach is proposed to solve this problem, which consists of the basic heuristic algorithm, the top control algorithm based on the sliding-window upper confidence bound algorithm, and a rapid metrics measurement approach. The proposed optimization approach can optimize PID parameters according to both the widely used metric sensitivity and the metric current-compliance, relating to the displacements during rotations, without any AMB system parameters. The proposed optimization approach is tested on benchmarks to validate its ability to avoid local Pareto frontiers and maintain measurement accuracy with higher efficiency. Also, the proposed optimization approach is applied to a real AMB motor. Compared to the baseline and the results from the grid search, the proposed optimization approach performs better in the metrics optimization and the efficiency across 20 experiments on average.

title: ST-HMR: A Robust Collision Detection Framework for Collaborative Robots Based on Dual-Domain Interaction and Manifold Regularization
authors: Chenyang Song, Shuwang Rui, Yechen Fan, Jie Zheng, Zhiguo Yang, Yixuan Wang, Jiange Kou, Liman Yang, Yan Shi
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: As physical human–robot interaction (pHRI) becomes increasingly prevalent in unstructured industrial environments, ensuring contact safety under complex dynamics remains a critical challenge. External sensors can detect contact effectively, but their cost and integration complexity limit industrial scalability. Sensorless approaches are more economical, yet many data-driven methods ignore frequency-domain mechanical noise, rely on single-scale temporal modeling, and remain vulnerable to unknown anomalies. To address these issues, this article proposes ST-HMR, a sensorless collision-detection framework that relies exclusively on intrinsic proprioceptive signals, including joint angles, joint velocities, and measured joint motor currents. The framework is organized around three interrelated components: ST-GIM derives frequency-aware gates from each proprioceptive window to suppress drivetrain noise, dilated temporal pyramid aggregation aggregates short- and long-range temporal responses for both abrupt impacts and compliant contacts, and hypersphere manifold regularization constructs a compact normal-operation manifold for robust anomaly scoring. Experiments on a UR5e robot show that ST-HMR achieves 94.75% accuracy and an AUC of 0.9814 while maintaining specificity above 96%, providing a practical sensorless solution for industrial robot safety.

title: SP-FSOD: Semantic-Visual Purification Guided Few-shot Object Detection on Remote Sensing Images
authors: Jingzheng Tu, Yan Yan, Ying Lu, Cailian Chen, Xinping Guan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Few-shot object detection (FSOD) on remote sensing images is a promising technology for remote sensing applications, as it enhances generalization capability. Most works are devoted to excavating discriminative and compact visual feature representations for FSOD, without considering other domain knowledge. With the rapid development of large language models, promoting FSOD performance with the help of large language models becomes possible. However, the domain gap between text and image is unignorable. In this paper, we propose a semantic-visual purification guided FSOD method for remote sensing images (SP-FSOD) to improve performance. Firstly, a feature purification memory is designed to store old knowledge. Secondly, a semanticvisual triplet loss is proposed to alleviate the domain discrepancy between text and image. Thirdly, a flexible fusion module is presented to fuse the feature maps of the semantic and visual domains adaptively. Experiments on large-scale public datasets demonstrate that the proposed SP-FSOD method achieves the state-of-the-art performance on three-shot, five-shot, and ten-shot settings.

title: Knowledge-Grounded Nut Inspection and Report Generation via Two-Stage Retrieval-Augmented Language Models
authors: Kuei-Jung Hung, Peng Wen Lai, Bing-Fei Wu, Jau-Woei Perng
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Nut Inspection; Multi-Scale CNN; Large Language Models; Vision Language Models; Retrieval-Augmented Generation

title: Symbolic Formal Analysis of Quantum-Resistant Messaging Protocols Based on the Split KEM Mechanism
authors: Katarina Boras, Ante Derek
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Quantum computers, growing only stronger with time, are threatening to break all asymmetric cryptography that is in use today. To mitigate such a devastating scenario, the cryptographic community is putting great effort into developing and evaluating quantum-resistant (post-quantum) primitives and protocols. An important part of the evaluation of such protocols is formal analysis using automated tools such as the Tamarin prover, used to formally prove security properties of a given protocol. Signal is an instant messaging protocol used by millions of people every day. In this paper, we use Tamarin to model and formally verify the security properties of Signal’s session-establishing subprotocol, X3DH, and two proposed post-quantum variants of X3DH, both based on a new notion of the split KEM mechanism. Using our new generic model for X3DH, we are able to construct and directly compare the security levels of the three protocols. We find that the protocols have differing levels of authentication and impersonation resistance; namely, the two proposed post-quantum solutions do not reach the levels of authentication of X3DH in the symbolic model. We discuss the reasons for this and offer solutions to mitigate the issue. We also explore and scrutinize the real-life consequences of these vulnerabilities.

title: Unnoticeable Community Deception Based on Comprehensive Modularity
authors: Li Ni, Haoran Chen, Lin Mu, Yiwen Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Existing community deception methods focus on perturbing edges to hide target communities, but they often overlook the unnoticeability of perturbations. Such neglect makes the attacks susceptible to anomaly detection. Therefore, we propose the “unnoticeable community deception” problem, which aims to hide the target community while requiring perturbations to be unnoticeable. To address this, we introduce local community deception based on comprehensive modularity (LCDC). Specifically, LCDC first constrains the perturbation scope to ensure newly added edges are indistinguishable from existing inter-community edges, thereby enhancing unnoticeability. LCDC uses only local information within the perturbation scope, without requiring access to the entire network. It then employs a perturbation cost to guide the selection of edges for addition or deletion. This cost is formulated as a weighted combination of comprehensive modularity and a node-level tendency to depart. Comprehensive modularity combines global and local components: global modularity ensures the overall network structure within the perturbation scope remains stable before and after perturbation, while local modularity aims to disperse nodes from the target community into other groups. LCDC only accesses the target community and its neighboring communities, not the entire network. Experimental results show that LCDC outperforms baseline methods in terms of unnoticeability and is competitive with them in terms of hiding effectiveness.

title: Edge-VisForeCast: Energy-Efficient Predictive Autoscaling for Containerized Crowd Analytics on Constrained IoT Edge Clusters
authors: Chandra Wijaya, Feng-Wei Hsu, I-Jan Wang, Chao-Tung Yang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The default Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) in Kubernetes is inherently reactive, often taking 15–30 seconds to respond to workload changes on resource-constrained edge devices. In latency-sensitive applications such as real-time crowd analytics, these delays frequently cause thermal throttling and QoS violations during sudden surges in crowd density. To overcome this limitation, we propose Edge-VisForeCast, a proactive autoscaling framework that leverages YOLOv11-detected crowd density as a Granger-causal leading indicator of future resource demand. By integrating these application-level insights with a quantized Informer transformer model enhanced by a Vision-Aware Attention mechanism, the system can forecast power consumption 5–15 seconds ahead. Granger causality tests (p < 0.001) confirm that person count statistically precedes CPU and power saturation by 10–15 seconds. We deployed the proposed system on a 3-node NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano cluster at the Tunghai University Library, using a real-world dataset of 39.01 million raw frames collected over 14 days. Experimental results show that Edge-VisForeCast achieves 17.8% better forecasting accuracy (MAE) than LSTM baselines, while delivering a 23.7% reduction in inference latency and a 36.3% reduction in energy consumption. Most importantly, the system maintains stable operation within the 15W thermal envelope, effectively avoiding the throttling issues commonly observed with reactive HPA. This work demonstrates the value of bridging application semantics with system orchestration for energy-efficient predictive autoscaling in IoT edge environments.

title: A Multifunctional Grid Connected Electric Vehicle Charging Station with Photovoltaic System Support
authors: C M Nirmal Mukundan, Mohamed El-Moursi, Ahmed Al Durra, Tarek H. M. EL-Fouly
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: The rapid proliferation of electric vehicle charging stations (EVCSs) introduces significant power quality concerns, including harmonics, DC offsets, current imbalance, reactive power, and fluctuating power demand. Existing control approaches for EVCS-grid integration often rely on complex controls and limited filtering, resulting in slower dynamic response and insufficient harmonic suppression. To address these gaps, this work proposes a generalized integral-based current controller with dual tuning parameters for seamless EVCS-grid integration. The proposed controller eliminates frequency tracking requirements, thereby achieving fast transient response while ensuring robust harmonic filtering. Stability is analytically verified using the Routh-Hurwitz criterion, with filtering performance validated through Bode and pole-zero analysis. Numerical results from simulations and a 1 kVA hardware prototype confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method: grid current’s total harmonic distortion (THD) is reduced to 3.4%, DC offset is completely eliminated, current imbalance and reactive power are suppressed, and a grid power factor of 0.99 is maintained under distorted grid conditions. Furthermore, a Hermite interpolation-assisted incremental conductance-based maximum power point tracking (MPPT)algorithm ensures optimal photovoltaic (PV) power extraction with 98% efficiency across different operating modes. These results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively enhances grid power quality while enabling reliable power management in EVCS-integrated photovoltaic systems.

title: NormalMVS: Learning Efficient Geometry-Aware Multiview Stereo with Surface Normal Priors
authors: Xingchen Lv, Qingjie Zhao, Lei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Imaging
abstract: Learning-based multiview stereo (MVS) has achieved remarkable progress, largely driven by cascade-based architectures. However, existing methods inevitably suffer from error accumulation across stages because of simple upsampling. Furthermore, relying solely on RGB images often leads to photometric ambiguity in textureless or non-Lambertian regions and a lack of explicit 3D constraints, resulting in erroneous correspondences. To address these challenges, we propose NormalMVS, an efficient framework that explicitly incorporates surface normal priors to enhance reconstruction quality. Specifically, we design a geometry-aware fusion (GAF) module that synergistically combines RGB and normal features and extracts discriminative geometric cues to resolve photometric ambiguities. To mitigate error propagation, we design a normal-guided depth refinement (NGDR) module. Unlike conventional bilinear interpolation, NGDR refines upsampled depth maps on the basis of local coplanarity and is supervised by a novel neighbor-weighted consistency loss that enforces geometric coherence. Additionally, a four-stage architecture and a sparse sampling strategy are introduced to significantly reduce the computational and memory overhead of 3D CNNs. Extensive experiments on the DTU, Tanks & Temples, and ETH3D benchmarks demonstrate that NormalMVS outperforms most existing methods in terms of efficiency, effectively balancing reconstruction quality and resource consumption.

title: An Electromagnetic-Thermal Collaborative Design Framework for Patch Antennas
authors: Zu-Qin Tong, Wei Shao, Xiao Zheng, Haiyan Ou, Li-Ye Xiao
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The rapid design of patch antennas faces strict electromagnetic (EM) and thermal constraints due to EM‑thermal coupling. In this letter, a machine learning framework based on multilayer perceptron (MLP) Mixer and U-Net is proposed for the EM-thermal collaborative design of patch antennas. The framework uses MLP-Mixer to reversely generate the antenna topology from the EM response and then quickly predicts its surface temperature distribution through U-Net, achieving a coherent mapping from the EM index to the structure and then to the heat distribution. In the design process, both the EM and thermal performance metrics are simultaneously considered. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated through two numerical examples, including generation of training datasets, and comparison with the multi-objective optimization algorithm. The designed patch antenna is fabricated and both EM responses and thermal field distribution are measured to further verify the performance of the proposed framework.

title: Expanding Intra-modal and Compressing Inter-modal Embedding Space for Incomplete Multimodal Emotion Recognition
authors: Zhibin Wu, Wenhao Li, Zheyun Qin, Qiangchang Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Multimodal Emotion Recognition (MER) often suffers from incomplete modality inputs caused by sensor failures or privacy constraints. Recent studies generally fall into two categories: reconstruction-based methods and joint representation learning. The former recovers missing modalities from available ones, while the latter projects modalities into a shared latent space. However, these methods remain sensitive to single-modality variations and struggle to achieve effective alignment due to the gap between homogeneous intra-modal and heterogeneous inter-modal embedding spaces. To address these limitations, we propose a novel ECI framework that simultaneously Expands intra-modal and Compresses Inter-modal embedding spaces. ECI consists of three key components: Inter-modal Volume Alignment (IVA), HyperGraph-augmented Self-attention (HGS), and Intra-modal Distribution Normalization (IDN). Firstly, IVA reduces geometric inconsistency by minimizing the kernelized volume they form, encouraging compact and well-aligned cross-modal distributions. Secondly, HGS integrates hypergraph convolution with self-attention to capture high-order dependencies and pairwise interactions, enabling effective reconstruction of missing modalities. Finally, IDN applies contrastive suppression to expand feature distributions within intra-modal spaces to avoid overfitting and strengthen the discriminative power of each modality. Consequently, ECI achieves global alignment and complementary information flow while enhancing individual modality discriminability. Experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that ECI achieves state-of-the-art performance across various missing modality scenarios.

title: Federated Unlearning Activated Backdoor Attacks
authors: Jian Chen, Wenlong Shi, Chengyu Hu, Jianfeng Lu, Ahmed M. Abdelmoniem, Chen Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Federated unlearning (FU) has recently emerged as a promising paradigm for removing client data from trained models, enabling privacy preservation and regulatory compliance in federated learning (FL) systems. However, existing FU methods primarily focus on the efficiency of data removal, leaving security vulnerabilities largely unexplored. In this paper, we propose FedUBA, a <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Fed</u>erated <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">U</u>nlearning activated <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</u>ackdoor <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>ttack, which exploits the unlearning process itself to stealthily trigger backdoor behaviors. Unlike traditional backdoor attacks that embed backdoors via direct data poisoning during the learning process, FedUBA exploits the post-training unlearning process to covertly embed backdoor behaviors. The core of FedUBA lies in misleading the global model to unlearn more information associated with influential samples for the backdoored samples than intended, thereby adversely affecting the backdoored samples. To achieve this, FedUBA employs a principled three-stage framework, which involves generating stealthy backdoor triggers, selecting influential samples with the greatest impact on backdoored samples' predictions via black box sensitivity-based analysis, and crafting malicious unlearning requests to induce the global model into activating the backdoor behavior. By doing so, we can significantly alter predictions on backdoored samples by initiating malicious unlearning requests. Extensive experiments on five realistic datasets demonstrate that FedUBA effectively achieves an 80% attack success rate on backdoored samples by triggering only 0.5% malicious unlearning requests.

title: Differential Random Fault Attack of Ultra-Lightweight Cipher ANU for Industrial Internet
authors: Zhongya Zhang, Jingqi Wei, Zhiyong Zhang, Lili Zhang, Han Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Lightweight ciphers are widely used in the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), covering data encryption, communication encryption, and authenticated encryption. Due to their efficiency and low resource consumption, these cryptographic algorithms have become core solutions for network security, yet they still face security challenges. The ANU algorithm, characterized by its small hardware implementation area and low power consumption, is suitable for securing the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT). In this article, we propose two differential fault attack methods for ANU based on the random nibble and byte models, respectively. Theoretically, the two attacks require 82.4 and 41.2 faults respectively to recover the master key. Simulation results show that the two attacks require 85.66 and 42.86 faults, respectively. The proposed attacks demonstrate significant improvements in terms of analysis difficulty, model applicability, and the number of faults required, offering a new perspective for algorithm security research and evaluation in IIoT environments.

title: IEEE Standard for Local and Metropolitan Area Networks--Timing and Synchronization for Time-Sensitive Applications Amendment 2: Support for the IEEE Std 802.3 Clause 4 Media Access Control (MAC) operating in half-duplex
authors: N/A
publish: N/A
abstract: This amendment to IEEE Std 802.1AS™-2025 specifies protocols, procedures, and managed objects that support IEEE Std 802.3 Clause 4 Media Access Control (MAC) operating in half-duplex while retaining existing functionality and backward compatibility, and remaining a profile of IEEE Std 1588™-2019.

title: PurifyFD: Mining Latent Domains for Trustworthy Single-Domain Fault Diagnosis Under Open-World Shifts
authors: Bowen Yu, Zhonglei Shen, Donghai Han, Shaocheng Kong, Zhibin Zhao, Liuyang Zhang, Ruqiang Yan, Xuefeng Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Single-domain generalization (SDG) is critical for Industrial Internet of Things edge scenarios where only limited single-source data are available. Existing SDG approaches predominantly rely on generative models or heavy augmentation to synthesize auxiliary domains, which often introduce unrealistic artifacts and training instability. In contrast, we articulate that single-source industrial data inherently contain latent nonstationarities (e.g., temporal drifts) that can be mined rather than synthesized. Based on this insight, we propose PurifyFD, a trustworthy and edge-efficient framework that reframes SDG as a feature purification problem. Specifically, we devise a closed-loop max–min adversarial game: the maximization step automatically amplifies the worst-case discrepancies of mined latent domains, while the minimization step suppresses feature sensitivity to these shifts via spatiotemporal disentanglement. This process yields purified representations with high intra-class compactness and shift-invariance. Rather than relying on heavy auxiliary out-of-distribution modules, the resulting compact representation naturally supports lightweight parameter-free energy scoring for more reliable open-set rejection in edge deployment. Extensive experiments on case western reserve university (CWRU), JNU, and a dual-testbed dataset demonstrate that PurifyFD achieves a favorable trade-off among cross-domain accuracy, open-set reliability, and inference efficiency compared to state-of-the-art representative methods.

title: Development and Application of a Fuzzy Logic Toolbox for Teaching the Basics of Fuzzy System Design
authors: Pavel Varshavskii, Aleksei Varshavskii, Savelii Shikhaleev
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper discusses the development and application of a domestic software toolkit (Fuzzy Logic Toolbox) for building and studying fuzzy inference systems in the educational process of the Department of Applied Mathematics and Artificial Intelligence (AM&AI) at NRU "MPEI". The relevance of the work is driven by the need to replace foreign analogues (in particular, the MATLAB Fuzzy Logic Toolbox) and by the need for a beginner-friendly interface for students. The main focus is on developing the user interface (UI) and a setup wizard that guides users step by step through all stages of model creation: selecting and configuring parameters of the fuzzy inference algorithm, defining linguistic variables and membership functions, forming the rule base, and selecting the defuzzification method. Examples of using the developed Fuzzy Logic Toolbox to complete laboratory assignments for the master’s course "Actual Problems of Applied Mathematics and Computer Science" are provided.

title: Development of a Prototype Ka-Band Aircraft Telemetry Transmission System
authors: Anastasiya Y. Kudryashova, Irina S. Kretova, Anastasia V. Ermakova
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: The development and research of a prototype Ka-band aircraft telemetry transmission system using a geostationary satellite is a currently relevant task. This system ensures stable operation in both normal and adverse weather conditions. This study included calculating the key parameters of the telemetry transmission system, simulating the prototype system's operation under various weather conditions using Matlab, analyzing the obtained results, and assessing the technical feasibility of the proposed solutions. The article presents results that are significant for the design of satellite-based aircraft telemetry transmission systems, as well as for the development of advanced aviation, aerospace, information and telecommunications systems.

title: Energy Efficiency Research in Variable Stiffness Actuator-Based Exoskeletons
authors: Yang Liu, Guoyang Hong, Hesheng Han, Jianqing Peng, Lin Liu
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: We present a framework for investigating the intrinsic correlation between load, frequency, joint stiffness and motor energy consumption under multidimensional dynamic conditions to facilitate energy efficiency optimization in a variable stiffness actuator (VSA) exoskeleton. In this study, the focused analysis involves a nonlinear dynamic model of VSA, which can represent the dynamics of the exoskeleton applications. However, existing research on VSA energy efficiency often relies on openloop control or linear model assumptions, where the confounding effects of trajectory tracking performance and nonlinear gravitational torque are frequently neglected. To acquire a precise evaluation benchmark, a closed-loop joint position control strategy is implemented to meet the stand requirement of exoskeleton application. Furthermore, a multidimensional parameter decoupling simulation is conducted to elucidate the distinct influence mechanisms of excitation frequency, gravitational torque, and system inertia on the stiffness-energy consumption patterns and reveal the potential for energy efficiency. Finally, comparative verifications are conducted through practical experiments, confirming the inherent relationship between multidimensional parameters and energy consumption, and thereby clearly demonstrating the significant energy efficiency effectiveness of the proposed stiffness adjustment strategy.

title: Comprehensive Review of Phishing Website Detection using Machine Learning and Deep Learning
authors: Divyanshu Pandey, Gaurav Garg, Shonak Bansal
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Phishing websites remain a popular tool for cybercriminals to steal sensitive information from users through malicious URLs, fake webpages, and misleading communication methods. Since the attackers have significantly upgraded their skills, the traditional rule, based detection methods have failed to cover the problem, so machine learning and deep learning, based solutions have quickly become the main focus. This review paper extensively covers the most recent phishing website detection techniques, especially those using CNN architectures and hybrid intelligent frameworks. Different approaches such as SVM, GWO, CNN, GAN, FCNN, and optimization, based methods like Bat Algorithm and Brown Bear Optimization are discussed and compared with each other, considering accuracy, F1 score, precision, recall, and generalization capability as the main criteria. Fully convolutional neural networks specifically were able to come up with an accuracy as high 99.21%, which was at the top of the methodologies discussed, whereas, domain adaptation models performed better on the out, of, sample URLs. Furthermore, the article suggests that feature selection, attention mechanisms, and explainable AI can be used to achieve both detection transparency and robustness. Some of these research works have demonstrated high performance, however, real, life application problems such as scalability, generalization, interpretability, and computational complexity still remain unsolved.

title: Comparative Analysis of Neural Network Architectures for Pulmonary Pathology Classification on X-Rays
authors: Viktoriya V. Bozhenko
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This study presents a comparative analysis of the performance of various deep learning architectures for the classification of pulmonary diseases based on chest X-ray images. The study was conducted using a public dataset, and several popular architectures were selected for comparison. Model performance was evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. The training and prediction times were also evaluated. The research confirms the applicability of such models in the classifying X-ray images.

title: An Overview of Low-Rank Structures in the Training and Adaptation of Large Models: From Implicit Low-Dimensionality to Efficient Training and Fine-Tuning [Special Issue on the Mathematics of Deep Learning]
authors: Laura Balzano, Tianjiao Ding, Benjamin D. Haeffele, Soo Min Kwon, Qing Qu, Peng Wang, Zhangyang Wang, Can Yaras
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Magazine
abstract: The advent of deep learning has immeasurably changed the ways we process data in signal processing and machine learning. However, training and deploying modern deep learning models demand substantial computational resources, raising concerns about exorbitant training costs, GPU shortages, and heightened energy consumption. Several lines of research over the last decade have explored the emergence of low-dimensional structures during the training process, where basic elements such as weight matrices and representations tend to be approximately low rank even though not explicitly trained to be. These low-dimensional structures arise in part due to the implicit bias of the methods used to train deep networks, providing the potential to partially explain why deep models need fewer samples than the number of model parameters. This implicit low dimensionality has inspired the exploration of low-rank structures in training and fine-tuning large-scale deep learning models more efficiently. In this article, we review recent exciting advances in using low-rank structure in deep learning and aim to clarify the mathematical foundations underlying their design. Specifically, we highlight key insights from a rich line of research focused on theoretically understanding and leveraging low-rank structures in deep learning both at every iteration of training as well as at the global minimum.

title: An Intelligent and Scalable Machine Learning Approach for Food Recipe Rating Systems
authors: Joshi Rajeshwari, Kanneboina Ashok, Ramya Sri Kotta, Thota Anitha, Adlakadi Anand
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The fast growth of online food preparation websites has led to huge amounts of recipe and user information, which opened up the necessity of smart systems that can effectively predict food preparation ratings. This paper presents a machine learning-based program of smart food recipes ratings prediction on the Food.com Recipes and Reviews dataset in Kaggle. The framework combines the systematic data preprocessing, feature engineering, and learners in a supervised manner to improve predictive reliability and scalability. There were three popular Machine Learning (ML) algorithms such as Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) being implemented and tested in the Jupyter Notebook with the help of Python and Scikit-learn. The evaluation has been performed in terms of Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-Score with a growing sample size of 1000 up to 5000 instances. The experimental findings indicate that the Random Forest model is always able to perform better than SVM and XGBoost in all the metrics of evaluation. RF had an Accuracy of 94.8%, Precision of 94.3%, Recall of 95% and F1-Score of 94.6% at 5000 samples. Random Forest (RF) outperformed SVM with improvements of 5.3% in Accuracy, 5.9% in Precision, 7.0% in Recall, and 6.4% in F1-Score. Similarly, compared to XGBoost, RF achieved performance gains of 3.1% in Accuracy, 3.1% in Precision, 4.2% in Recall, and 3.6% in F1-Score. These results verify that the ensemble-based learning offers better scalability, strength and balanced classification capabilities of large-scale culinary data. The proposed architecture is a competent and effective solution to intelligent food recommendation and rating prediction systems.
