title: The Effects of AI-Based Product Recommendations and Targeted Ads on Impulse Buying Behavior on E-Commerce Platforms in Vietnam
authors: Hong Minh Ngoc Tran, Anh Phan Ngoc Que
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study examines the role that artificial intelligence based marketing features play in impulse buying behavior in E-commerce platforms among leading E-commerce platforms in Vietnam. Based on Stimulus Organism Response perspective and previous research on the use of AI for personalization, the research looks into the impact on Impulse Buying Behavior of AI Product Recommendations, AI Targeted Ads, Perceived Personalization, Perceived Usefulness of AI, Emotional Arousal, and AI Trust. Data was collected from consumers in Vietnam using an online questionnaire survey via the online shopping platform Shopee, Lazada, or TikTok Shop for Vietnamese consumers. Convenience sampling was adopted and all the constructs were measured on multi item Likert scales adapted from previous studies. Reliability was measured using Cronbach's alpha followed by exploratory factor analysis and multiple linear regression tests were conducted to test the proposed hypotheses. The findings indicate that the model explaining overall variance of impulse buying behavior is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 5. 1}$</tex> percent and all six predictors of AI also have significant positive effects on IB. Emotional arousal serves as the most valuable predictor, followed by the AI product recommendations and AI targeted ads, which validates the central importance of affective activation and algorithmic curation in making unplanned purchases. Perceived personalization, perceived usefulness of AI and AI trust also show smaller effects, showing that consumers are more likely to act on their purchase impulses if AI mediated interactions are experienced as tailored, helpful and trustworthy.

title: ASIoU: A Domain-Informed Frequency-Weighted IoU Loss for RF Spectrogram Drone Detection
authors: Akshat Sharan, Mohammad Rostami, Atik Faysal, Hongtao Xia, Hadi Kasasbeh, Ziang Gao, Huaxia Wang
publish: 2026 35th Wireless and Optical Communications Conference (WOCC)
abstract: Standard bounding box regression losses are spatially isotropic, treating temporal and spectral errors as equivalent. In RF spectrogram-based drone detection, this is operationally dangerous: A frequency-axis error directs Electronic Countermeasures to jam the wrong channel, directly causing mission failure. We propose Anisotropic Spectral-IoU (ASIoU), a frequency-weighted regression loss integrated into a lightweight YOLOE architecture, which explicitly penalizes spectral misalignment over temporal drift. Evaluated on the CageDroneRF (CDRF) dataset, our model achieves 0% FAR, 97.87% precision, and 91.77% mAP@0.90–0.95, surpassing all baselines at strict localization thresholds. On a challenging out-of-distribution real-world rooftop deployment, the proposed model achieves a 3.7× FAR reduction over a heavyweight baseline and 8.8× over standard YOLOe, while maintaining a competitive 93.1% classification rate, demonstrating that aligning the training objective with RF domain physics is a more effective path to operational reliability than scaling model capacity. Codes are available at https://github.com/Akshat-Sharan/YOLOE

title: Fostering Sustainable Engineering Education Through e-Learning: Cognitive and Motivational Drivers of Conceptual Change
authors: Md Abdullah Al Mamun, Mahbub Hasan
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Facilitating conceptual change in engineering education remains a critical challenge for technology-enhanced learning environments, particularly in the Global South. This study adopts a student-centred perspective to investigate how eLearning platforms influence students' conceptual change toward sustainable engineering education. This study examined the relationships between cognitivemotivational constructs (e.g., motivation, dissatisfaction, plausibility) and conceptual change metrics. Results revealed that cognitive restructuring is primarily driven by these human-centred, affective pathways. Specifically, higher plausibility judgments and motivational orientations were the strongest predictors of successful conceptual change. Further analysis of learner attributes (e.g., gender) showed no significant differences in perceived conceptual change. These findings suggest that conceptual change outcomes are more strongly associated with cognitivemotivational factors than with demographic characteristics. To foster equitable and sustainable engineering education through e-learning platforms in contexts such as Bangladesh, instructional design must effectively support these critical factors.

title: Methodology for Automated Assessment of the Mentor Efficiency in an Educational Organization
authors: Alexander Sokolov, Tatiana Margaryan, Elena Urazova, Polina Petrushina
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper, using the example of Bauman Moscow State Technical University, presents the definition of a mentor's profile and a methodology for assessing the effectiveness of their work. The relevance of this study is due to the importance of systematizing the activities of mentors in educational organizations. The methodology is based on the construction and application of a model describing the mentor's labor costs depending on scores based on pre-defined evaluation criteria. Linear regression in a multidimensional space of criteria (key performance indicators) was used, with the weight of each proposed to be determined by the least squares method based on a pre-defined set of scores obtained by a group of experts in advance.

title: Research on Global Velocity Planning for Lunar Rover via Energy Consumption Optimization
authors: Jianfei Cao, Yaobing Wang, Jiyang Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: For the high-speed planetary vehicle, speed planning is of great significance for the accessibility of driving tasks and energy balance. Therefore, this paper proposes a global optimization algorithm for the speed planning of high-speed lunar rover in rugged terrain, which can generate the optimal speed sequence to guide the task execution or task planning evaluation. The simulation results show that this method can obtain the vehicle speed sequence with the energy saving effect of no less than $68 \%$, and can also obtain the energy saving effect of no less than $40 \%$ when the time cost extension is no more than 20%.

title: Meclib – A Tool for Efficient Authoring of Advanced Digital Questions in Moodle
authors: Martin Kraska
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The Moodle learning management system features the question type STACK, which integrates computer algebra using Maxima and interactive graphics using JSXGraph. In the context of Engineering Mechanics, the author has explored the potential of these tools for designing interactive teaching resources with automated formative assessment. Authoring such material is a complex task due to the multiple tools involved. Advanced features like interactive dynamic graphics or elaborated adaptive feedback require programming tasks in Maxima and JavaScript along with HTML and LaTeX. Meclib is a collection of reusable components that implement such features. Meclib was developed for lecturers who want to design and maintain advanced question pools under the conditions of limited resources. It enables them to address not only algebraic modelling and calculation skills but also physical modelling, such as creating system schematics. One of the most advanced use are questions with embedded editor for free body diagrams in Engineering Mechanics.

title: Design of In-orbit Communication Link-Setup Method for Multi-Module and Multi-Target Lunar Relay Missions
authors: Yang Zhao, Ruikang Zhang, Zhuo Tao, Baobi Xu, Hao Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: With the continuous development of deep space exploration missions, the ground tracking and control capabilities are limited. In some critical mission processes, the probe is not visible from Earth and is not accessible by ground communication stations. Therefore, relay satellites are required to provide communication links between the probe and ground stations. For complex deep space exploration missions, implemented by multiple modules and targets of the exploration probe, the establishment of relay communication links requires taking the following aspects into consideration, including the visibility and obstruction avoidance of communication linkage, scheduling of ground tracking stations for different targets, and safety issues related to energy and temperature conditions while the probe adjusts its attitude for communication with relay satellite. This is a systematic and comprehensive mission design process. In this paper, a multi-target communication link establishment method is proposed for deep space exploration missions, with a focus on lunar exploration. The implementation of this method has been validated through in-orbit verification, demonstrating its broad application prospects in multi-target deep space exploration missions. The corresponding operational procedures have been verified through engineering applications, providing valuable guidance for future deep space exploration missions.

title: Integrated Accident Risk Analysis: KDE-based Hotspot Detection and XGBoost Severity Classification for Safer Urban Mobility
authors: P.N.V Lakshmi, A.Naga Venkata Sailu Sri, K. Prasanth Kumar, D. Sujith, A. Pranavnath Reddy
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Road accidents remain a critical threat to public safety, causing severe human and economic losses in rapidly urbanizing regions. Traditional accident analysis methods depend on static summaries that fail to capture evolving spatial–temporal risk patterns and do not support real-time decision-making. This study presents an integrated accident risk analysis framework combining Kernel Density Estimation (KDE) for hotspot detection and XGBoost for accident severity classification, enabling dynamic and interpretable risk assessment. KDE is used to generate continuous spatial risk surfaces that highlight high-density crash zones, while XGBoost predicts severity levels—Slight, Serious, or Fatal—using roadway, environmental, and temporal attributes. The severity predictions and hotspot intensities are fused into a Composite Risk Score (CRS), which powers a risk-aware routing engine capable of recommending safer travel paths. Experimental evaluations demonstrate high predictive performance, accurate hotspot localization, and effective risk-informed route optimization, contributing to safer and more resilient urban mobility.

title: Cognitive Data Guardian: An Intelligent AI Framework for Secure and Automated Privacy Protection
authors: Senthilnathan T, Hare Preya P S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The third reason is the exponential development of digital documentation within businesses that has greatly amplified the risk of vulnerability of the sensitive information of personally identifiable data, financial statements, medical information and confidential company information. Manual redaction and rule-based tools do not always give a contextual meaning and thus there is potential of masking errors and compliance risks. In this paper, the author introduces Cognitive Data Guardian, an intelligent document protection system that is based on AI and is aimed at automated privacy protection. The proposed system combines Natural Language Processing-based Named Entity Recognition with pattern-based detection to detect sensitive entities on multi-format documents such as scanned documents by incorporating OCR. An adaptive masking, redaction, or encryption policy can be achieved by using a dynamic mechanism of risk classification and document risk index. Experimental analysis shows detection accuracy of 98.9%, average F1-score of 98.3%, redaction accuracy of 99.2% and a false positive of 2.3, which are higher than the results with conventional systems of keyword-based evaluation and standalone NER. The system also has a document format preservation of more than 99 % as it is automatically protected. The findings confirm that the suggested hybrid AI model is much more effective in relation to privacy compliance, less manual work, and offers a scalable answer to managing intelligent document security in enterprise settings.

title: Debris Reentry Time Prediction with Information Learning from History Data
authors: Weiyue Chen, Yu He, Wei Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: To accurately predict the time span for space debris reentry into the Earth’s atmosphere, this study investigates a method using two-line element data and space weather forecast data as inputs, employing a high-precision orbital dynamics model for reentry time prediction. Based on the conversion between mean elements and osculating elements in the SGP4 model, and utilizing the analytical relationship between the atmospheric drag coefficient and the mean semi-major axis, this paper proposes a method to identify the upper and lower boundaries of the space debris drag coefficient. Using the identified atmospheric drag coefficient boundaries, a high-precision dynamics model is applied, updating the geomagnetic index and solar radiation flux values daily, to predict the leading edge and the trailing edge of the space debris reentry time. Historical two-line element data from the Shenzhou XVIII orbital module is used to identify the upper and lower boundaries of the atmospheric drag coefficient and predict the reentry time. The results show that the reentry time median obtained using the method proposed in this paper has a deviation of less than one day from the actual reentry time, validating the effectiveness of methods proposed in this study.

title: Scalable Deep Learning Models for Automated Fish Species Classification in Real-World Aquatic Scenarios
authors: K. Malarvizhi, T. Srinivas Reddy, R. Abitha, P. Saravanabhava, S. P. Santhoshkumar, A. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The essential principle of aquatic biodiversity monitoring, fisheries management and ecological sustainability is an accurate and scalable species classification of fish, especially in the real world setting, where species display visual similarity, with multifaceted backgrounds and information asymmetry. This paper details a scalable and systematic deep learning architecture to identify the species of fish in a variety of aquatic environments. The suggested method combines the strong image processing, deep feature extraction, and refined detection and classification methods. CNNs based on ResNet50 and VGG16 architecture, which are fine tuned to learn discriminative visual representations, are used to localize fish, and a two-step pipeline, including improved region-based fully convolutional networks, is used to learn to localize fish precisely. The optimized CNN architectures with the attention mechanisms to enhance the emphasis of the features allow the lightweight and efficient classification. A class-wise loss formulation and a trained strategy are used in order to solve practical problems like imbalance in classes and multi-instance detection. Extensive benchmark underwater and reef fish experimental evaluation shows excellent generalization ability, can perform with high accuracy, precision, recall and F1-scores with performance up to 95 in classification and near-perfect performance in detection-based analysis. The findings confirm the utilization of the scalable deep learning models in credible fish species classification in complicated aquatic conditions, which can be used in real-time ecological monitoring and smart fisheries management systems.

title: Elastic-Mode Conversion for Acousto-Optic Applications in Paratellurite
authors: N. V. Polikarpova
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: Broadband excitation of slow shear ultrasound in optically transparent crystals remains an important practical problem for acousto-optic instrumentation. The difficulty is especially pronounced in paratellurite, where the most efficient interaction geometries involve a low-velocity shear mode that is inconvenient to launch directly by a piezoelectric plate. This paper considers an alternative scheme in which a longitudinal wave is first excited in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TeO}_{2}$</tex> sample and is then converted into the required quasi-transverse mode after reflection from a free crystal-vacuum boundary. The geometry of the reflecting face, the distribution of reflected acoustic energy between the allowed modes, and the consequences for a tunable acousto-optic cell are analyzed. Experiments with a fabricated cell confirm that the working slow shear component dominates over parasitic modes. The measured spectral bandwidth of the cell is about 82 angstroms, in agreement with the expected performance for the selected interaction configuration.

title: A Tiered Patient-Centric Emergency Medical Access System using Dynamic QR-Codes
authors: Riddhi Khatri, Riddhi Jadhav, Dhruv Gherwada, Vinit Kotak, Rashmi Malvankar, Swati Nadkarni
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: In emergency situations, quick access to a patient’s medical information can play a crucial role in providing proper treatment.However, displaying the entire medical details of a person risks the security of the medical data. This project presents a QR-based, tiered framework using the MERN stack to bridge this gap. The primary contribution is a functional access model that bifurcates data visibility based on the user’s role: providing immediate "First Responder" access to essential lifesaving details while requiring a verified "Medical Professional" login for comprehensive records. Unlike regular static QR codes, the proposed system supports dynamic QR generation and QR revocation with the ’QR rotation’ and ’revocation’ features, reducing the risk of misuse in case the code is copied, shared or photographed. Basic access logs are maintained to track the medical data access of the patient. Therefore, this prototype shifts data governance from centralized administrators to the patients, thereby achieving a highly responsive solution for modern healthcare.

title: A Privacy-Preserving Federated Averaging Framework with Homomorphic Encryption and Hybrid Convolutional Transformer for Secure Distributed Utility Data Analytics
authors: Pullaiah Chowdary Vutla, Miss. Triveni Yenugu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Recently, the rapid growth of data-driven applications in sectors like electric power generation, distribution, and smart billing systems has introduced serious issues concerning data privacy and security, which require efficient prevention aids. Conventional Machine Learning (ML) architectures involve centralized information acquisition, which poses a threat of information leaks and unauthorized information access. To enable a significant privacy-preserving system, this research provides a Privacy-Preserving Federated Averaging Model of Secure Data Analytics Network (P2FA-SDANet), which combines Federated Learning (FL) and Homomorphic Encryption (HE) to allow the secure model training without exposing utility data environments. In the proposed P2FA-SDANet architecture, the data remain locally distributed, and encrypted model updates are shared with a central aggregation server. Moreover, each client runs a hybrid Convolutional Transformer Network (CT-Net), which is a combination of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and transformer, to perform local model optimization via Federated Averaging (FedAvg), and HE is used to securely aggregate encrypted weight updates at the central server. The aggregated ciphertext is then securely decrypted to obtain the updated global model, which is redistributed to the participating clients to perform the refinement in an iterative way. Through experimental validation, it is shown that the proposed P2FA-SDANet has a highly competitive predictive performance, whereby the privacy-utility trade-off is low relative to traditional models. The proposed system provides regulatory compliance, user trust, and scalability of the solution of secure data-driven decision-making in a distributed environment.

title: Design of Adaptive Multi-Band Frequency Reconfigurable Antenna for Next Generation Wireless Networks
authors: B. Bharathi Devi, N. Lahari, M. Gayathri Sri, M. G. H. S. Phaneesh, N. Saathvik
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The speedy evolution of wireless communication technologies has increased the demand for antenna capable of operating across multiple frequency bands, particularly for upper 5 g force and come out 6 G wireless applications. Frequency reconfigurable antennas (FRAs) have emerged as an effective solution due to their ability to dynamically adjust manoeuvre frequencies based on communication requirement. This work presents the aim and development of an adaptive multi band frequency reconfigurable feeler for next - multiplication wireless meshwork. The proposed aerial operates at multiple resonant frequencies of 5.2 GHz, 9.6 GHz, 13.5 GHz, 18 – 22.8 gigacycle per second, and 25.8 GHz, covering several key wireless and high - oftenness communication bands. Frequency reconfigurability is achieved using a PIN diode - based switch mechanism, enabling flexible band selection with minimum hardware complexity. The antenna have a compact planar social structure with a simple predetermine network, making it suitable for integration into modern wireless communication devices. Critical design parameters such as substrate material, antenna geometry, feedline configuration, and tuning mechanism are optimize to accomplish suitable performance in terms of resistivity matching, bandwidth, profit, and radiation sickness characteristics across all operating bands. The proposed design offers a promising and efficient result for next adaptive multi band wireless communication systems.

title: Federated, Real-Time AI Analytics for Vaccine Effectiveness and Variant Surveillance Across Distributed Healthcare Systems
authors: Vijitha Uppuluri
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The rapid emergence of infectious disease variants poses significant challenges for continuously assessing vaccine effectiveness and enabling timely public health responses. Conventional surveillance systems often rely on centralized data collection, which raises privacy concerns and limits collaboration across healthcare institutions. This study proposes a federated, real-time artificial intelligence analytics framework for monitoring vaccine effectiveness and detecting emerging variant risks across distributed healthcare systems while preserving sensitive patient data. The proposed framework integrates federated learning for privacy-preserving collaborative model training, AI-based predictive modeling for infection risk and vaccine breakthrough analysis, and natural language processing techniques to extract variant-related signals from clinical reports and scientific literature. In addition, a real-time analytics pipeline and interactive business intelligence dashboard support continuous epidemiological monitoring and decision support for health authorities. The framework was evaluated using distributed datasets representing vaccination records, clinical outcomes, and genomic variant information in a simulated multi-node healthcare environment. Experimental results demonstrate strong predictive performance, achieving 94% accuracy and an AUC-ROC of 0.96 while maintaining efficient convergence across federated training rounds. The proposed system contributes a unified federated framework that integrates real-time analytics, predictive modeling, and NLP-based variant surveillance for privacy-preserving public health monitoring.

title: AI-Powered Crop Recommendation System using FT-Transformer for Precision Agriculture
authors: M. Ramya, Nisvan Mohamed, M. Devesh Reddy
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: This paper presents an AI-powered crop recommendation system using a Feature Tokenizer Transformer (FT-Transformer) for tabular agronomic prediction. The model takes seven inputs, including soil nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, pH, temperature, humidity, and rainfall, and predicts crop suitability with top-k confidence scores. A dual-source data pipeline integrates Kaggle and Mendeley datasets through schema mapping, label normalization, and rainfall imputation where required. The revised training protocol uses stratified train-validation-test splitting, train-only feature scaling, validation-guided checkpointing, adaptive learning-rate reduction, and early stopping to reduce leakage and improve generalization reliability. The final system is deployed through a Streamlit web application with optional OpenWeatherMap-based weather integration and agronomy-aware advisory outputs such as NPK gap guidance, seasonal suitability, and crop calendar recommendations. On the held-out test set, FT-Transformer achieves 0.9054 accuracy and 0.9042 weighted F1-score. Comparative experiments show strong competitiveness against classical baselines, with superior performance to SVM and MLP and performance close to Random Forest.

title: Enhancing Public Welfare Access Through an Intelligent AI-based Digital Assistant
authors: Vinodh Kumar S, Sivathanu K P, Vishal P, Pravin P
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: SmartBot has served the needs of informed citizens in terms of getting a hold of government welfare schemes, but there is room for further improvement on its personalization part, operational efficiency, and recommendation accuracy. In Phase 2, the SmartBot system will be upgraded with the most advanced AI and ML algorithms to provide accurate, context-aware, and real-time welfare scheme recommendations to suit individual needs. The enhanced system integrates automated document verification, multilingual communication support, and voice-enabled interaction, allowing engagement with citizens requiring different levels of literacy and language proficiency. Besides, SmartBot integrates with government information systems to ensure dependable, updated, and transparent welfare data delivery. A unified Smart Dashboard enables its users to track application status, deadlines, eligibility criteria, and benefit updates on a single interface. These enhancements transform SmartBot into an integrated digital welfare assistance platform that reduces manual effort and administrative errors, enhancing citizen engagement and building transparency between the government and public at large. The proposed Phase 2 framework demonstrates the potential of AI-driven intelligent systems in increasing the accessibility, efficiency, and inclusiveness of the delivery of public welfare services.

title: Bridging Accuracy and Interpretability in Deep Learning for Pancreatic Tumor Segmentation
authors: Vaishnavi D, Savarna K, Sushmitha B, Sanjana M S, Akshaya Swati R, S. Anandaraj
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Pancreatic cancer is also one of the most lethal ones as it is often diagnosed at its last stage, or it is hard to detect small tumors during medical examination. Deep learning has demonstrated potential in the automatic segmentation of pancreatic tumors using computed tomography (CT) images; nontransparent decision-making of these models is the barrier to clinical use. The paper introduces a basic CNN network to pancreatic tumor segmentation on the U-Net architecture based on Dice loss to overcome class imbalance. To tackle novelty, the suggested system uniquely combines Grad-CAM explainability with anatomy-constrained post-processing in a 2D U-Net baseline. This is a method that has not been used in previous 2D pancreatic segmentation baselines. It offers both quantitative tumor overlap metrics and qualitative attention analysis. According to the findings of the experiments, although the model reveals the global anatomical structures, the localization of the small tumor areas is not yet accurate. The visualizations of grad-cam indicate that the early-stage models pay attention to the general abdominal areas, but do not concentrate on the tumor areas, which proves that pancreas-oriented and context-inspired learning is necessary.

title: Reconfigurable Action Sequence Planning for Modular Robots Based on Contraction Hierarchy Graph Search Algorithm
authors: Ying Yan, Gang Chen, Jiuqing Liu, Xiaokang An, Jingqi Nie, Dongyi Xia, Huawei Liu, Yuqiang Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: This paper introduces a reconfigurable modular robot, and for the loopless multi-module robot system composed of this module, a topology modelling and reconstruction method of a spatially reconfigurable modular robot based on topological configuration matrix is proposed to achieve an efficient search for the best structural match. Taking a self-developed heterogeneous modular robot as the research object, the motion degrees of freedom and connection characteristics of the spherical module are analyzed in detail. By transforming modules and their connection relationships into directed graphs and introducing shortcuts in node neighborhoods for combinatorial matching, a contraction hierarchy graph search algorithm is employed to generate the topological reconfiguration action sequence of the modular robot. Finally, path planning for spacecraft transportation and maintenance scenarios is carried out on the Matlab simulation platform, and the algorithm is deployed to a spacecraft reconfigurable space robot system. The results show that the method effectively achieves reconfiguration between different chains, providing a feasible solution for flexible operations in space missions.

title: Adaptive PID Gain Tuning for Quadrotor Hover Control via Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Mohamed Amine Oumlik, WeiChen Dai
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: Quadrotor unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) require robust control systems capable of maintaining stable hover under environmental disturbances such as wind gusts, payload variations, and sensor noise. Traditional PID controllers, while widely adopted for their simplicity and interpretability, require manual tuning and struggle to adapt to changing conditions. This work presents a hybrid reinforcement learning-PID (RL-PID) control architecture that leverages deep RL agents to perform real-time adaptive PID gain tuning for stable quadrotor hover. We formulate the gain adaptation problem as a Markov Decision Process and compare four algorithm variants: PPO-FF, PPO-LSTM, SAC-FF, and SAC-LSTM. The system is trained using curriculum learning progressively increasing disturbance difficulty across wind, payload, and sensor noise domains. Experimental results over 5 million training steps demonstrate that all variants achieve robust hover performance with position RMSE below 0.21 m under maximum disturbance conditions. PPO-LSTM shows the lowest tracking error (0.21 m RMSE) in our experiments, while PPO-FF shows the most stable training dynamics. The hybrid architecture successfully combines the stability guarantees of classical PID control with the adaptive capabilities of learned policies, providing a practical framework for autonomous UAV control in uncertain environments.

title: Postgraduate Students' Initial Publication Background Impact on the Probability PhD Degree Within the Planned Time Obtaining
authors: Svetlana S. Dymkova, Irina S. Kretova, Oleg V. Varlamov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This article analyzes the relationship between publication activity of applicants to doctoral programs and the likelihood of successfully academic degree obtaining within the required timeframe. The issue of empirical verifying of this hypothesis is not sufficiently covered in the scientific literature, making this research topic relevant. This empirical study was conducted using the Moscow Technical University of Communications and Informatics (MTUCI) doctoral program and the Russian Science Citation Index (RSCI), a national bibliographic database of scientific citations. Using initial data on those enrolled in doctoral programs in 2017-2020 and those who graduated from doctoral programs in 2021-2024, bibliometric indicators for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 1}$</tex> doctoral students were collected. The study developed a methodology for integrating the publication background of postgraduate students. The analysis of the resulting dataset confirms the relationship between the bibliometric indicators of applicants to doctoral programs and the likelihood of successfully receiving PhD degree in the required timeframe. Applicants wishing to obtain a PhD degree with a probability greater than 0.5 must have at least 20 publication background points before admission to graduate school. The target for effective graduate school performance, 25%, is achieved when all accepted applicants have a publication background score of at least three. As a follow-up study, it would be appropriate to examine the graduate students' supervisors publication background impact on the obtaining a PhD degree within the planned time probability.

title: Mechanical Performance Optimization of Eco-Friendly Rice Straw Nanocellulose Bioplastic
authors: Aswan Munang, Ahmad Wahyu Setiawan
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Bioplastics are a type of natural polyester material that decompose more rapidly than petroleum-based polyesters. One of the raw materials used to produce bioplastics is nanocellulose fiber derived from plants. Nanocellulose can be extracted from rice straw through a series of processes, including extraction and acid hydrolysis, and is then combined with Polyvinyl Alcohol (PVA) as the primary matrix, along with plasticizers such as glycerol, sorbitol, and polyethylene glycol (PEG). The experimental design employed an L9 (3<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup>) Orthogonal Array, involving four main factors: nanocellulose concentration (3%, 5%, 7%), PVA content (2%, 4%, 6%), type of plasticizer (glycerol, sorbitol, PEG), and drying temperature (40° C, 50° C, 60° C). The results were analyzed using DOE and Taguchi methods with Minitab software to identify the dominant factors and determine the optimal composition for the mechanical and thermal properties of the bioplastics. The results showed that formulation F7 demonstrated the most optimal performance compared to the other formulations. This was indicated by the lowest length shrinkage (0.8%), minimal mass loss (1.2%), the highest thermal resistance (100° C), and the lowest biodegradation rate (3.08%), indicating good material stability. In contrast, formulations F6 and F3 were considered less optimal because they exhibited high length shrinkage (up to 6.5%), significant mass loss, and higher biodegradation rates, indicating a less stable material structure.

title: Integration of Artificial Intelligence and Total Quality Management in Manufacturing Projects for Industry 5.0
authors: Saleem Ahmed Al-Azazi, Mohammed Abdulrazzaq Alaghbari, Mourad Mansour, Basheer M. Al-Ghazali, Abdullah O. Baarimah, Aawag Mohsen Alawag
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Industry 5.0 shifts the focus of manufacturing from mere productivity to sustainability, resilience, and humancentric value. This evolution demands that quality management systems (QMS) are not only effective but also explainable and auditable. This paper is a conceptual framework study that synthesizes TQM, Quality 4.0, trustworthy AI, and digital twin principles to propose a governance-ready reference architecture rather than report statistically generalizable field results. While Quality 4.0 brings in IIoT data, analytics, and AI into quality engineering, many implementations remain tool-focused; models are often integrated into inspection or prediction tasks without being embedded in a PDCA-driven continuous improvement framework, lacking end-to-end evidence preservation and systematic controls for data quality, model drift, and AI risks. This paper presents AITQ-5, a framework governed by PDCA that integrates AI into Total Quality Management for Industry 5.0 manufacturing projects, structured around three design pillars: (i) a PDCA-aligned AI capability model, (ii) human-in-the-loop governance checkpoints throughout the AI lifecycle, and (iii) a Semantic Digital Twin (SDT) that connects Critical To Quality (CTQ) factors, process context, observations, model versions, decisions, and corrective actions into a provenance-aware knowledge graph. The paper offers three implementation artifacts: a PDCA–AI capability map, a maturity model for QMS integration across four levels, and a Quality 5.0 KPI scorecard. Additionally, it outlines an evaluation protocol, case-based sampling guidance, and a roadmap for deployment to support rigorous future validation and audit-ready adoption on the shop floor.

title: Enhancing Agricultural Productivity Through Deep Learning-Based Disease Detection
authors: S. Murugavalli, R. Gopi
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This paper tackles one of the most important problems facing sustainable agriculture and global food security by offering a thorough solution for the detection of plant diseases using deep learning techniques. Plant diseases significantly reduce crop quality and yield, and because they require specialized intervention and human inspection, early detection is challenging. To get around these restrictions, we suggest a machine learning-based disease classification system that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with data augmentation and transfer learning strategies. We collected and preprocessed a high-resolution dataset of leaf photos from different plant species, both healthy and diseased. To improve generalization and robustness, random rotations, flips, zooms, and brightness modification techniques were applied. In order to leverage large-scale visual feature prior knowledge, the CNN architecture was also optimized using pre-trained models of ResNet50, VGG16, and InceptionV3. These models were then refined over the plant dataset. The model was extensively trained and validated on stratified data splits to guarantee a balanced representation of all disease classes. Several experiments were carried out, and the system was tested on an independent test set of unseen plants images. Performance measures such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score were calculated, where the suggested system outperformed traditional machine learning methods. The results also indicate that transfer learning profoundly outperforms training from scratch, establishing its importance for agricultural purposes where datasets tend to be small. Comparative evaluation with state-of-the-art models also exhibits the advantageous and disadvantageous aspects of CNN-based methods, specifically their scalability, field conformity, and real-time applicability.

title: Smart Parking Occupancy Prediction using Bidirectional LSTM and Real-Time Dashboard Visualization
authors: Yuvaraja Mahesh, Vanita Jaitly
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The alarming rate of urbanization and the concentration of vehicles in urban areas have equally raised the issues surrounding parking management in the contemporary towns, which translate into traffic jams, wastage of nuclear energy, and wastage of time in the quest to locate a parking area. To help reduce such problems, the present paper will suggest a smart parking occupancy prediction neural network based on the Bi-LSTM deep learning model. The proposed system takes advantage of the physical parking sensor data obtained at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Center (KLCC) to forecast the occurrence of a car park given its historical accuracy in forecasting its use. The application of time-series preprocessing and sequence-based learning are used to model complex temporal dependencies in the parking behavior. Bi-LSTM model takes data forward and backward directions enhancing accuracy in prediction relative to traditional models. Through experimental results, the model proposed attains a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.0169 and an R2 of over 0.95 which is indicative of good predictive performance and generalization. Moreover, a real-time dashboard, using Flask and designed to illustrate the projected and real parking occupancy patterns, is created to increase the interpretability and usability of the system. The suggested framework suggests a scaled and data-driven intelligent parking management solution and can be efficiently implemented in intelligent cities.

title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in Enhancing Leadership, Decision-Making, and Sales Performance: Evidence from Jordanian Service Organizations
authors: Abdallah Ali Mohammad Alrifae, Hasan Alhanatleh, Asmahan Majed Altaher, Esraa Reda Zaid, Ameen Al Htibat
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The application of artificial intelligence (AI) to facilitate leadership and managerial activities in service organizations continues to become strategic decision-making and sales drive. The implementation of AI-based technologies in Jordanian service organizations is associated with high potentials to improve the performance of leadership and to increase the datadriven decision-making process; however, the empirical evidence explaining these processes is still insufficient. This study explores how AI applications can be useful in improving the performance of the leadership, quality of management decisions, and sales in the Jordanian service organizations. A quantitative research design was adopted using structured questions that were administered through questionnaires to managers, team leaders, and sales supervisors who work in Jordanian telecommunications and service companies. The valid answers were completed and analyzed by Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) through SmartPLS 4. The findings show that AI applications have enormous positive impacts on leadership performance and sales. Moreover, the leadership performance has a positive impact on the quality of decisions made by the manager, which consequently leads to an increase in sales. The structural model exhibits good explanatory power as AI applications, and quality of decisions take a significant part of the variance in sales performance. Based on reliability and validity, measurement and structural validity were established. The results are beneficial to the service organizations in Jordan as they highlight the necessity to combine the capabilities of AI with the development of leadership to improve the managerial decisions and the sales performance.

title: Interpretable Neural Radiomics for Brain Tumor Mapping and Progression Analysis
authors: Abhishek Jogur, Deekshith Gowda J, G Vishwas, Harshad S N, E. G Satish
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Tumor delineation and consistent determination of tumor growth with time are the accurate parameters of brain tumor analysis. Although the deep learning-based segmentation and radiomics have demonstrated encouraging outcomes in the characterization of the tumor in the stationary form, the issue of the monitoring of the development of tumors on a time basis is an important medical challenge. The paper proposes an interpretable neural radiomics model of mapping brain tumors and exploring their evolution based on magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). The convolutional neural network proposed is made to produce consistent tumor segmentation mask across multiple time points of the MRI. It subsequently derives radiomic features. The longitudinal evaluation of tumor progression is the volumetric, spatial, and radiomic feature changes. This enables objective characterization of the tumor growth, stability or regression. Explainable artificial intelligence methods, including Grad-CAM and feature level interpretability are added to guarantee clinical transparency. The experimental data demonstrates that it is possible to map tumors reliably and extract radiomics reliably to achieve the feasibility of interpretable and clinically meaningful monitoring of progression.

title: A Robust Hybrid Ensemble for Tri-Axial Vibration-Based Running Speed Estimation With Leakage-Free Stacking
authors: Chenghang Liu, Haocheng Lyu, Bowen Wang, Wenbo Fan, Quan Zhang, Jie Sun, Mark Leach
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: Accurate running-speed estimation from tri-axial vibration signals remains challenging in realistic sensing environments due to heterogeneous sampling rates, nominal-speedinduced regime shifts, and the presence of stop or near-idle segments that introduce heavy-tailed residuals. This paper presents a robust hybrid pipeline that combines engineered signal descriptors with strong feature-based regressors and a corrected leakage-free stacking strategy trained strictly on out-of-fold predictions. The feature construction stage integrates time-domain statistics, frequency-domain summaries, Hjorth parameters, Teager-Kaiser energy statistics, harmonic-band energies, and cross-axis correlations to characterize both global and regime-dependent signal structure. In the final design, CatBoost, XGBoost, and Random Forest are used as candidate base learners for stacking, while a waveform CNN is retained as an auxiliary baseline for comparison. Experimental results show that the corrected stacked ensemble achieves the best overall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> and RMSE, whereas XGBoost attains the lowest MAE. Residual diagnostics further show that the final system is globally centered and well calibrated, while nominal-speed-binned analysis reveals that high-speed regimes remain the most challenging. These results indicate that, for the present dataset, a carefully designed structured-data ensemble offers a more reliable and deploymentoriented solution than a heavier end-to-end deep alternative.

title: Brain Stroke Detection from Neuroimaging Data using XGBoost Algorithm
authors: Gowri R, Dr.V.Govindaraj, Janaranjani C, Harshini J, Mohammed Thoufiq T
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Stroke is a life threatening neurological condition that has to be properly and quickly diagnosed in order to minimize brain injuries and mortality. The manual interpretation of the Computed tomography (CT) scan and Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans is a lengthy process and, it is likely that these methods are subject to inter-observer variation thereby retarding treatment. The given paper describes an automated framework of brain stroke detection that is based on a hybrid DL and XGBoost algorithm. CNNs are used to get high-level spatial and texture features of neuroimaging data that identify key patterns related to stroke lesions. Such deep features are then transformed into the XGBoost classifier to carry out powerful binary stroke and non stroke cases. Integrated CNN and XGBoost models are also more predictive, less prone to overfitting, and more generalizing as compared to individual models. With the proposed system, it has been shown that experimental results have a better accuracy, sensitivity and specificity, which proves the effectiveness of the system. The model developed helps to diagnose early, aid clinicians with making decisions, and decrease the workload in an emergency. This smart structure is towards effective and sound, scalable computer-aided stroke detection in clinical practice.

title: Context-Aware and Confidence-Guided Recognition of Ancient Tamil Stone Inscriptions using Multimodal Graph Learning
authors: Balasubramanian Murugan, Dr.P.Visalakshi
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The historic, cultural and linguistic information about the ancient Tamil people is abundant in the stone inscriptions of the antiquity however, the automatic identification of such inscriptions is hard because of the surface erosion, uneven carving and absence of text boundaries. The majority of the current inscription recognition systems are concerned with transcription accuracy without paying attention to the reliability and explainability of the obtained results, therefore, limiting their use in real-world epigraphic studies. As a solution to these problems, this paper introduces a context- sensitive and trust-based deep learning model of the identification and deciphering of ancient Tamil inscriptions. The suggested approach is a combination of image improvement and restoration models with denoising and super-resolution networks with adaptive and style-sensitive data augmentation to deal with the changing nature of the inscriptions. State-of-the-art models of scene text detection are used to locate text regions and transformer- based optical character recognition is used to perform character recognition. A Bayesian neural network is applied to obtain word- level confidence and uncertainty to determine the reliability of the predictions. Besides, graph attention-based reasoning model which creates a graph based on space and linguistic dependence is used to construct contextual sentences. The system delivers the modern Tamil translations with a confidence estimate and areas with uncertains hence enabling a credible documentation of the heritage.

title: Quantitative Time-Resolved Imaging of Surface Acoustic Waves by Dual-Comb Interferometry with a Fiber-Based Sagnac Interferometer_supp3-3701200.mp4
authors: Kazuki Maezawa
publish: N/A
abstract: The interferometer is constructed with polarization-maintaining fibers, providing high phase stability, robustness against environmental disturbances, and a compact configuration that significantly reduces alignment effort. In addition, the introduction of a stroboscopic scheme into the dual-comb interferometry enables rapid scanning of the surface acoustic wave (SAW) temporal waveform for both fundamental and nonlinear surface vibration components. Using this method, we present quantitative two-dimensional movies of propagating SAWs on a gold thin film. The proposed approach enables fast, stable, and quantitative visualization of both fundamental and higher-order SAW dynamics, and is expected to serve as a powerful tool for characterizing a wide range of linear and nonlinear acoustic devices.

title: A Universal Framework for Remote Sensing Image Color Correction via Style Transfer_supp1-3702755.pdf
authors: Xinyi Liu
publish: N/A
abstract: Color inconsistency in optical remote sensing imagery, caused by variations in imaging platforms, illumination, and atmospheric conditions, can reduce the reliability of multi-temporal and multi-source downstream applications. Existing deep learning–based color correction approaches, largely adapted from natural-image processing, often struggle with two intrinsic challenges in remote sensing scenarios: (i) preserving fine geometric structures during appearance distribution alignment, and (ii) scaling to ultra-high-resolution imagery without compromising global color coherence. In this paper, we propose a photorealistic style transfer framework for reference-guided remote sensing image color correction. The core idea is to inject target appearance statistics via a lightweight, learnable mapping of Gram representations into the content feature space dynamically, aiming to improve color consistency while mitigating structural distortions. To address high-resolution processing challenges, we introduce a Pixel Unshuffle/Shuffle strategy, which avoids stitching artifacts and ensures global consistency. Additionally, we design a style fusion strategy that adaptively merges the stylized output with the original content image to restore fine structural details. Extensive experiments on diverse remote sensing datasets demonstrate the stability and consistency of the proposed approach. Further evaluations on change detection, image matching, and 3D reconstruction show that our method offers potential improvements in real-world remote sensing workflows.

title: Design Criteria for Torque Enhancement in Modular Permanent Magnet Machines with Two Wound Teeth per Module for EV Applications
authors: Ji Qi, Yuxuan Sun, Tengfei Xi, Zuohui Wang, Wei Hua
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Modular permanent magnet (PM) machines with fractional-slot concentrated windings (FSCWs) are attractive for electric vehicle (EV) applications due to their manufacturing flexibility and fault-tolerant potential, however, modular stator structures are often associated with reduced torque capability. Moreover, reported torque enhancement effects are largely limited to specific slot/pole combinations and configurations with a single wound tooth per module. This paper investigates the torque enhancement mechanism of modular FSCW PM machines with two wound teeth per module and establishes general design criteria for torque enhancement based on analytical analyses. By examining the armature magnetomotive force (MMF) within an individual module, it is shown that torque enhancement can be achieved only when a nonzero average MMF is generated and an appropriate slot/pole pitch relationship is satisfied. Representative 18-slot machines with different pole numbers are analyzed to illustrate the proposed criteria. Finite element analysis and experimental measurements validate the analytical predictions. The analyses provide a design basis for EV-related electromechanical actuator applications.

title: Sem-NaVAE: Semantically-Guided Outdoor Mapless Navigation via Generative Trajectory Priors
authors: Gonzalo Olguín, Javier Ruiz-del-Solar
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This work presents a mapless navigation approach for outdoor applications. It combines the exploratory capacity of conditional variational autoencoders (CVAEs) to generate trajectories and the semantic segmentation capabilities of a lightweight visual language model (VLM) to select the trajectory to execute. Open-vocabulary segmentation is used to score and select the generated trajectories based on natural language, and a state-of-the-art local planner executes velocity commands. One of the key features of the proposed approach is its ability to generate a large variability of trajectories and select them to navigate in real-time. In real-world outdoor experiments, Sem-NaVAE achieves a 90% success rate across routes of 120–240m in unseen environments, outperforming the nearest baseline by 10% while remaining within 7% of a map-based upper bound. A video showing an experimental run of the system can be found in <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://youtu.be/i3R5ey5O2yk</uri>.

title: Multi-Solution Inverse Kinematics for Robotic Manipulators via Permutation-Invariant Set Prediction
authors: Duc Tien Nguyen, Van Thanh Tri Nguyen, Truong Do, Vu Linh Nguyen
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Inverse kinematics (IK) for 6-DoF manipulators is inherently set-valued, since multiple distinct joint configurations can realize the same end-effector pose. Nevertheless, learning-based IK is frequently posed as single-output regression, which can induce mode collapse and unstable predictions near branch boundaries. In this work, IK is cast as a deterministic set-prediction problem, in which an unordered set of feasible joint candidates is produced in a single forward pass. A multi-head network is trained with permutation-invariant supervision via optimal bipartite matching, eliminating the need for a fixed global ordering of IK branches. High-fidelity multi-solution supervision on a UR3 platform is obtained through a hybrid analytic–numerical procedure, in which branch seeds are enumerated and refined using Levenberg–Marquardt optimization, followed by feasibility filtering. At deployment, the predicted set is mapped to an executable joint-command sequence through a continuity-aware selection rule, thereby promoting temporally consistent branch choices. On real UR3 trajectories, improved set fidelity and branch coverage are observed relative to single-solution and fixed-assignment baselines, while accurate joint-space and task-space tracking is maintained on held-out motions. Runtime benchmarks further indicate that multi-branch inference is faster than repeated numerical IK when multiple solutions are required.

title: Phase-Adaptive Sequential Convex Optimization for Array Synthesis with Bilateral Mask Constraints
authors: Xin-Yu Duan, Yi-Xuan Zhang, Ye Hui, Yong-Chang Jiao
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a Phase-Adaptive Sequential Convex Optimization (PASCO) algorithm to efficiently tackle nonconvex bilateral mask constraints in array synthesis. By leveraging the reverse triangle inequality, intractable lower-bound constraints are iteratively reformulated into tractable convex sub-problems. To overcome the inherent search space contraction of conventional transformations, PASCO introduces a flexible phase-adaptive mechanism. This mechanism dynamically aligns the sub-problem search space in the complex plane, preserving maximum degrees of freedom to achieve optimal synthesis without requiring post-optimization correction. Five representative examples, including shaped beams with minimized ripples and strict dynamic range ratio (DRR) control, demonstrate the algorithm's robust, initialization-insensitive convergence. The results verify PASCO's superior performance with efficiency and its extensive versatility for array synthesis scenarios with complex constraints.

title: Neighbors help you best: Geographical Adjacent Perception for Remote Sensing Long-Tailed Semantic Segmentation
authors: Shijie Liu, Zhongle Ren, Biao Hou, Jiawei Ning, Cheng Zhang, Kai Wang, Weibin Li, Licheng Jiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: While semantic segmentation has witnessed remarkable advancements in remote sensing, the deployment of these models is severely hampered by the inherent long-tailed distribution. Despite extensive progress in classification and object detection tasks, long-tailed learning in pixel-wise segmentation remains critically underexplored. Major challenges include the lack of dedicated datasets, ambiguity in defining long-tailed classes, and the inability of generic methods to leverage intrinsic geographic relationships for bias mitigation. To bridge these gaps, we first construct the GaoFen Multi-class Semantic Segmentation Dataset(GMSD), a large-scale long-tailed semantic segmentation dataset comprising 21 fine-grained categories with a spatial resolution of 0.8 m. Then, a critical threshold is identified to define head and tail classes based on empirical performance variations in different proportions, thus providing a unified foundation for balanced learning and consistent evaluation. Finally, we propose the Geographical Adjacent Perception-based (GeoAP) framework, which leverages boundary-dependent relationships between land cover types to achieve robust predictions. Specifically, GeoAP first explicitly models inter-class contextual adjacency and incorporates a dynamic tail sample container, expanding tail-category distribution coverage while preserving geographical plausibility. After that, it further captures spatial adjacency intensity to guide unbiased learning and suppress implausible predictions. Based on our partition strategy, extensive experiments on GMSD, GID, and FBP datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed GeoAP. The code and dataset will be released at https://github.com/skyflyi/GeoAPSegmentation.

title: Multidimensional Analysis of the Diagnostic Performance of Machine Learning Models in Disease Classification: Implications for Clinical Decision Support Systems
authors: Michał Młodawski, Stanisław Deniziak, Mirosław Płaza, Aleksandra Kwiatkowska, Dawid Jaszczyk, Arkadiusz Gajda, Karol Twardowski
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Clinical decision support systems face a fundamental challenge in the form of incomplete medical data. This study presents a methodology for designing an expert system for classifying disease entities according to ICD-10. The system operates under conditions of incomplete clinical data and diagnostic uncertainty. A total of 1,655 clinical cases from internal medicine (n = 616) and pediatrics (n = 1,039) were analyzed. Nineteen classification algorithms were compared. A two-stage diagnostic model (TOP-1 and TOP-3), reflecting clinical practice, was applied. The main hypothesis assumed that machine learning algorithms can effectively compensate for diagnostic uncertainty through mechanisms of differential diagnosis. In internal medicine, Gradient Boosting achieved the highest performance (84.74% accuracy), whereas in pediatrics the Multi-Layer Perceptron performed best (87.20% accuracy). Pediatrics demonstrated higher overall performance (mean TOP-3: 81.99%) than internal medicine (77.43%). The TOP-3 mechanism ensured a probability of identifying the correct diagnosis exceeding 94% in pediatrics and 91% in internal medicine, which substantially enhances the clinical safety of the system. The identification of different optimal algorithms for each specialty confirms the need to tailor modeling strategies to the clinical context. On external validation on 120 cases from a separate facility, treated as the primary benchmark of real-world performance, TOP-1 accuracy was 73.33% (internal medicine) and 78.33% (pediatrics), with TOP-3 accuracy of 86.67% and 90.00% respectively, and SHAP and LIME confirming clinically relevant predictions. The TOP-3 methodology developed constitutes an important contribution to the design of decision support systems under limited data availability.
