title: Meta-Spectroscopy of the Rayleigh Wave Scattering on Topological Structures of a Surface Roughness
authors: V. N. Chukov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: Solution of the theoretical problem of the surface acoustic Rayleigh wave scattering on deterministic three-dimensional cylindrical solid surface roughness, having spatial statistical symmetry of arbitrary order and containing lattice of discontinuities, in the Rayleigh-Born approximation of the perturbation theory in a roughness amplitude is used for theoretical modeling of the new forms of scattering spectrum, unknown in a literature earlier, in the present work. These scattering spectra can be widely distributed or well localized in a broad range of wavelength: from the Rayleigh up to the Laue-Bragg-Wulff limits of scattering on arbitrary nonperiodic lattices, in a general case, of roughness discontinuities.

title: A Novel Methodology Design to Predict Heart Disease with Risk Factor Evaluation using Hybrid Deep Learning
authors: J.Sarojini Premalatha, S.Gowri
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Heart disease (HD) is among the most prevalent causes of death across the globe and therefore requires proper and precise predictive methods to aid clinical judgment. The most recent developments in artificial intelligence have made data-based diagnosis possible, but a large number of current methods make heavy use of convolutional or recurrent neural networks, which do not give optimal results with structured clinical data and are not interpretable. This paper hypothesizes a new hybrid deep learning framework to predict HD with a strong focus on the holistic assessment of risk factors without relying on the traditional CNN and LSTM frameworks. The paradigm incorporates the strong data preprocessing, ensemble feature selection, AutoEncoder-Transformer fusion, attention-based TabNet learning, and residual multilayer perceptron to represent strong interdependencies between clinical variables. Moreover, explainable AI mechanisms are also provided to increase transparency and clinical trust. The proposed model was tested on a stratified cross-validation of an improved HD dataset and found a better accuracy value of 97.25%, a high value of precision and recall, and F1-score. The experimental findings prove that the hybrid architecture is more effective compared to the traditional machine learning (ML) models and isolated deep learning variants. There is a combination of explainability and risk-oriented analysis that makes the suggested framework applicable to the real-world healthcare setting, providing predictive accuracy and practical information to detect HD at an early stage.

title: A Collaborative Mission Planning Method for Mars Sampling
authors: Jian Gao, Jie Ji, Hao Wang, Haiyang Zhang, Zhiyun Tan, Xin Zou, Jian Tian, Jinpeng Song
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: China has proposed a Mars sampling return mission, which is expected to be launched around 2030. The Mars sampling task faces a series of challenges, such as extreme low temperature, Mars dust environment and dust devil impact. The probe system must be scheduled to execute sampling and conduct scientific investigations at optimal temporal intervals, which requires a comprehensive mission planning. This paper presents a novel collaborative mission planning method for Mars sampling. Under the low-data-rate, high-latency communication conditions, this method ensures both the security and autonomy of Mars sampling missions. Firstly, we give an overview of Mars orbiter and Mars lander system, which support key functionalities including communication, sampling, control and data management. Secondly, a framework for mission and submission planning is established, which involves ground-based decision-making at critical stages within the workflow to minimize operational risks. Consequently, a set of experiments is conducted by utilizing a simplified sampling platform to validate the feasibility of the method. The results demonstrate that the task execution time and the current of the robotic arm are consistent with the mission planning results, establishing a robust foundation for future Mars sample return mission.

title: Research the Error Probability of Decoding Quasicyclic Codes in Error Burst Correction
authors: M. N. Isaeva, A. A. Ovchinnikov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper examines the correction of error bursts whose length exceeds the error burst correction capacity of codes, using dense information sets. Reed-Solomon codes, which are widely used for error burst correction, were considered as errorcorrecting codes. In addition, block permutation Gilbert codes were examined, as well as quasi-cyclic codes, which have a blockcirculant structure. The influence of the circulant weight values on the possibility of finding dense information sets was demonstrated. Experiments were conducted which demonstrate that the use of dense information sets allows for the correction of error bursts of more length than the code's correction capacity.

title: Application of Engineering Parameter Measurement Technology in Mars Exploration
authors: Xin Zou, ZeZhou Sun, Yang Jia, Jian Gao, Ying Li, ChengZhi Han, ZhiHui Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: The Tianwen-1 probe was China’s first Mars exploration mission and achieved three types of missions orbiting, landing and roving - by a single launch. The comprehensive and reliable acquisition of engineering measurement data for the first Mars exploration mission played a crucial role in the inversion evaluation of some design elements for the first entry into Mars, the reference for the improvement of related designs for subsequent Mars exploration missions, the improvement of the ground-based monitoring capabilities for key on-orbit links, and the enhancement of the engineering presentation of the Mars exploration mission. In light of the characteristics of China’s first Mars lander and rover mission, an engineering parameter measurement system has been designed, which includes the collection and storage of environmental data such as aerodynamic force, aerothermal, and impact data during the entry, descent and landing phase of Mars, as well as image information for each key phase from the off-orbit landing to the rover phase. This paper presents the mission analysis, functional composition, design and on-orbit application results of the system. The large amount of flight data obtained can support the design of subsequent Mars and other deep space explorations.

title: An Intelligent Secure Financial Document Classification Framework Using CNN and OCR-Extracted Text
authors: Sonali Mondal, Moti Ranjan Tandi, Sotivoldiyeva Sarvinoz Kahramon Kizi, Abo Tabik Al Hussein Abdulmunem Ahmed, Varalatchoumy M, M. Murali
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Banking and financial institutions need to have the correct and safe automated categorization of sensitive records, including invoices, tax forms, and bank statements. Standard rule-based systems and simplistic machine learning models are commonly unable to handle unstructured document layout, template variability, and Optical Character Recognition-induced noise, and therefore scale poorly and achieve low classification rates. To solve these problems, introduce a privacy-aware convolutional neural network architecture to classify financial documents based on text extracted by Optical Character Recognition. In the proposed pipeline, document images are converted to machine-readable text via OCR, followed by preprocessing steps, including token cleaning, normalization, and word embedding, to minimize recognition errors and noise. The convolutional neural network architecture is designed to identify contextual and semantic patterns in text sequences, enabling high-accuracy classification across a wide range of document types. To ensure compliance with financial data protection requirements, the framework incorporates mechanisms for data security, encrypted and controlled model storage, and a controlled inference process during training and deployment. The experimental findings indicate that the Secure convolutional neural network - Financial Document Classification is more accurate, efficient in processing, and more resistant to OCR noise than baseline classifiers. The proposed framework will facilitate scalable, secure, and automated financial document management across cloud and on-premises environments.

title: An Intelligent Crop Selection using Machine Learning for Precision Agriculture
authors: Nallakumar R, Anjana S, Sathyanarayanan S R, Akash R, Monika R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The global population is growing and there is increasing uncertainty in agriculture due to climate change, and given this, farmers can no longer afford to be solely reliant on traditional methods/practices that they have inherited. This decision making approach without real-time data-driven intelligence usually results in lower crop yields, wasted resources, and higher financial risk, and consequently, a new approach is needed. In this work, we propose an AI-powered crop recommendation application that aims to address this challenge, and the application makes use of machine learning to provide farmers with real-time, hyper-local intelligence on the most optimal crop to plant based on specific field conditions. Specifically, it takes into account the key soil parameters, such as Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium, and pH, and local climate conditions, including Temperature, Humidity, and Rainfall, to predict the most suitable crop for a given field. The application will deliver actionable intelligence to farmers via a simple mobile app, and moreover, the Random Forest classifier has been used to make predictions on crop suitability, and the model has been evaluated using a popular agriculture dataset with strong results, with a prediction accuracy of more than 98%.

title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence and Technology in Enhancing the Position of Middle States in the International System
authors: Fahad Alsaffi, Hamza Bouzoubaa, Rashed Alrasheed, Nawfal Dyaa Thalj
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The contemporary international system is increasingly shaped by technological innovation, with artificial intelligence (AI) emerging as a strategic instrument for enhancing state influence and autonomy. This study examines the role of AI and modern technology in strengthening the international standing of middle powers, focusing on how digital capabilities, governance, and infrastructure enable these states to navigate global competition. Employing a descriptiveanalytical approach, the research draws on case studies of European and Asian middle powers, including Austria, Switzerland, Finland, Singapore, and South Korea, and analyzes policy documents, government reports, and secondary literature. Findings indicate that AI enhances economic competitiveness, digital diplomacy, and institutional efficiency, while challenges persist in regulatory capacity, ethical governance, and technological dependency. The study concludes that middle powers can leverage AI strategically to achieve balanced international influence and sustainable digital sovereignty.

title: Mars Exploration Sensor Measurement System Design and Implementation
authors: Ying Li, Xin Zou, Chengzhi Han, Wei Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: To acquire the mechanical and thermal characteristics during the Entry, Descent, and Landing (EDL) process of Mars exploration missions, a Mars Sensor measurement system was designed. In this system, pressure sensors were implemented using a distributed system design. Through pressure tubes, the boundary flow field pressure was introduced into the pressure transducers to collect flow field pressure data from the outer surface of the lander. Temperature sensors were specifically designed for the heat shield and structural characteristics of the heat shield and backshell. Two types of installation structures, a piston-type and a flexible-type, were developed to measure the temperature data at different depths of the probe during the EDL phase. To verify the impact of local orifices on the overall safety of the probe and to prevent hightemperature gas from entering the lander cabin through openings in the heat shield, ground wind tunnel simulation tests were conducted. On May 15, 2021, the Tianwen-1 Mars probe successfully acquired pressure and temperature data during the EDL process. The in-flight data obtained provides significant guidance for the aerodynamic, thermal control, and structural design of future Mars exploration missions.

title: Computer Modeling of Pulsed X-ray Sources in Industrial Radiography
authors: Vladimir Kuvshinnikov, Evgeny Kovshov, Andrey Sudarev
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This article addresses the task of modeling portable pulsed X-ray sources for an industrial radiography simulator in training applications using virtual reality. In order to create digital twins of such pulsed systems, their unique spectral output is modeled, which significantly differs from that of constant-voltage devices. A simplified mathematical model has been developed and validated through computational experiments, employing a piecewise-linear approximation of voltage and current pulse shapes, enabling an analytical solution for the integrated spectral intensity. As a result of parameterization for a typical pulsed system and evaluation using relevant standard exposure nomograms, the model demonstrated a symmetric mean absolute percentage error (SMAPE) of 6.86%, with optical density values within the normatively permissible range. One of the most beneficial and useful features of the virtual reality simulator is to visually demonstrate the cause-and-effect relationships between the actions and decisions of a specialist-user and the quality of the resulting X-ray image, while simultaneously developing the necessary professional skills for the safe and effective execution of nondestructive testing operations. The results obtained indicate that the developed simplified model possesses sufficient accuracy for addressing educational tasks through integration into a virtual reality simulator. The model can also be applied in tasks related to increasing diversity and balancing datasets when training AI-based systems.

title: Transitive Priors for LLM Weight Initialization in Spatiotemporal Transport Analytics
authors: N. G. Kuftinova, O. A. Tetchenko, A. V. Ostroukh, I. A. Artemyev, A. A. Podberezkin
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This article discusses an approach to initializing weights of language models based on the analysis of structural properties of data related to transitivity of relations in transport networks. In the context of the study, the “set of transitive numbers” is understood as a formalized representation of the quantitative characteristics of transitive relationships (e.g., node accessibility, cumulative delays, cascading effects in logistics chains), which can be used to construct informative initial weight distributions. This approach allows taking into account a priori knowledge about the topology of transport infrastructure and movement dynamics already at the initialization stage, minimizing the number of convergence iterations and increasing the model's resistance to data noise. The paper formalizes a set of numerical characteristics reflecting the degree of transitivity of relations in transport networks, develops an algorithm for mapping these characteristics into the space of initial weights of a neural network architecture, and evaluates the impact of the proposed initialization on the convergence rate and the quality of solving traffic prediction and incident classification problems. Experimental results show that the proposed transitive-based initialization preserves gradient stability, improves forecasting accuracy by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${2. 1 \%}$</tex> (MAE: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${8. 7 4} \rightarrow {0. 5 6}$</tex>), and accelerates convergence by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${1 5}-{2 0}{\%}$</tex> compared to standard methods such as Xavier and Kaiming. The obtained results contribute to the development of domain-based adaptation methods for language models in transport logistics and urban mobility management.

title: Crypto-Enhanced RPL: An AI-Assisted Intrusion Detection and Lightweight Cryptographic Framework for Securing IoT Networks
authors: Gollapudi Mounika, V. Raghavendra Sai, V. Yeshaswini, P. Hemalatha, A. Sai Sharan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has made it possible to deploy large numbers of interconnected devices in various application domains such as smart cities, agriculture, healthcare, and industrial automation. These networks are usually implemented using Low-Power and Lossy Networks (LLNs), where the Routing Protocol for Low-Power and Lossy Networks (RPL) is predominantly used due to its efficiency in such networks. However, RPL is found to be vulnerable to various types of routing attacks such as sinkhole attack, rank manipulation attack, selective forwarding attack, and hello flood attack. These types of attacks are likely to compromise the integrity of data transmitted through such networks. This paper proposes a Crypto-Enhanced RPL framework, which is a dual-layered adaptive security solution aimed at enhancing the overall security of RPL-based IoT networks.The Authenticated Encryption with Associated Data (AEAD) mode of operation is used for securing critical RPL control messages. On the other hand, Elliptic Curve Diffie–Hellman (ECDH) is used to establish a common session key among nodes in the network for secure communication. To ensure the energy efficiency of devices in a resource-constrained network, an adaptive cryptographic policy is used. It dynamically chooses a suitable encryption algorithm from a set of lightweight encryption techniques such as ChaCha20-Poly1305, SIMON, or PRESENT based on the energy levels of nodes in the network, network roles, and perceived threat conditions.Furthermore, a TinyML-based intrusion detection technique continuously monitors routing operations in the network and identifies anomalies in routing operations, including abnormal rank changes in nodes, abnormal transmission of control messages in the network, and inconsistencies in routing operations. Once an intrusion in the network is detected, the proposed framework isolates the nodes with malicious activities and initiates an adaptive RPL repair process [18]. Simulation results show that the proposed framework improves network security, reliability of packet transmission in the network, and computational overhead in the network.

title: Deep Feature Reuse for Monkeypox Skin Lesion Classification with DenseNet121
authors: Shyam Sunder Pabboju, S. Selvakanmani, R Sujitha, Avani Mittal
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study proposes a deep learning-based classification approach in collaboration with the DenseNet121 architecture to classify Monkeypox lesions and normal skin images. A publicly available dataset of Monkeypox skin images was utilized, and a lesion-aware preprocessing technique that involves color normalization, contrast enhancement, denoising, and region of interest (ROI) extraction was employed to reduce the background variability and emphasize the relevant features. The DenseNet121 architecture was utilized along with the ImageNet pre-trained model, and it was employed as a frozen model. Experimental evaluation on a held-out test set showed good performance with an overall accuracy of 93% with high precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{F 1}$</tex>-scores in both classes. Confusion matrix analysis showed very few false negatives, which is important in the context of screening applications. The results confirm the effectiveness of DenseNet121 combined with a domain-specific preprocessing to obtain an effective and reliable solution for automated Monkeypox skin lesion classification, which is applicable in clinical decision support and resource-limited diagnostic environments.

title: Markerless Assessment of Rehabilitation Exercise Quality from Monocular Video
authors: Vera Petrosyan, Vladislav Agapov, Artem Pavlikov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper presents a markerless method for assessing the quality of rehabilitation exercises from monocular RGB video in remote rehabilitation settings. The proposed approach uses BlazePose skeletal keypoints as input and includes coordinate normalization and filtering, computation of geometric and kinematic movement metrics, active phase detection, and threshold-based analysis of exercise execution. The method enables automatic identification of movement deviations and generation of a quantitative quality score. An experimental prototype was validated on a standardized upper-limb task involving shoulder flexion to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0}^{{\circ}}$</tex> with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex> participants and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5}$</tex> repetitions per participant. The results showed stable quantitative motion assessment, with an average angular error of about 2°, limits of agreement within 10°, and real-time performance on end-user devices, which supports the practical applicability of the proposed video-based assessment pipeline in remote rehabilitation systems.

title: Poverty Mapping in Zamboanga City using Geospatial Analysis and Ensemble Models
authors: Jamal A. Al Badi, Jerard J. Regalado, Stephanie Mei A. Bobon, Marverick O. Francisco, Marvic A. Lines
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Poverty mapping plays a critical role in evidence-based planning and targeted social interventions, yet traditional survey-based approaches are costly, infrequent, and limited in spatial coverage. This study proposes a machine learning–driven poverty mapping framework for Zamboanga City, Philippines, integrating publicly available geospatial data with barangay-level socioeconomic indicators. Three predictive models which are Random Forest, CatBoost, and a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), were developed and evaluated to assess trade-offs between accuracy, interpretability, and operational feasibility. Results show that tree-based ensemble models outperformed the CNN, with CatBoost achieving the highest predictive accuracy, while Random Forest demonstrated competitive performance with greater interpretability. Spatial analysis revealed that poverty patterns in Zamboanga City are strongly associated with infrastructure access, geographic isolation, and uneven urban development across the city. The findings demonstrate the potential of combining official poverty statistics with geospatial intelligence to generate scalable, cost-effective, and spatially granular poverty estimates. While constrained by the availability of up-to-date socioeconomic data, the proposed framework provides a replicable foundation for supporting local government units in planning, targeting, and monitoring poverty reduction programs.

title: Building Information Modeling-Enabled Safety Digital Twin for Proactive Construction Safety and Schedule Reliability
authors: Abdullateef Ali, Saleem Ahmed Al-Azazi, AAA Ghaleb, Amin Saif, Farag Qasem, Ezzaldeen Al-Tayar
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Construction projects are under rising pressure to deliver reliable schedules while meeting stronger safety and governance expectations. However, safety analytics are often disconnected from building information models and project controls, making it difficult to quantify how severe incidents translate into delay and indirect cost exposure. This paper presents a reproducible workflow that links structured hazard and control information management, data-driven severeinjury archetypes mined from publicly available reports, and a Monte Carlo disruption simulator that outputs percentile-based contingencies and tail-risk measures. Using construction records from the Occupational Safety and Health Administration severe injury reporting program (2015-2023), we derive interpretable injury archetypes based on event, source, injury nature, and body part descriptors and use them to parameterize four digitalmaturity scenarios ranging from conventional safety management to an integrated safety digital twin. Across 2 0, 0 0 0 simulation iterations for a representative specialty-trade project profile, the integrated scenario reduces both expected disruption and worst-case outcomes, compressing high-percentile delay and indirect cost distributions. The proposed approach is unique in combining standards-aligned information requirements with transparent, auditable risk quantification, enabling project teams to translate proactive safety actions into schedule buffers and governance-ready risk metrics.

title: A Multimodal Emotion-Aware AI Chatbot using SER and RAG-based LLMs for Real-Time Mental Health Support
authors: S. P. Chokkalingam, M. Ganesh
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Mental health problems are occurring more frequently now, but many people cannot find adequate help immediately. The proposed solution is called MindEase (an AI health Chatbot). MindEase is an interactive AI (Artificial Intelligence) Companion that is available around-the-clock for user’s emotional well being. MindEase’s method of operation is different from many other AI health Chatbots. MindEase uses Generative AI and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to provide responses based on its knowledge of multiple forms of mental health patterns using a validated mental health database. Using this kind of generative AI allows MindEase to have comforting recommendations as well as contextually accurate. MindEase offers traditional text interface and real-time voice, thus providing the capability for efficient use of user sentiment analysis using voice and text. In addition, MindEase has incorporated crisis routing algorithms to ensure users can be referred to a professional for appropriate care; daily customized breathing exercises; and motivational assistance daily. Although MindEase does not replace professional therapy; the system operates without judgment, it’s simply a companion for the user to manage user’s emotional state, and to provide tools for better self-care.

title: Advanced Machine Learning Models for Identification of Network Intrusion for Enhanced Cybersecurity
authors: Vishakha Tomar, Dr.Shashikant Pandey
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: In an era of ubiquitous connectivity and enterprise digital transformation, network security against increasing cyber threats is crucial. Network Security Intrusion Detection Systems (NSIDS) are crucial for network security. This paper suggests an improved ML-based IDS to improve cybersecurity based on the NSL-KDD benchmark dataset of 125,973 and 43 features. The suggested methodology involved data preprocessing, class balancing by means of the Random Over Sampler, feature selection on Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE), as well as classifying with the help of the Random Forest and Decision Tree models. The dataset is divided into training and evaluation for train and evaluate the models. The experimental outcomes show that the RF classifier outperforms the DT classifier on all evaluation measures, with the RF classifier scoring 0.9998 as opposed to 0.9997. The high detection power and low incidence of false predictions of the suggested models are further shown by the confusion matrix and ROC analysis. The suggested framework performs better in terms of intrusion detection efficiency and reliability than the existing ML and DL techniques.

title: AI-Driven Simulation and Prediction of Urban Microclimate Dynamics using GNNS and PINNS
authors: M. Sobhana, Tejaswi Kondabolu, Surya Sanjay Damuluri, Chandrika Kanuru, Smitha Chowdary Ch
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Rapid urbanization has significantly altered local climate patterns, resulting in increased urban heat islands (UHI), elevated land surface temperatures, and environmental degradation, all of which impact public health, energy consumption, and urban sustainability. Urban areas are usually warmer than nearby rural areas because of factors like reduced vegetation, excessive concrete surfaces, and intensified human activities, leading to increased energy demand, air pollution, and heat-related diseases. Traditional climate models struggle with fine-scale accuracy due to low-resolution data and generalized assumptions, while many predictive models fail to consider spatial dependencies and physical laws governing climate behaviour. The primary objective of this research is to create a hybrid spatio-temporal model that includes GNNs and PINNs to classify UHI effectively and make precise forecasts. To overcome these limitations, a high-resolution urban climate simulation framework is developed using Google Earth Engine (GEE), Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), and Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs). Using 10 years of satellite data from Vijayawada, Guntur, and Visakhapatnam, the system analyses key environmental indices such as NDVI, NDWI, SAVI, NDBI, and UHI. GNNs are used to model spatial relationships between regions, whereas PINNs integrate physical laws for improved prediction accuracy. The experimental results show that the proposed model obtained 97.7% test accuracy and makes stable long-term forecasts. A user-friendly interface visualizes these simulations using colour scales, histograms, and descriptive summaries, making the insights easily interpretable for stakeholders. This system offers detailed climate simulations and 10-year projections, helping urban planners, policymakers, and researchers devise effective heat mitigation strategies, improve green space planning, and support environmental sustainability through accurate AI-powered geospatial analysis.

title: Development of the Computer Geometry course in the direction of graduate training Mathematics and Computer Science
authors: Andrey Zakharov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper examines the main approaches to developing the Computer Geometry course in the direction of graduate training Mathematics and Computer Science. The course is studied during the third year of the Bachelor program, its extent is 3 ECTS points, and it is divided into two modules. The paper describes the course’s basic content, which includes two-dimensional and three-dimensional computer graphics software technologies, mathematical methods and algorithms for constructing and rendering geometric shapes, the general processing steps of the viewing pipeline, geometric transformations such as translation, rotation, and scaling, as well as lighting models and texturing of surfaces. The paper highlights the distinctive features of the course for the following educational program specializations: Mathematical and Computer Modeling, Mathematical and Computer Modeling in Aerospace Engineering (Industry), Supercomputer Modeling and Artificial Intelligence in Engineering Problems, and Mathematical and Computer Modeling in Economics. Students in these specializations learn graphics programming using the <canvas>, WebGL, WebGPU, and Three.js graphics libraries, GLSL ES and WebGPU shading languages, glMatrix and webgpu-matrix libraries for vector and matrix operations. The paper also provides lists of practical exercises, including their duration and a brief description of their content, as well as the general content of midterm examination questions and description of different types of homework assignments.

title: AI Enabled Upskilling and Work Engagement in Sustainable HRM: A Study of Education Employees in the Mekong Delta, Vietnam
authors: Hong Minh Ngoc Tran, Thuy Truc Nguyen
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: AI enabled upskilling is increasingly adopted in the educational institutions to build the capability of the workforce as part of sustainable human resource management. This research examines the relationship between four dimensions of AI enabled upskilling dimensions and work engagement of education employees in the Mekong Delta, Vietnam. Survey data were gathered at universities and colleges in the Mekong Delta, Vietnam in January 2026 of 241 staff and lecturers using snowball recruitment methods. The study measured personalization fit, adaptive learning quality, AI feedback quality, and practice simulation support, along with work engagement, using multi item scales. Scale reliability was determined using internal consistency testing and results of an exploratory factor analysis verified a four-factor structure for the independent variables and a one factor structure for work engagement. Multiple linear regression results showed that the four AI enabled upskilling dimensions all have positive and significant impacts on work engagement, with the quality of feedback from the AI having the strongest association followed by the AI quality of adaptive learning, AI quality of personalization fit and AI quality of practice simulation support. The model explains a meaningful proportion of variance in engagement implying that appropriately designed AI supported development may help strengthen involvement of employees in education workplaces. Practical implications include improving the actionability of AI feedback, strengthening adaptive learning pathways, aligning recommendations with job roles, and expanding task based simulations.

title: An Intelligent DDPG Framework for Cost-Efficient Task Scheduling in Multi-Cloud Environment
authors: Sonam Marathe, C Veena, Johnson Kolluri, V Sravan Kiran, Santhosh Kumar Medishetti, Raveendrababu Vempati
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The scheduling of tasks in multi-cloud environments has proven a challenging issue because of the heterogeneous availability of resources, dynamic workloads, and optimization of various conflicting performance metrics. Conventional scheduling algorithms are not always effective in the adaptation to the real-time changes in the demand of the resources and lead to higher costs of operation and latency, as well as, execution time. In order to overcome these difficulties, this paper suggests a Multi-Objective Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-based adaptive task scheduling framework based on the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) algorithm. The suggested model maximizes the critical performance measures such as resource cost, memory consumption, latency and execution time using a weighted multi-objective reward function. The scheduling model is assessed with the help of Google Cloud workload traces in the CloudSim simulation environment to model multi-cloud realistic settings. As it has been proven in experimental studies, the proposed DDPG-based scheduling method is significantly more effective than the classical optimization algorithms like Genetic Algorithm (GA), Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). The model attains an average of 24.2% increase in resource cost, 19.0% in memory usage, 29.6% in latency reduction and 28.6% in execution time when compared to the current methods. The adaptive decision-making and resource distribution of heterogeneous cloud platforms, DDPG has a continuous learning capability and actor-critic architecture, which allow efficient resource allocation. The results justify that the presented Reinforcement Learning (RL) framework featuring as a multi-objective approach can be used as a scalable intelligent and high-performance system of next-generation adaptive scheduling of tasks in dynamically changing multi-cloud computation conditions.

title: Attention-Balanced Variational Autoencoder Framework for Credit Card Fraud Detection under Extreme Class Imbalance
authors: Mukkara Indira, Rasta Mahaboob Basha, Pamisetty Sivamma, Mastik Suresh Babu, Seemakurthy Hupesh Naga Ketan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The extreme imbalance of classes is a major challenge to credit card fraud detection in real-world financial transaction data because the fraudulent transactions form less than 0.2 percent of the records. Traditional supervised and generative models are all biased to the majority-class, and they cannot well represent the rare patterns of fraud in the latent feature space. Attention-Balanced Variational Autoencoder (AB-VAE) The paper presents the Attention-Balanced Variational Autoencoder (AB-VAE), a single end-to-end model that incorporates feature-wise attention in the encoder-decoder structure of a Variational Autoencoder and jointly optimizes the model with an imbalance-sensitive loss. The attention mechanism literally enhances minority-sensitive characteristics in the latent representation learning stage, whereas attention-balanced loss, which consists of reconstruction, KL-divergence regularization, attention regularization, and class-weighted classification words, avoid the dominance of majority-classes through the training process. The European Credit Card Fraud (ECC) and IEEE-CIS datasets experiments show that the Recall of AB-VAE is equal to 0.812, AUC-PR equals 0.842, and MCC equals 0.846, and it remains higher than that of the imbalance-handling, generative, attention-based, and hybrid deep learning baselines. The analysis of ablation proves that the attention mechanism and balancing loss are the complementary parts that should be considered indispensable. The weight visualization attention also confirms that the model is selective on the features of fraud-discriminative transactions which maximizes the sensitivity of minority detection and model interpretability.

title: TRIDENT-NET: A Multi-Stream Attention-based Hybrid Intrusion Detection Framework for Securing IoMT Networks
authors: Parkavi S, Saravanan V, Sangeetha A, Abiramikayathiri S, Ramkumar M P, Emil Selvan G S R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) has increased the demand for effective intrusion detection systems capable of defending critical healthcare infrastructures against sophisticated cyberattacks. However, existing single-stream deep learning models often struggle to simultaneously capture both spatial and temporal anomalies present in heterogeneous network traffic patterns. To address this limitation, this study proposes TRIDENT-NET, a novel multi-stream intrusion detection framework that integrates a 1D Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN), Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (BiGRU), and Autoencoder through a dynamic Multi-Head Attention Mechanism. The proposed fusion strategy adaptively emphasizes critical threat signatures while suppressing irrelevant noise, enabling more accurate and robust intrusion detection. Furthermore, the model is optimized using the Optuna Bayesian optimization framework to achieve optimal hyperparameter configurations. The effectiveness of TRIDENT-NET is extensively evaluated using three benchmark datasets: CICIoMT2024, ECU-IoHT, and WUSTL-EHMS. Experimental results demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed framework, achieving a binary classification accuracy of 99.57% and a multi-class classification accuracy of 98.34% on the large-scale CICIoMT2024 dataset. Additionally, TRIDENT-NET exhibits remarkable robustness in handling class imbalance within the WUSTL-EHMS dataset, outperforming existing state-of-the-art approaches. The framework consistently delivers high detection accuracy, reliability, and generalization across diverse healthcare network environments.

title: Transport Scene Image De-Noising with Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Kamil R. Kharrasov, Egor A. Osipov, Marina S. Moseva
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of restoring images degraded by adverse weather noise in traffic surveillance systems. Such distortions, including raindrops, heavy rain, snow, and fog, significantly reduce image quality and impair the performance of downstream object detection algorithms. To solve this problem, a method based on a modified generative adversarial network (GAN) architecture is proposed. The approach combines two Deep Convolutional GAN models built on U-Net and U-Net-RCBAM structures for sequential noise localization and image restoration. The All-Weather dataset was used for training and evaluation. The effectiveness of the proposed method was assessed using the PSNR and SSIM image quality metrics, as well as through experiments with the YOLOv11n vehicle detection model. The results demonstrate that the developed method improves visual image quality while preserving acceptable processing speed and increases detection accuracy under adverse weather conditions.

title: FGSat: A New Dataset and Performance Benchmark for Fine-Grained Satellite Classification
authors: Yifan Li, Jiayu He, Yang Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Fine-grained satellite classification provides essential information for on-orbit servicing, formation flying, and active debris removal, as it enables discrimination between structurally similar satellites. However, existing public datasets are confined to single targets and lack fine-grained annotations, diversity, and realism, limiting model generalization in real orbital conditions. To bridge this gap, we present a high-fidelity simulation and auto-annotation framework that systematically generates photorealistic and diverse training imagery. Based on this framework, we construct FGSat, the first large-scale benchmark dataset for fine-grained satellite classification, encompassing multiple satellites across Earth observation and scientific missions. We further establish a standardized benchmark using multi-scale YOLO models, reporting recognition, classification, and complexity metrics. The best model achieves an mAP@50:95 of 0.95 and 0.92 classification overall accuracy. In resource-constrained settings, YOLOv13n achieves 0.82 mAP @ 50: 95 with 55.70 ms CPU latency, demonstrating deployment feasibility. In addition, we analyze class-level error patterns and confusion, highlighting the fine-grained challenges captured by FGSat. The FGSat dataset will be available at https://github.com/evannli1/FGSat.

title: A Novel Privacy-Preserving Structure for Emergency Communication Systems: Homomorphic Encryption for Safe and Robust Edge-Cloud Computing Disaster Management
authors: M. Subalatha, Parveen Banu S, M Kanniga Parameshwari, Babitha Lincy, Mahmoud Odeh, D. Rajeshwari
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: During times of emergency, communication systems are critical for organizing recovery functionality; dissemination warnings; and real-time data transmission from first responders, emergency management infrastructure to affected populations. Nonetheless, the trustworthiness, privacy, and reliability of these communication systems are regularly compromised during emergencies through cyber-attacks (industrial espionage or terrorism), failures of critical infrastructure including unauthorized data release. The rise of edge-cloud computing as a lucrative platform for real-time decision-making and data analytics in disaster management requires strong privacy-preserving solutions. Based on this, we present a privacy-preserving architecture for emergency communication systems using homomorphic encryption technique bringing the capability of computing secure-text over-encrypted data (preserving confidentiality). The placement of edge nodes facilitates low-latency data acquisition and processing, whereas the cloud platforms enhance resource-intensive analytics, decision support, or coordination. To protect the privacy of sensitive content (e.g., where about, health metrics, or situational imagery) by running homomorphic encryption locally at the edge before transmitting data to cloud. It allows for important calculations such as pattern discovery, resource planning and routing in emergencies to be carried out confidentially over encrypted datasets without the need to release decrypted sensitive information during any analytic process. The hybrid cloud-edge infrastructure balances workloads, offloading real-time tasks to edge devices while delegating complex analysis to the power of cloud services, providing a resilient and vastly scalable system. While providing level security with Leveled-Homomorphic encryption the computational efficiency of these algorithms also respects practical latency requirements for emergency communication scenarios. The framework is further complemented by a combination of privacy-preserving secure data aggregation and scalable federated model training in order to improve situational awareness, while ensuring the sovereignty of data and conforming with privacy regulations. We validate the architecture with simulations and using the application domain of earthquake and flood response case studies. Performance results show that if encryption is enabled, the tool will perform better (some up to 700X), and still it lost zero (or almost) packets and was quite resistant to eavesdropping or Man in The Middle attacks. The approach offers 40% lower data privacy risk than conventional encrypted systems, such as the technique that require decryption and processing of encrypted contents. It sees that it preserves data protection end-to-end within distributed nodes.

title: Phishlyze: A Hybrid AI Framework Combining Gradient Boosting And LLM Analysis For Advanced Phishing Detection
authors: M. Rajasekaran, K Manjunadha Swamy, T Sai Ganesh
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: Quick changes in phishing attempts make sophisticated detection techniques absolutely vital. This work presents a phishing detection system aiming at increasing accuracy and adaptability by combining machine learning with artificial intelligence-driven analysis. Along with Google’s Gemini 2.0 Flash LLM for more in-depth contextual analysis, it makes use of a Gradient Boosting Classifier (GBC) taught on 30 URL characteristics. SMote for class balance and tuned hyperparameters help to improve the model by means of fine- tuning of the detection performance. Two parts characterize the development process: the first phase concentrates on establishing the ML model, which comprises URL categorization, shortlink identification, and phishing pattern analysis; the second phase employs JSP and NetBeans to build a Java-based web application. Three portals comprise the system: an administrative portal for user management, phishing trend analysis and URL classification; a user portal for secure web browsing, suggestions, and automatic phishing alerts; and an attacker simulation site to test phishing detection capability. Python is connected with NetBeans to provide real-time classification and security analysis; an automatic access control method warns or temporarily limits users who surpass limits on browsing harmful URLs. This research presents a very accurate phishing detection system with real-time alarms and enhanced protection against changing threats by combining machine learning with AI-driven analysis.

title: Heritage as a Basis for Predicting the Future Using the Example of Additional Professional Education at the National Research University "MPEI"
authors: Sergey Piliak
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: Succession issues in the field of educational activity, as a rule, are not the subject of scientific understanding and analysis. The active development of educational technologies provokes a researcher to find new forms of educational activity, and forces an employee of an educational organization to pursue a successful professional career, limiting himself to analyzing the work of his closest competitors. At the same time, a careful study of the historical heritage in the field of continuing professional education makes it possible to effectively and reasonably build a development strategy in the future and determine the necessary priorities for activities. The article is devoted to the application of historical heritage data in the current activities of the Moscow Power Engineering Institute in the field of continuing professional education. Special attention is paid to the issues of technical improvement of educational activities. In the modern era, technological development is often perceived as the most important vector for the development of various types of activities. The creation of progressive methods and techniques, perceived as an independent process, often unrelated to tasks, acquires an independent value. Understanding the technological support of the process of additional professional education at the Moscow Power Engineering Institute not only emphasizes the necessity of reasonable goal setting, but also provides opportunities to rely on the past in the development of technological leadership in the future.

title: PAF-DMAO: A Privacy-Aware Federated Diffusion Framework for Multi-Agent Resource Orchestration in 6G NTN-V2X Networks
authors: Abegaz Mohammed Seid, Halima Elbiaze, Muhammet Hevesli, Tilahun M. Getu, Rutvij H. Jhaveri, Pronaya Bhattacharya
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: The convergence of non-terrestrial networks (NTNs) and vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communications is emerging as a key enabler of AI-native 6G intelligent transportation systems, providing seamless connectivity across urban, rural, and remote environments through an integrated space-air-ground architecture. However, efficiently orchestrating communication, computation, and energy resources remains challenging due to high mobility, network heterogeneity, stringent latency requirements, and privacy concerns. To address these issues, we propose PAF-DMAO, a privacy-aware federated diffusion framework that integrates diffusion-based optimization, federated learning, distributed multi-agent coordination, differential privacy, and Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM)-based consensus for intelligent NTN-V2X resource orchestration without sharing sensitive vehicular data. Extensive simulations demonstrate that PAF-DMAO achieves performance comparable to centralized optimization while significantly improving throughput, energy efficiency, scalability, and privacy-utility trade-offs over state-of-the-art baselines. Aligned with AI-native networking and ongoing 3GPP and ETSI standardization efforts.

title: Parallel-Elastic Actuation with Reactive Latch Elevates Robotic Hopping Performance: Jump Height and Continuity_supp2-3701316.mp4
authors: Pakpong Chirarattananon
publish: N/A
abstract: While many animals exhibit impressive hopping capabilities, machines have struggled to match their performance. Current hopping robots face limitations in power density, energy efficiency, and control stability. Here, we present a parallel-elastic actuation mechanism with a reactive latch that optimizes energy transfer, enabling a legged robot to achieve hopping heights and continuity previously unattainable. This mechanism efficiently stores and releases energy, extending the actuation period over the aerial phase while minimizing stance time. Our robot achieves a maximum hopping height of 3.6 meters, surpassing both human and animal records while demonstrating sustained, high-frequency hopping cycles with minimal power requirement. By integrating inertia-based onboard sensorimotor autonomy, we demonstrate stable, controlled hopping in environments without external aid. These results represent a step toward bridging the performance gap between biological and robotic locomotion, with potential to influence the design of future legged systems.

title: Distributed Recursive State Estimation Over Sensor Networks Under the Push-Pull-Based Gossip Protocol: A Locally Minimum Variance Approach
authors: Jiahao Song, Zidong Wang, Qinyuan Liu, Xiao He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of distributed recursive state estimation over sensor networks utilizing the gossip protocol, where communication resources are conserved by allowing each node to randomly select neighboring nodes for data exchange. An easily implementable and scalable mathematical model is developed for the push-pull-based gossip protocol, facilitating bidirectional data exchange. Random variables are introduced to characterize node selection, with their probability distributions considered as customizable protocol parameters. Distributed state estimators integrated with the gossip protocol are subsequently formulated within the framework of recursive estimation. The relationship between estimation error and gossip protocol parameters is analyzed, leading to the derivation of computationally efficient upper bounds for estimation error covariance matrices. Estimator gains are then designed to minimize the traces of these upper bounds, thereby enhancing estimation accuracy. Furthermore, performance analysis is conducted to establish the monotonicity of estimation precision with respect to noise intensity. Finally, numerical simulations are presented to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Low-Rank Representation Learning with Spectral-to-Spatial Aggregation Transformer for Hyperspectral Image Super-Resolution
authors: Shibo Wang, Zhichang Guo, Qiting Li, Yao Li, Yuming Xing
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral image (HSI) faces significant challenges in achieving high resolution (HR) due to the inherent limitations of imaging devices. To tackle this issue, HSI super-resolution (SR) techniques have been introduced to reconstruct HR images from low-resolution (LR) inputs, demonstrating remarkable progress in enhancing spatial fidelity. Notably, the spectral domain of HSIs inherently exhibits low-rank characteristics resulting from strong inter-band correlations. Inspired by this observation, many approaches have been developed to leverage the low-rank property of HSIs for performance improvement. However, most existing methods rely on linear low-rank formulations, which are often insufficient to capture the complex spectral structures of HSIs. Moreover, approaches relying on singular value decomposition (SVD) or tensor factorization usually require explicit decomposition operations, which may increase optimization complexity. In this paper, we propose a nonlinear low-rank representation (LRR) learning strategy combined with a spectral-to-spatial aggregation Transformer for HSI SR tasks. Specifically, the learned nonlinear LRR is embedded into the spatial attention module to exploit both spatial self-similarity and spectral low-rank characteristics, enabling compact and informative representations that preserve fine spatial and spectral details. In parallel, the spectral-to-spatial aggregation Transformer bridges the spectral and spatial domains, enabling more effective integration of spectral priors into spatial feature learning. Furthermore, we incorporate a spectral convolution attention module into our Transformer framework to mitigate the limitation of Transformers in extracting high-frequency information. Experimental results demonstrate that our method effectively reconstructs SR images with sharper edges, finer details, and enhanced spatial-spectral consistency, outperforming existing approaches in both qualitative and quantitative evaluations.

title: SC-LUFormer: A Scenario-Conditioned Transformer for Long-Term Land-Use Prediction from Remote Sensing Data in Mountainous Urban Agglomerations
authors: Hanfei Wu, Rongyao Wang, Yunfei Li, Lingyan Bao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Long-term land-use prediction based on remote sensing-derived data remains challenging in mountainous urban agglomerations because fragmented landscapes, complex terrain constraints, and scenario-dependent differences jointly affect spatial allocation and future projection reliability. To address these challenges, this study proposes a unified scenario-conditioned Transformer framework (SC-LUFormer) for long-term land-use prediction in mountainous urban agglomerations. Rather than introducing an entirely new neural primitive, the proposed framework integrates non-local spatial representation, scenario-aware feature modulation, and change-oriented evaluation into a single modeling framework, in which future socio-environmental scenarios are incorporated into the deep feature space via feature-wise linear modulation (FiLM). Multi-temporal land-use maps derived from remote sensing observations, together with multi-source biophysical and socioeconomic drivers, were used to validate the model through a 2020 backcasting experiment and to generate scenario-based land-use projections for 2040–2100 under multiple CMIP6-based Shared Socioeconomic Pathway (SSP) scenarios in the Central Yunnan Urban Agglomeration, China. Comparative experiments against representative convolutional neural network, machine-learning, and rule-based models show that SC-LUFormer achieves better backcasting performance, with an overall accuracy of 94.88%, a mean intersection-over-union of 85.71%, an area deviation of 3.17%, and a figure of merit of 0.2917 in reconstructing the year 2020. Further interpretability analysis using Grad-CAM and SHAP provides post-hoc indications that SC-LUFormer focuses on urban expansion boundaries and transition zones and produces scenario-responsive driver-attribution patterns under alternative future assumptions.

title: Extra Vascular-Implantable Cardiodefibrillators: The Best of Both Worlds
authors: Jim Banks
publish: IEEE Pulse
abstract: Though their inventor’s vision was at first met with intense skepticism, implantable cardioverter-defibrillators have gone through many iterations to become the standard treatment for ventricular tachyarrhythmias. More than 40 years later, the advent of extravascular devices-EV-ICDs-represents another huge step forward.

title: A New Optimization Algorithm Integrated with Power Flow Analysis for Efficient Shunt Capacitor Bank Allocation in Radial Distribution Networks
authors: Abdelazim Salem, Aymen Flah, Amgad Aboraya
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The main objective of this paper is to enhance the performance of radial distribution networks (RDNs) by developing a new optimization technique integrated with an efficient load flow analysis for the optimal placement and sizing of shunt capacitor banks (SCBs). The key feature of the proposed method lies in the development of an innovative branch-bus incidence matrix (C-Matrix), which enables fast and accurate load flow computation with reduced computational complexity, combined with a mathematical programming-based optimization approach. The enhancement is achieved through power losses reduction, minimization of the annual energy losses cost, network voltage profile improvement, and enhancement of the power factor at the slack bus bar. The proposed technique has been implemented on IEEE 33-bus and IEEE 69-bus RDNs. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach consistently outperforms existing techniques while maintaining high effectiveness. The proposed method achieves a reduction in apparent power losses of 34.61% with annual savings of 11,816 USD and approximate unity power factor (0.9971 lag) at the slack bus bar for the IEEE 33-bus system, while for the IEEE 69-bus system, it achieves a loss reduction of 35.25% with annual savings of 12,857 USD at operating power factor of 0.9917 lag. In addition, a sensitivity analysis under ±20% load variation is conducted to evaluate the robustness of the proposed method. and reliability assessment of IEEE 33-bus RDN is presented which demonstrate by calculation of reliability indices, that RDN is more reliable.

title: A Feasibility-Driven Anytime Hybrid Search for the 3L-CVRP with CoM-Aware Load Balancing
authors: Thi-Huong-Giang Vu, Quoc-Khanh Nong
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper addresses the 3L-CVRP under practical loading constraints arising in multi-stop FMCG distribution. We consider heterogeneous vehicle payload and compartment capacity, non-overlap, orthogonal packing with restricted rotations, last-in-first-out unloading compatibility, fragility constraints, and minimum supporting-area requirements. The proposed method follows a feasible-first anytime design that preserves loading feasibility throughout the search and accounts for center of mass balance through a penalty integrated into the routing objective. The approach first builds a minimum spanning tree over customer locations to guide decomposition, then generates customer clusters via feasibility-driven merging to eliminate unloadable groupings early. Each feasible cluster is refined by an anytime ant colony optimization procedure, followed by greedy route consolidation and relocation-based neighborhood search to reduce routing cost while preserving loading feasibility. The method is evaluated on two benchmark suites (D1 and D2) and an FMCG-oriented dataset (D3) under repeated runs and fixed wall-clock budgets, with TS and ALNS-DBLF as baselines. Across these datasets, the proposed approach achieves 100% feasibility reliability, faster time-to-first-feasible solutions, stronger run-to-run stability, and better load-balance quality. On D1, time-to-first-feasible drops to 1.77 s, while the safe-vehicle ratio reaches 75.81%, with a 9.62% routing-distance penalty relative to the strongest distance baseline on the common feasible subset. On D2, the method maintains 100% checkpoint success at 15 s as instance size increases. On D3, it achieves the best average distance, fewest vehicles, highest fill rate, and highest safe-vehicle ratio. These results show a practical balance between routing efficiency, computational responsiveness, and load-balance quality for time-constrained FMCG distribution.

title: Does Your AI Image Generator Know What a Software Engineer Looks Like? We Checked
authors: Mohamad Kassab
publish: IEEE Reliability Magazine
abstract: Text-to-image (T2I) generators are now embedded in everyday software engineering workflows. Practitioners and educators increasingly use them to generate visuals for course slides, onboarding materials, recruitment pages, and technical documentation. Unlike consumer entertainment use cases, these deployments sit close to professional identity formation; they shape what a software engineer is visually understood to look like, in contexts that affect hiring, education, and team culture.

title: Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Training with Pipelining Optimization_supp1-3702749.pdf
authors: Shaohuai Shi
publish: N/A
abstract: Sparsely activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) has emerged as a key technique to expand the size of Transformer-based large language models (LLMs) while maintaining low computational costs. However, MoE layers require to route the input data to distributed devices, incurring significant communication latency. Existing studies have primarily focused on alleviating this problem by overlapping computation and communication tasks within a single MoE layer, which fails to achieve sufficient overlap and results in limited performance gains. In this work, we introduce an orthogonal partitioning dimension from existing task-parallel methods by leveraging the autoregressive nature of causal Transformer-based LLMs, i.e. partitioning tasks along the sequence dimension. This provides more flexible and efficient overlaps among tasks from both non-MoE and MoE layers. To this end, we propose an efficient MoE training approach, SP-MoE, with two innovative designs. 1) It incorporates non- MoE layers into the overlapping with not only the current MoE layer but also the preceding MoE layer, thereby facilitating more efficient training; 2) It identifies the optimal combination of pipeline degrees for non-MoE and MoE layers and devises the best scheduling plans for load-imbalanced non-MoE and uniform MoE layers to achieve the goal of minimizing the total training latency. Furthermore, we extend our algorithm to incorporate pipeline parallelism (PP) for training larger MoE models, which introduces peer-to-peer (P2P) communications between adjacent PP stages. To mitigate this problem, we propose S3P-MoE with two optimizations: 1) it partitions P2P communication into chunks and each P2P chunk is transmitted immediately to be overlapped with expert computation; 2) it allows the subsequent PP stage to commence upon receiving the corresponding P2P chunk. Moreover, we refine our algorithm to jointly optimize pipeline degrees for both MoE and non-MoE layers, as well as P2P communication, to minimize overall training latency. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SP-MoE and S3P-MoE effectively identify optimal pipeline degrees, reducing training time by up to 34:3% and 30:61%, respectively, compared to stateof- the-art methods.

title: Uniaxially Compressively Strained Ge (100)-OI p-MOSFET for High-Performance CFET
authors: Hyeongrak Lim, Seong Kwang Kim, Hojin Jeong, Jinha Lim, Songhyeon Kuk, Dae-Myeong Geum, Jinho Bae, Jaehyun Park, Daewon Ha, Jaehoon Han, Younghyun Kim, Jaeyong Jeong, Sanghyeon Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: In this work, asymmetric strain relaxation, induced by channel width (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${W}_{\text {ch}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> scaling, offers a scalable lever to realize high-mobility p-type channels for next-generation complementary field-effect transistors (CFETs). We demonstrate that the strained Ge-on-insulator (sGe-OI) p-channel MOSFETs (p-MOSFETs) in which <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${W}_{\text {ch}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> scaling induces quasi-uniaxial compression along [110] via asymmetric strain relaxation is realized by combining strain-relaxed buffer (SRB)-based heteroepitaxy and direct wafer bonding (DWB). Using a 32-nm-thick sGe channel, the quasi-uniaxially compressively sGe (100)/<110>-OI p-MOSFET with a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${W}_{\text {ch}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 90 nm, corresponding to an estimated compressive strain of approximately −0.2% based on Raman peak-shift analysis, achieves a high effective hole mobility (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {eff}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 1154 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s. These results indicate that the sGe channel realized by this approach is a strong candidate for p-MOSFET in high-performance CFET applications.

title: Analysis and Optimization of ESD-induced Soft Failure in a Microcontroller Unit
authors: Jiabei Pan, Ling Zhang, Yipeng Chen, Er-Ping Li, Shurong Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electromagnetic Compatibility
abstract: The continuous scaling of complementary metal oxide semiconductor process technology has necessitated a reduction in operating voltages, thereby diminishing the noise margins of chips. Consequently, the ground bounce induced by electrostatic discharge events is more likely to cause critical power supply fluctuations, leading to soft failures in the chip. To rapidly identify design vulnerabilities, this article developed a power supply noise simulation model and integrated it into the full microcontroller unit (MCU) performance simulation flow to predict noise levels and guide design modifications. A power supply noise measurement method that requires no additional circuitry is further proposed to monitor key signals associated with soft failures on the MCU. Through improvements in down-bond packaging and a redesign of the low-dropout regulator and phase-locked loop circuits, the chip’s soft failure level is enhanced by a factor of more than 10 without increasing die area or degrading chip performance.

title: A Highly Sensitive Dual-Band Metamaterial Sensor for Liquid Adulteration and Biomedical Detection
authors: Raghvenda Kumar Singh, Ashish Gupta, Priyanshu Singh, Anamika Singh, Vidhya Manglam
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: This work presents a novel dual-band terahertz metasurface sensor exhibiting notable performance in refractive index (RI) sensing. The designed structure integrates multiple resonator topologies on a polyimide substrate to achieve dual-band absorption operations with a high <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$Q$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-factor—an essential attribute in sensitive applications. The proposed metasensor is tested for RI sensing in the range of 1–2 with a high resolution of 0.05 and found to be demonstrating strong potential in various sensing domains, including adulteration detection in edible oils and fuels, as well as biomedical sensing such as bacterial and amino acid identification. Numerical results demonstrate that it provides the maximum sensitivities of 150, 155, 152, and 152 GHz/RIU (RI unit) and a figure of merit (FoM) of 6.531, 7.026, 6.597, and 6.596 RIU<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> for the detection of edible oils, fuels, bacteria, and amino acids, respectively. The dual-band nature of the proposed unit cell enhances its versatility, allowing operation in either frequency band, which provides a high degree of freedom as per the requirements. Moreover, it offers highly linear sensitive behavior across different sample thicknesses in the RI range of 1–2, exploiting linear regression analyses. Insensitivity of absorption performance with respect to variations in the polarization and incidence angles augments this sensor’s case for employment in the above applications. Key sensing parameters have been numerically calculated and demonstrate promising results when compared with existing state-of-the-art designs. The fabrication process and implementation setup are discussed to validate the sensor’s real-world applicability. Overall, the suggested metasensor shows promise for applications in advanced liquid adulteration analysis and biomedical sensing.

title: A Lightweight Large-Model Security Framework for Consumer Electronics Based on Sentiment Analysis in Online Consumer Platforms
authors: Peng Xu, Han Zhao, Chinmay Chakraborty, Manisha Guduri, Zhiwei Guo, Amr Tolba
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: With the widespread deployment of consumer electronics and online consumer platforms, user-generated reviews have become a key source of emotional information for consumer-facing intelligent services. Although large language models achieve strong sentiment analysis performance, their high computational cost limits practical deployment in resource-constrained consumer electronics environments requiring real-time responses and efficient resource utilization. To address this issue, this paper proposes a lightweight large-model security framework for consumer electronics based on sentiment analysis in online consumer platforms. The framework integrates the Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) technique into RoBERTa to construct a RoBERTa-LoRA model, enabling efficient fine-tuning with minimal additional parameters while preserving semantic representation capability. Based on this model, a lightweight framework, RoBERTaL, is developed to support real-time sentiment-aware security analysis in consumer-facing applications. Experimental results on a real-world product review dataset show that RoBERTa-LoRA achieves an accuracy of 81.2% with only 0.3M additional parameters, demonstrating improved computational efficiency, deployment feasibility, and effectiveness in mitigating AI-driven opinion manipulation in consumer electronics systems.

title: RF-MAF-AttnGRU: A novel hybrid machine learning model for stock price prediction and algorithmic trading
authors: Xuejiao Zhao, Guanzhi Li, Lingfeng Miao, Choujun Zhan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Accurate forecasting of financial market prices helps investors make financial decisions and even the government formulate appropriate macroeconomic policies in a country. In this paper, we design a new hybrid model called RF-MAF-AttnGRU, combining random forest (RF), moving average filter (MAF), and gated recurrent units (GRUs) based on an attention mechanism. Firstly, the RF is utilized to rank input features, based on their importance. Secondly, a MAF based decomposition procedure is developed to decompose the filtered feature time series into the trend and residual series, which can avoid the data leakage problem. Then, the GRU enhanced by an attention mechanism is used to model these two series. 40 stock datasets are adopted to assess the performance of the hybrid model. The prediction results of the RF-MAF-AttnGRU are compared with five classical prediction models and four state-of-the-art time series prediction models. Four evaluation metrics are used to assess the prediction performance, while the non-parametric testing, including Friedman, Iman–Davenport, and Nemenyi post-hoc test methods, are adopted to evaluate the significance of differences in the prediction performance of each model. Results indicate that the proposed model outperforms other benchmark models.

title: Bistatic SAR Target Recognition Using Transferred Monostatic Knowledge and Cross-Domain Feature Fusion
authors: Mingqing Han, Bing Sun, Zhirong Men, Yiheng Zhou, Yihang Zhi
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Bistatic synthetic aperture radar (SAR) systems offer advantages for target recognition but suffer from severe data scarcity, particularly for maritime targets, which hinders the development of data-driven deep learning methods. To address this, this paper constructs a comprehensive electromagnetic simulation dataset containing both monostatic and bistatic SAR images for three ship classes. Leveraging this dataset, we propose a novel target recognition framework that integrates transfer learning with a cross-domain dynamic feature fusion strategy. The core innovation is a hybrid attention-based feature fusion network, combined with domain-adversarial training, designed to effectively transfer and fuse knowledge from abundant monostatic SAR data to assist recognition under limited bistatic samples. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method, which achieves an F1-score of 68.08%, outperforming baseline approaches and confirming its capability to enhance bistatic SAR target recognition with scarce annotated data.
