title: An Intelligent Machine Learning Framework for Sustainable Public Data Governance
authors: S.R.Rithik Roshan, Ruban Blessing S, T. Prem Jacob
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The need to ensure the existence of a sustainable public data governance has gained more importance due to the increasing application of a data-based decision-making process by governments, with reference to issues that relate to transparency, accountability, data quality, and long-term sustainability. The present study presents an intelligent machine learning system to facilitate sustainable state management through prediction, active decision-making, and interactive policy analysis. The approach will be based on key public data indicators of the institution's economic performance, social status, and activity. It specifically ignores variables that are largely market-dependent, thereby keeping the focus on the interests of the factors of governance. The proposed method has an interactive interface through which policymakers and analysts can tune input indicators and immediately observe their impact on governance outcomes, enabling informed, proactive decision-making. The predictive performance is explained by quantitative findings, where the proposed IMLSPDG model has a consistent performance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 4. 2 \%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 8. 1 \%}$</tex> better than the baseline methods in all the years considered. These findings indicate strong and sound performance, which is corroborated by ROC curve analysis and stability parameters. Moreover, it provides interpretability via attention-based weighting and interactive analysis and ethical and practical concerns, including bias, privacy, and scalability are considered within the context. The interpretability is made possible with the help of an attention-based weighting mechanism which measures the contribution of each indicator of input to the final prediction. The importance scores based on ensemble models that are also used to determine feature importance also contribute to transparent decision-making. Interpretability is assessed in terms of consistency and relevance of these weights, in relation to key governance indicators.

title: Innovation-Pollution Dynamics in a Nonlinear Reaction-Diffusion Framework
authors: Luca Bargna, Davide La Torre
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This paper develops a spatial dynamic model of pollution and innovation in a reaction-diffusion framework. We start with a linear specification that provides a tractable benchmark to characterize existence, uniqueness, and stability of spatially homogeneous equilibria. The linear model clarifies cross-effects between pollution and innovation, showing how technological development and environmental feedback jointly shape local stability and the potential for diffusion-driven instability. We then extend to a nonlinear formulation with logistic pollution dynamics, pollution induced innovation, congestion effects, and endogenous abatement. This nonlinear structure yields richer behavior, including threshold effects, multiple steady states, and regime shifts between high-pollution/low-innovation and low-pollution/high-innovation equilibria. With spatial diffusion, differences in pollution and knowledge mobility can destabilize otherwise stable equilibria and generate spatial pattern formation. AI sector-specific simulations further show how policy stringency affects pollution and innovation across scenarios. Together, linear and nonlinear models provide a unified framework linking environmental pressure and technological change. The analysis offers theoretical foundations for studying endogenous decarbonization, regional interaction, and the spatial dynamics of green transitions.

title: An Intelligent AR–VR Assisted Cognitive Therapy Framework for Spasticity Treatment in Children
authors: ShobhaChristila S, Rajesh Kanna R, Namosri VG, Shwetha S, Oviya S, Satheeshkumar V
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The Potential of augmented reality and virtual reality technologies might be used to improve spasticity in children through better therapeutic interest, less muscle stiffness, and motor skill acquisition. The secondary research methodology is selected, which is a thorough review of the available academic articles, clinical studies, and technical reports on augmented reality-based rehabilitation and cognitive therapy using virtual reality. Thematic analysis is employed to analyze qualitative data obtained as a result of the extraction of the chosen literature and identify common patterns, therapeutic effects, and design principles that are applicable to the treatment of pediatric spasticity. The corresponding findings are that augmented reality and virtual reality have significant positive impact on the level of therapy engagement due to the presence of immersive and gamified interactive activities that encourage the maintenance of engagement and the development of better motor coordination. Virtual exercise programmes led by instructors also aid in limiting muscle tension as it promotes repetitive task-focused movements within a supportive and inspiring atmosphere. Besides, adaptive difficulty adjustment, real-time performance feedback, and multisensory integration are also suggested as key design factors of successful augmented reality and virtual reality-assisted cognitive therapy. All these factors contribute to the individualized rehabilitation process and enhanced compliance with treatment. The paper states that intelligent augmented reality and VR-based therapeutic frameworks have the potential to add to traditional spasticity treatment methods and enhance functional outcomes among children.

title: IoT based Smart Home Surveillance System with ESP32-CAM, PIR Sensor & Telegram App
authors: Rishav Kumar, Koushik Ghosh, Sudipta Chatterjee, Payel Khatun, Sayan Mondal, Debaparna Sengupta
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: This research work proposes an IoT based Smart Home Surveillance System based on PIR Sensor, ESP-32 CAM and Telegram application to secretly observe the intruders’ activities and give intimation by text and image to the user about the intrusion. After scrutinizing the intruders’ activities, the user can command for live video streaming and more images of the intruders remotely; and call the police immediately to prevent the theft/robbery. The proposed system comprises of a PIR Sensor for identifying the entry of the intruders, the ESP32 CAM module for capturing the still photo of the intruders, the inbuilt IoT interface of ESP32 microcontroller to send the captured photo along with text massage to the user through the Telegram bot. Nevertheless, the user has the facility of requesting and receiving live video streaming and more still pictures of the intruders. The proposed system is cost-effective, compact, easy to hide, easily deployable and very efficient in averting theft and other safety lapses. The key paybacks of the proposed system are fast response; very high recall and precision, very low false negative and false positive rates, high accuracy, less sensitivity to detection accuracy, controllability, reliability and cost effectiveness. The amalgamation of the local hardware components diminished the system cost, the Android-based application augmented its controllability and usage of a protected Wi-Fi or 4G network guaranteed the data security. It is capable to identify multiple simultaneous intrusion events using frame level object identification independently using PIR sensor and offers innovative ideas for increasing home security with the help of modern technologies and communication channels.

title: Investigation of a Contactless Encoder Based on the Doppler Effect
authors: G. F. Karlova, M. S. Yegunov, E. A. Belozyorova
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This article presents the results of computational and experimental studies of a contactless encoder prototype based on the Doppler effect. The basic calculation formulas for a contactless microwave encoder are provided. It is shown that the higher the reference frequency emitted by the transmitter, the higher the frequency at the Doppler receiver output and the greater the target velocity. A schematic diagram of the encoder and its printed circuit board have been developed. The encoder printed circuit board was manufactured, and experimental verification of the contactless Doppler effect-based encoder prototype functionality was conducted on it.

title: Adaptive Event-Triggered Lag Consensus for Nonlinear Multiagent Systems With External Disturbances
authors: Xue-Ke Li, Jian-Qiao Wang, Yan-Fen Song, Jin-Liang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: This paper respectively tackles two types of lag consensus problems for a nonlinear multiagent systems (MASs) suffering external disturbances. Based on an adaptive eventtriggered control (ETC) protocol, the lag consensus can be realized for MASs by utilizing Barbalat’s lemma and inequality techniques. By employing the adaptive ETC method, lag $H_{\infty}$ consensus is also addressed for MASs. Furthermore, we also prove the facts that MASs with and without external disturbances under the adaptive ETC schemes don’t exhibit Zeno behavior. Finally, the effectiveness of the devised adaptive ETC protocols are demonstrated via the numerical example.

title: Project-Based Learning in the Belorusian-Russian University Cyber-Physical Systems Laboratory: a Clinical Decision Support System for X-Ray Image Analysis
authors: I.I. Mishchenko, A.E. Misnik
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper reports a case study of integrating education, research, and applied development within the Research Laboratory of Cyber-Physical Systems at the Belarusian-Russian University. The work focuses on project-based learning in the "Expert Systems" course, implemented as a long-term, client-driven team project in partnership with the Smolensk Federal Center of Traumatology, Orthopedics and Endoprosthetics. Students work in cross-functional groups aligned with Clinical Decision Support System modules (data preparation/annotation, preprocessing and model development, integration and interface, testing/validation) and are assessed through iterative deliverables (requirements and experiment logs, reproducible code, validated prototypes, and expert-reviewed results) rather than isolated exercises. The project aims to develop a Clinical Decision Support System for automated analysis of X-ray images. The critical role of image preprocessing as a foundation for diagnostic accuracy is highlighted, and an ensemble neural network architecture with parallel brightness and contrast correction is presented. The paper shows that this laboratory-based Project-Based Learning model develops industry-relevant skills in medical AI engineering and produces tangible outcomes, including deployable software components and research dissemination (e.g., student conference publications and participation in academic competitions).

title: SoulSynth:Multimodal Emotion Recognition and secure Authentication for Mental Wellness
authors: Lavanya K, Shanmadhi Rg, Nivethitha Devi M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: More people are seeking help for their mental health now than at any other point in history and this is true of every age group. At the same time, many people are not getting the care they need as quickly as they should be getting it. This is particularly true for older adults who generally have difficulty adopting new technologies and, therefore, face challenges accessing online services for mental health care. To help address this issue, we have designed Soul Synth to be user-friendly and secure as a mobile app intended to support mental well-being by providing tools for mood tracking, encouraging participation in leisure activities and giving encouragement to change lifestyles as needed. Soul Synth aims to make sure your information is safe, easy to use for all users, and that you are not left behind due to technological advancements. As a result, the app contains evidence-based tools such as mood tracking, guided relaxation, brain exercises, reminders to socialize, and personalized features that adapt to your unique experience. Initial testing indicates that Soul Synth is user-friendly for individuals who have limited experience using technology, and that the app has the ability to use artificial intelligence (AI) to=create customized user experiences.

title: AI Assisted Performance Feedback Quality and Employee Satisfaction with Appraisal Systems: Evidence from Service Sector Employees in Mekong Delta
authors: Hong Minh Ngoc Tran, Nhu Tran Huynh Tam
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Artificial intelligence is increasingly applied to facilitating performance appraisal in service sector organizations, however, employee satisfaction with performance appraisal systems depends on the experience of AI assisted feedback in practice. This study examines the perception of the employees in terms of AI assisted performance feedback and their level of appreciation regarding appraisal systems in the Mekong Delta, Vietnam. Data were obtained from 168 employees of the service sector in the Mekong Delta by snowball sampling and a structured questionnaire. Scale reliability was evaluated in the framework of internal consistency testing, and exploratory factor analysis for the validation of measurement structure. The test of multiple linear regression was then used to examine the relationships proposed. The results indicate that the hypothesized effects are all supported, which means that higher perception of the quality of AI assisted feedback is associated with higher satisfaction with appraisal systems. The strongest effects are found for actionability and clarity; however, accuracy and explainability and transparency have a positive contribution as well, and timeliness shows a smaller but significant positive association. The findings suggest that feedback for service firms should be understandable and development-oriented and keep credible explanations and accuracy signals. This is an important study, as it contributes practice based evidence on employee reactions to AI assisted appraisal and it provides advice for how AI supported feedback can be designed to improve appraisal satisfaction in service work contexts.

title: An Intelligent Deep Learning Framework for Brain Tumor Segmentation and Classification Using MRI Images
authors: K. Mythili, Aswin G, Kavin A J, Heemanush C P
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The segmentation and classification of brain tumor based on the magnetic resonance image (MRI) are of paramount importance in the early diagnosis and treatment planning, as well as in the clinical decision making. Nonetheless, the changes in tumor size, shape, texture, and location complicate the accurate detection of the tumor by traditional image processing and the general deep learning methods. The proposed study is an intelligent deep learning framework that combines automated learning of features and parameter-based characterization of tumors to better analyze brain tumors. The first step of the proposed approach is MRI preprocessing and segmentation of the tumor region with the help of a deep neural network architecture. Textural and geometrical parameters are then derived on the segmented tumor areas to reflect surface intensity changes and spatial structural stances. Deep features are used in conjunction with these complementary features to improve the performance of the classification of tumors. The integrated model enhances the reliability of prediction by minimizing misclassification and making the medical imaging analysis more interpretable. The experimental analysis of MRI images reveals that the suggested framework obtains high segmentation and better classification rates than the traditional deep learning models do. The suggested framework is highperforming when it comes to analyzing brain tumors with MRI images. The fact that the EfficientNetB5-based model has a classification accuracy of 99.3 and the segmentation level can recognize tumor regions with high accuracy proves the effectiveness of the combination of deep feature and textual and geometrical parameters. The findings suggest that deep feature learning with parameter estimation is a reliable and effective way of automated brain tumor analysis and can be used to aid and make clinical diagnosis and treatment planning more accurate.

title: TabTransformer-X Driven Feature-Optimized and Explainable Hybrid Ensemble Learning for Early Parkinson’s Disease Detection
authors: Steve Jachin Peniel S, Sophia S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Parkinson disease (PD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disease in which there is a disability of early diagnosis because it is characterized by mild and overlapping symptoms. The primary objective of this study is to develop a precise, interpretable, and computationally efficient machine learning framework of the early detection of PD via voice-based biomarkers. The most important novelty is that it introduces TabTransformer-X, which is explicitly trained on the clinically significant interactions of features between correlated measures of the voice, coupled with a hybrid stacking ensemble and dual-level interpretability. The suggested framework incorporates TabTransformer-X-based optimization of features with Random Forest, XGBoost, Logistic Regression, and MLP base learners, which are followed by a meta-classifier, and attention analysis and SHAP-based interpretation. Experimental analysis on a publicly available voice dataset of Parkinson patients indicates that the proposed model has an accuracy of 99.4, an AUC of 0.98, and better cross-validation stability that is more effective than the traditional baselines. The findings demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves feature interaction learning, enhances prediction reliability, and provide clinically meaningful results compared to conventional models. In this paper, it has been concluded that the proposed framework presents a scalable, clinically interpretable and robust solution to early screening of the Parkinson disease.

title: Continuous Compliance Monitoring with Real-Time Risk Quantification
authors: Deepalakshmi Perumalsamy, Caleb Gunalan, Ganapathi Subramanian Pechiappan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Cybersecurity compliance models produce a lot of data but do not quantify the relationship between the position of control and financial risk. Historical risk models like FAIR take point-in-time inputs, which are static and are independent of controls, thus overlooking the ongoing evidence streams and inter-control relationships that modern automated environments generate. We present the Continuous Compliance Real-Time Risk Quantification (CC-RRQ) framework where every automated control test is considered Bayesian evidence that refines real-time loss estimates. CC-RRQ has five new elements: the Compliance Entropy Index (CEI), using Shannon entropy to measure compliance disorder; Cascade Risk Propagation (CRP), a directed-acyclic-graph algorithm model of cascading control failures; Adaptive Bayesian Risk Scoring (ABRS), a Beta-Bernoulli model that incorporates the FAIR methodology with continuous evidence; Temporal Risk Velocity and Acceleration (TRVA), which learns risk dynamics using time derivatives; and an LLC-ADLM-based Compliance Anomaly Detection model. These elements are used in an open-source platform and tested using a 12-month empirical research study of 63 organisations across four industries. These findings show that the breach rate is strongly negatively correlated with governance maturity (r = –0.741, p = 0.001), the median detection time has decreased by 87.4 days to 3.2 hours, and the annualised loss expectancy of organisations that have improved from maturity level 2 to maturity level 4 has reduced on average by 62.3%. Such results combine large-scale empirical findings that sustained quantified compliance governance generates quantifiable financial risk reduction.

title: Procedural Algorithms for Generation of 3D Scenery Using Open GIS Data and Computer Vision
authors: Ivan Gil, Vasily Kharitonov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: In the present paper, existing methods for preparation and rendering of generic 3D scenery for flight simulators are explained. Methods for generating of 3D building models using photogrammetry and procedural algorithms are described and compared. To generate 3D building models for generic scenes, a procedural algorithm is chosen instead of photogrammetry due to the low complexity of scene preparation and the preservation of building silhouettes during rendering. Process of preparation of data for 3D scenery is explained. A procedural algorithm for generation of 3D buildings using open cartography data is presented. A demo implementation including rendering of terrain, water surfaces is presented. The YOLO computer vision model is proposed as an alternative approach for attaining cartography data. The pre-trained YOLO 11 object recognition model was refined using custom dataset that includes satellite imagery of Innsbruck and Sochi. The fine-tuned model was then used to retrieve predicted cartography data that was further applied for 3D buildings generation. For future work and further improvement, a segmentation recognition model for water surface detection is proposed to prepare data for water visualization.

title: Suppression of Flow Excursion Instability for HighPower Thermal Management Systems with SeriesParallel Evaporators
authors: Keyi Huang, Guiping Lin, Yuandong Guo, Kai Tang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Flow excursion instability in mechanically pumped two-phase loops (MPTLs) remains a critical challenge limiting their application in thermal management of high-power distributed systems. This study investigates two-phase flow instability in series-parallel evaporator MPTLs through both experimental and simulation approaches. An R134a-based test system was established to acquire pressure drop and flow distribution data, while a high-fidelity Amesim model was developed to reveal underlying mechanisms and assess suppression strategies. Results identify the negative-slope region in the pressure drop curve as the root cause of flow excursion, which expands and shifts with heat load. Non-uniform heating, especially exclusive upstream heating, aggravates instability, whereas distributing heat to downstream evaporators suppresses it effectively. Furthermore, inlet throttling and gradient expansion were validated as practical suppression measures, despite tradeoffs in pumping power and heat transfer area. This work offers valuable insights and design strategies for high-reliability twophase cooling systems in aerospace applications.

title: Enhanced Glaucoma Detection Framework Integrating Generative Adversarial Networks and Attention U-Net
authors: Jayaprakash B, Mohan Raj S, Gnanaprakasam C N
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Glaucoma is a chronic, irreversible optic neuropathy that progressively leads to vision impairment and remains one of the leading causes of blindness worldwide. Early-stage detection is critical to prevent permanent visual loss. In recent years, automated glaucoma screening based on retinal fundus images has gained considerable attention; however, existing deep learning–based frameworks are often constrained by limited annotated data, severe class imbalance, and inaccurate localization of the optic disc and optic cup, particularly under challenging imaging conditions. To address these limitations, this study proposes an automated glaucoma detection framework that integrates data augmentation using Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) with an attention-based U-Net segmentation model. A Deep Convolutional Generative Adversarial Network (DCGAN) is employed to synthesize realistic fundus images, thereby increasing data diversity and alleviating class imbalance. Subsequently, an attention-guided U-Net model accurately segments the optic disc and optic cup by suppressing irrelevant background information. Clinically significant indicators, such as the Cup-to-Disc Ratio (CDR), are extracted from the segmented regions for glaucoma classification. Experimental results demonstrate a mean classification accuracy of 98.2%, a sensitivity of 97.5%, and a Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) of 0.97, outperforming conventional U-Net and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)–based approaches. These findings indicate that the proposed framework offers a reliable and clinically effective solution for automated glaucoma screening.

title: Intelligent Access Control Models in Telecommunication Systems: Application of Artificial Intelligence for Adaptive Security
authors: D. V. Gadasin, D. S. Kalininsky, L. A. Tremasova, D. V. Ignatov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This article explores and compares modern access control systems used in telecommunications systems, with a particular focus on the potential integration of intelligent technologies. Three key structures are considered: role-based access control (RBAC), attribute-based access control (ABAC), and privilege-based access control (PBAC). As an example, the study uses the bank's telecommunications infrastructure, consisting of 800 workstations, 200 servers and 80 employees in an office space, as well as a data center with 50 servers of mission-critical applications. The bandwidth between offices and data centers is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \text{Gbit} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>, and in public areas it is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \text{Gbit} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. Security is provided using Active Directory with support for Kerberos and SIEM monitoring. The study evaluates performance indicators such as response time, throughput, and resilience under peak load conditions. A security test is also conducted that evaluates the system's resilience to attacks, its adaptability to response, and the level of protection it provides in various work scenarios: typical daily employee workloads, peak periods of intensive resource use (for example, at the end of the reporting period), and emergencies resulting from security incidents or equipment failures. In addition, the integration of artificial intelligence methods for adaptive access control is being considered. Intelligent algorithms automatically analyze the user context, assess risks and adjust access rights in real time, thereby increasing the effectiveness of protection and reducing the number of human errors. This approach demonstrates the potential of intelligent technologies to provide dynamic, contextdependent security in telecommunication systems.

title: An Intelligent Early Warning System for Student Dropout Prediction using Deep Learning on the OULAD Dataset
authors: Angel Catherine M, Himasri S, Vinodh Kumar K
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Online learning platforms produce considerable amounts of educational data, which can be effectively utilized to detect students at risk of dropping out or failing in their academics. The detection of such students at an early stage will enable institutions to provide timely interventions to these at-risk students. This paper presents an intelligent student dropout prediction system using the Open University Learning Analytics Dataset (OULAD). Various features were extracted from student interaction data, assessment data, and registration data. Advanced feature engineering techniques were employed to extract meaningful features from the data, such as early engagement, engagement variation, submission behavior, and academic performance. A Multilayer Perceptron neural network was employed to classify at-risk and non-at-risk students. Resampling techniques were also applied to balance the dataset since it was highly imbalanced. The experimental results show that the proposed model is effective in detecting high-risk students and can be used as an early detection system for online learning platforms.

title: BES: Quantum-Inspired Deadline-Aware Task Scheduling in Cloud-based Machine Learning Systems
authors: Santhosh Kumar Medishetti, Seshu Bhavani Mallampati, Sudipta Priyadarshini, Kannam Manjusha, Marupaka Pranay, Baddam Harshavardhan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Machine Learning (ML) systems in the cloud need to have effective Task Scheduling (TS) tasks to handle large workloads and meet all deadlines and system reliability. Nonetheless, traditional scheduling methods are usually affected by high response time, execution delay, and violation of deadlines in such dynamic cloud conditions. To overcome these problems, this paper suggests Quantum-Inspired Metaheuristic which is a Bald Eagle Search (BES) algorithm to schedule tasks with deadlines using cloud environments. The proposed algorithm combines quantum-inspired exploration operations with the BES optimization strategy to improve the allocation of tasks and adaptively distribute the use of computational resources. The Alibaba cluster workload is used to test the algorithm in the CloudSim simulation platform and its results are compared with the currently existing scheduling methods such as an Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). The conducted experimental data prove that the suggested BES-based scheduler can substantially increase the overall system performance. Particularly, the method decreases response time by 18.6%, execution time by 16.9%, task failure by 21.3% and deadline violation by 19.7% over the baseline algorithms. These enhancements suggest that quantum-inspired BES algorithm will be effective in achieving deadline compliance and still efficient resource turnover in cloud-based machine learning systems. The findings confirm that the suggested time management plan offers a scalable and trustworthy solution to dealing with machine learning workloads that are time sensitive in dynamic clouds.

title: Simulation Analysis of Planetary Vehicle’s Parking Performance via Bulldozing Resistance Correction
authors: Jianfei Cao, Dan Huang, Ling Lu, Yaobing Wang, Jiyang Yu, Menglin Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: To address prediction deviations caused by ignoring dynamic bulldozing resistance in traditional slope-holding simulations, this paper conducts a planetary rover simulation analysis. A dynamic correction model is derived, considering wheel embedment and slip displacement, and integrated into a virtual prototype. Comparative results show that the corrected model improves the predicted maximum slope-holding angle by $\mathbf{8 \%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 2 \%}$ and reduces slip prediction errors to below 5%, better matching actual conditions. The bulldozing resistance’s contribution to slope stability increases with slope angle, providing theoretical and simulation support for rover design and mission evaluation.

title: Intelligent Multi-Modal Edge Framework for Real-Time Railway Crack Detection and Automatic Braking
authors: Nithish M, Naveena N
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Early identification of cracks on the tracks and structural defects is a very important aspect in the safety of railways since small faults that go unnoticed can turn out to be very disastrous as a derailment. The traditional inspection techniques like hand-inspections and ultrasonic are intermittent, laborious, and cannot perform real-time evaluation at a high speed of operation. The research paper suggests Real-Time Railway Track Crack Detection and Localization System which includes Acoustic Analysis, Vision-Based Deep Learning, GPS Geo-Tagging, Edge AI Deployment, and an Automatic Braking Mechanism. The signals of acoustic vibration are analyzed with frequency-domain feature extraction, whereas surface images are approached with the help of a CNN-based detection model. Multi-modal sensor fusion framework is better than acoustic and visual frameworks in terms of classification reliability. Experimental performance shows maximum accuracy of 99.05% in classification with false alarm rate of less than 2 % and the detection latency of real-time of less than 100 ms even at train speeds of high speed. The system also performs 100 % safe braking activation in every major crack that was ever tested. The proposed framework offers a high-precision, scalable, and cost-effective solution to smart railway infrastructure and predictive maintenance through the combination of smart sensing, edge computing, and automated response to safety in the suggested framework.

title: Deep Denoising Improved Super-Resolution Framework based on Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Pawan Kumar Mishra, Gaurav Sharma, Pramod Mehra, Jagdish Chandra Patni, Jaydeep Kishore, Piyush Chauhan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The best method currently being employed in computer vision is super-resolution. This method is used to create high-resolution images from a single low-resolution image with the goals of boosting the frequency component, retrieving lost data, and removing down sampling and noises produced during the image capture process. The fundamental objective of the super-resolution technique is to create a high resolution image by combining non-repetition information inside one or more low-resolution frames. Numerous methods have been put forth in the past where numerous photos are used as low resolution images of the same scene with various transformations. Multi-image super resolution is the term used for this. By using deep learning method high resolution image can be reconstructed very easily. In this research, a deep neural network has been proposed to reconstruct the high resolution image from low resolution image very effectively. Proposed method is name as DDSR i.e. Deep Denoising Super Resolution.

title: A Hierarchical Attention-Driven Semantic Matching Framework for Intelligent Resume–Job Description Alignment
authors: Karunamoorthi R, Mohanapriya G
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The process of recruitment is being challenged with the recent rapid increase in online job applications where manual screening of resumes is ineffective and susceptible to errors. Conventional applicant tracking systems are built on mostly keyword match which are not effective to defining the semantic links between candidate qualifications and job requirements. This weakness often leads to the rejection of the apt candidates whose resumes are written using different language as job descriptions. In order to overcome such issues, in this study, a hierarchical multi-granularity attention model of semantic matching of resumes and job descriptions is proposed. The suggested system combines resume data and job description data, job-resume match benchmark, and O*NET occupational skill database to form a complete recruitment dataset. Sentence-BERT is used to encode and decode parts of the resume (skills, experience, and education) to produce contextual semantic embeddings. A conditioned attention mechanism (based on job description) is used to single out those areas of a resume that are most relevant to each job requirement. The model uses a LambdaRank based optimization strategy of ranking to select candidates that are very relevant. The experimental results prove that the framework proposed has a similar accuracy of 91.8, NDCG@10 of 0.93 and Precision@5 of 91.2 that is higher than the traditional semantic matching models. The findings support the assertion that contextual embeddings and hierarchical attention have a significant positive effect on the candidate-job matching accuracy and the quality of ranking in automated recruitment systems.

title: MindPulse: A Multimodal AI Framework for Early Detection of Mental Health Crisis in Young Adults
authors: Abhishek Sriram, Ishwarya K
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The prevalence of mental health disorders among young adults has increased markedly, necessitating the development of intelligent systems for early detection and intervention. This study proposes MindPulse, an AI-driven multimodal early warning system for detecting mental health crises using machine learning, explainable artificial intelligence (XAI), and large language model (LLM) approaches. A mental health dataset containing 15,147 records and 47 features was used for model training and evaluation. Several machine learning techniques, including XGBoost, logistic regression, and random forest, were compared, with random forest showing superior performance in real-time prediction. To assess clinical risk, the risk escalation engine (REE) created a weighted composite by combining machine learning (ML) predictions with clinical thresholds, wellness, and safety alerts. Through Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP), the method incorporates explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) to analyze predictions and pinpoint significant risk factors. Temporal modeling was used to track participants’ mental health over time. The Llama-3.3-70B-Versatile model was incorporated to deliver personalized recommendations based on predicted risk levels. The system enables real-time prediction, risk visualization, and crisis alerts using the FastAPI and React frameworks. The experimental results validated the efficacy of the system in predicting mental health risks and provided interpretable insights into the model. This framework presents a scalable solution for the early detection of mental health crises and digital healthcare support.

title: Acousto-Optic Modulation of Bessel Light Beams by Longitudinal Ultrasonic Waves in Paratellurite Crystals
authors: V. N. Belyi, P. I. Ropot, G. V. Kulak, L. A. Tozik, V. I. Kazakov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: Acousto-optic (AO) modulation of Bessel-type optical beams with a wavelength of 532 nm induced by longitudinal ultrasonic waves in paratellurite crystals is analyzed. Based on a two-dimensional coupled-wave approach, it is demonstrated that for an idealized Bessel beam the diffraction efficiency can approach unity as the acoustic power increases, while noticeable degradation occurs when the ultrasonic frequency deviates from the Bragg condition. For a practical AO modulator operating at a central frequency of 100 MHz, the experimentally observed diffraction efficiency of the Bessel beam reaches approximately 56%, with a corresponding 3 dB bandwidth of about 40 MHz.

title: Compliant Control Parameter Optimization for Continuum Robots in Space Grasping Tasks
authors: Lihua Wang, Yaobing Wang, Jianfei Cao, Zezhou Sun, Chuliang Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Continuum robots are capable of performing capture tasks in space through whole-arm grasping, offering both broad adaptability and reliable capture performance. Ensuring safe and robust grasping requires compliant control, where the selection of compliant control parameters plays a decisive role in overall performance. This paper proposes a physics-based surrogate model-driven optimization framework for compliant control parameter selection in whole-arm grasping with continuum robots. First, a computationally efficient physics-based surrogate model is developed to replace high-fidelity Simulink simulations, enabling rapid evaluation of key performance metrics such as peak contact force and grasping duration. Then, a particle swarm optimization strategy is employed to identify optimal compliant control parameters. A dynamic normalization scheme is incorporated into the fitness evaluation, allowing multiobjective optimization criteria to be compared on a unified scale. Finally, comparative studies verify that the proposed method can effectively guide the selection of compliant control parameters.

title: Identity Shield-Anti Spoof detection
authors: Nikitha Sree Uppala, Jagarlamudi Chandana, Angeline Lydia, Ignatious K Pious
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Face recognition systems are widely deployed in smartphones, banking platforms, surveillance systems, and secure access control applications. However, these systems remain vulnerable to presentation attacks such as printed photographs, replayed videos, and three-dimensional masks. Conventional texture-based and motion-based anti-spoofing techniques often suffer from performance degradation under varying illumination conditions and high-quality spoof artifacts. This paper proposes a lightweight and efficient face anti-spoofing framework based on Active Coded-Light (ACL) projection and Pattern Warping (PW) analysis. The proposed method projects structured light patterns onto the facial surface to capture geometric deformation and reflectance characteristics that are difficult to replicate using spoof media. By analyzing deformation metrics, displacement vectors, curvature variations, and temporal consistency across frames, discriminative features are extracted and classified using a lightweight decision module suitable for real-time deployment. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves a detection accuracy of 98.2% with low False Acceptance Rate (FAR), False Rejection Rate (FRR), and Equal Error Rate (EER). Comparative analysis confirms improved robustness under varying illumination conditions while maintaining computational efficiency on mobile-grade hardware. The proposed approach provides a scalable, cost-effective, and practical solution for enhancing the security and reliability of modern biometric authentication systems.

title: AI-Driven Adaptive Quantum PSO-Optimized TabNet Framework for Intelligent Crop Recommendation
authors: Velukumarasamy A, Manohar E
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The smart- Agriculture would require the use of brilliant decision-support software or mechanisms that can help decode complex environmental and soil information into productive crop advice. The following paper provides an AI-based model that combines Adaptive Quantum Particle Swarm Optimization (AQPSO), the TabNet deep learning model and SHAP-based explainability of intelligent crop recommendation among 56 crop varieties. The framework process 57000 curated agricultural records of soil nutrient records of nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, pH, climatic records of temperature, humidity, rainfall, and time cultivation metadata. AQPSO directs the hyperparameter tuning and feature selection using quantum-inspired probabilistic particle paths where model exploration of optimization landscapes is performed better with no plateauing towards any single model. The sequential attention process of TabNet determines task relevant feature at each decision step that captures non-linear interdependencies between soil, crop and environment features as well as maintains the intrinsic interpretability. The SHAP values are leveled at features to give a prediction its individual attribution, which is required to be transparent enough to build trust with a farmer and agronomist confidence. The classification accuracy of the experiment is 100 percent on an independent test set of 8550 samples with cross-validated rates of 99.97 percent and a variation of 0.04 proving robustness with five independent runs. The model narrows down in six epochs with a training loss of 0.0008 through the help of dropout, L2 regularization, batch normalization, and early stopping. This is proved by comparative analysis with Cat Boosting, SGDC Classifier and XAI-CROP, which is the only model that achieves perfect classification and dual-level interpretability, i.e., intrinsic (TabNet attention masks) and post-hoc (SHAP). The deployed system is an example of a lightweight Flask web application which provides real-time crop recommendations with over 99 percent confidence, and which can support making farm-level and agronomist-level precision agricultural decisions.

title: Construction of a Data-Driven Model for Boost Converter Loss Based on Convolutional Neural Networks and Current Spectrograms
authors: Guihua Liu, Xinyu Wang, Kun Liu, Ye Tao
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: Power electronic DC-DC converters have extensive applications in numerous fields. As nonlinear systems, the inductor current, input current and output current of these converters exhibit strong periodicity, containing rich information. Traditional linearized modeling inevitably introduces deviations in loss estimation compared to actual values. This paper proposes a loss modeling and analysis method for synchronous rectifier (SR) digitally controlled boost converters based on convolutional neural network (CNN) and current spectrograms. The operational mechanism and loss sources of the synchronous rectifier boost converter are analyzed to determine the optimal data acquisition points for the data-driven model. Subsequently, a data-driven modeling approach based on CNN and current spectrograms is introduced. And a 300 W SR Boost converter prototype is constructed for experimental validation. Unlike traditional mechanism-based modeling, this method utilizes periodic current information to estimate losses, enabling users to achieve more precise parameter design through loss estimation.

title: Employment of AI for Predicting the Quality of Stem Cells
authors: G Alekya, Neeli Mahaboob Peera, Mary Posonia. A, Albert Mayan J, Bernatin T
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Stem cell quality is also important in deciding whether to use it in regenerative medicine and experimental biomedical studies. Traditional evaluation methods of stem cell quality are based on manual microscopic evaluation, which is a resource-consuming, subjective, and variable method of evaluation across laboratory settings. In order to address these shortcomings, this paper introduces an artificial intelligence-based model to predict the quality of mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) by analysis of microscopic images automatically. The first step in the pre-labelling of stem cell images is done by a preprocessing pipeline that includes resizing and normalization of the stem cell images, background removal and noise removal to provide more visual consistency. To overcome the shortage of data and imbalance of the classes, a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) is used to create natural synthetic MSC images, thus enhancing the diversity of the dataset. A convolutional neural network based on EfficientNetB0 is subsequently trained to categorize validated MSC images into three quality levels, including low, medium and high, depending on acquired morphological features. In the inference step, images uploaded via the frontend interface undergo preliminary screening to ensure that they are MSC and that non-MSC images are discarded, and that those that are authenticated as MSC are subjected to quality prediction. Approved MSC samples are put up to aid in the refinement of future models. The given framework offers scalable, objective and reliable quality assessment in case of the stem cells and minimizes human intervention and facilitates effective biomedical research processes.

title: Timestamp-Free based Cooperative Clock Synchronization for Time-Sensitive Human-Machine Systems
authors: Long Tang, Haiyong Zeng, Shoulin Huang, Xiaohao Wen, Mengchu Zhou
publish: 2026 35th Wireless and Optical Communications Conference (WOCC)
abstract: With the widespread application of Human-Machine Systems (HMS), clock synchronization has become a fundamental technology for enabling cooperative operation and ensuring the validity of perceptual data and the accuracy of collaborative decision-making in situational awareness enhancement. Nevertheless, existing synchronization solutions for HMS confront challenges such as excessive communication overhead caused by timestamp exchange and insufficient synchronization accuracy, all of which hinder performance in time-sensitive scenarios. To address these limitations, we put forward a timestamp-free based cooperative clock synchronization (T-CCS) mechanism where a set of associated relay agents collaboratively synchronize with an unsynchronized node to achieve superior and robust synchronization. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first attempt to investigate cooperative timestamp-free clock synchronization for time-sensitive HMS. Thanks to the timestamp-free design, T-CCS can be seamlessly integrated into the periodic perceptual data collection process inherent to HMS, thus incurring no additional communication overhead dedicated to clock synchronization. Furthermore, the maximum likelihood estimator is developed to jointly estimate the clock skew and fixed delay, with the corresponding Cramér-Rao Lower Bounds derived for validation. Simulations demonstrate that T-CCS achieves higher estimation accuracy and stronger robustness compared to existing non-cooperative method.

title: Design and Implementation of a Hybrid PD-Fuzzy Controller to Improve Trajectory Tracking in Differential Drive Mobile Robots
authors: Herlis Galdamis García Pérez, Carla Alexandra Chávez Acuña, Josmell Henry Alva Alcántara
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Line follower robots are a category of differential mobile robots widely used in industrial applications and robotics competitions. The primary objectives in these competitions are high-speed performance and precise motion control. In this work, a hybrid fuzzy PD controller was developed and implemented in a competition-level line follower robot equipped with 16 infrared sensors and an ARM Cortex microcontroller. A comparative analysis between the conventional PD controller and the proposed hybrid controller PD Fuzzy demonstrates significant improvements with the latter: an $8.71 \%$ reduction in lap time, $42.62 \%$ lower overshoot, $45.58 \%$ shorter settling time, and $52.65 \%$ reduction in mean squared error (MSE), and a $9.6 \%$ increment in average speed, as well as more stable movement and better adaptation to variations in the track trajectory.

title: AI-Enabled Multimodal Prenatal Assessment Platform Utilizing Ultrasound Imaging and Amniotic Fluid Analysis for Early Anomaly Detection
authors: B. Mohanraj, S. Dhivyadharshini, A. Abisri, Zokir Mamadiyarov, C. Anusuya, M.Priya
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The possibility of early detection of embryonic abnormalities has been a challenge as it depends on the use of imaging of the embryo through the use of ultrasound, which is prone to the acquisition noise and variability among the operators. The research reveals a framework of multimodal prenatal assessment, which is a combination of anatomical features identified by ultrasound and systematic biochemical biomarkers of an amniotic fluid to identify defects during the early stages of development. A convolutional neural network is used to gain hierarchical spatial representations of ultrasound scans, a multilayer perceptron to encode physiological biomarker data. The fusing of the heterogeneous feature spaces is done by employing a probabilistic neural network which estimates the posterior class distributions and the probability of prediction error is explicitly captured. The developed framework was tested on 1, 200 prenatal cases through stratified training and testing procedures. It is shown that experimental outcomes exhibit an accuracy of 93.8, a sensitivity of 91.6, and a AUC of 0.96, which is better than unimodal baselines. Uncertainty-aware fusion with SHAP-based interpretability offers the clinically transparent risk assessment, which can be effective in business early prenatal decisions.

title: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning-based Intrusion Detection Approaches for Securing IoT Networks
authors: Jagdeep Singh, Harjit Singh
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) systems across domains such as healthcare, smart cities, and industrial automation has significantly increased network vulnerabilities, making traditional rule-based security approaches ineffective against evolving cyber threats. Machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) techniques have emerged as promising solutions for intelligent intrusion detection; however, challenges such as high computational cost, lack of interpretability, and absence of unified comparative evaluations persist. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative study of ML-based intrusion detection approaches for IoT networks within a unified experimental framework. The study evaluates classical machine learning models, a deep learning-based convolutional neural network (CNN), and a lightweight decision tree model using standardized preprocessing and evaluation protocols. Performance is assessed using metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. Experimental results indicate that while deep learning models achieve superior detection performance, classical ensemble and decision tree-based approaches offer competitive results with lower computational overhead and better interpretability. The findings provide valuable insights into selecting suitable intrusion detection models for secure and scalable IoT deployments.

title: Features of Teaching Students Parallel Programming for Heterogeneous Architectures Using the SYCL Standard
authors: Olga Shamayeva, Andrey Chernetsov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper examines the theoretical and applied aspects of parallel programming for heterogeneous architectures using the SYCL standard and the DPC++ language, used in the educational and scientific process of the AM&AI department of MPEI. It focuses on analyzing existing heterogeneous programming models. SYCL is highlighted as a compromise between low-level control and the convenience of a high-level C++ API, as well as its extension in the form of DPC++. SYCL, together with DPC++, provides developers with flexible yet expressive tools for writing portable and efficient code for a variety of architectures.

title: Hybrid Convolutional and Bidirectional LSTM Network for Mental Health Severity Analysis
authors: Ravita Chahar, Pankaj Kawadkar, Ashutosh Kumar Dubey
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Mental health disorders such as anxiety, stress, and depression have become significant global health concerns due to their growing prevalence and impact on individuals and society. Early identification of these disorders is essential for providing timely intervention and improving overall well-being. Recent advancements in artificial intelligence, particularly machine learning and deep learning, have shown promising potential in analyzing large-scale health data for early diagnosis. A hybrid deep learning framework was implemented which consist of convolutional neural network (CNN) and bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM). The purpose of this framework is to predict mental health conditions at the early stage. The experiment was conducted on a dataset of 9879 records gathered from individuals of different age groups through a questionnaire in both online and offline modes. It contains a total of 36 attributes responses based on standardized mental health assessment scales. The CNN-BiLSTM model captures both local feature patterns and long-term contextual dependencies within the dataset. The performance was evaluated and compared with CNN model across multiple classification scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that the CNN-BiLSTM model significantly outperforms the standalone CNN model, achieving accuracy values ranging from 96.2% in binary classification to 89.4% in multi-class classification. The findings suggest that the hybrid model is effective in improving predictive accuracy and supporting early detection of mental health disorders.

title: EfficientNet based Deep Learning Framework for Significant Classification and Early Detection of Skin Diseases
authors: Subha S, Paranthaman M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Skin diseases, especially melanoma, need to be diagnosed early and precisely in order to enhance patient survival and decrease health care expenses. In this paper, the researcher suggests an EfficientNet B4 deep learning architecture with a Spatial Attention Module to classify multiclass skin diseases and predict their early stage severity using the HAM10000 dataset. Generalization was enhanced with the help of a complete preprocessing pipeline consisting of hair removal, contrast enhancement, resizing, normalization and augmenting the data. The model incorporates two output heads to forecast the category of disease and level of severity at the same time, and the Grad CAM visualization is interpretable. The accuracy of classification is shown to be 96.7 percent in case of the experiment, which is higher than the results of ResNet 50, DenseNet 121, and EfficientNet B0. The severity prediction module had a total accuracy of 95.5 percent. The large values of precision, recall, and F1 scores in all the classes are a reassurance of the strength and clinical stability of the proposed framework of automated and early skin disease detection.

title: The Dctnn With L2 Regularization for Parameter Identification of DC-Bus Capacitor in AC-DC Power Electronic Transformer
authors: Xiaohui Li, Qian Chen, Xu Han, Qian Guo
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: The DC-bus capacitors (DBCs) are critical component in AC-DC power electronic transformers (PETs), whose aging condition directly affects the quality of the DC-bus voltage. Accurate parameter identification of the DBC is therefore essential for condition assessment and timely maintenance of the PET. Existing deep learning-based identification methods often suffer from high model complexity and long training and execution times. This work introduces a dual-scale convolutional tensor-train neural network (DCTNN) enhanced by L2 regularization to overcome these limitations. By employing dualscale convolutional channels, the network captures both low- and mid-frequency features, while tensor-train decomposition preserves essential spatial structures. Validation on an LLC-based PET platform using both simulated and experimental data indicates that the DCTNN outperforms conventional deep learning architectures in identification accuracy while offering substantial reductions in training and execution time.

title: A Social Cognitive Perspective on AI Use: Moral Disengagement, Epistemic Agency, and First-Principles Reasoning Among Engineering Students
authors: Yankuba Danso, Ebrima Badjie, Md Abdullah Al Mamun
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The rapid adoption of generative artificial intelligence (AI) tools among engineering students in Bangladesh raises important questions about their potential influence on foundational reasoning skills. While prior studies have primarily focused on patterns of AI use and student perceptions, limited research has examined the cognitive mechanisms that shape deeper analytical engagement. Grounded in Social Cognitive Theory, this study investigates the relationships between social norms, trust in AI, moral disengagement, epistemic agency, and first-principles reasoning. Survey data were collected from engineering students in Bangladesh and analysed using hierarchical regression analysis. The results indicate that social norms and trust in AI significantly predict moral disengagement, which negatively influences epistemic agency. Epistemic agency emerges as the strongest predictor of first-principles reasoning. Furthermore, social norms and trust do not show significant direct effects on first-principles reasoning when cognitive variables are included in the model. These findings suggest that the influence of AI on reasoning may operate through changes in students' self-regulatory and epistemic orientations rather than through direct effects. The study offers implications for engineering pedagogy and highlights the importance of fostering epistemic agency in the responsible integration of AI in higher education.

title: Robotics in the Cath Lab: Precision, Safety, and the Rise of Remote Cardiac Interventions
authors: Tejas Padliya, Forhad Hossain
publish: IEEE Pulse
abstract: This article examines the growing role of robotics in the cardiac catheterization laboratory as interventional cardiology moves toward more complex coronary and structural heart procedures. Robotic systems improve precision through motion scaling and tremor reduction while reducing operator fatigue and cumulative radiation exposure. The article argues that successful adoption depends not only on mechanical accuracy, but also on workflow integration, human-machine collaboration, safety design, regulatory readiness, and financial viability. It highlights the challenges of fitting robotic platforms into fast-paced cath lab environments where setup time, team coordination, and rapid conversion to manual control are critical. The discussion also explores remote robotic intervention to distribute specialist expertise across sites with limited staffing depth. Finally, the article considers how artificial intelligence, computer vision, and predictive analytics may extend robotic platforms from assistive tools into more intelligent systems that support safer, more scalable, and more accessible cardiovascular care.

title: Multi-Sensor Fusion Based Indoor Localization and Navigation System for Dual-Ackerman Steering Mobile Robots
authors: Yufei Lu, Qun Hao, Shaohui Zhang
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: Simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) has been playing an important role in robotics perception, due to its ability to simultaneously localize the robot's pose and build maps of the surrounding environment. Concurrently, path planning algorithms are widely utilized to achieve autonomous and efficient robot navigation. However, there remains a lack of robust, flexible, and fully integrated systems that tightly combine both technologies for practical deployment in narrow indoor spaces. To address these issues, we develop a multi-sensor fusion-based indoor localization and navigation system, and propose a flexible motion control algorithm specifically tailored for dual-Ackermann steering vehicles. Real-world experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves robust indoor localization and mapping, while enabling highly flexible steering and accurate navigation. The average translational Euclidean error of the system's navigation remains at 6.8 cm.

title: Adaptive fault-tolerant control for hypersonic vehicles with unknown control direction and actuator fault
authors: Yuchen Cui, Cheng Zhang, Jun Wang, Chenming Zheng, Yuxin Huang, Zhangyao Zheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: In this paper, an adaptive fault-tolerant control method is proposed for hypersonic vehicle systems with timevarying actuator faults and unknown control direction. The method considers time-varying faults and the unknown control direction condition. Considering the unknown control direction, the Nussbaum function technique is used, which overcomes the effect of unknown fault parameters. The backstepping method is used to design the controller for the system, and a first-order filter is used to avoid the ‘differential explosion’ phenomenon. Theoretical analysis demonstrates the stability of the system under the designed control scheme. Finally, numerical simulation results verify the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed control strategy.

title: Optical Two-Channel Airborne System for Forest Fire Detection
authors: Yuliana A. Novikova, Maksim B. Ryzhikov, Ekaterina A. Boeva
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: Timely warning of a forest fire occurrence allows for prompt protective measures to prevent its spread. Analysis of information sources shows that ground-based and space systems cannot respond to a fire at the initial stage as quickly as monitoring systems from airborne platforms. The article proposes and substantiates the design of an onboard technical vision system, including the development of hardware and software solutions based on the existing scientific and technical groundwork, focused not only on providing the coordinates of the fire source but also on preparing visual confirmation of the presence of a forest fire. The greatest attention is paid to the optical receiving scheme and information processing, ultimately generating coordinates and a graphical representation of the fire, which can be quickly transmitted over long distances via radio communication channels.

title: A Deep Transfer Learning Framework for Fresh and Rotten Fruit Classification Using EfficientNet-B3
authors: Shyam Sunder Pabboju, Ananadan Shailaja, Kanegonda Ravi Chythanya, Nitika Verma
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Fruit automatic inspection can be used to minimize the losses of food products and improve the quality management of food chains. A deep learning approach for the classification of fresh and rotten fruits based on transfer learning, EfficientNet-B3, is proposed in this paper. A selected dataset consisting of images of apples, bananas, and strawberries was organized and divided into two classes and screened to remove corrupted or duplicate samples. All of the images were RGB, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$300 \times 300$</tex> & normalized (to [0], [1]). The data set was stratified into 363 training, 78 validation, and 79 test images. The model has been trained using an ImageNet pretrained backbone, and retrained with class weighting due to imbalance. Performance was taken with the help of accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and the confusion matrix. The test accuracy of the proposed approach was 93.67 %, in which F1-scores of 0.9515 and 0.9091 were obtained for Fresh and Rotten, respectively. Qualitative results make correct predictions across fruit types, indicating model acquisition of spoilage cues, such as discoloration, bruising, and texture degradation. The results demonstrate EfficientNet-B3 as a good baseline for freshness classification of fruits and a base for a real-world fruit inspection system.

title: Automated Industrial Surface Defect Detection on Bolts Using Deep Learning
authors: Oğuz Ergin, Emre Güçlü, İlhan Aydın, Erhan Akın, Yunus Emre Uyanık
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Defects in the head region of bolts used as fasteners directly affect load transfer, the proper transmission of tightening torque, and the safety of on-site maintenance and repair operations. In mass production lines, monitoring such defects primarily through operator inspection and infrequent sampling is both prone to human error and insufficient for highvolume manufacturing. In this study, a computer vision and deep learning-based inspection system capable of automatically monitoring bolt head defects in a production environment is designed and evaluated. The proposed prototype is based on capturing bolt head images as they pass over a rotating platform under a fixed-position monochrome industrial camera and homogeneous LED illumination. An adaptive region of interest (ROI) is defined to process only the head region, thereby suppressing background noise and automating the data acquisition and labeling process. A DETR (DEtection TRansformer) model with a ResNet-50 backbone is adopted as the baseline method on the dataset labeled as defective and nondefective, and its performance is compared with YOLOv8x, YOLO11x, and YOLO12x architectures under identical training settings. Experimental results indicate that YOLObased models achieve balanced and high performance in terms of mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95, while the DETR-ResNet50 model, in addition to comparable accuracy levels, significantly reduces the false negative rate by decreasing the number of missed defective bolts.

title: Creating an Immersive Language Learning VR Environment for Engineering Students
authors: Natalia Alexandrovna Kopylova, Marina Anatolievna Slepneva
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The use of virtual technologies in education, particularly in foreign language teaching, is seen as a promising technology due to its potential to create an authentic language environment that allows students to practice a foreign language in real-life situations, interact with native speakers, and visit virtual analogues of real-world locations. This article studies the theoretical premises and practical aspects of creating an immersive educational environment for learning foreign languages using virtual reality technologies, as well as its obvious advantages and potential challenges. The examples of the practical virtual technology application are provided, including with students specializing in non-linguistic fields at a technical university. The key psycholinguistic and pedagogical principles underlying this approach, such as situated learning theory and the immersion hypothesis, are analyzed. The empirical research data are presented demonstrating the effectiveness of VR in developing communicative competence, reducing language anxiety, and fostering sociocultural skills. This article describes the architecture and components of an immersive language VR environment model using virtual travel as an example, as well as pedagogical scenarios for integrating it into the educational process at a higher education institution. This article is intended for researchers in the fields of language teaching methods, instructional design, and educational technologies.
