title: A Comprehensive Survey on Secure and Graphenhanced Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Scalable HetNet Resource Allocation
authors: Pothula Pavan Kumar Reddy, C. Kamalanathan
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: A review of Graph-Enhanced Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for scalable resource allocation in Heterogeneous Networks. It discusses how to combine graphbased techniques to overcome the scalability and security challenges that multi-agent systems face. The paper will overview classical approaches to Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning and explain the improvements to graph-based approaches to agent communication and coordination. It highlights the importance of graph neural networks for improving scalability by facilitating localized interactions between agents and simplifying the system. The paper also briefly discusses the primary security threats of Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning, such as adversarial attacks and data poisoning, and privacy preservation methods. Graph-based Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning scales better since it facilitates easier information sharing of agents, a feature that is especially suitable in large systems. The question of security is also a threat, and it should be appropriately defended with efficient defense systems, such as secure communication protocols and privacy-preserving methods. This combination of graphs is more beneficial to coordination, adaptability, and learning in changing environments. The paper's findings can be applied in practice to autonomous cars, swarms of robots, and the corresponding wireless communication networks, where the efficient distribution of resources and coordination of agents are essential. Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning with graph-based solutions offers scalable, secure solutions for real-world multi-agent systems. The paper makes contributions to the knowledge of graph-enhanced MultiAgent Reinforcement Learning, scalability, and security concerns, and guides further research in these areas. Future work should explore dynamic graph adaptation and robust defenses to enhance real-time coordination, resilience, and trustworthiness of large-scale multi-agent reinforcement learning systems.

title: A Vision-Guided Learning Framework for Robotic Pick-And-Place Based on Demonstrated Patterns
authors: Yeh Huann Goh, Max Wee Ming Liang, Yan Kai Tan, Tsung Heng Chiew, Sara Lee Kit Yee, Kar Mun Chin
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: Programming industrial robots for high-mix, sequential pick-and-place tasks remains a significant bottleneck, requiring specialized expertise. This paper presents an intelligent, vision-guided system that autonomously learns, optimizes, and replicates cube arrangement tasks from a single, non-expert human demonstration. Leveraging a Learning from Demonstration (LfD) paradigm, the system integrates a dualcamera 3D perception setup with a custom-trained YOLOv11 object detection model. The core contribution moves beyond simple mimicry by introducing a three-stage intelligent pipeline. First, a novel grid-snapping algorithm idealizes imprecise human input into a geometrically perfect, machine-executable goal plan. Second, a heuristic-based task planner autonomously re-orders this plan into a stable and efficient “inside-out” assembly sequence. Third, the system employs dynamic, perception-driven strategies for picking and placing, including adaptive grasp orientation and real-time collision avoidance. The system's end-to-end performance was validated through 400 trials across four test patterns, achieving an overall task success rate of 92.5 % and a mean placement error of 2.26 mm. This research demonstrates that by interpreting and optimizing human intent, a vision-guided system can achieve a level of precision and flexibility that surpasses the original demonstration, enhancing automation in line with Industry <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4. 0}$</tex> principles.

title: Dense Feature Learning for Canola Disease Classification Using DenseNet201
authors: Nageswara Rao Putta, K.K. Manivannan, Eshika Jain, Jayapal Lande
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Canola diseases have a major impact on crop productivity, and identification of diseases is important for timely intervention. This study proposes a binary classification algorithm with the deep learning technology and DenseNet201 to classify healthy and diseased canola samples based on RGB images. The dataset of 640 images was balanced and consists of 318 healthy and 322 diseased images. Images were normalized by RGB conversion, center cropping, and resizing to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224 \times 224$</tex> and normalized with DenseNet201 input requirements. A stratified strategy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(70: 15: 15)$</tex> was used to separate the dataset into training, validation, and testing. The model was highly performing with an overall accuracy of 92.71 with an F1-score of 0.9247 in healthy and 0.9293 in diseased samples. The confusion matrix analysis does not exhibit significant misclassification, i.e., good generalization. The proposed DenseNet201 approach is an efficient and scalable solution to the problem of automated canola disease screening in precision agriculture.

title: Implementation of Network Digital Platforms for Additional Professional Education Based on Multy-Agent Technology
authors: Yury Filippovich Telnov, Rustem Ilyasovitch Diveev
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article presents a solution to the problem of implementing a networked digital platform for additional professional education (APE). The relevance of this task is driven by the dynamic and evolving needs for workforce competencies in the labor market, which necessitates the integration of educational resources from multiple stakeholder organizations. Implementation methods are developed based on designing multi-agent systems using the technology of asset administrative shells from the 4th generation industry. The article proposes a conceptual model of a digital network APE platform which allows for the distribution of educational resources across administrative shells associated with educational programs (EP), training modules, educational institutions, and teachers. The article presents a generalized structure of the domain ontology, ensuring the integration of knowledge about various educational resources and their use during the stage of designing EP content and coordinating components among stakeholders. The developed algorithm for generating an educational program outlines the core implementation scheme for processing a request: decomposing the required competencies, selecting necessary modules for them, coordinating with teachers and educational institutions for the possibility of organizing training for these modules, and ultimately creating a schedule for the constructed EP. The advantage of the proposed approach to building APE programs based on a networked educational program is the flexibility and adequacy of the EP to the competencies demanded in the labor market.

title: Multi-Stage Mental Health Monitoring System using BERT-based Sentiment Analysis and AI-Driven Response Engine
authors: Chettiyar Vani Vivekanand, Sakthi M S, Sanjay M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Posts portraying mental health concerns (depression, anxiety, and suicidal ideation) are appearing more frequently across various social media platforms, including Twitter and Reddit. There are existing approaches for analyzing social media also consider the categorization of mental distress, however, the approaches primarily leverage conventional sentiment analysis approaches which classify posts as positive, negative or neutral based on simply understanding the post overall sentiment. In addition, conventional sentiment analysis approaches do not adequately account for the nuances of emotion or severity of mental distress depicted in a post. In this proposal, we have developed a new approached that combines the emotional detection of posts, our own risk rating scale for severity of concern, and then suggest positive responses to the user. The emotional detection is based on a specific BERT-based transformer model for emotional detection, and risk severity will assessed with a subsequent classifier that assigns a low, medium, or high severity.The framework can use the NLP techniques in the social media posts to further produce safe and empathetic responses through a generated chatbot module. In addition, a web-based dashboard will be developed to convey visual emotional trend data, risk severity trends, and provide generated responses. Overall, this project uses the integration of this Natural Language Processing (NLP) framework, Machine Learning (ML) approach, and AI-driven stated chatbot to achieve a desired goal of taking a step beyond the use of sentiment analysis, in order to make preliminary steps toward providing a supportive online mental health support.

title: Algorithmic Intelligence in Human Resource Management: Enhancing Workforce Decisions Through AI-Driven Analytics
authors: Sakher Alnajdawi, Saed Adnan Mustafa, Mahmoud Alghizzawi
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Artificial intelligence is increasingly transforming human resource management by enabling data-driven and predictive workforce decisions. This study examines how AI-driven HR analytics influence workforce decision quality, focusing on the mediating role of human-AI decision interaction and the moderating role of ethical HR governance. Using survey data from HR professionals and Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM), the study finds that AI-driven analytics significantly enhance decision quality both directly and indirectly through improved human-AI interaction. The results further demonstrate that ethical HR governance strengthens the positive impact of AI analytics on decision outcomes. By integrating technological, behavioral, and governance perspectives, this research advances understanding of intelligent HRM and highlights the importance of responsible AI adoption. The findings offer practical insights for organizations seeking to leverage AI analytics for effective and fair workforce decision-making.

title: Performance Analysis of Approximate Adders in Image Fusion
authors: Bhavani Dantuluri, Bala Sindhuri Kandula, Relli Purna Chandra, Yandra Mohana Laxmi, Syed Razack Akbar Ali, Viswanadh Maddala
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The rapid advancement of imaging technologies, from medical to satellite sensors, has enabled powerful data analysis through image fusion. In this technique, multiple source images are combined to produce a single output that is more informative and easier to interpret than the individual inputs. Image fusion plays a significant role in areas such as medical imaging, where CT scans provide detailed bone structures and MRI highlights soft tissues, giving a comprehensive view to physicians. In this paper, we present a multimodal image fusion framework based on the Haar Discrete Wavelet Transform (Haar-DWT). The input images are decomposed into frequency sub-bands. The approximation coefficients are combined using an averaging rule, while the detail coefficients are fused using a maximum selection rule to preserve edges and important features. The final image is reconstructed using the inverse Haar-DWT, resulting in improved visual quality and better interpretability. The main contribution of this work is the integration of approximate adders into the Haar-DWT-based image fusion process. This approach reduces hardware cost, including area, power, and delay, while maintaining acceptable image quality. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves an effective balance between hardware efficiency and image quality, making it suitable for real-time applications such as medical imaging and surveillance.

title: Energy Measures of Dominating Sets in Northeast India Graphs for Intelligent Network Analysis
authors: N. Anbarasi, S. Hemalatha
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Energy-aware graph-theoretic analysis has become essential for intelligent network design in geographically complex regions. In this context, energy measures of dominating sets provide an effective mechanism for achieving optimal node coverage and efficient resource utilization in network graphs of Northeast India. Existing dominating set-based approaches primarily focus on topological coverage while neglecting spectral energy characteristics, dynamic node influence, and regional constraints such as sparse connectivity and terrain-induced instability. As a result, these methods often lead to higher energy consumption, poor fault tolerance, and reduced adaptability in realworld intelligent networks. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an Energy-Aware Dominating Set Optimization framework based on the Spectral Graph Energy. The proposed method integrates spectral graph energy derived from the eigenvalues of the adjacency matrix with dominating set theory to identify energyefficient and structurally influential nodes. By assigning energy influence scores to nodes and applying optimization strategies, the framework minimizes the network's overall energy while preserving full domination coverage. The proposed approach is applied to intelligent network analysis of graphs in Northeast India, enabling optimized monitoring, control, and communication in infrastructure networks such as smart grids and sensor-based systems. Experimental analysis demonstrates that the proposed method significantly reduces total network energy, improves robustness against node failures, and enhances monitoring efficiency compared to conventional dominating set techniques. The results confirm the suitability of the framework for intelligent, energy-efficient network deployment in region-specific complex environments.

title: Electronic University: Ontology-Driven Support for Engineering Education Based on Meta-Associative Graphs under Dual Regulatory Frameworks
authors: A.E. Misnik
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: Engineering education is evolving at an exceptional pace: technological shifts introduce new industrial practices, new digital tools, and new curricular emphases; project-based and laboratory-intensive formats grow in importance; internships become more tightly integrated with enterprises; and student portfolios increasingly include rich evidence artefacts such as laboratory reports, design documentation, test protocols, software modules, and computational models. In parallel, regulatory requirements governing educational processes and legally significant documents change frequently. At the Belarusian-Russian University, these challenges are compounded by the need to comply simultaneously with the requirements of the Republic of Belarus and the Russian Federation, which creates semantic discrepancies in terminology, rules, and document structures. This paper describes the "Electronic University" system, which provides end-to-end digital support for the student lifecycle-from admissions and portfolio management to graduation, document generation, printing, and archiving-using an ontological core implemented with a meta-associative graph. The technical novelty lies in representing graph nodes as objects that unify data, relations, and executable behaviour through events and methods. This enables dynamic ontology reconfiguration in response to regulatory updates and to changes in engineering education itself, while preserving overall system functionality and maintaining consistency of processes, documents, and accumulated data.

title: Optimization of Production Costs: A Numerical Methods Approach
authors: Stephane Kim Asis, Hazel De Torres, Anne Margarette Sagun, Vince Hero Factor, Mariz J. Prado, Mirasol C. Dilay, Anela L. Salvador, Selverino A. Magon
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study aims to optimize production cost through the application of numerical methods in order to determine the production level that minimizes total cost. Representative cost functions incorporating fixed, variable, and nonlinear components were constructed using analytical expressions and sample data to reflect realistic production behavior. Numerical differentiation techniques were applied to estimate marginal cost for both smooth and discrete datasets. Root-finding methods including Bisection, Newton, and False Position were implemented to identify critical points where the derivative equals zero. Results show that the methods converged to a critical point of approximately 12.2515 units with a corresponding cost of $ 1, 5 6 6. 6 0. Comparison with the analytical optimum of 54.4152 units and cost of - $307.34 indicates that the numerical solution is highly dependent on the initial bracketing interval and may converge to local rather than global minima. Error analysis revealed that numerical differentiation achieved high accuracy, with an error of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9.09 \times 10^{-8}$</tex>. The study highlights the effectiveness of numerical methods in handling complex and nonlinear cost functions, while emphasizing the importance of proper interval selection, method choice, and data handling to ensure reliable optimization results in practical engineering and production systems. Furthermore, the study demonstrates that numerical approaches remain robust even when analytical solutions are unavailable or difficult to obtain, making them suitable for real world applications involving noisy data, incomplete information and complex cost structures commonly encountered in modern manufacturing environments and support decision making in cost analysis.

title: Priority-Aware Dual-Objective BERT for Fair and Interpretable Automated Recruitment
authors: Vandana Ahuja, Sandip kumar Goyal, Ambuj kumar Agarwal, Manik Rakhra, Sargam, Amandeep Kaur, Preeti Kaushal
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The proliferation of digital application of jobs has increased the pressure on recruiting systems that are smart enough to be able to read resumes and at the same time, accurately rank the applicants. The common approaches of single-task deep learning pipelines do not consider the processes of semantic understanding and ranking independently, which complementary decision-making and divergent hiring results. This paper presents Dual-Objective BERT Intelligence (DO-BERT) a shared multi-task Transformer model that combines semantic resume comprehension and candidate ranking of a shared BERT encoder and a new architecture Priority-Aware Contrastive Alignment (PACA) postulate. The model proposed optimally selects both a classification and a regression based job-fit scoring objective so that there is coherence between what the system understands and how it prioritizes. DO-BERT is assessed using the multi-domain ResumeFit-5000 data, with significant gains observed between the architecture and ten baseline ones. The model is semantically classified with an accuracy of 94.6 % and a precision of 93.8 %, a recall of 94.1 % and a macro-F1 score of 0.92, which is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-4$</tex> points higher than RoBERTa. DO-BERT in candidate prioritization brings the mean absolute error to 3.12 and RMSE to 4.87, which is 35 % improved in candidate prioritization compared to single-task BERT regression. The performance ranking is further reinforced by NDCG at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10=0.89$</tex>, kendall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$t=0.71$</tex> and Spearman <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{r}=0.83$</tex> that gives a high correlation with human hiring judgment.

title: A Hybrid Intelligent Modeling Framework for Closed-Loop Pallet Reverse Logistics: Structural Analysis, Simulation, and Decision Optimization
authors: Dzikra Hanaya Farizqi, Miftahol Arifin, Ratih Windu Arini
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Closed-loop pallet reverse logistics systems often experience inefficiencies caused by asset damage, loss variability, administrative delays, and inconsistent operational discipline. This study proposes a hybrid intelligent modeling framework that integrates Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) and discrete-event simulation to analyze and optimize pallet return operations. The structural model identifies the causal relationships among timeliness, asset loss, asset damage, agent compliance, return process control, and overall system efficiency. Empirical results demonstrate strong explanatory power, with 72 % of the variance in return process control and 65 % of system efficiency explained by the proposed model. Asset damage emerges as the most significant negative driver, while agent compliance and timeliness contribute positively to process stability. The standardized path coefficients are subsequently embedded into a simulation-based decision engine to operationalize compliance-weighted scheduling and damage-sensitive inspection prioritization. Comparative simulation results indicate substantial improvements, including a 65.7 % reduction in inbound cycle time, a 66.7 % decrease in asset loss rate, and a 133.3 % increase in pallet turnover rate. The findings confirm that operational efficiency in closed-loop systems is primarily achieved by stabilizing inbound process control rather than through isolated structural adjustments. The proposed framework provides a data-driven roadmap for integrating behavioral analytics and operational optimization in asset-intensive logistics environments.

title: The Role of Big Data Analytics in Personalized FinTech Services and Digital Payments
authors: Ziad M.S. Zurigat, Ohoud Khasawneh
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The recent growth of FinTech services has increased the use of big data analytics in providing personalized financial services, especially in the context of digital payment. Despite the prevalence of big data analytics capability (BDAC) as an essential technological resource, empirical studies are scarce in their description of how this capability will turn into effective personalized FinTech services. This paper analyzes how BDAC can be used to maximize the efficiency of customized FinTech services and in particular, the mediating effect of the personalization quality is examined. Based on the Resource-Based View and the Dynamic Capabilities Theory, a quantitative research design was introduced, and data gathered among the users of FinTech applications and digital payment sources were used. A questionnaire was used, the structured questionnaire, and about 300 effective responses were formed. Structural Equation Modeling with Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modelling (PLS-SEM) was used to analyses the data through SmartPLS. The findings suggest that BDAC has a strong positive influence on the quality of personalization, which, in its turn, results in a high level of effectiveness of personalized FinTech services. In addition to this, BDAC has a positive direct effect on service effectiveness. It is proven by mediation analysis that the quality of personalization mediates the connection between BDAC and the success of personalized FinTech services. These results can be used to expand the body of literature on FinTech, as they clarify how big data analytics capabilities create customer value. Pragmatically, the research highlights the need to invest in the analytics infrastructure and, moreover, the design of personalization to increase the efficiency of digital financial services.

title: Robust Multimodal Modulation Classification in Realistic Wireless Channels using Hybrid CNN-BiLSTM with Attention
authors: Gayathri Devi S, Carmel Abigail Shirley Royan, Ramachandra Reddy K
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Automatic Modulation Classification (AMC) is a critical enabler of intelligent spectrum monitoring, cognitive radio, and adaptive demodulation in modern wireless systems. Existing deep learning AMC models typically process either time-domain or frequency-domain representations in isolation and lack principled mechanisms for generalizing across varying channel conditions. In this work, a robust multimodal deep learning framework is proposed that jointly exploits in-phase/quadrature (IQ) time-domain features via a 1D Convolutional Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) branch and frequency-domain spectrogram features via a 2D CNN branch. A cross-modal attention fusion module dynamically weighs complementary representations, suppressing channel-induced noise. Evaluated on the RML2016.10b dataset across ten modulation types (BPSK, QPSK, 8PSK, QAM16, QAM64, AM-DSB, WBFM, CPFSK, GFSK, PAM4), the proposed model achieves a peak classification accuracy of 86.8% at SNR = +2 dB and maintains 82–85% accuracy across high-SNR conditions, consistently outperforming standalone CNN, LSTM, and hybrid CNN-LSTM baselines. Training and validation losses converge smoothly over 20 epochs with no evidence of overfitting, confirming strong generalization under realistic wireless channel conditions.

title: Design of High Autonomy TT&C/DT Test System for Lunar and Earth Space Communication Scenarios
authors: Liu Yiming, Fu Xiaojing, Wen Xin, Liu Qi, Duan Yuxiao, Tian Guoliang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of China’s lunar exploration program, lunar missions have become increasingly complex, and the level of digitalization in spacecraft design has significantly improved. To support the implementation of autonomous and intelligent applications onboard, the performance requirements for Telemetry, Tracking, and Command/Data Transmission (TT&C/DT) systems have been progressively enhanced. Communication scenarios-including communication methods, objects, systems, and performance metrics-have also grown more diverse. These developments pose further challenges to ground-based testing and verification of TT&C/DT systems.In response, this thesis integrates TT&C/DT system design with the comprehensive testing requirements of China’s lunar probes. From a test system perspective, it proposes a design for a multi-scenario, highly autonomous TT&C/DT test system tailored to lunar exploration missions. The proposed system is intended to meet the increasingly demanding test requirements for lunar probe TT&C/DT systems and to achieve a high degree of autonomy in adapting to varied lunar communication scenarios. It is expected to be applied in subsequent TT&C/DT testing activities.

title: A Robust Preprocessing–EfficientNet-B4 Pipeline for Multi-Disease Retinal Fundus Image Classification
authors: K. Umamaheshwari, Prasanth N, Aditya Sain, Md Ankushavali
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Automated analysis of retinal fundus images can help in the early screening of vision-threatening diseases. This paper introduces a Deep learning method of four-class retinal disease classification based on Transfer learning of EfficientNet-B4. A high-quality dataset of 3588 retinal images was used and labeled as Age-related Macular Degeneration (409), Glaucoma (1007), Diabetic Retinopathy (1098), and Normal (1074). Preprocessing of images involved extracting the retinal field-of-view, center square cropping, re-sizing at 380*380, CLAHE in LAB color space to improve visibility of lesions and normalize intensities on a [0, 1] scale. The dataset was split into training, validation, and test datasets, and the model was trained for 25 epochs by the frozen-backbone stage and fine-tuning with class weighting to handle imbalance. Performance was assessed with the help of accuracy, per-class Precision/Recall/F1-score, and confusion matrix. The proposed model showed 90.52 % test accuracy with good F1-scores between classes and low inter-class confusion, showing the use of EfficientNet-B4 as a good baseline for automated analysis of retinal diseases.

title: A Hybrid CNN–Transformer Architecture for Accurate and Interpretable Kidney Abnormality Classification
authors: B.Gracelin Sheena, Alasakani Sairam, Y.V. Pavan Kumar Reddy, R.S.Amshavalli, G.Kavitha, M.Gowri
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Computed tomography (CT) images provide a challenging task on kidney abnormality classification because of the complicated anatomy and the variation in the pathological appearance. This paper provides a hybrid deep learning architecture, which consists of a convolutional neural network (CNN) backbone with a Transformer encoder, to simultaneously predict local spatial terms and global contextual relation in CT images. The suggested framework is created and tested on the basis of the KiTS21 dataset of kidney abnormality classification. Attention heat maps and Grad-CAM visualizations are used to show areas of the images that contribute to model decisions in order to increase model transparency and facilitate clinical interpretability. The findings show that the combination of convolutional and attention-based representations contribute to the improved learning of features as well as allows the meaningful visual explanation. This study has shown how hybrid CNN-Transformer architectures can help solve interpretable kidney image analysis tasks and serves as a baseline to future studies in automated kidney abnormality assessment.

title: A LiDAR-Anchored Synchronization Framework for Robust LVI-SLAM
authors: Chenxi Gu, Huizhong Wu, Haolong Jiang, Jia He, Fanxin Wang
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: LiDAR-visual-inertial (LVI) simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) systems rely on accurate hardware synchronization among sensors. an assumption often violated on low-cost platforms using consumer-grade cameras. Timestamp jitter and sensor latency can therefore lead to unstable estimation and degraded mapping performance. This paper proposes a LiDAR-anchored software synchronization strategy for tightly coupled LiDAR-visual-inertial SLAM without hardware triggering. Each LiDAR scan is treated as the temporal reference, while visual measurements are associated using nearest-timestamp matching with temporal gating to enforce consistency. Experiments in an indoor corridor environment show that, without synchronization, repeated runs on the same dataset exhibit significant trajectory variability and corridor misalignment. The proposed method substantially improves run-to-run stability and mapping repeatability, demonstrating that robust multisensor SLAM can be achieved through software-based temporal alignment alone.

title: An Intelligent Computational Study on the Effect of Corrugated Limiting Tab Geometry on Supersonic Jet Mixing
authors: Yograj Chawan, Uday kiran U, Saikumar, Maruthupandiyan K
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The reduction of aeroacoustic noise, thermal signatures and infrared emissions is very important in defence and aerospace applications, especially in high-speed platforms. The role of mixing of the supersonic jet is to minimise the visibility of the exhaust plume and plume acoustics. This paper introduces a numerical analysis of triangular corrugated limiting tabs in detail, as a useful passive strategy to improve jet mixing at various expansion regimes: under expanded with NPR 6, optimally expanded with NPR 4.25, and overexpanded NPR 2.5. The proposed tabs will be placed close to the nozzle exit and geometrically divide the jet, but will block 5 per cent of the exit area, which enhances the effective mixing surface. The corrugated structure favours vortex dynamics, higher growth of shear layers and higher entrainment of ambient air. ANSYS Fluent was used to conduct Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) simulations at a pressure-based solver and SST k-o turbulence model with a design Mach number of 1.6. The important parameters of performance that were examined are jet core length, centerline pressure decay, and turbulent flow structures. Findings show that the corrugated tab geometry greatly enhances the rate of pressure decay and the spreading of jets, increasing mixing efficiency with less performance compromise. The results indicate that triangular corrugated tabs present a viable and energy-saving passive flow control methodology that can be applied in high-speed aerospace engines that demand less noise and thermal emissions.

title: Exploring the Impact of Cultural Diversity on Female Employees' Work Behavior: An Empirical Study at King Fahd University of Petroleum and Minerals
authors: Tahani bin Ashwan, Dr. Khairi Omar
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The study aimed to examine the impact of cultural diversity on work behavior among female employees at King Fahd University of Petroleum and Minerals in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, as well as to identify the reality and level of the study variables. The descriptive analytical quantitative approach was employed, based on quantitative questionnaires, and applied to a representative sample of 300 female employees randomly selected from a research population of 1,000 employees across various administrative and academic departments of the university. The findings revealed a significant and positive impact of cultural diversity on employees' work behavior. Among the dimensions, the diversity of cultural environments ranked first in terms of influence, followed by the social and cultural background dimension. The results also indicated variations in employees' perceptions of both cultural diversity and work behavior attributable to demographic variables, where Saudi employees, those older, more experienced, and holding a bachelor's degree expressed stronger affirmation of cultural diversity and work behavior compared to their non-Saudi counterparts. The study recommended strengthening internal communication channels among employees from diverse cultural backgrounds, encouraging the formation of culturally diverse work teams, and organizing regular meetings for experience exchange.

title: A Simplified Deep Learning Pipeline for Comprehensive Lung Disease Identification
authors: D.Chaithanya, Bhuvanagiri Karunakar Reddy, BoyaReddy Gari Dharmesh, Balineni Abhiraami, C. Madhusudhan Rao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Early and precise detection of lung diseases such as COVID-19, Pneumonia and Tuberculosis is a big challenge in the medical field. This current framework uses a simplified deep learning model pipeline which combines transfer learning with adaptive feature optimization for identifying multi-class lung disease using chest X-ray images. In this proposed method, we use a pre-trained lightweight convolutional network model to extract deep semantic representations from images and then refine them using an attention-based system to maintain a balance between structural and texture information in balanced. Unlike complex ensemble frameworks, the proposed model while maintaining a high diagnostic performance but still focuses on being computationally efficient. A lot of experiments were conducted on open-source dataset shows that the proposed method achieves a higher accuracy, precision, and recall more than the traditional CNN-based and manually created feature fusion models. The results confirm that this proposed pipeline provides a strong, easy-to-interpret and resource-efficient solution for automated lung disease classification, suitable for real-time clinical environments.

title: Implementation of Numerical Simulation of Solid Insulation Curing Processes in High-Voltage Structures into the Educational Process
authors: Dmitry Golubev, Alexandr Nesterenko, Dmitriy Kruglikov, Sergey Graudyn, Elizaveta Kiseleva
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper presents an approach for integrating numerical simulation of epoxy curing in cast solid insulation into higher-education training for electric power and electrical engineering students. The proposed educational workflow combines heat transfer, curing reaction kinetics, and structural mechanics within a unified finite-element multiphysics model and links model setup decisions (geometry preparation, meshing quality, domain selection, and boundary conditions) to physically interpretable outputs. A straight 1 kV busduct section with cast epoxy insulation is used as a case study to demonstrate typical result fields, including temperature, degree of cure, chemical shrinkage, and von Mises equivalent stress, as well as the identification of near-surface and transition regions prone to elevated residual stresses. Parametric simulations over a curing-temperature range provide a practical basis for teaching process selection using quantitative criteria, such as achieving a target degree of cure within a specified time while limiting shrinkage and stress levels. The presented methodology supports the development of transferable CAE competencies and strengthens the connection between fundamental coursework and applied design of high- voltage insulation structures.

title:
Short-Term PM
                    <sub>2.5</sub>
                    Prediction Using a Hybrid SSA-Transformer-BiLSTM Model
authors: Omar Ennayri, Zhou Jinzhi, Fadel Ihsane, Yassir Sbihi
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Deep learning models are increasingly applied in $\mathbf{P M}_{2.5}$ concentration prediction, but the majority of current models struggle with the complex nonlinear, multivariate, and spatio-temporal characteristics of $\mathbf{P M}_{2.5}$ sequences, particularly in capturing intricate temporal relations and deconstructing inherent signal components. Therefore in this study, a new hybrid model proposed, called the Singular Spectrum Analysis-Transformer-Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (SSA-Transformer-BiLSTM). This model first uses Singular Spectrum Analysis (SSA) to decompose the $\mathbf{P M}_{2.5}$ time series into isolated trend, oscillatory, and noise components and thereby derive meaningful temporal patterns. then uses a Transformer encoder with a multi-head self-attention mechanism to derive global dependencies and long-term temporal relationships. Finally, a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network handles the encoded representations for learning local temporal features in both directions for sequential learning. Empirical outcomes demonstrate the predictive benefit of proposed hybrid model, with excellent accuracy of an RMSE of 2.0134, MAE of 0.5801, SMAPE of 1.802, and an Adjusted R2 of 0.9985. This far outperforms several baseline and hybrid models, which proves the robustness and effectiveness of the model in dealing with the challenges of air pollution data.

title: Dynamic Method for Solving Non-Conservative Problems of Stability of Cantilever Rod
authors: Vladimir P. Radin, Viktor P. Chirkov, Elena V. Poznyak, Olga V. Novikova, Valerian E. Tsoy, Vasily S. Gudoshnikov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This article discusses the application of the dynamic method to the calculation of a cantilever rod subjected to both potential and non-conservative loads. The solution of the dynamic boundary value problem for determining the characteristic exponents is carried out using methods for minimizing functions of several variables. The behavior of the characteristic exponents under varying load parameters is studied to determine the type of instability loss. On the plane of loading parameters, the boundaries of the stability region are constructed: the divergence boundary and the flutter boundary. For constructing the boundaries of the stability region, another method is also applied, involving the representation of the solution to the perturbed motion equation as a series in terms of the mode shapes of the cantilever rod. The analysis of the location of the roots of the characteristic polynomial in this case is performed using the Routh-Hurwitz criterion.

title: Smart Traffic Safety: Predictive Modeling and Risk Factor Analysis with Explainable Deep Learning
authors: R.Jeberson Retna Raj, Suriyaa S V, Thrurakaprabakar T
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The work provides a single model of prediction of road traffic accidents and sorts out the most powerful risk factors that precondition the probability of cases being experienced. This method combines the global spatial-temporal feature learning with the dynamic policy-based assessment in an effort to improve the forecasting credibility. An extractor of features that uses transformers to record heterogeneous traffic patterns and an assessment layer that uses reinforcing thoughts to increase decision stability in different roadway scenarios, are strengthened. To guarantee clear-cut reasoning of predicting accidents, an explainability mechanism measures the relative relevance of the contributing factors and risk attribution becomes understandable. Real-world traffic dataset evaluation shows that the predictive performance is high, being better than traditional baselines and making high accuracy in predicting scenarios that have potential accidents. It has been found that the reduction of the predicted probability of accidents is significant when one focuses on and enhances the identified influential factors. This system is an integrated prediction-explainability system to provide a flexible and realistic system to manage intelligent transportation safety in order to both precisely predict events as well as provide practical insight into how risk conditions are deterministic.

title: Using Simulators in e-Learning to Completing Learning Tasks of Varying Difficulty Levels
authors: Tatyana Shindina, Nina Knyazeva, Natalia Usmanova
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This article examines a methodology for mastering skills in solving production problems, moving from a simple to a more complex task within a thematic section of an academic discipline, implemented through e-learning. The materials describe practical experience in organizing student training in the "Logistics" discipline, which focuses on developing enterprise supply skills and calculating the optimal inventory levels based on specified production needs. The aim of the study is to review and replicate the experience of practicing skills in an e-learning format, as well as to describe the impact of visual learning and improving the memorability of educational material through the use of simulators, which help increase student engagement in the learning process. The paper considers the following objectives: organizing educational materials in order of increasing complexity, increasing student engagement in the learning process through game-based teaching methods, and increasing the level of material mastery through the use of training simulators. The presented materials have been implemented in the educational programs of the National Research University "MPEI". The training simulator "Material Inventories" described in the article is registered in the Register of the Intellectual Property Fund No. 25-1430 dated April 24, 2025.

title: Implementation of a Digital Placement Management System for Academic Institutions
authors: P.Sumalatha, V.Sankarnath, N.Deepika, N.Leelanjali, T.Priyanka
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The increasing demand for employable graduates has highlighted the importance of a systematic and technology-driven placement process. Traditional recruitment practices in academic institutions are often burdened with manual procedures, data repetition, and limited support for structured training. To overcome these challenges, this paper presents a campus Placement and Recruitment Framework that integrates skill enhancement modules with automated recruitment workflows. The system maintains comprehensive student profiles and enables efficient communication between institutions and recruiters. Automated features such as real-time updates, candidate filtering, and digital record management reduce administrative workload and enhance process transparency. Initial implementation demonstrates improvements in student readiness, placement success rates, and efficiency of recruitment drives. This approach offers a scalable model that reinforces industry–academia partnerships and contributes to sustainable employability outcomes. The outcomes show that compared to traditional methods, students are more ready, placement success rates are higher, and recruitment drives are more efficient. Overall, this method creates a scalable and long-lasting model that makes it easier for businesses and schools to work together and helps students get better jobs.

title: Design and Simulation of Multi-UAV Cooperative Target Localization Algorithm Based on Multi-Source Sensors
authors: Yilin Dong, Jianyong Yang, Xiaozhou Guo, Yanling Ji, Xiaojun Xing
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: Target localization is one of the critical military applications of UAVs. This paper addresses the common problem of difficulty in obtaining high-precision real-time target positioning when multi-UAVs carry sensors such as laser and vision sensors at the same altitude for ground/sea surface target positioning. It proposes a positioning method that uses sensors of three UAVs to obtain target positioning and processes the target positioning results with particle filtering to improve the target positioning accuracy. Firstly, a model of the ground/sea surface target is established. Then, the approximate target positioning results are obtained through Multi-Source Sensors carried by three unmanned aerial vehicles. Finally, the particle filter algorithm is adopted to process the target positioning results. By comparing the simulation results before and after filtering, it is verified that the adopted filtering method can greatly improve the target positioning accuracy.

title: IoT-based Wearable Elderly Assistance System with Real-Time Fall Detection and Emergency Alerts using Deep LSTM Prediction Algorithm
authors: Mahesh M, Monicka E, Pavithra V, Mahilvarasi M, Priyadharshini S, Rathika S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Falls among elderly individuals can lead to severe injuries and delayed medical assistance. With the advancement of smart healthcare, IoT-based wearable devices enable continuous monitoring of mobility and physiological parameters. However, traditional threshold-based and conventional machine-learning approaches often suffer from low detection accuracy, high false alarm rates, and poor adaptability to diverse motion patterns. This paper presents an IoT-based Wearable Elderly Assistance System using a deep Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) prediction algorithm for accurate fall detection and timely emergency alerts. Accelerometer and gyroscope data are collected and analyzed using LSTM to capture temporal motion features, and alerts are transmitted via the cloud. Experimental comparisons with conventional classifiers show improved detection accuracy, higher sensitivity, and reduced false positives. The proposed system enhances safety, reliability, and rapid response in elderly healthcare.

title: An Intelligent Multilingual Text Transformation Framework for Inclusive Digital Communication
authors: Geetha, J. Aruna, N. Manjushri, S. Harishini, S. Dhanya Shree, K. Yazhini Prithika
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The high rate of worldwide digital communication has necessitated advanced language translation systems with the ability to overcome language barriers in multi-ethnic population. Since the world has a wide variety of languages, with more than 7,000, and the digital resources are predominantly written in both English and several major languages, a significant demand to have correct, reference inconceivable and culturally sensitive translation systems. This letter is a detailed overview of the current nerve machine translation system that capitalizes on the usage of modern natural language processing technologies, deep learning architecture, and continuous teaching system that translates English text into foreign languages without affecting the integrity and cultural peculiarities of economics. We conduct a study of the application of models that are based on transformers, attention and adaptive teaching algorithms which achieve the rate of 8595 percent translation accuracy between major language pairs. In the suggested system, there will be the real -time processing capabilities, pertinent understanding and user response integration to keep on enhancing the quality of translation. We provide compelling gains in both translation flow, cultural appropriateness and user satisfaction over both conventional statistical machine translation methods cross-lingual communication, relevant translation. The proposed framework emphasizes robustness in low-resource language settings by leveraging transfer learning and multilingual pretraining strategies. This enables effective knowledge sharing across language families, reduces data dependency, and improves translation performance for underrepresented languages, thereby promoting inclusive access to digital information across diverse linguistic communities.

title: Mapping Research on Lighting and Cognitive Performance in Educational Buildings: A Bibliometric Analysis (2015-2025)
authors: Shaikha Buti Almadhloum, Emad S. Mushtaha, Shouib Mabdeh
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Lighting conditions are increasingly examined as environmental factors influencing students' cognitive performance in educational settings; however, the evidence base remains dispersed across disciplines, lighting typologies, and regional contexts. This study presents a bibliometric analysis of Scopusindexed, English-language journal articles published between January 2015 and March 2025 that examine links between lighting conditions and cognitive or learning-related outcomes in educational environments. Using defined database filters, 536 records were screened, resulting in a final dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3 0}$</tex> articles. Results indicate sustained publication growth after 2019, reflecting rising interest in lighting-cognition relationships. Research output is concentrated in building science and sustainability-oriented journals, led by China, the United States, and the United Kingdom, indicating geographically clustered activity. Keyword co-occurrence mapping reveals a strongly student-centered structure dominated by students, lighting, and cognitive performance, suggesting a focus on learner outcomes but a narrow framing around specific lighting variables. The field also exhibits structural fragmentation: while daylightfocused studies account for 40 % of the literature, only 6.7 % examine daylight, artificial lighting, and cognitive performance concurrently, limiting integrated understanding of real classroom conditions. Regional representation remains uneven, with minimal coverage in hot, humid Gulf climates and no identified studies in the United Arab Emirates, highlighting a gap in climate-responsive educational design research. Overall, the study maps the field's intellectual structure, identifies overlooked intersections, and provides a quantitative foundation for more integrative and context-sensitive future research.

title: Application of the Multi-Agent approach and Its Software Implementations in the Educational Process of Training IT Specialists
authors: Konstantin Aksyonov, Olga Aksyonova, Elena Aksyonova, Lina Sun
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This work is dedicated to the analysis of teaching hybrid modeling in the educational process at The Engineering School of Information Technologies, Telecommunications and Control Systems, Ural Federal University. The subject area, directions, and training profiles for students are focused on IT specialists and developers of specialized software of various classes: from business process automation systems, question-answer systems (robotic call systems, chatbots), to the development of enterprise automation systems, technological production systems, and management systems of various levels automated control systems (corporate information systems, Enterprise Resource Planning systems - ERP), Process Control Systems (PCS), Manufacturing Execution Systems - MES and Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition systems (SCADA). The training is aimed at studying the theoretical foundations of hybrid modeling and the skills of software development and design. As part of laboratory work, domestic software is studied: the multi-method simulation environment AnyLogic, the dynamic simulation system BPsim.MAS, the decision support system BPsim.DSS, the management automation and simulation system SimInTech, an automated system for metallurgical product output, the question-answer system TWIN, and the simulation modeling system Alina GPSS.

title: A Deep Learning-Driven System for the Segmentation, Visualization, and Diagnostic Augmentation of Cerebral Tumors from Volumetric MRI
authors: D. Rajesh, J. Swapna, S Bindu Priya, P Pradeepika
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Accurate detection and clear delineation of cerebral tumors in volumetric MRI scans are of great importance in neuro-oncology for diagnosis, treatment planning, and patient management. However, the manual segmentation of brain tumors is usually a time-consuming task with high interobserver variability, apart from the complexity added by the three-dimensional structure of the brain. To overcome these challenges, a deep learning-based system is presented in this study, aimed at automatic segmentation, visualization, and diagnostic support of cerebral tumors with the help of multimodal MRI data. The proposed framework relies on a 3D-CNN architecture that processes multi-modal MRI sequences comprising T1, T2, FLAIR, and contrast-enhanced images. By modeling both spatial and contextual information across slices, the model accurately identifies diverse tumor regions such as the enhancing tumor, peritumoral edema, and necrotic core. Besides segmentation, the system is also equipped with an interactive 3D visualization module that allows clinicians to perform real-time reconstructions of tumor structures for better and more intuitive viewing. Furthermore, a diagnostic augmentation component extracts radiomic and morphological features from the segmented regions to assist in tumor grading and malignancy prediction. Experimental results on the BraTS benchmark dataset demonstrate high performance with strong Dice similarity coefficients and low Hausdorff distances compared to state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, the diagnostic module results have shown high sensitivity and specificity to differentiate between high-grade and low-grade gliomas. Overall, this research provides a comprehensive and reliable end-to-end framework that enhances clinical workflows with automation, advanced visualization, and intelligent diagnostic assistance.

title: Privilege Level Dynamics and Their Impact on Conditional Branch Prediction Performance in FaaS
authors: Louka Yerly, Arthur Perais
publish: IEEE Computer Architecture Letters
abstract: Cloud providers are proposing microservice-based infrastructures in the form of Function-as-a-Service (FaaS) offerings. These workloads are very short-lived and spend significant time in privileged execution as they communicate collectively through the network. Current processors are not well adapted to running these workloads as the many predictors and caches used to provide performance struggle to warmup due to the short duration of Functions. This work thoroughly analyzes the interplay of user and privileged execution in a set of FaaS workloads and its impact on conditional branch prediction, which is a critical performance and efficiency feature. We first highlight that execution mode changes at high frequency, and that few (around 10<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{3}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) instructions are executed in a given mode before switching. We then provide possible directions to improve CBP performance on such workloads.

title: 100° non-contact retinal imaging in mice using a multimodal vis-OCT and SLO system
authors: Siyu Song, Guangru Ben Liang, Shuibin Ni, Tristan T. Hormel, Yakub Aqib Bayhaqi, Yukun Guo, Min Gao, Siyu Chen, Yifan Jian, J. Peter Campbell, Yali Jia
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: This study reports a non-contact, multimodal visible-light OCT (vis-OCT) and scanning laser ophthalmoscopy (SLO) system for in vivo mouse retinal imaging that achieves a single-shot 100° (visual angle) field of view (FOV). By performing repeated B-scans at the same location, the system enables OCT angiography (OCTA). With a long working distance (6.4 mm), the system also allows ready realignment without disrupting native ocular optics or physiology. We validated system performance in transgenic mice expressing fluorescently labeled microglia, including both healthy mice and oxygen-induced retinopathy (OIR) models. Images from the device clearly resolve individual nerve fiber bundles, inter-plexus capillaries, and individual microglia. Hyaloid vessel shadows and tortuous vessels in OIR mice are also discernible. Together, these capabilities provide a platform for the comprehensive assessment of vascular dysfunction and microglia behavior, offering a promising tool for investigating retinal diseases that affect the peripheral regions.

title: Federated Reinforcement Learning for Efficient Mobile Crowdsensing under Incomplete Information
authors: Sumedh J. Dongare, Patrick Weber, Andrea Ortiz, Walid Saad, Oliver Hinz, Anja Klein
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Mobile crowdsensing (MCS) is a distributed sensing architecture that utilizes existing sensors on mobile units (MUs) to perform sensing tasks. A mobile crowdsensing platform (MCSP) publishes the sensing tasks and the MUs decide if they want to participate in their execution in exchange for money. The MCS system is characterized by its dynamic nature in which the task requirements, the MUs’ availability, and their available resources change over time. The MUs aim to find an efficient task participation strategy to maximize their income while the MCSP focuses on maximizing the number of completed tasks. As optimal task participation strategies require perfect non-causal information about the MCS system, which is unavailable in realistic scenarios, the main challenge in MCS is to find an efficient task participation strategy for the MUs under incomplete information. To this aim, a novel fully decentralized federated deep reinforcement learning algorithm, termed FDRL-PPO is proposed. FDRL-PPO enables every MU to learn its own task participation strategy based on its experiences, available resources, and preferences, without relying on perfect non-causal information about the MCS system. To replenish their batteries, the MUs rely on energy harvesting. As a result, their available energy varies over time, leading to varying availability and fragmented learning experiences. To mitigate these challenges, the proposed approach leverages federated learning, enabling MUs to collaboratively improve their models without having to share private raw data like their own experiences. By exchanging only learned models, MUs collectively compensate for individual limitations, and find more scalable, robust, and efficient task participation strategies. Comprehensive evaluations on both synthetic and real-world datasets show that FDRL-PPO consistently outperforms benchmark algorithms in terms of task completion ratio, fairness in task completion, energy consumption, and number of conflicting proposals.

title: CIRNet: Compact Iterative Refinement Network for Real-time Stereo Matching
authors: Haopeng Wang, Zhanhong Chen, Yu Zhang, Yanbiao Sun, Jigui Zhu
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Stereo matching is crucial for 3D perception in autonomous driving and generalist robotics. Recent advancements transitioned from cost volume method to iterative refinement approach, offering superior details and spatial consistency. However, iterative methods remain constrained by expensive matching encoding and numerous refinement iterations, limiting latency-sensitive practical application. To unleash the accuracy superiority of iterative paradigms in real-time systems, we present Compact Iterative Refinement Network (CIRNet). Specifically, we design Compact Correlation Encoding (CCE) to allocate essential matching evidence by functional attributes, constructing global geometric context at deep level and fine-grained local similarity at shallow level to reduce redundant computation and provide reliable coarse initialization. Meanwhile, we introduce Staged Iterative Refinement (SIR) using early coarse correction for large residuals and a lightweight shared operator for fine details, improving the accuracy-latency trade-off and reaching near-saturated accuracy at lower latency. Evaluations on SceneFlow and KITTI show CIRNet sets new state-of-the-art in balancing accuracy and efficiency. Furthermore, CIRNet exhibits robust cross-domain generalization on multiple real-world datasets and practical deployability on embedded edge platform.
